DE FREITAS, ANTHONY, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1869; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn; ag. pol. mag., Grenada, 1900 and 1901; ag. atty.-gen., St. Vincent, 1902; ag. regis., sup. ct., Grenada, 1902 to 1904; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, May to Oct., 1904; stip. mag. and coroner, 1st dist., St. Lucia, Oct., 1904; ag. atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 1st July, 1906; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 4th Apr., 1907; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, Oct., 1908 to July, 1909; ag. admstr., St. Vincent, Jan.-Feb. and Apr.-May, 1909; ag. admstr., St. Lucia, Nov.-Dec., 1909; Jan., 1910, May-Oct., 1910, and Jan.-Feb., 1911; ag. col. sec., St. Lucia, July-Oct. and Oct.-Dec., 1911; ag. admstr., St. Lucia, Oct., 1911, Jan.-Feb., 1912, Mar.-Aug., 1912, and Oct.-Nov., 1912; ag. col. sec., St. Lucia, Aug.-Oct., 1912, and Nov., 1912; ag. admstr., St. Lucia, Aug.-Oct., 1913.

DE GAYE, JULES AUGUSTIN, F.L.S., F.E.S.—B. 1872; ed. at Royal coll., Mauritius; ag. asst. 2nd cls. mast., Royal coll., Mauritius, 1st Sept., 1891; asst. 2nd cls. mast., 4th Jan., 1893; 2nd cls. mast., 26th July, 1894; 2nd mast., Victoria schl., Seychelles, 25th Apr., 1902; ag. headmast. and ag. inspr. of schla., 7th Apr. to 12th Aug., 1908; ag. headmast., Victoria schl., 2nd June to 12th Aug., 1909; mem. of Fisheries enquiry comtee., Nov., 1908; mem. of comtee, for re-organiztn. of educn., Seychelles, Aug., 1909; science and math. mast., King's schl., Lagos, S. Nigeria, 8th Feb., 1910; ag. principal, King's Coll., 22nd Dec., 1910, to 13th Apl., 1911, and from Mar. to Aug., 1913; author of a Geography of the Seychelles Is., a Geographical Reader of Mauritius, and a text-book on the Science of Common Things and Nature Study.

DE GLANVILLE, BERTRAM GEORGE.—B. 1886; ed. Merchant Taylors' sch., Crosby, and Worcester coll., Oxford; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1908; asst. coll. of cust. and pol. mag., Trincomalie, Dec., 1909; pol. mag., Matale, June, 1911; ag. additional comsnr. of requests and adutl. pol. mag., Kurunegala, Aug., 1911; office asst. to gov't. agt., W. Prov., Oct., 1911; pol. mag., Panadura, Nov., 1911; asst. settmtn. offr., Feb., 1912; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Mar., 1912; seconded for serv. under the excise comsnr., June, 1912; ag. comsnr. of excise, N. Divn., Jan., 1913.

DE HAMEL, HARGRAVE BARRY.—Major, Londonderry Artillery; seconded to G. Coast Hausas; July, 1895; Ashanti expdn., Dec., 1895 (star); asst. supt. of pol., Straits Stltms., June, 1897; seconded as ag. asst. comsnr. of pol., Kinta, Perak, Dec., 1905; supt. of pol., Penang, May, 1907.
DE JAGER, SAMUEL JACOBUS, B.A., LL.B., Camb.—B. 1879; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1902; admitted advocate, O.R.C., 1903; atty.-gen., O.F.S., 1911.

DE KRETZER, EDWARD, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1854; employed in col. sec.'s off., Ceylon, 8th May, 1872; asst. aud.-gen., 1st Jan., 1901; ass't cont. of rev., Mar., 1907; sec. savings bank, ii addition to his duties, Aug., 1908.

DELAFAYE, SIR LOUIS VICTOR, KT. BACH. (1898).—B. 1842; called to bar Mid. Tem., 1863; mun. councillor, St. Louis, 1870; prov. judge, sup. ct., Mauritius, Mar., 1879; mem. prison bd., Jan., 1884; mem. civ. ser. inquiry comsnr., Feb., 1884; mem. civ. ser. coman., 1884-95; ag. puisne judge, 1884-5, and May, 1890, to July, 1892; Q.C., Aug., 1892; puisne judge, Nov., 1894; ag. chief justice, June, 1898; chief judge, Dec., 1898; ret.; 1912.
DE LIVERA, WALTER.—B. 1863; local dir. Ceylon civ. ser.; comsnr. of requests and pol. mag., Chilaw, Feb., 1898; Gampola, July, 1902; dist. judge, Kegalla, May, 1910; dep. fisca., Colombo, Aug., 1913.
DELMEGE, J. DE G.—Ed. Haileybury Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; asst. collr., Uganda Prot., Dec., 1908.

DE MARTIN, G. P. —B.A.. Trin. Coll., Dublin; jun. asst. master, Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1904; senr. asst. master, 1st May, 1910; lecturer, H. Kong Tech. Inst., 1908-1912; ag. inspr. of English schla., 1913.

DEMUTH, WALTER EDGAR.—Ed. Clifton and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; M.A.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1906; asst. to atty.-gen., Nyasaland Prot., Aug., 1913; ag. atty.-gen., Feb., 1914; ag. judge, July-Sept., 1914.

DENHAM, HON. DIGBY FRANK.—B. 1859; M.L.A. for Oxley, Queensland, since 1902; mem. for agric. and pub. wks., Sept., 1903; mem. for agric. and rlyws., Jan., 1906; resigned, Feb., 1907; premier of Queensland since Feb., 1911.

DENHAM, EDWARD BRANDIS.—B. 1876; ed. at Malvern and Merton Coll., Oxford (exhibitor); B.A., 1899; cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1899; extra of asst. gov't. agt., West. Prov., Dec., 1899; asst. gov't. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1902; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., May, 1904, Mar., 1905; asst. gov't. agt. N. Prov. for pearl fishery work, Mar., 1905; 2nd asst. col. sec. and sec., agric. bd., May, 1906; priv. sec. to Sir A. Ashmore, O.A.G., Sept., 1905; organising sec., agric. bd., Dec., 1906; landmg survr., cust., Colombo, June, 1906; dist. judge, Negombo, Sept., 1908; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1909; and priv. sec. to Sir H. Clifford O.A.G., June, 1909; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., ii addition to his own duties, 2nd May to 6th June, 1910; seconded as supt. of census, Aug., 1910; priv. sec. to Sir H. Clifford, O.A.G., June, 1911; attached to C.O., 1st Jan. to 13th Oct., 1913; prin. asst. col. sec., Ceylon, 25th Apr., 1914; joint author of "Rubber in the East."

DENMAN, LORD.—3rd Baron (cr. 1834), Rt. Hon. Sir Thomas Denman, P.C. (1907); G.C.M.G. (1911); K.C.V.O. (1909); b. 1874; educ. Sandhurst; lieut., reserve of offrs.; late lieut. Royal Scots, and major, Middlesex I.Y.; served in S. Africa war (wounded, Queen's medal, 3 clasps lord-in-waiting in ordinary, 1905-7; capt. Loc. corps of gentlemen-at-arms, 1907-11; J.P., Sussex; gov. gen. and comdr.-in-chief, Commonwealth of Australia, 1911-1914.

DENMAN, ROBERT.—B. 1860; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Guy's Hosp.; M.R.C.S. Eng., 1886; L.S.A. Lond., 1886; D.P.H., Cantab., 1905; gov't. med. offr., Seychelles, 1896; ch. med. offr., 1900; health offr., Perak, 1908; director of med. and heal't dept., Mauritius, 1911.

DENNE, ALURED BARKLEY.—B. 1862; ed. at Harrow and R.M.A., Woolwich; lieut., R.A., 1882; major, 1900; ret. pay, 1903; passed advanced cl., Ordnance coll.; has served on instructional staff, R.M. academy, and insyn. staff, R. Arsenal; ch. insyn. of explosives, Transvaal, 1st Jan., 1903; also for Natal, 1st June, 1910; also for the U. of S. Africa.

DENNIS, R. E.—Ed. at Marlborough; served in the Congo, 1879-1902; insyn. of forests, S. Nigeria, 1902; asst. conservator of forests, 1st grade, 1904; deputy conservator, 2nd Mar., 1910; author of "Seven Years among the Fjord," "Notes on the Folklore of the Fjord," "At the Back of the Black Man's Mind," and "Nigerian Studies."
DENNISTON, HON. JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1845; puisne judge, N. Zealand, Feb., 1889; senior juiane judge, 1913.

DE NOON, GEORGE.—M.A., Aberdeen (Hons. Nat. Sci.); M.A., Cape Univ.; LL.B. Cape Univ.; clerical asst., res. mag.'s off., Swellendam, C. of G.H., Feb., 1897; mag.'s elk., Swellendam, May, 1897; elk. to high sheriff, Cape Town, July 1897; ag. elk. to Mr. Justice Solomon, and regis. of E. circuit, C. of G.H., Mar., 1900; elk., atty.-gen.'s off., Cape Town, July, 1900; examr., deeds off., Cape Town, Nov., 1900; asst. regis. of deeds, Transvaal, Jan., 1902; regis. of deeds, O.F.S., Nov., 1911.
DENT, E. J.—Asst. jun. staff survr., E.A.P., June, 1914; asst. dist. consnr., Dec., 1913.
DENT, FRANKLAND, M. Sc., Ph.D., F.I.C.—Ast. govt. analyst, Singapore, Aug., 1905; govt. analyst and science lecturer, S. Stlntms., Mar., 1906.

DE RINZY, GEORGE CASTRILLO.—B. 1865; ed. at St. Columba Coll., Co. Dublin; lieut., 3rd batt. E. Yorks. regt., 1885; transf. to Donegal Artil., 1887; capt., 1890; dist. insyn. of pol., B. Guiana, 1891; on spec. serv. to Venezuelan boundary (Uruan), 1895; county insyn. of pol., 1896; "extra" certif. musketry, Hythe, 1900; chief county insyn. of pol., and supt. of Georgeown fire brig., Nov., 1903; author of "Fireman's manual for B. Guiana"; ag. insyn.-gen. of pol. on several occasions; insyn.-gen. of pol., and col. omdng. local forces, B. Guiana; also insyn. of risons, 31st July, 1908.

DE ROME, F. J.—B. Sc. (Lond.); Borough road Coll., Isleworth, Lond., 1906-1908; teacher's certf. (with distinction) Bd. of Educn., Lond.; graduated B. Sc. Lond. Univ., Oct., 1908; Jena niv. (Germany), 1908-1909; mathematical and erman mast., St. Augustine's Benedictine Coll., amagate, Sept. to Dec., 1909; asst. mast., queen's Coll., Hong Kong, Jan., 1910; lecturer ech. Inst., 1910-1912.

DE ROS, COLIN JOSEPH FERNANDES STANISLAUS.—Ed. at Catholic gram. schl., Georgetown, B. Guiana; served as a volunteer, customs, 1st Apr. to 31st Dec., 1885; 6th cls. offr., 1st Jan., 1886; 5th cls. offr., 16th Dec., 1886; 4th cls. offr., 1st Sept., 1892; 3rd cls. offr., 4th June, 1896; 2nd cls. offr., 14th Sept., 1905; ag. offr. asst. to comptroller in excise matters, 1st June to 31st Aug., 1902; ag. 1st cls. offr., 9th Aug., 1906.

DE SARAH, J.H., C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1844; writer to the govt. of Ceylon, 1865; pol. mag. of Balapiti-madara, 1866; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1867; consnr., of requests, Colombo, 1868; dist. judge, Kurunegala, 1875; regis.-gen., Colombo, 1886; dist. judge, Kalutara, Dec., 1883; regis.-gen., May, 1886; dist. judge, Jaffna, Aug., 1891; ditto, Galle, Dec., 1891; ditto, Kandy, Aug., 1893; ret.

DE SARAH, RICHARD OWEN.—B. 1872; ag. office asst. to prin. collr. of customs, Ceylon, July, 1905; ag. landing survr., customs, Galle, Dec., 1907; supernumerary mem. of cls. V. of civ. ser., Jan., 1913; 3rd landing survr., Colombo customs, July, 1913.

DESBARATS, GEORGE JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1915), B. App. Sc., M. Can. Soc. C.E., P.L.S.—B. 1861; ed. Pub. Schls., Montreal, Terrebonne Coll., Montreal Polytechnic Sch. and Laval Univ.; asst. engr., Carillon Canal, 1878; asst. engr., office of ch. engr. of canals, Ottawa, 1886; insyn. of hyds., B. Columbia, 1892; in charge of hydrographic survey on River St. Lawrence, 1899; dir. govt. shipyard, Sorel, Quebec, 1901; dep. min. of marine and fisheries of Canada, 1909; dep. min. and comptroller of the naval service, 1910.

DESHON, EDWARD, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1836; mem. of land bd., Queensland; ensign in 68th L.I., June, 1854; lieut., Dec., 1854; served with 68th L.I. at siege and fall of Sebastopol, from Nov., 1854, to end of the war; medal and clasp, and Turkish medal; instructor of musketry to the 68th L.I. from Nov., 1857, to Oct., 1861; passed compet. exam. for admission to Staff Coll., in July, 1861; ret. by sale of coman. in Oct., 1861; audit.-gen., Queensland, 1890; ret. 1901.

DE SMIDT, HENRY, C.M.G. (1901), B.A. (Cape Univ.), F.S.S.—B. 1845; entered the service in Mar., 1865; permanent under col. sec. of the col. of Cape of Good Hope, and perm. head of convicts and prisons dept., holding in addition the following offices: (1) accounting offr., col. sec.'s dept.; (2) controller printing and statury.; (3) mem. civ. ser. consm.; (4) mem. tender bd.; (5) custodian stamps; (6) admstr. food and drugs act; (7) insyn. emigr. coolie ships. Was specially charged with the duties of dir. of the census of 1891; received congratulations of the govt., and was awarded an honorarium of 500l. specially voted by parlmt.; elected fellow of roy. statistical soc., 1892; asst. treasr., July, 1898; ret. 1904.

DEVANE, JAMES.—B. 1887; ed. Royal Univ., Dublin (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; pol. mag., Puttalum, July, 1910; pol. mag., Negombo, May, 1911; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avisawella, Nov., 1912; municipal mag., Colombo, Feb., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Galle, Aug., 1914.

DEVILLE, EDOUARD GASTON DANIEL.—D.T.S., LL.D., Toronto Univ., F.R.S.C.; B. 1849; apptd. mem. of the bd. of examrs. for Dominion land surveyors, Canada, 1879; insyn. of surveys, 1881; survr. gen. of Dominion lands, 1885.
DE VILLIERS, A. M. N.—Provincial sec., O.F.S., Union of South Africa, 1910.

DE VILLIERS, CHARLES WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—B. 1876; ed. at Boys Pub. Schl., Worcester, Cape Colony, and S. African Coll., Cape Town; admitted advocate of sup. ct., Cape Colony, Jan., 1901; law adviser to Transvaal govt., July, 1908; ag. atty.-gen., Transvaal, Oct., 1910, to May, 1911.

DE VILLIERS, HON. JACOB, B.A.—B. 1868; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Middle Temple; state atty., O.F.S., 1890-98; atty.-gen. and min. of mines, Transvaal, 1907-10; judge pres. of supreme ct. of S. Africa (Transvaal provincial division), and additional judge of appeal, May, 1910.
DE VILLIERS, JOHANNES HENDRICUS BRAND.—Clt. to prov. coun. and clk. to exec. comtce., O.F.S. Prov., Union of S. Africa, 1910.
DE WAAL, HON. SIR NICHOLAS FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1910).—M.L.A. for Colesberg, Cape Colony, 1904; col. sec., Cape Colony, 1908; administrator, Cape Province, 1910.

DEWAR, ARTHUR ROBERT JOHNSTONE.—B. 1869; ed. Mil. Coll., Oxford and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; major, 3rd R. Warwickshire Regt. (resig.); 29th Gord. Highrs., Sept., 1889; Matabeleland mtd. pol., June, 1895; New Zealand defence force, May, 1897; adjutant, 5th New Zealand Regt., Mar., 1900; served in S. African war (medal and five bars); Malay States Guides, Apl., 1902; adjutant, Selangor vol., Oct., 1902; supt. of prisons, Selangor, May, 1903; asst. supt. of pol., Singapore, Sept., 1905; ag. supt. of pol., Penang, Mar., 1910; ditto, Malacca, Aug., 1911; 2nd supt. of pol., Singapore, July, 1912.

DEWHIRST, CHARLES HAY.—Clt., architect-in-chief's office, S. Australia, July, 1878; asst. acctnt., wks. and blds. dept., Feb., 1881; ch. clk., pub. wks. comsrr's office, July, 1882; also sec. to supply and tender bd., 1899-1914; ag. sec. to comsrr. of pub. wks., 1st Jan., 1901 to 31st Aug., 1902; sec. to comsrr. of pub. wks. and min. of marine, July, 1914.
DE WET, HON. N. J., K.C.—Minister of justice, Union of S. Africa, 1913.

DE WILTON, MAJOR ALBERT WALTER.—B. 1862; licut., Lanark Militia, 1881; Roy. Mil. Coll., Sandhurst, 1882; passed out 5th with hons., receiving a prize and five special certifs.; licut., Connaught Rangers, 10th Mar., 1883; licut., Indian Army, 1886; capt., 1894; major, 10th July, 1901; retired, 1903; served in Burma campaign, 1885-1889 (medal and two clasps); inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, 29th Oct., 1898; thanked by govt. for economies effected in pol. force; on special secret mission to "Oil Islands" to enquire into grievances of inhabitants, 1901; inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Ceylon, 18th Aug., 1902; inspr.-gen. of prisons and supt. of convict establishment, Ceylon, 16th July, 1905.
DHONDY, SAYAKSHA DHUNJISHA.—B. 1889; B.A., Oxon.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 19th Dec., 1913; office asst. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, Jan., 1914.

DIAS, FELIX REGINALD, M.A., LL.M. Trin. Hall, Camb.—Called to the bar, Inner Tem., May, 1887; pol. mag. and comsrr. of requests, Gampola, Ceylon, Oct., 1889; crown counsel for the island, July, 1893; additnl. dist. judge, Colombo, July, 1900; ag. dist. judge, Colombo, Apl., 1907; ag. dist. judge, Kandy, Nov., 1907; dist. judge, Kandy, Jan., 1908.

DICKSON, JOHN QUAYLE, D.S.O.—B. 1860; ed. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man; capt., Field Intell., S. African War, 1900-02; compensation offr., native claims, O.R.C., 1902; offr. in charge of native repatriation, 1902; advisor for native affairs to govt. of O.R.C., 1903-09; mem. of S. African native affairs comn., 1903-05; res. comsrr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Prot. and deputy comsrr. for the Western Pacific, 16th June, 1909.
DICKSON, T. A.—Customsasst., E.A.P., 1909; asst. dist. comsrr., 1911.

DILLON, ALBERT BARROW.—B. 1867; ed. Queen's Roy. Coll.; clk. to inspr. of schls., B. Hond., 1893; ag. clk. to auditor and in treasy. and customs, 1893; clk. to col. engnr., 1896; ag.-insp. of schls., 1895-8; 2nd clk., treasy. and cust., 1897; insp. of schls., June, 1898; ag. supt. hot station, 28th Sept. to 31st Dec., 1904; ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk. of couns., 29th June, 1905, to 14th Jan., 1906.

DISCOMBE, JOHN.—Apptd., after compet exam., 3rd class clk., sup. ct., Gibraltar, 1892; 2nd class clk., 1899; asst. regisr. and dep. clk. of arraigns, 1900; called to the bar, Gray's Inn; regisr. and clk. of arraigns, admistr.-gen., c. of J.P., and mem. of cemetery comtce., 1911.
DIXON, CHARLES WILLIAM.—B. 1888; ed. at Clifton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st cl. mods., 1909; 1st cl. Lit. Hum., 1911; apptd. after compet exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1911.

DOBIE, EDWARD DAVID, I.S.O. (1911).—B. 1857; barrister, etc., sup. ct., Tasmania, admitted July, 1882; joined civ. ser., Tasmania, Mar., 1883; was partly drafterman and afterwards solr.-gen., from Mar. to May, 1887; clk. solr. from 30th May, 1887, to 1890; sec., law dept., to 1893; then recorder, Launceston, to 1st Apr., 1891; solr.-gen. and crown solr., and grand juror, 1st Apr., 1901; puisne judge, sup. ct. of Tasmania, 1914.
DOBBS, CECIL MOORE.—Ed. St. Columba Coll., Rathfarnham, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. Classics (honours); asst. collr., E. Afr. Prot., 25th Oct., 1906; asst. dist. comsrr., 1910.
DOCKER, E. B., M.A.—Formerly crown prosecutor, New South Wales; dist. court judge, 1881; apptd. judge to hold court at Norfolk Island, 1894.

DODDS, JAMES HUGH HAMILTON.—B. 1890; ed. at private schls. and Eastbourne Coll.; enlisted in I.Y. for serv. in S. Africa, Jan., 1901; recd. coman. as licut. in I.Y. 12th Oct., 1901; resigns coman. and granted hon. rank of licut. in Arty., 6th Feb., 1902 (Queen's medal, five clasps); licut., S.A.C., 6th Feb., 1902; resident J.P., Transval, 7th Mar., 1905; sub.-inspr., S.A.C., 1st July, 1906; retrenched from S.A.C., 31st July, 1907; licut., Bedfordshire I.Y., 1st Nov., 1907; asst. dist. offr., Somaliland Prot., Sept., 1908; dist. offr., Somaliland Prot., Apl., 1910; represented H.M.'s comsrr. of Somaliland on mission to Abyssinia, July-Aug., 1910; seconded for service in Abyssinia, to be vice-cons. Harrar.
DODDS, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1854; M.P. (Edin.), 1879, gold medallist, M.B. and C.M., 1876; 1st class honours, D.Sc., 1878; inspr. of asyls. and med. supt. of Valkenberg asyl., Cap., 1889.
DOHERTY, A. G.—Veterinary offr. E.A.P., Sept., 1906.

DOHERTY, HON. CHARLES JOSEPH, K.C., D.C.L., LL.D.—B. 1855; ed. Christian Brother School, St. Mary (Jesuit) Coll., McGill Univ., at Montreal; advocate; judge of the sup. ct., Quebec, 1891 to 1906; elected to H. of Canada, 1908; re-elec., 1911, representing St. Ann's division, Montreal City; member of... Privy Coun. for Canada and min. of just. in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911.
DONALD, RANALD.—Uganda Rly. mag., 9th Oct., 1900; mag., Nairobi, E. Africa Prot., 13th Sept., 1902; inspr.-gen. of pol., 24th Nov., 1905; mag., 1908.
DONNAN, J., C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1837; mast. attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, 1863; was comdr. of govt. steamers, "Manchester" and "Pearl," from July, 1859; ret., 1902.

DONNELLY, W. J. S.—Surv.-gen., Newfoundland, to 1882; rec.-gen., 1882; resig., 1889; M.L.A. for Placentia, 1893; rec. gen. and mem. exec. coun., Apr. to Dec., 1894; M.H.A., Placentia, 1897; rec-gen., 1898; inspr. of cust., 1902.
DOORLY, A. N.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912.

DORAN, PIERCE TROLLIP.—Field asst. to conservator of forests, Transkeian conservancy, Cape, 1894; elk. and acctnt. to conservator, 1896; dist. forest offr., Umtata, 1898; ditto, Butterworth, 1903; 3rd grade conservator of forests, Transkeian conservancy, 1st July, 1910.

DORMEHL, PIETER JOSEPH.—Clk., C.O., Cape, 1891; elk., agric. dept., 1892; elk. to conservator of forests, Western Conservancy, 1892; ag. dist. forest offr., Uitvlugt, 1898; dist. forest offr., Western Conservancy, 1899; sent by colonial govt. to undergo forest training at Coopers' Hill, Germany and France, 1899-1900; dist. forest offr. in charge of working plans, Knysna Conservancy, 1902; 3rd grade conservator of forests, Western Conservancy, 1906.
DOUGHTY, ARTHUR GEORGE, C.M.G. (1905).—M.A., Litt.D.—B. 1860; priv. sec. to min. of pub. wks., Quebec, Canada, 1896; librarian, Quebec, 1900; Dominion archivist, Canada, 1904.

DOUGHTY, CHARLES EDGAR.—B. 1891; apptd. after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd div.; served temporarily in L.G.B., Jan. to Mar., 1909; bd. of agric., Mar., 1909, to Mar., 1910; B. of T., Mar., 1910 to 1911; apptd. to C.O., Mar., 1911.
DOUGLAS, R. STAIR.—Cadet, Sarawak serv., Nov., 1896; asst. res., June, 1899; res. 2nd cl., Jan., 1902; 2nd cls. res., Baram dist., 1904.

DOUGLAS-JONES, C. D.—Ed. Harrow; priv. sec. to res. comsnr., S. Rhodesia, Aug., 1898; lieut., S. Rhodesia volrs., July, 1908; capt., May, 1910; musketry instructor's certif., Hythe, 1908; sec. to res. comsnr., Rhodesia, Apl., 1911.

DOUGLASS, WALTER JOHN, B.A., LL.B.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Camb.; admitted solr., 1889; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1897-1900; admitted solr., Leeward Is., 1901; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1903; Leeward Is. bar, Jan., 1904; stip. ag. and coroner, 1st dist., St. Lucia, Oct., 1907; g. atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 4th Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; sole assessor of income tax, 1910-1912; stip. ag., Berbice dist., B. Guiana, 1st Apl., 1912.

DOWER, EDWARD EBENZER.—Clk., ch. mag.'s T., Kokstad, C. Colony, Oct., 1891; Matatiele, in., 1896; nat. affairs offr., Aug., 1896; prin. k., 1900; ch. elk., July, 1901; also acctng. offr., ime Minister's dept., July, 1904; asst. sec. to t. affairs dept., July, 1906; sec. to ditto, Sept., 1907; head of dept. of native affairs, Union of nth Africa, 1910.

DOWNER, THE HON. SIR J. W., K.C.M.G. (87), K.C.—B. 1844; att.-gen., S. Australia, June, 1881, to June, 1884, and att.-gen. and unier, 1885-7; del. to col. confere., 1887; ch. sec. 1 premier, 1892-3; mem. of senate, Australian -16., 1901-1903; mem. leg. coun., S. Aust., 1905.

DOWNES, MAJOR-GEN. M. FRANCIS, C.M.G. (1885), late R.A.—B. 1834; served in the Crimea till June, 1856, medal and clasp and Turkish medal; instructor of fortificn., R.M. Coll., Sandhurst, for two years; commanded artillery in Mauritius and St. Helena for four years; instructor of gunnery at Woolwich five years; col.-comdt., S. Australia, June, 1877; mem. of roy. comn. on defences, Sydney, 1881; sec. of defence, Victoria, Apr., 1885, to Mar., 1888; comdt., S. Australian forces, Apr., 1888-92; ret., 1902.

DOWNIE, H. F., B.A. (1912).—B. 1889; ed. at Christ's Hospital and Oxford; Lodge exhibn. (class.) Univ. Coll., 1908-1912; 1st class mods., 1910; 2nd class Lit. Hum., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. elk., C.O., Dec., 1912.

DOWSE, THOMAS ALEXANDER.—B. 1875; educ. at Epsom; M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.C.P., (Lond.); D.P.H. (Camb.); mem. R. Inst. of Pub. Health; formerly hse.-surg. and res. obstetrical offr., Charing Cross hosp., London; civ. surg., Roy. Mil. hosp., Colchester; surg. to Roy. Nat. Miss. to Deep Sea Fishermen; govt. med. offr., Fiji, 1902-8; port. med. offr., Levuka, Fiji; dist. comsnr., and govt. med. offr., Cockburn Harbour, Turks Island, 1909.

DOYLE, EDWARD ANGEL GAYNES.—B. 1859; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lond.; obstet. soc., Lond.; mem. Br. med. assoc.; late asst. house surg., Westminster hosp.; joined med. service, Trinidad, 1884; ag. dist. med. offr., 1886; res. surg., San Fernando hosp., Nov., 1892; ag. res. surg., col. hosp., Port of Spain, 1893; res. surg., ditto, 1897; D.M.O., S. Naparima, 1906.

DRAKE, THE HON. JAS. GEORGE.—Mem. leg. ass., Queensland, 1888, 1893, 1896, 1899; M.L.C., 12th Dec., 1899; sec. for pub. instn., and P.M.G., Queensland, Dec., 1899, to Feb., 1901; senator for Queensland, Commonwealth parlt., Mar., 1901; P.M.G., Commonwealth, Feb., 1901, to Aug., 1903; min. of def., Aug.-Sept., 1903; atty.-gen., Sept., 1903; vice-pres. of ex. coun., 1904; ret., 1906.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, RALPH EVELYN.—B. 1875; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, Dulwich Coll. and St. George's hosp.; open scholarship, 1st year's proficiency prize and Pollock prize; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.); apptd. surg.-capt., attached to railway pioneer regt., S.A.F.F., Dec., 1899 (medal and 2 clasps); med. offr., Uganda Prot., 26th Sept., 1900; Nandi expdn., 1900 (medal and clasp); Suk Turkana expdn., 1901 (ment. in desp., reed. appreciation of serv. from S. of S. for Foreign Affairs); med. offr., Somaliland Prot., May, 1904; reed. appreciation from S. of S. for the Colonies for serv. during smallpox epidemic in Berbera, 1904-5; passed in Somali language, Dec., 1905; served with Anglo-Abyssinian bndry. comsnr., 1908-1909; Somaliland expdn., 1908-1910 (clap-p); assisted in the compilation of the military rept. on Somaliland (reed. thanks of Army Coun.), 1907; author of "The Mammals of Somaliland," "British Somaliland," and numerous papers to scientific journals.

DRAYSON, MATTHEW JOHN.—B. 1865; Apptd., after compet. exam., a clerk in the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., May, 1883; asst. registr., May, 1892; promoted to higher grade 2nd div., July, 1896; seconded for service as chief clerk, col. sec.'s off., Hong Kong, 11th Jan., 1902; acted as elk. of coun., Aug., 1902; retld. to C.O. as dep. ch. registr., 22nd July, 1905.

DRAYTON, EDWARD RAWLE, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; elk. col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Sept., 1878; 2nd clk. in office of gov.-in-chief of the Windward Is., July, 1879; ag. ch. clk., May, 1880, and May, 1881, to Jan., 1882; ch. clk., Jan., 1882; ag. col. sec., Grenada, Apr., 1883; priv. sec. to Maj.-gen. Browne, July to Oct., 1884; provost-marshal, Grenada, Apr., 1885; ag. col. reg., Sept., 1885; regist. sup. ct., J.P., July, 1886; mem. legis. coun., May, 1887; inspr. of prisons, Mar., 1888; mem. exec. coun., Nov., 1888; treas., May, 1890; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1890, to June, 1892; col. sec. and regis-gen., June, 1892; admst. govt. of Grenada whenever govt. absent, 1890-1914; govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1893, Feb. to Sept., 1897, and Feb., 1900, to Apr., 1901; and of Windward Is., Jan., 1897, Oct., 1907, and Sept., 1913; delegate to W. Indian quarantine conf., Apr., 1904; deleg. to Canadian trade conf., Jan., 1908; admstr., Dominica, Mar., 1914.
DREW, HON. JOHN MICHAEL.—M.L.C., W. Australia since 1900; min. for lands, 1904-5; col. sec., 1905; col. sec. since Oct., 1911.

DREYER, PETER.—Ck. to civil comsnr., and res. mag., Oudtshoorn, Cape Col., Feb., 1879; ditto, Port Elizabeth, June, 1881; 1st clk., Calvinia, Aug., 1882; asst. mag., Nov., 1882; deptm. auditor, atty-gen.'s office, Cape Col., Mar., 1885; asst. mag., Paarl, Sept., 1885; 1st asst. mag., Cape Town, Sept., 1893; civil comsnr. and res. mag., Prieska, June, 1895; mag., Montagu, Sept., 1896; civil comsnr. and res. mag., Carnarvon, Jan., 1898; ditto (ag.), Kenhardt, April, 1900; ditto, Calvinia, Dec., 1900; ag. mag., Montagu, Feb., 1901; ag. ass't. mag., Somerset West, Mar., 1901; civil comsnr. and res. mag., Albert, July, 1901; ditto, Uitenhage, July, 1906; civil comsnr., res. mag. and regis. of deeds and regis. of mines, Kimberley, July, 1908.
DRUMMOND, ROBERT, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1887; ed. at Glasgow Univ. and Univ. Coll. Hosp., London; med. offr., Nyassaland Prot., Feb., 1911.

DRURY, EDWARD HERBERT MERIVALE.—Trinity Hall, Camb., 1890; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1896; priv. sec. to Hon. Sir R. G. W. Herbert, G.C.B., Mar., 1899, to June, 1900; priv. sec. to Govr. Sir C. A. Moloney, K.C.M.G., Windward Is., July, 1900; ditto, Trinidad, Dec., 1900, to July, 1902; admitted to practice at Trinidad bar, July, 1902; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, St. Helena, Oct., 1902; emigrn. agt., Oct., 1902; clk. to ex. coun., Apr., 1903; J.P., Nov., 1902; schl. attend. offr., Aug., 1904; col. of govt. rents and taxes, Jan., 1903, to Dec., 1906; asst. stip. mag., Nov., 1904; clk. to res. comsnr., Bech. Prot., Jan., 1907; ag. ch. clk. and regis., June, 1907; J.P., Bech. Port, 1910; ag. asst. res. mag., Southern dist., 1st Mar. to 30th Nov., 1911; ditto, 7th May, 1912; asst. res. mag., 8th May, 1913; 2nd clk. to ast. comsnr., Southern Prot., 1st May, 1912; ast. res. mag., May, 1913; chmn., Bangwaketse and Bakwena schl. comtee., Jan., 1914; marriage offr., Bech. Prot., Oct., 1914.

DRYSDALE, HON. ARTHUR, K.C.—B. 1857; ed. public schools, Nova Scotia; called to the bar, 1883; K.C., 1895; elec. to Nova Scotia legis., 1891; re-elec., 1894, 1897, 1901, 1906; comsnr. of pub. works and mines, 1901 to 1905; attorney-general, Nova Scotia, 1905 to 1907; just. of the supreme ct., N.S., 1907; local judge in admiralty of the exchequer et.

DU BOULAY, ED.—Ag. clk. to audr. and treas., St. Lucia, Jan. to May, 1890; 4th clk., treas., Mar. to May, 1892; 3rd clk., May to Dec., 1892; 2nd clk., treas., and accnt., Jan., 1893, to Apr., 1894; ag. ch. clk., treas., Feb. to Nov., 1893; audit. clk., Apr., 1894; ag. audr., St. Lucia, Mar. to Sept., 1901; attached to Somerset house for service in W. African colonies, Dec., 1901; local auditor, S. Leone, 24th Apr., 1903; auditor, Cyprus, April, 1909.

DU BOULAY, G.C.—Appd. after compet. exam., asst. audt. clk., St. Lucia, 25th Apr., 1899; 3rd clk., govt. office, 1st Aug., 1899; 4th clk., treas. and customs, 17th Mar., 1903; ag. 2nd clk., treas., from 17th Mar. to 16th Nov., 1903; chief clk., post office, 16th Jan., 1905; ag. revenue offr. and warden, Gros Ialet and Anse Le Rang districts, Oct., 1907, to Mar., 1908; ag. harbour master, St. Lucia, Sept., 1909; priv. sec. and clk. to gov. of Seychelles, clk. to councils and dept. of printing, 11th Nov., 1912.

DUDER, T. C.—J.P., Newfoundland, 1874; M.H.A., Fogo, since 1893; financial sec., 1894; chmn. bd. of wks., 1894; survr.-gen. and chmn. bd. of agricul., 1897; min. agricul. and mines, 1898; stip. mag., Bonne Bay, 1901.

DUDLEY, RT. HON. EARL OF, P.C.(192); G.C.B. (1911); G.C.M.G. (1908); G.C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1867; L.C.C., a D.L. and co. ed. to Worcester; Lord High Steward of Kidderminster; major, Worcester, I.Y.; served in S. Africa, 1900; parlv. sec. to B. of T., 1895-1902; lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1902-1903; gov.-gen. and condr-in-chief of Commonwealth of Australia, 1908 to 1911.

DUFF, HECTOR LIVINGSTONE.—B. 1872; appd. by F.O., asst. collr., B.C. Africa, 1897; ag. collr. dist. of W. Nyasa, 1898; Zomba, 1899; resig. from serv., 1903; re-apptd., Apr., 1904; collr., Manji dist., May, 1904; ag. collr., Blantyre dist., 1905; res. of Manji, Apr., 1906; ag. res., Blantyre, Feb., 1907; seconded, Feb., 1909-10; admitted mem. of Inner Temple, Jan., 1909; 1st cls. hon. and spec. prize, constitutional law, 1909; ag. dep.-gov., Nyassaland, June, 1910; ag. dep.-gov., July, 1910; 1st grade res., Jan., 1911; ag. dep.-gov., Apr. to July, 1911; ag. dep.-gov., July to Oct., 1911; sec. to the admin., 1st April, 1912; ag. govt. sec., Apr., 1913; ch. sec., Mar., 1914; member exec., and legis. coms.; author of "Nyassaland under the F.O." 2nd edition; and a history of Nyassaland in the native dialect, published by the Prot. govt.
DUFF, HON. JAMES STODDART.—B. 1856; ed. pub. sch. and Collingwood Coll. Inst.; elec. to Ontario legis. for West Simcoe, 1898, 1902, 1906, 1908, 1911 and 1914; min. of agric., Ontario, 1906.
DUFF, HON. LYMAN POORE, B.A., LL.B.Toronto Univ.—B. 1865; appd. judge, sup. ct., British Columbia, 1904; judge, sup. ct. of Canada, 1906.

DUFFIELD, S.—Clk. to accntg. offr., Cap. govt. rly., 1897; bkpr. to ch. accnt., O.F.S. (afterwards Imp. military) rly., Oct., 1897, to 11th June, 1900; 1st clk. and bkpr. to oph. manager, O.R.C., 11th June, 1900, to 1st Apr., 1903; ast. mast. of High Ct., O.R.C., 1st Apr., 1903; ag. mast. of High Ct., Jan. to June, 1906; chief clk., master of sup. ct., Nat., 1912.

DUFFIELD, THOMAS.—Entered crown land office, S. Australia, Mar., 1867; ch. clk. as imgrn. offr., crown lands and imgrn. dept., 1871; capt. mil. force, 1881; sec. crown lands dept., mem. of central land and pastoral bds., 1896-1905; sec., crown lands dept., and ch. impr. fisheries, Nov., 1901 to 1910.

DUFFY, CHAS. GAVAN, C.M.G. (1904). LL.B.—B. 1855; 3rd son of late Sir Chas. Gavan Duffy. K.C.M.G.; ed. at Stonyhurst Coll., St. Patrick's Coll., Melbourne, and Melbourne Univ.; clk. in chief sec's. off., Victoria, 1871-8; transf'd. to Victoria legis. assem. staff, 1878; called to the bar, 1880; apptd. clk. astt. to legis. assem., 1891; has been clk. to H. of R., C. of A., since 1901; was assist. sec. to Fed. Convtn., Melbourne, 1897.
DUFFY, HON. FRANK GAVAN, K.C., M.A., LL.B.—B. 1852; ed. at Stonyhurst Coll., and Melbourne Univ.; called to the bar, 1874; judge of the high ct. of Australia, Feb., 1913.

DUFFY, THE HON. JOHN GAVAN.—B. 1844; eldest son of the late Sir Charles Gavan Duffy; ed. at Stonyhurst; vice-chancellor's prize, English essay, Melbourne Univ.; succeeded his father in representation of Dalhousie in legis. assem. of Victoria, 1874; pres. of bd. of land and wks. of Victoria, 1880; postm.-gen. of Victoria, 1890; also atty.-gen., 1892; mem. of cabinet without portfolio, 1892; resig. Jan., 1893; again postm.-gen., 1894 to 1899; rep. Victoria at premiers' confes. at Sydney, 1896; rep. Victoria at Fed. Coun. of Aust., Hobart, 1893, when he was chmn. of standing comtee; rep. Victoria at postal confes., 1891, 1892-5-7-8; rep. Aust. and Fiji at postal congress at Washington, 1897.

DUGDALE, CAPTAIN G. FRANK.—Ed. at Winchester Coll.; cadet in the mercantile marine 1892 to 1896; offr., 1897 to 1904, serving in the Shire and White Star Lines; sub-lieut. R.N.R., 1905, serving in H.M.S. "Daedalus" and "Defiance"; ag. lieut., R.N.R., 1906, serving in H.M.S. "Cambridge," and "Cornwallis"; promoted to lieut., R.N.R., 1907, and served in H.M.S. "Diadem" and "Mars"; apptd. 2nd offr., Uganda Railway Lake Steamers, 1908; ag. chief offr., 1901; transf'd. to the Uganda Marine and apptd. capt. of the protectorate armed vessel, "William Mackinnon," 1910; ag. supt. of marine, 1911; offr. in charge of Albert marine, 1913.

DUKE, MANSERGH PACE, M.R.C.P., and L.R.C.S.I., L.M. (1875).—B. 1852; ed. Academic inst., and Meath hosp., Dub.; med. offr., dist. 2, Dominica, 1875 to 1880; asst. surg., Gold Coast, 1880-1; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, May, 1886; offr. mem. legis. coun., Jan., 1888; med. offr., dist. 1, and med. offr. of health, Oct., 1889; mem. exec. coun., Sept., 1895; mem. pub. lib. comtee., Oct., 1896; ex officio mem. bd. of health, Jan., 1897; J.P., Apr., 1897; ag. escheator-gen., July, 1898; holds dormant coman., July, 1893, to act as coman., and has so acted on numerous occasions; resig. dormant coman., July, 1907.

DUKES, HUGH ROBSON.—Senr. asst., Boys' Model Sch., Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 1st Feb., 1893; headmaster, Stanger schl., 1st July, 1893; ag. inspr. of schls., 15th May, 1902 to 30th June, 1903; headmaster, Richmond schl., 1st Oct., 1902; inspr. of schls., 1st July, 1903; sec. and chief inspr. educn. dept., Natal, 1913.

DUNCAN, ANDREW, H. F. (late lieut. R.N.)—Joined R.N., July, 1868; served in Mediterranean, S. America, Cape, India, Australia, and W. Coast of Africa; resig. Nov., 1883; passed exam. in theoretical survey, July, 1883, and practical survey, 1884, at the Cape Univ., in honours (first of his year on both occasions); from Apr., 1884, employed by the survr.-gen. of the Cape, and in priv. practice; mem. land comsn. of Br. Bechuanaland, Sep., 1885; survr.-gen. of that col., July, 1886; survr.-gen. to Br. S. Africa Co., 1891.

DUNCAN, CLAUDE WOODRUFF.—Ck., govt. sec's. office, B. Guiana, May, 1899, to Feb., 1901; sub-inspr., B. Guiana police, 1st Mar., 1901; passed school of musketry, Hythe, 1901; passed school of instruction, Chelsea Barracks, 1905 dist. inspr. and J.P., 5th May, 1907; ag. country inspr., 28th Jan., 1908, to 30th June, 1909; passed in Hindi, 1909; in command of special expeditions to Venezuela frontier, Wenamu, Oct. to Dec., 1910, and May to June, 1911; dep. inspr.-gen. police, Mauritius, 17th Sept., 1912.

DUNCAN, PATRICK, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1870; ed. at Oxford, schol. Ball. Coll.; 1st cls. class. mod., 1891; 1st cls. final class. school, 1893; Craven scholarship, 1890; clk., after compet. exam., int. rev., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir A. Milner; prin. clk., 1898; treas., Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., 1st Dec., 1903; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; mem. I.C.C.; ag. lieut.-gov., Oct., 1906.
DUNCOMBE, F. A. C.—B. 1880; 4th clk., P.O., Bahamas, 1903; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1904; clk., record office, 1909; comanr., 4th div., 1912.

DUNCOMBE, HENRY FEVRENSHAW.—Entd. col. serv. as an acting, treas., Bahamas, 1891; ag. mag. and collr. in various dists., 1894-5-6; res. mag., Abaco, 1897; dist. comanr., Lagos, 1898; has acted on various occasions as pol. mag., collr. of cust., and treas.; passed govt. exam. in Yoruba language, 1900.

DUNCOMBE, ROGER KELSALL.—B. 1867; messenger, legis. coun., Bahamas, 1890; 2nd clk., P.O., 1890; storekeeper, general hosp., 1890; ag. clk., legis. coun., 1892; clk., Nassau market, 1895; ag. registr. supreme ct., 1901, 1905, 1906, 1908, 1909; ag. registr. of records, 1908, 1909, 1911; ag. coroner, 1909, 1910, 1913; supervisor of census, 1911; registr. of records, 1912.

DUNCOMBE, W. KELSALL.—B. 1878; 2nd clk., tel. dept., Bahamas, Dec., 1895; rev. offr., Inagua, Mar., 1897; asts. res. just., Watlings Island, May, 1900; res. just., Cat Island, July, 1900; supervisor of cust., Lagos, Jan., 1905; ag. collr. of cust. from Aug. to Nov., 1906, and from Apr. to May, 1907; 1st grade supervisor of cust., 1st May, 1907; ag. prov. collr. of cust., Lagos, May to Dec., 1908; prov. collr. of cust., 1st Jan., 1909; ag. compt. of cust., S. Nigeria, June to Nov., 1910, and from Oct. to Dec., 1912; temp. M.L.C., 1910; ag. compr. of customs, 1912 and 1914; M.E.C.
DUNDAS, HON. C. C. F.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 4th Sept., 1908; dist. comsnr., Aug., 1914.
DUNDAS, HON. KENNETH ROBERT.—Ed. in Germany, studied engnrng. in Norway; ast. collr. E. Africa Prot., 6th May, 1904; dist. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1908.
DUNDAS, L. M.—Land ranger, E. Africa Prot., June, 1910.
DUNDERDALE, G., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.B., Bac. Surg.—Medical offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1913.

DUNIENVILLE, R. M.—B. 1879; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1904; ag. dist. mag. and ag. crown proser., Mauritius, 1906-11; ag. dist. mag. and substitute master of supreme ct., Oct., 1911; ag. crown proser., Dec., 1911; ag. admtn. substitute procureur-general, Sept., 1912; legal adviser and crown proser., and pol. mag., Seychelles, Dec., 1912; ag. chief just., Seychelles, 23rd Mar. to 22nd Aug., 1914.

DUNLOP, J. M. M., B.A., LL.D., D.C.L.—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; barr. at law, King's Inns, Dublin, and Inner Temple; dist. commr., Lagos, 1900; ag. pol. mag., 1901-2-3; ag. atty.-gen., 1904; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., 1905; ag. atty.-gen., 1906; ditto, S. Nigeria, 1906-7-12; ag. puisne judge, 1906-7-8-9-11-12; ag. solr.-gen., 1909-10-11; atty.-gen., N. Nigeria, 1912; ag. chief just., 1913.

DUNLOP, COLONEL SAMUEL, R.A., C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1888; ag. commr. of pol., S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1870; ag. pol. mag. and commr. of ct. of requests, P.W., Apr., 1871; ag. dep. commr. of pol. and coroner, Apr., 1871; ag. dep. commr. of pol., Singapore, fire commr. and coroner, Aug., 1871; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., Dec., 1872; commr. to Perak, Jan., 1874. In Nov., 1874, commr. with the forces sent to quell the disturbances in the native state of Sungei Ujong; inspr.-gen., Straits pol., May, 1875. In Nov., 1875, on the murder of Mr. Birch, apptd. special commr. (temporarily) for Perak affairs; organised the expedi. which captured the Passir Salak stockades, and was present at the capture, Nov., 1875; commr. to forces during operations in Perak, Dec., 1875; accompanied Gen. Colborne's force up the Perak river, and across country to Kinta; ag. res. comm., Penang, 1884 to 1885; pres. of Singapore mun. commn., 1889; ret., 1890.

DUNLOP, WALTER RONALD.—B. 1887; ed. Univ. Coll. sch., London, and at South-east. Agric. coll., Wye, Kent; diploma with honours of Wye coll., 1908; asst. lect., staff, Wye coll.; agric. and science master, St. Kitts'-Nevis gram. sch., Feb., 1910; ag. head master, Aug., 1910; prov. insp. cattle in Nevis, Sept., 1910, in connection with outbreak of disease; scientific asst., Imp. dept. of agric. for the W. Indies, 1912; writer of many contributions to British agric. journals.
DUNN, C. de S.—Served in South Africa, 1901-1902 (medal and five clasps); S.A.C., 1903-1906; camel constab., Somaliland, 1912.
DUNN, C. M.—B. 1881; ed. Clifton Coll. and King's Coll., Cambridge; hons., 3rd cls., modern and medieval languages tripos, 1904; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1909.

DUNN, ROBERT ERNST.—Clerk and interp. to the R.M., Umgeni div., Natal 1880; to R.M., Lions River, Mar., 1883; to R.M., Alfred, Oct., 1887; to R.M., Upper Umkomazi, 1894; mag. for Colony, mag., Weenen div., 1901; asst. mag., Pinetown, 1912; asst. mag., Himeville, 1913.

DUNSTAN, WYNDHAM ROWLAND, C.M.G. (1913).—M.A. (Oxon.); LL.D. (Aberdeen); F.R.S.; ed. Bedford schl.; lec. and demonstr. of chem., Oxford, 1884-86; prof. of chem., Pharm. Soc., 1886-96; lec. on chem., St. Thomas' Hosp., 1892-1900; sec., chem. soc., 1903-5; vice-pres., 1903-6; pres., section of chem. and agric. science, Brit. Assoc., 1906; mem. of coun., Royal Soc., 1904-7; mem., of advisory comtce. on trop. agric., C.O., 1908, vice-pres., Internat. assoc. of trop. agric., 1907; pres., 1910; pres., Internat. Congress trop. agric., London, 1914; mem. commn. intell. comtce. Bd. of T., 1900-6; dir., scientific and tech. dept., Impl. Inst., 1896-1903; dir., Impl. Inst., since 1903; reported on agric. resources of Cyprus for C.O. in 1904, and of Asia Minor in 1907 (reports presented to parlmt.); visited Ceylon at request of colonial govt., 1910, and again in 1913; visited Newfoundland at request of colonial govt., 1914; arranged and supervised govt. min. surveys in Ceylon, N. and S. Nigeria and Nyasaland (reports presented to parlmt.); author of numerous scientific papers in the Phil. Trans. and Proceedings of Royal Soc., chem. soc., etc., and of ten reports relating to the utilisation of the resources of the colonies, including "Cotton Cultivation in the Brit. Empire and in Egypt," 1904, "Em. Cotton Cultivation," 1908; edited "Tech. Rep. and Scientific Papers," Impl. Inst., 1903; "Select Reports," Impl. Inst., 1909 (presented to Parlmt.); "Reports on work of Impl. Inst., 1906-14 (presented to Parlmt.); "Report on present position of Cotton Cultivation," 1910; editor, Impl. Inst. Handbooks on Commercial Resources of India; author of article, "Rubber," in last edit. "Ency. Brit."

DUPIGNY, E. G. MORSON.—Asst. gort. of Dominica, Feb., 1889; clk. commrs. of valuator, 1890; clk. mag. dist. F., 1890; do. presid. off. Dom., 1890-92; do. registrar, o.f., 1892; do. treas., Antigua, 1896; stud. Md. Temp., 1900; asst. res. N. Nigeria, 1903; 3rd class res., 1904; 2nd class res., 1911.
DURMAN, F. J.—B. 1882; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of treas., and assigned to C.O., 15th April, 1901; clk. col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1914.

DURRANT, F. C. WELLS, M.A.—K.C.H.: B. 1864; ed. in St. Vincent, Univ. Coll. sch., Lond. and Univ. of Edin.; called to the bar, Mid Temp., 1886; practised as barr. and solr., in St. Vincent; ag. pol. mag., St. Vincent, 1889; ag. atty.-gen., St. Vincent, 1898; stip. mag. and ag. atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 1902; resig., 1904; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1909; ag. chief just., July, 1910, May to Aug., 1911, and from May to July, 1913; M.L.C.

DU TOIT, PIETER JOHANNES.—Clerk, dept. of agric., Cape of Good Hope, Jan., 1890; 2nd cls. clk., May, 1891; 1st cls. clk., June, 1900; sec. lands relief b.d., 1899 to 1905; ag. chief clk. nat. dir. of agric., Aug., 1905; apptd. confirmed, July, 1906; ag. under-sec. for agric., Mar., 1907; apptd. confirmed, Jan., 1909; ag. under-sec. for agric. for Union of S. Africa, May, 1910, apptd. confirmed, 23rd June, 1912.

DUTTON, CHARLES LEONARD O'BRIEN—I. 1877; ed. Ellesmere Coll. and Bradford Ga. Schl.; clk., Impl. mil. rly.s., S. Africa, Dec., 1900; chief clk., fincl. bd. of rly. control, Transvaal and O.R.C., Jan., 1902; chief clk. to his commr. for S. Africa, Nov., 1904; ag. Impl. sec., Aug., 1913.
DUTTON, ERNEST GODFREY.—B. 1886; clk. Impl. mil. rly.s., Apl., 1902; clk., high commr. for S. Africa, Apl., 1903; apptd. to Swaziland service, Apl., 1907.

DUTTON, FREDERICK HUGH, M.A.—Ed. Kings' schl., Worcester and Hertford (o.), Oxford; elementary schl. teacher under Lancs. schl. bd.; principal, Ficksburg govt. schl., O.E., 1904-1907; dir. of educn., Basutoland, 1907.
DUVIVIER, AIME.—Clerk, archives offic., Mauritius, 1st Nov., 1879; custodian of archiv., 1st Jan., 1891; ag. poor law commr. and regen., 1911.

DYER, EDWARD BURRINGTON, B.A.—Fors. offir., Eastern Conservancy, King William's Town, Cape Colony, 1889; dist. forest offir., Kisku, Hock, 1890; Stutterheim, 1893; Port Elizabeth, 1898; ag. conservator of forests, Natal estate agency, 1912-13; conservator of forests, Transvaal conservancy, Nov., 1913.
DYER, FRANCIS.—B. 1870; F.R.G.S.; matric. at Galway gram. schl.; matric. at Queen's [...]
DYER, SYDNEY WHITEMORE.—B. 1885; asst. ir., rly. and elec. dept., Zanzibar, 12th Dec., 911.

DYER, SIR WILLIAM T. THISELTON, M.A., R.S., K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1843; thr. Ch., Oxon., 1873; asst. dir., Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1873; dir., 1885; ret., 1905; botanist to S. of S. for Col.

DYETT, EDWARD FARLEY.—B. 1869; 2nd reasy. offr., Montserrat, Aug., 1886; ad-juster of vessels, 1889; 1st treasy. offr., June, 1891; comsnr. oaths, Nov., 1895; lk. of waterworks comsrs., 1896; sec. bd. of health, Jan., 1897; held a comsnr., Dec., 1897, as act treas. of Montserrat in absence of comsnr., and so acted on numerous occasions until Jan., 1900, when authorised by law to perform duties of ens. in conjunction with those of 1st treasy. offr.; p.l., May, 1898; ag. reg. of supreme ct. ad clk. of S.J. from May to Sept., 1898; fl. rec. in bankruptcy, Aug., 1898; mem. legis. jun., Mar., 1900; visiting just. of prison, Nov., 900; ag. col. postmr., Antigua, Nov., 1903; rovnl. M.E.C., 10th Nov., 1906; asst. treas. offr., cts. of cus., postmr. and harbour, 1st Apr., 907; acted as dist. mag., dist. I, July and Sept., 911; acted as comsnr. of Montserrat on several occasions in 1911 and 1912.

DYETT, HENRY.—Supernumerary, P.O. and reasy., Montserrat, Leeward Is., Mar., 1894, to Feb., 1896; clk. to comsrs. for assessing taxable value of real estate, 1895 and 1904; comsnr.'s and nag.'s clk. and clk. of exec. coun., Feb., 1896; ag. and treasy. offr., Apr., May and Sept., 1897; clk., cts. coun., Mar., 1898; ag. 2nd treasy. and quarantine offr., July, 1902, to Apr., 1903; ag. jun. audt. clk., Antigua, Oct.-Nov., 1903; gov't. offr. and ex-officio rev. and excise offr. and clk. to N. ist. waterworks comsrs., St. Kitts, Nov., 1906; sanitary inspr., Jan., 1907; gov't. offr., treasy., Roseau, Dominica, Jan., 1909, and assigned duties s 2nd outdoor offr. of cust., and a harbmr., Roseau; ag. 1st outdoor offr., Oct.-Dec., 1909, and from Sept., 1910, to Nov., 1911.

DYETT, RICHARD HENRY KORTRIGHT.—B. 1862; served in pres.'s office, Virgin Is., 79 to 1881, and 1882 to 1884; clk., regist'r's office, Nevis, 1886, and regist'r. of pub. cemetery there, Apr., 1886; 2nd clk., regist'r's office, Antigua, 1887; clk. to trustees, supt. ct. lib., Univ., 1888; ag. 1st clk. regist'r's office, Mar., 89 to Aug., 1890; comsnr. to admn. oaths, 1889; arshal V.-A. et al., L.I., Dec., 1889; clk. to mag. at A., 1890; dep. cor. dist. A., 1892; ag. stmr., Antigua, May to Sept., 1894; 1st clk. griff. offr., Jan., 1895; dep. cor. dist. B., Oct., 96; ag. mag. dist. A., Jan., Feb., Apr. to Nov., 97; sec. to H.M. Diamond Jubilee comtee., June, 1897; ag. mag., dist. B., Apr., 1898, to n., 1899, during which period, May-June, 1898, acted also as mag., dist. A., and from May to pt., 1899, and 25th Oct., 1899; ag. mag., dist. Nov., 1899, to May, 1900; during which period (u. to Apr.) acted also as mag., dist. B.; Sept., 1899; ag. mag., dist. A., June, 1900, Mar., 1901; and Apr. to Nov., 1901; also in Mar., 1902; mem. of "Coronation" comtee. and hon. sec. to same, May, 1902; ag. mag., dist. A., Aug., 1902, to Feb., 1903; regisr. and prov.-marshal and regisr. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, 11th June, 1903; mem. of Basseterre town bd., and deputy chmn. of same, June, 1903; chmn. of same, Jan., 1905; J.P., St. Kitts, 1904; regisr. and prov.-marshal, Antigua, and ch. regisr., Leeward Is., July, 1905; trustee, sup. ct. library, Aug., 1905; regisr. of joint stock companies, Leeward Is., Nov., 1906; M.L.C., Antigua, Mar., 1907; chmn., St. John's City comsrs., May, 1907, to 31st Dec., 1909; chmn., trustees of pub. library, July, 1907, to 30th June, 1910; ag. mag., dists. L and C, Sept. to Oct., 1909.

DYSON, CECIL VENN, B.A. Cantab.—Cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1890; supt. of posts and telegraphs, Negri Sembilan, Jan., 1899; mag., Seremban, Feb., 1900; sec., central census comtee., Nov., 1900; 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kinta, Jan., 1903; sheriff and dep. regisr., Penang, Aug., 1906; regisr., sup. et., dist. judge and mag., Malacca, Apr., 1908; promoted to Cls. III, 23rd July, 1910; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, Oct., 1910; ag. dist. judge, Singapore, Dec., 1910.

DYSON, EDWARD TREVOR, B.A., Oxon., B.A., Wales.—B. 1886; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 26th Nov., 1910; attached to Trincomalee Kachcheri, Dec., 1910; ag. pol. mag., Puttalam, May, 1912; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Sept., 1912; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Aug., 1913.

EAGLESOME, JOHN EGAN, C.M.G. (1905).—M. Inst. C.E.; served in P.W.D., rly., India, 1890; recd. thanks of Indian govt., Khojak tunnel, 1892, Godavari bridge, 1900; services lent to C.O., 1st Sept., 1900, as D.P.W., Northern Nigeria; in charge of Baro-Kano rly. from 1907.
EAKIN, JAS. W.—B.A., M.D., M. Ch. Univ., Dublin; govt. med. offr., Trinidad; dist. med. offr., Port of Spain, S., and consulting surg. Col. hosp., San Fernando; surg.-major, local force.

EALES, SHIRLEY.—B. 1883; 2nd cls. clk., govr't. office, Transvaal and O.R.C., 7th July, 1902; 1st cls. clk., 1st July, 1904; transf'd. to Basutoland service and attached to office of high comsnr. for S. Africa, 1st July, 1908; prim. clk., Dec., 1911.
EARLE, HON. JOHN.—M.H.A., Tasmania; leader of labour party since Mar., 1906; premier of Tasmania, 1914.

EARLE, LIONEL C.B. (1911); C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1866; ed. at Marl., Univs. of Göttingen and Paris, and Me.ton Coll., Oxford; asst. sec. to royal coms. on Paris exhibtn., 1898-1900; ag. 2nd sec. of embassy, 1900; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1902-1903; priv. sec. to lord pres. of the coun. (Earl of Crewe), 1907; priv. sec. to S. of S. for the colonies (Earl of Crewe), Apr., 1908; priv. sec. to S. of S. for the colonies (Mr. Lewis Harcourt), Nov., 1910; sec. to H.M. Office of Works, 1912.

EASON, HARRY VERNON.—Ed. Westminster and Royal Coll. of Science, Lond.; joined Bechuanaland Border Police, July, 1896; cust. offr., Francistown, 6th Mar., 1902; sub-instr., Bechuanaland Prot. Police, 1st Nov., 1905; ag. asst. res. mag., Kalahari, Oct., 1905, to Apr., 1907; asst. res. mag., 1908; granted local rank of instr., 1908; mem. of Bechuanaland Prot.-Rhodesia bndry.coman., 1907; served throughout S. African War, 1899-1902; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1911; acctnt., Bech. Prot., April, 1913; ag. govt. sec. and master, May to Sept., 1913; ditto, May to Dec., 1914.
EASTERBROOK, ARTHUR DOVE.—Asst. collr., B.Cent. Africa Prot., June, 1896; 2nd cls. asst., May, 1902; dist. mag. and collr., N. Nyasa, May, 1904; 1st grade res., Nov., 1908.
EASTWOOD, BENJAMIN.—Ed. at Fleetwood; with John Aird & Sons (now Sir John Aird & Co.) from 1877; chief acctnt., Uganda rly., 1897.

EATON, HENRY FRANCIS.—Ck., col. storekeeper's office, Melbourne, Jan., 1853; transfd. to civil commissariat, Feb., 1854; acctnt., govt. stores, Feb., 1855; transfd. to treasury, Mar., 1865; ag. acctnt., treasury, 1869-70, 1876, 1886-7; acctnt. to treasury, 1887; under treas., Sept., 1889-1895; capt. volr. artillery, 1876; is in the commission of the peace of the Colony.
HATON, H. R. R.—B. 1868; entd. cust. ser., Cape Colony, 1888; comsrr. of cust. and excise, Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1914.

EBDEN, LEONARD POWNEY.—B.A., barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; b. 1864; ed. Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Camb., (scholar); asst. collr. and mag., Rawang, ag. sec. to res., Selangor, Jan., 1889; astt. mag., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1889; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, June, 1890; collr. land rev. and registr. of titles, Selangor, Jan., 1891; ag. chief mag., Selangor, in addition, Jan.-Mar., 1895; collr. land rev., Larut, and registr. of titles, North Perak, Mar., 1897; warden of mines, North Perak, in addition, Jan., 1899; ag. sen. mag., Selangor and Negri Sembilan, Feb., 1900; chmn., W. and O. Fund, F.M.S., 1901; sen. dist. offr., Larut and Krian, and registr. of titles, N. Perak, Jan., 1903; sen. mag., Selangor and Negri Sembilan, Feb., 1904; ag. 1st mag., Penang, Dec., 1904; inspr. of prisons, S. Settmts., Jan., 1905, continued to act as 1st mag., Penang; ag. legal adviser, F.M.S., July, 1906; ag. judicial comsrr., F.M.S., Nov., 1909; legal adviser, F.M.S., Jan., 1910; puisne judge, S. Settmts., and judicial comsrr., F.M.S., Feb., 1911.

EDEN, CHARLES WILLIAM GUY.—Served in colonial audit dept., 1894-1899; astt. treas., Entebbe, Uganda Protectorate, 11th Sept., 1899; transfd. to adminn. as astt. collr., May, 1901; collr., 14th Sept., 1904; prov. comsrr., 1st April, 1912.

EDMONDSON, RICHARD.—M.R.C.V.S., 1887; chief veterinary surg. to the Canadian agricultural coal and colonisation company, 1888 to 1901; St. Helen's, 1891 to 1905; veterinary offr., E. Africa Prot., Jan., 1906.

EDWARDES, H. S. W.—B. 1879; ed. privately; S.S. "Conway," 1893-4; served in S. Africa with 62nd I.Y. (medal and four clasps) 1900-1901; astt. res., N. Nigeria, 18th Nov., 1905; 3rd cls. res., 9th Feb., 1909; in charge of Bia Emirate from May, 1910; ag. in charge, Nig. Prov., 1st-31st May, 1911.

EDWARDS, COL. ALFRED HAMILTON MACKENZIE, C.B.—Served in 1st Dragoon Guards Jan., 1883, to Apr., 1892; adjutant, Behar Ligh. Horse Volunteers, India, Apr., 1892, to Apr., 1895; 1st and 5th Dragoon Guards and Imperial Ligh. Horse, Apr., 1897, to Oct., 1900; Hazara (N.W. Frontier, India), 1883, astt. prov. marshal (ment is desp., medal and clasp); commanded Imperial Ligh. Horse, S. African war, Nov., 1899, to June, 1901; dist. comsrr., Potchefstroom and Krugersdists., June to Oct., 1900; commanded "A" division, S.A.C., Oct., 1900, to end of war (ment in desp., Queen's medal, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps); substantive lt.-col. in the army dated 29th Nov., 1900; commdt., Transvaal volunteers; left the service, 1906.

EDWARDS, HERBERT ARCHER, F.R.A.S.—Cadet, school ship H.M.S. "Conway," Jan., 1885; passed out 1st on list, Dec., 1887; midshipman, R.N.R., 1889; sub-lieut., 1895; lieut., 1900; served in Royal Navy in several ships; 1st cls. certif. in torpedo and gunnery; extra master's certif.; naval instr., school ship H.M.S. "Conway," 1902-5; marine offr., Lagos, Aug., 1905; lent to govt. of G. Coast for special service, Feb., 1906; diploma in surveying, R.G.S., 1907; comdr., S.Y. "Ivy," S. Nigeria, Dec., 1908.

EDWARDS, CAPT. WM. FREDERICK SAYERS, D.S.O.—4th Batt. Devonshire regt.; B. 1872; ed. at Christ's Hosp.; served in ranks of Duke of Cornwall's L.I., 1892; 2nd lieut. 4th Devonsh. Jan., 1899; served in Ashanti, 1900; astt. instr. S. Leone frontier pol., 1899; in command of S. Leone frontier pol. during Ashanti rising (twice wounded, mentioned in despatches); D.S.O., medal with two clasps; promoted for services, instr. S. Leone pol.; capt., 1901; seconded to S.A.C., 1901; instr.-gen. of pol. and prisons in Uganda, 1906; instr.-gen. of pol. E. Africa and Uganda, 22nd June, 1908; awarded King's Police Medal, 1911.
EDWARDS, HON. WORLEY BASSETT.—Judge of supreme court of New Zealand, 11th July, 1896.
EGAN, ALEXANDER HOWARD, B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1912; attached to Batticaloa Kacheter, Jan., 1913; office asst. to govt. agt., Easter Prov., Aug., 1913.

EGAN, JAMES DAVIS O'DOWD, L.R.C.S. Ireland (1886); LL.M.; K.Q.C.P. Ireland (1887).—B. 1863; ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; res. surg. astt. Sir T. Stoker, Richmond hosp., 1885; res. clin. doc. to Sir J. Banks, K.C.B., Whitworth Hosp., 1886; res. surg., Richmond, Whitworth, and Hardwicke hosps., 1886; govt. med. offr., B. Guiana, 1894; astt. surg., pub. hosp., Georgetown, 1890-1894; ag. med. offr., Buxton dist., 1897; Tuschen dist., 1899; Belfield dist., 1901; med. offr., Buxton dist., 1903; mem. of medical bd., B. Guiana since 1896; is also mem. of bd. of agric., and J.P. for the colony.

EGERTON, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G. (1906), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1858; ed. Tonbridge School cadet, S. Stttmts., Oct., 1880; astt. to Chinese prot., Jan., 1881; mag., Singapore, Jan., 1881; passed final exam., Malav., May, 1881; collr. of land rev., Penang and P.W., Jan., 1882; 2nd mag. of pol., Penang, May, 1883; also offr. assignee under the Bankruptcy Ordinance (1882); ag. sen. dist. offr., Butterworth, P.W., Apr., 1890; a.J.P. and coroner for S. Stttmts., and adv. justice for Penang; offr. in char., Sungei Ujong, July, 1893; ag. res., Pahang, May to July, 1894; offl. assignee and regis. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prisons, S. Sttlmtns., May, 1897; dep.-pres. of mun. comsrr., Singapore, Mar., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1897; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Jan., 1898; ag. res. councillor, Malacca, Mar., 1898, and Mar., 1899; 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1898; but continued to act as res. coun.; ag. col. treas., S.S., Nov.-Dec., 1898; ag. col. sec., S.S., Apr., 1900; ag. res. councilr., Penang, Feb. to Apr., 1901; ag. col. treas., S.S., Aug.-Oct., 1902; res., Neg. Semb., Aug., 1902; high comsrr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; and gov., Lagos, 30th July, 1904; govrr. and comdr-in-chief of the amalgamated colony and prot. of S. Nigeria, 1st Mar., 1906; represented W. African Colonies and Prot. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; gov., B. Guiana, 5th Mar., 1912; assumed govt., 5th July, 1912.

EGGETT, W. H.—B. 1862; apptd., after a compet. exam., to be a clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Nov., 1889; promoted to higher grade 2nd div., Apr., 1896; asst. acctnt., 6th Apr., 1899.

ERRHARDT, ALBERT, K.C.—B. 1862; ed. King Edward's High Schl., P'ham., and Worc. Coll., Oxford; graduated with honours in mods. and lit. hum., 1886; Inner Temp. schlrshp., common law, 1888; called to bar, 1889; dist. comsrr., Lagos, 1896; travelling comsrr. on the E. Indr., Aug., 1897; ag. res. of Ibadan, 1898; ag. treas. and mem. exec. and legis. couns., June, 1900; ag. att.-gen., Sept., 1900; rly. comsrr. at Aro, June, 1901; ag. treas., Aug., 1901, to Mar., 1902; ag. atty.-gen., Mar. to June, 1902; apptd. res., Ibadan, Feb., 1902; apptd. col. treas., May, 1902; atty.-gen., Fiji, mem. ex. and leg. coun., May, 1903; apptd. as comsrr. to produce revised edition of ordinances of the colony, 1903; ag. ch. just., May to Oct., 1904; produced revised edition of ordinances, 1906; ag. ch. jus. and ch. jud. comsrr. of W. Pacific High Comsrr., 1907; chmn. of comsrr. to inquire into customs duties and admntr., 1907; 2nd puisne judge, E.A.P., Apr., 1914.

ELCUM, JOHN BOWEN, B.A., Oxon.—Ed. Highgate Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; scholar of Queen's Coll., 1878; cadet, S. Sttlmtns., Oct., 1884; passed in Malay, 1886; ag. dist. offr., S. Malacca, 1887; dist. offr. Bukit Mertajam, P.W., Jan., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Dec., 1890; sheriff, Penang, Mar., 1892; ag. sheriff, Singapore, June, 1893; 2nd mag., Penang, Nov., 1893; ag. collr. land revenue, Singapore, Dec., 1893, to Feb., 1894; ag. inspr. of schlrs., S. Sttlmtns., June, 1895; also ag. official assignee and regis. of deeds, Singapore, Mar. to June, 1896; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treas., July, 1897; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Mar., 1898; inspr. of schlrs., Aug., 1898, but continued to act as first mag. till Feb., 1899; dir. pub. instrn., Aug., 1901; inspr. of prisons, July, 1904; fin. comsrr., F.M.S., Oct., 1904; dir. of educn., S. Sttlmtns., and F.M.S., 1906; ag. gen. advsr. to Johore govt., Jan., 1912.
ELDER, J. H. C.—B. 1870; ed. at Marlborough, served in South African war, 1900-1901; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 22nd Sept., 1906.

ELDER, WILLIAM ALEXANDER.—B. 1881; ed. Reading and Edinburgh; graduated M.R.C.V.S., 1902; served in S. African war as civ. vet. surg. with army vet. serv., 1902-3; vet. offr., Swaziland admntr., since May, 1903.

ELDRED, ARTHUR GEORGE.—M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.) 1899; Lond. Hosp. and Coll. Certif. Lond. S.T.M. 1906; apptd. to W.A.M.S. (G. Coast), 5th May, 1906; recd. thanks of S. of S. for services in connection with outbreak of plague at Accra, 1908; apptd. M.O.H., Accra, 1st Jan., 1911; recd. thanks of govt. for services in connection with outbreak of yellow fever at Accra, 1911; transf. to Nyasaalnd, Oct., 1911.

ELDRIDGE, A. E. G.—Cltk., P.O., Antigua, Mar., 1880; clk. to pres. and isld. sec. and clk. to exec. coun., 1882; postmr. of Antigua, Aug., 1891; ag. mag. and coroner, Dist. B., 1894, 1896, and 1897; ag. mag. and coroner, Dist. H., Nevis, Apr., 1899, to Feb., 1900; mem. St. Kitts-Nevis legis. coun., and gen. legis. coun., 1899; ag. comsrr. of Virgin Is., and ag. asst. col. sec., Leeward Is., 1903; ag. treas., Antigua, June, 1906; treas. and shipping mast., Apr., 1907.

ELFFERS, PIETER.—T. ii. (Holland), T. i. (O.F.S.); ed. at Normal Training Coll., Nymeyer, Holland; Dutch mast., pub. schl., Stellenbosch, 1882-84; ditto, Grey Coll. Schl., 1884-93; prin., govt. schl., Fauresmith, 1893-1901; Dutch mast., boys' high schl., Wynberg, Cape Colony, 1901-03; ditto, pub. schl., Bethulie, 1903-04; ditto, pub. schl., Winburg, 1904-06; inspr. of schlrs., O.R.C., 1st July 1905.

ELGEE, CAPT. C. H.—B. 1871; ed. Lancing and Sandhurst; 1st comsrr., 16th Foot, Feb., 1892; capt., Oct., 1899; active service, Chitral, 1895 (medal with clasp); Ashanti campaign, relief of Kumasi, 1900 (medal); adjutant, Lagos Hausa Force, 1899; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Sir W. Macgregor, gov. of Lagos, 1900 to 1902; and clk. of ex. coun.; ag. inspr.-gen., 1902; trav. comsrr., Lagos, 1902; ag. col. treas., mem. of ex. and leg. couns., 1902; resident, Ibadan, 1st Jan., 1903; mem. of S. Nigeria liquor trade inquiry comtee., 1909; comsrr., Montserrat, 1915; author of "Memo. on Negro Education."

ELGIN AND KINCARDINE, 9th EARL OF (creat. 1633), VICTOR ALEXANDER BRUCE, K.G. (creat. 1899); G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., P.C., LL.D., D.C.L.; Baron Bruce, 1603; Earl of Kincardine and Baron Bruce of Torry, 1647; Baron Elgin (U.K.), 1849—B. 1849; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford, M.A.; treas. of Household and 1st comsrr. of wks., 1886; Lord-Lieut. of Fifeshire since 1888; Viceroy of India, 1894-99; chrmn. Roy. Comsrr. on S. African War, 1902; chrmn. Roy. Comsrr. on Free Churches Controversy in Scotland, 1904; chrmn. Roy. Comsrr. to administer Scottish Churches Act, 1905; sec. of state for the Colonies, Dec., 1905, to Apr., 1908.

ELIOT, EDWARD CARLYLE.—B. 1870; ed. Bradfield Coll. and Uppingham; apptd. 5th class clk., govt. secretariat, B. Guiana, 1895; seconded ag. clk. to govt. agent, N.-W. dist., Br. Guiana, for periods 1896 to 1899; 4th class clk., govt. secretariat, 1899; pay and quartermr. to Hausa force, Gold Coast Col., 1900; apptd. senior asst. treas., Oct., 1902; dist. comsrr., G. Coast, 5th May, 1905; with powers of travelling comsrr., 1906; provincial comsrr., 18th Oct., 1907; transf. to Tobago as warden and stipendiary J.P., Feb., 1911; title changed to comsrr. and warden, Tobago, from April, 1913; res. comsrr., Gilbert and Ellice Island Protectorate, May, 1913.

ELIOT, JOHN ALFRED ROY.—Ed. at Hartford House, Winchfield and Marlborough Coll.; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 4th May, 1903; Rabai, 1st Aug., 1903; Machakos, 15th June, 1903; resigned, 20th Dec., 1905; asst. treas., 22nd May, 1907.

ELIOT, LAURENCE S., C.M.G. (1915), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1845; clk. to mags., W. Australia; landing and tide waiter at Bunbury, 1863; also postmr. and asst. dist. regiar.; clk. in col. sec.'s office, 1872; sec. to central bd. of educn., and asst. clk. in legia. coun., 1873; regisr.-gen., regisr. of deeds, and regisr. of brands, 1876; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1878, to Jan., 1880; 1st clk. and regisr.-gen., col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1880; app't. to take the census, Apr., 1881, and to superintend its compilation; ch. clk. and acctnt., treasy., Apr., 1881; ag. asst., col. sec., Dec., 1889, to Oct., 1890; under treasy., Jan., 1891; is a J.P.

ELLARD, JOHN BRANDON.—Ed. at Northampton Gram. Sch.; Thorneycroft's M.I., Jan., 1900; transf'd., comsn., Driscoll's Scouts; Queen's medal, 5 bars, King's medal; sec. and acctnt., Repath, Comsn., Hoopstaad, O.R.C., to close of Comsn.; asst. to loc. auditor, B. C. Africa Prot., 1903-04; treasy. asst., E. Africa Prot., 28th Sept., 1906; asst. pay and qrtmr., pol., E.A.P., Oct., 1908.

ELLENBERGER, JULES.—Clk.toast., comsnr., Bech. Prot., 1890; interpreter to concessions comsn., Bech. Prot., 1893; ditto to Sir S. Shippard's boundy. comsn., 1894; J.P., 1893; asst. mag., 1898; served in S. African war, including relief of Mafeking; asst. comsnr., So. dist., Bech. Prot., 11th July, 1902.

ELLIOT, JOHN AUGUSTUS GILBERT.—B. 1881; Lieut. reserve of officers; ed. privately and at Edin. Univ.; enlisted in C.I.V. (M.I.) for S. African war, Jan., 1900; 2nd lieut. R. Scots Fus., Aug., 1900; dangerously wounded at battle of Frederikstad, Oct. 1900; 1st lieut., May, 1903; served with 8th and 4th regts. of M.I., S. Africa, 1903-4; intell. dept., S. Africa, 1904-1906; rejoined 2nd R. Scots Fus., Salisbury Plain, June, 1906; M.I. certif.; musk. certif., Hythe, 1907; pub. health certif., Edin. Univ., 1908; asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., 14th Apr., 1909; passed Swahili exam., 1910.

ELLIOTT, COULTON.—Lieut. R.N.R. (retired); Assoc. Inst. Naval Architects; B. 1871; ed. at Plympton gram. schl. and H.M.S. Worcester; in merchant service to 1898, ex-master (Lond.), passed in steam; Royal Navy, 1896 to 1898; hydrographer, Great Pacific Cable survey, 1899-1900; deputy marine supt., N. Nigeria marine, Mar., 1902; marine supt., 1st Jan., 1905.
ELLIOTT, F.—Asst. dist. supt. of pol., E.A.P., Aug., 1907.

ELLIS, FRANCIS ROBT., C.M.G.—B. 1849; writer in the Ceylon civ. ser., July, 1871; additional pol. mag., Kurunegala, 1874; office asst. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., 1876; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, 1878; ag. asst. agt., Matale, 1879; ditto, Mullaitivu, 1880; dist. judge, Ratnapura, 1st Apr., 1883; pol. mag., Colombo, 1885; dir. and inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1891-6; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., May to Nov., 1893; ag. prin. collr. of cust., Jan., 1896; gov't. agt., S. Prov., Jan. to Mar., 1897; gov't. agt., W. Prov., Sept., 1897; aud.-gen., Mar., 1902; ag. col. sec., 19th Nov., 1903; gov', N. Borneo, 1911-1912.

ELLIS, FRANK THOMAS.—B. 1881; cadet, S. Sttlmts., 27th Nov., 1903; attached to Chinese Prot., Penang, Jan., 1904; sent to China to study Hokkien, Aug., 1904; passed final in Chinese, Aug., 1906; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, Sept., 1906; ag. 2nd ast. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Oct., 1906; passed cadet, Jan., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Xmas. Is., Apr., 1907; ag. second dist. offr., Tupah, Jan., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Island, May, 1909; ag. 2nd ast. prot. of Chinese, Nov., 1909.

ELLIS, CART. R. FAIRFAX.—B. 1876; ed. at Beaumont Coll., Old Windsor; joined 5th Batt. Roy. Fus., 1897; seconded for service under Roy. Niger Co., May, 1899; served with 2nd N. Nigeria Rifles, Jan., 1900, to Nov., 1901; ass't. comsnr., N. Nigeria police, 21st Jan., 1903.

ELLIS, WALTER DEVONSHIRE.—B. 1871; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxon.; scholar, 1893; prov. acc. Hertford Univ. scholarship, 1891; 1st class classical mods., 1892; Univ. jun. Great testament prize, 1893; 1st class litera: humaniores, 1894; B.A., 1894; M.A., 1897; chancellor's prize Latin essay, 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the C.O., Apr., 1895; priv. sec. to Lord Selborne, June, 1897; 1st class clk., 1899; prin. clk., 1st Feb., 1909; mem. of India emigtn. comtee., 1909; mem., W. African leg. comtee., 1912.

ELLIS, WILLIAM GILMORE, M.D., M.R.C.S.—B. 1860; med. supt., lunatic asylum, Singapore, 1888; ag. also as municipal health offr., Singapore, May, 1889, to Feb., 1892, again 1894; ag. col. surg. in 1901, 1902 and 1903; prin. civ. med. offr., S.S., Jan., 1910; police surg. since 1897; J.P. for Singapore.
ELPHINSTONE, LANCELOT HENRY.—B. 1879; ed. Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Lincoln's inn, 1904; atty.-gen., B. Hazlitt, 1913.

ELY, HERBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1871; clk. dept. of agr.-gen. for Tasmania; ch. clk., 1881; sec., 1899; acted as priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir Edward Braddon, P.C., K.C.M.G., ex-premier of Tasmania, during his visit to England for the Diamond Jubilee celebration, 1897.
EMERSON, HON. CHARLES H., K.C.—B. 1884; M.H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Burgeo and La Poile 1900, Fortune Bay, 1908 and 1910; mem. exec. coun. without portfolio, 1909.

EMERSON, GEO. HX., K.C.—Speaker house of assem., Newfoundland, 1890-94; mem. of official delegation to London, 1890, and of delegation from legislature in 1891 on the fisheries question; mem. exec. coun., 1895; asst. judge, eq't., Nov., 1896.
EMMENS, JOHN HOWARD.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 11th Mar., 1901.

EMMOTT, 1ST BARON (U.K.) (creat. 1911). HON. ALFRED EMMOTT.—P.C. (1908); G.C.M.G. (1914); B. 1858; ed. at Grove House, Tottenham; B.A., Lond.; J.P., Lancs. and Oldham; mem. of town council, Oldham, for 12 years; mayor, 1891; M.P., Oldham, 1899-1911; chmn. of ways and means and deputy-speaker of H. of C., 1906-1911; under sec. of state for the colonies, 23rd Oct., 1911; chmn. of West African currency comtee., 1911-12; first comsnr. of works, 1914.

EMTAGE, O. DE C., M.A.—Ed. Harr. Coll., Barbados; Barbados scholar, 1886; scholar, Worc. Coll., Oxon, 1887; 1st class math. mods., 2nd cls. finals, 1890; hon. nat. sc. schools, 1891; asst. mast., Harr. Coll., 1891; examiner, 2nd grade schools, Barbados, 1891-1902; ditto, grn school, St. Lucia, 1894-95; ditto, Grenada, 1897-98; headmaster, The Lodge School, 1899.

EMTAGE, WM. THOS. ALLDER.—B. 1862; M.A., Oxford; late Fell. of Phys. Soc. Lond.; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; scholar, Pembroke Coll., Oxford, 1880; 1st cls. math. mods., 1881; 1st cls. final maths., 1883; 1st cls. nat. sci., 1883; University Coll., Nottingham, math. lecturer, 1885; asst. prof. math. and phys., 1889; prof. math. and phys., 1891; principal, tech. inst., Wandsworth (L.C.C.), 1895; dir. of pub. instr., Mauritius, 1900; examnr., Oxford Locals, mats., 1888, 1889; examnr., final hon. school nat. sci., Oxford, 1890, 1891; author of "Mathematics and Electricity" (Oxford Press), and other works.

ENGELBACH, A. H. H., I.S.O. (1902).—1850; apptd., 29th Dec., 1869, after passing an exam. before the civ. ser. comsrs., clk. in the accs. ranch in the office of the sec. of state for the J.M., having acted in that capacity since Mar., 1883; 1st class asst. clk., 1st Sept., 1879; bookkeeper and sen. asst. to the financial clk., 1884; cted for financial clk., June, 1886, to Apr., 1887; accountant, Apr., 1896.
ENGEBER, HENRY W.—Lt.-col. (retired) L.O.M.R. of Militia; clk., post office, Malta, 1881; deputy P.M.G., 1908; supt. of posts, 1914.

ENGLAND, WILLIAM WENTWORTH.—Enfd. ov't ser., Antigua, 1st Feb., 1907; clk. to regis.-gen. of births and deaths; ag. treas. off., Onterrat, 11th Jan. to 30th Dec., 1908; ag. clk., comsrs., Virgin Is., 5th Mar. to 25th Aug., 1909.

ENNIS, GEORGE FRANCIS MACDANIEL.—B. 368; at King's Coll. school and privately; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1892; sec. gov., B.N. Borneo, 13th Nov., 1894; sessions judge, B.N. Borneo and Labuan, 24th Dec., 1894; judge of genl. ct., Labuan, 1895; judge of ch. ct., N. Borneo, 14th Aug., 1895; ag. res., Province lock, B.N. Borneo, 13th Dec., 1895; resign., ct., 1897; ag. ass't judge, Zanzibar, 18th May, 7th July, and 1st Aug. to 24th Oct., 1899; ag. gir., E. Africa Prot., 1st Oct., 1899; town ag., Mombasa, 20th July, 1900; ag. judge, E. Africa Prot., 6th Sept., 1900; ag. ass't judge, anizbar, 25th Jan., 1901; legal vice-consul, ganda Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; judge of high ct. of ganda, 11th Aug., 1902, and judge of H.B.M. court of Appeal for Eastern Africa; puisne judge, cydon, 1912; joint author of "The Registration Transfers."

ENRAIGHT-MOONY, F.—Sub-inspr., Basuto and police, Mar., 1884; acted as govt. sec., Basutoland, 1893-1895; commanded Bech. Protective police with rank of capt., 1896; asst. comsrs., Basutoland, 1897; acted as govt. sec., 1900-1901; mentioned in com.-in-chief's desps., 1901; native comsrs., Zoutpansberg, Aug., 1901; commanded Ft. Edward and Spelonken dist., Northern Transvaal, and acted as intell. offr. until close of war; special comsrs., Swaziland, June, 1902; res. mag. and native comsrs., Swaziland, 1905.

ERNST, NOEL EDWARD.—B. 1891; cadet, local division, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1912; attached to Ratnapura Kachcheri, May, 1912; rimcomalee Kach., Mar., 1913; office asst. to ov't. agt., N. Prov., July, 1913.

ESPIE, HENRY PATTULLO.—Ed. at Glasgow cad.; associate, Chartered Institute of Accntrs. and Actuaries, Glasgow, Apr., 1891; ass't chief actn't., Imperial Brit. East Africa Compy., 1891; asst. treas., July, 1895; dep. treas., 1st Apr., 1903.

ESPEUT, CLAUDE VYVIAN ARMIT.—Ed. at St. Paul's schl., London, and Crystal Palace schl. of engineering; asst. engrnr., P.W.D. Jamaica, June, 1914 to 1900; Lagos gov't. rly., 1900-1901; dist. engrnr., Gold Coast gov't. rly., 1901 to 1904; cc. engrnr., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 9th June, 1905; ag. ass't dir. of pub. wks., 1908 and 09; dir. of pub. wks., Uganda Prot., 28th Jan., 11.
ESTELL, HON. JOHN.—M.L.A., New South Wales; min. of labour and industry, 1914.
EVANS, BENJAMIN DAVIES.—B. 1887; comsrs., Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1902; 1st st., Royal Observatory Hong Kong, June, 1912.
EVANS, CHARLES, C.M.G. (1914).—Comsrs. of rly., Queensland.

EVANS, E. P.—Educ. at Radley Coll. and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Hons. Mod. Hist.), 1906; asst. dist. comsrs., E.A.P., 7th Feb., 1908; 2nd asst. sec., 1910; clk. to exec. coun., 1911; clk. to legia. coun., 1912; sen. asst. sec., Apl., 1912.

EVANS, SIR FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.V.O. (1912), C.M.G. (1881), C.V.O. (1903), Comdr. of Order of Isabel la Catolica, 1912; Knight Comdr., 1914.—B. 1849; entered the civ. ser., Admiralty, Somerset house, 13th Dec., 1867; transf'd. to the sec.'s dept. at Whitehall, Jan., 1872; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, May, 1878; priv. sec. to Sir S. Rowe, K.C.M.G., Feb., 1879, to Feb., 1881; ag. clk. of legis. coun., Sept., 1879; acted as col. sec., Dec., 1879, to Feb., 1880; ch. sec. and ch. of the staff to Sir S. Rowe during threatened hostilities with Ashanti, 1881; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, 1882; ag. collr. of customs and treas., Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; ag. col. sec., Aug. to Dec., 1883, and on other occasions; clk. of legis. coun., Feb., 1882, to Apr., 1884; dep. gov., Lagos, July to Aug., 1883, and May, 1885, to Jan., 1886; dep. gov., G. Coast, Sept., 1883, and Apr., 1885; col. sec., Lagos, Jan., 1886; ag. admntr., July, 1886; col. sec., G. Coast, 1887; Leeward Is., 1888; Jamaica, 1895; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Feb., 1900; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1901; ret., 1914.
EVANS, H. A.—Baillif mag.'s ct., Virgin Is., Jan., 1885; bailiff, sup. ct., Apr., 1890; clk., post office, 1890; govt. offr. and cashier, 1901.
EVANS, H. G.—Asst. dist. comsrs., E.A.P., Apl., 1913.

EVANS, HON. JOHN WM., C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1855; M.L.A. for Kingborough, Tasmania, since 1896; warden and master warden of marine bd. of Hobart for many years; premier and min. of educ., Tasmania, 11th July, 1904, to 10th Oct., 1905; premier and treas., 11th Oct., 1905, to 1st May, 1906; premier and ch. sec., 1st May, 1906 to June, 1909; speaker, 1913.

EVANS, W. SCOTT.—Apptd. clk. col. sec.'s off., Jamaica, 16th Sept., 1890; sec., retrenchment comte., 1900; sec., Falmouth watersupply comsrs., 1901; ag. sec., Montego Bay riot comsrs., 1902; asst. priv. sec. to Sir S. Olivier when admntrg. govt., 1904; ag. sec., Jamaica schls. comsrs., and comte. selection Rhodes schls., 1904; asst. sec., N. Nigeria, June, 1905; 2nd asst. sec., 1st Apl., 1912; 1st asst. col. sec., S. Nigeria, 22nd Oct., 1912.

EVELYN, EDWARD A.—2nd clk. to regis., St. Kitts, 1899; govt. offr., Sandy Point, Apr., 1903; govt. offr., Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, June, 1905; clk., regis.'s off., May, 1906; registr. of births and deaths, St. George's parish; ag. ch. clk., regis.'s offr., Antigua, Dec., 1910, to July, 1911; ag. registr. and provost marshal, St. Kitts, May to Nov., 1913.

EVELYN, EDWARD ERNEST, I.S.O. (1912).—B. 1864; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; ent. col. sec.'s off., Barbados, 1883; asst. clk., govt. savings bk., 1883; ch. clk., 1884; rev. offr., St. Lucia, Jan., 1885, to Nov., 1890; accont., P.W.D., Nov., 1890, to Jan., 1893; audit clk., Jan., 1893, to Mar., 1894; ch. clk., govt. offr., and clk. of coun., Apr., 1894; asst. col. sec., S. Leone, Nov., 1900; acted as col. sec. for several periods since 1901.

EVELYN, MERRITT.—2nd clk. to pres., St. Kitts, Dec., 1878; ch. clk., treas., Nevis, 1881; ag. treas., Nevis, July, 1882-1883; rev. offr. in charge, Nevis treas., 1883; prot. of imgrts., Nevis, 1883; land and house tax comsrs., 1892; ag. mag., Nevis, 1894-5, 1895-6 and 1912; water comsnr., Nevis, 1901; chmn., quarantine bd., Nevis, 1911.

EVERARD, W.—Supernum. col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Sept., 1868; landing waiter, customs, Dec., 1868; ag. inspr. of int. rev. offrs., Jan., 1884, to Dec., 1883; ch. clk., P.O., Mar., 1885; sen. landing waiter, customs, June, 1886.
EWART, DAVID, I.S.O.—B. 1843; entd. civ. ser., Canada, 1871; chief architect, dept. of pub. wks., 1897-1914.

EWART, RAYMOND MARMADUKE.—Ed. at St. David's, Glas., 1891; Manipur field force, asst. to chief transport offr.; 1892 to 1894, 1st clk. Brit. vice-consulate, Arabia; ag. Brit. vice-consul for Hodeida and Cameron, 1894-95; Aden pol., 1895; supt. of Zanzibar pol.; asst. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 8th Nov., 1895; Mbaruk rebellion, 1896; supt. of pol., 1906.

EWING, Hon. Sir THOMAS THOMSON, K.C.M.G. (1908).—Min. for defence, C. of Aust.; M.P. for Richmond (N.S.W.); vice-pres. of exec. coun., 6th July to 13th Oct., 1906; ag. postm. gen., 1906; min. for home affairs, 13th Oct., 1906, to 24th Jan., 1907; by profession a licensed surveyor; M.L.A., for the Richmond, 1883-94; and for Lismore, 1894-1901; was sometime chrmn. of party, pub. wks. comtee., N.S.W.; chrmn. of royal comm. on rly. extn. into city of Sydney; ret. to 1st house of reps., C. of Aust., Mar., 1901, re-elected Dec., 1903, and Dec., 1906; joint author of "Progress of Australia during the Nineteenth Century," and of various publications on Australian subjects, retired from politics, 1910.

EZECHIEL, P. H.—B. 1875; B.A., Bombay, 1889 (agt. 14); first in Woolwich competn., 1893; B.A., London, 1894 (2nd exhibn. at matric., 1892); major schlr., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1890; 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1898; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1903; sec. to crown agts. for the colonies, May, 1905; mem. of Fair Wages advisory comtee., 1909.

FACKY, LUTHER HERBERT.—3rd cl. clk., prisons dept., Jamaica, June, 1877; 2nd cl. clk., June, 1879; 1st cl. clk., police and prisons dept., Nov., 1892; chief clk., constab., and prisons dept., 1st Apr., 1913.

FAFUNWA, W. K.—Cadet, Lagos civ. ser., 1896; 5th cls. clk., Jan., 1898; 4th cls. clk., Dec., 1898; 3rd cls. clk., audit off., Nov., 1902; paymaster, P.W.D., May, 1903; ag. acctnt., P.W.D., Mar.-May, 1904; 2nd cls. clk., treasury, Sept., 1906; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1907; asst. ch. clk., Jan., 1910.

FAIR, Lieut.-Col. J. G., D.S.O.—B. 1864; entd. Army (21st Hussars—now Lancers) 1885; capt., 1892; major, 1900; staff off. to Gen. Gatare during Atbara campaign, 1898 (desps., Khedive's medal and clasp); with 21st Lancers at Omdurman (Queen's medal, clasp, 4th cls. Medjidieh); adjut., cavalry depot, Canterbury, 1899-1900; S. African war, 1901-2 (desps., D.S.O., Queen's medal, 5 clasps); commanded div.
