Hoog Kong, 29th Oct., 1907; ag. puisne jud. and chmn. of squatters' bd., 21st Mar., 1908; puisne judge, Hong Kong, 21st Mar., 1909; ch. just., 12th-23th Oct., 1905, and from 16th Sept. to 16th Nov., 1911; ag. chief just., 16th Apl. to 3rd Sept., 1914.

GOODLIFFE, JOHN HENRY.—Ed. at Repton schl., Univ. Coll., London, and Aberdeen Univ. M.B., C.M., and M.D. (with commendation), Aberdeen; medallist in midwifery and pathology, civ. surg., Ashanti campaign, 1900 to 1901 (medal); med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prov., Oct., 1905.

GOODMAN, GERALD AUBREY.—Ed. at Lodz schl. and Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; 1st com. law schlr., Mid. Temp. Trinity term, 1885; called to the bar, Mid. Temp. June, 1885; ag. judge of petty debt ct., and asst. ct. of appeal, Barbados, 1889; J.P., 1889; ag. solr.-gen. in 1890-12; M.H.A. since Oct., 1889; mem. bd. of educ. since Oct., 1887; solr.-gen., Mar., 1896; ag. atty.-gen., 1891, 1898, 1900; 1-2-3-4; K.C. for Barbados, 1903; mem. of quar. bd. and gen. bd. of health, 1902.

GOODMAN, SIR WILLIAM MEIGH, KT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1847; ed. at Univ. Coll.; graduated B.A. (honours) at the Univ. of Lond., 1867; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Nov., 1870; went S.E. circ. and Surrey sessions; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1883; ch. just., 1886; comsrr. to revise and consolidate the laws of the col., 1886; atty.-gen. Hong Kong, 1889; ag. col. sec., May, 1891, to Mar., 1892; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Aug., 1895, Jan. to May, 1896, and Apr., 1899 to Feb., 1900; K.C., 1900; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1st Apr., 1902; ret., 1905.

GOODRIDGE, HON. A. F.—Mem. of house of assm. for Ferryland, Newfoundland, 1880 to 1882, and 1882 to 1883; for Twillingate, 1886 to 1889; returned for same dist., 1893; mem. exec. coun., 1885 (without portfolio) to 1889, and aga. in 1894; after the resign. of the Whites' ministry in Apr., 1894, he was called upon to form a ministry, and became premier of the col. which office he held till Dec., 1894; was a mem. of the bd. of wks. and rly. comsrs. under the Thorburn admstr. (1885-9); he is also a mem. of the Newfoundland chamb. of com., and has been its pres. and vice-pres.

GOODSHIP, HAROLD EDWIN.—B. 1877; in service of G.N.R., 1895 to 1903; asst. acctnt. S. Leone govt. rly., Jan., 1914; ag. chief acctnt. ditto, on several occasions; asst. chief acctnt. Uganda rly., Sept., 1908; ag. chief acctnt.: 1912, 1913 and 1914.

GOOLD-ADAMS, MAJOR SIR HAMILTON JOHN.—G.C.M.G. (1907), K.C.M.G. (1912), C.B. (1898), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1858; Rot. Scots fus.; comdg. a troop Bechuanaland border police, Aug., 1885; comit., 1888; maj. 1889; again comdt., 1893-5; commanded field force against Matabele, 1893; res. comsrr. for Bechuanaland Protectorate; dep. admr., Oran River Col., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., 7th Aug., 1901; gov., O.R.C., 1907-1910; high comsrr., Cypr., 1911; gov., Queensland, 1914.
GOONETILEKE, TATODUS.—B. 1891; cad. local div., Ceylon civil serv., Mar., 1913; attached to Ratnapura Kachcheri, Mar., 1913; Batticaloa Kach., Dec., 1913.
GORDON, Hon. A.—Judge of sup. ct., New South Wales, 27th Apr., 1910.

GORDON, ARTHUR JOHN LEWIS, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1847; priv. sec. to the gov. of Trinidad, 1866 to 1870; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, Apr., 1870; priv. sec. to gov. of Mauritius, 1870; priv. sec. to the gov. of Fiji, 1875-80; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Canada (Earl of Aberdeen), 1893.
GORDON, CHARLES FORBES.—Subaltern, W.A.F.F., N. Nigeria, 15th May, 1901; asst. res., N. Nig., 30th May, 1906; 3rd cls. res., 1st Oct., 1908.

GORDON, JAMES GEORGE WESTLAND.—B. 1874; served with and attached to Gen. Buller's staff, S. African War (Queen's medal and six clasps, King's medal and two clasps); cont., stationery dept., O.R.C., 17th Nov., 1900.

GORDON, Sir JOHN HANNAH, K.T. BACH. (1908).—B. 1850; admitted to S. Aust. bar, 1876; M.L.C., 1888; min. of ed., 1889-90 and 1892; ch. sec., 1893-6; atty.-gen., 1899-1902; atty.-gen. and min. of ed., 1902-3; 3rd judge of sup. ct., 1903; 2nd judge of sup. ct., 1905.

GORDON, BRIG.-GEN. JOSEPH MARIA, C.B. (1901).—B. 1856; lieut. in R.A., 1875; raised S. Australia artil., 1882; comdt., S. Australia, 1892; mil. adviser in England to Austn. cols., 1898-99; served in S. African war, 1899-1900, as chief staff offr. with Austn., Canadian, and oversea volr. troops (ment. in desps., four clasps); comdt., Commonwealth mil. forces of Victoria, 1902-05; ditto, N. S. Wales, 1905; late ch. of the gen. staff, Commonwealth military forces.

GORDON, WILLIAM MONTGOMERIE.—B. 1855; ed. Edin. Acad.; clk. to consul-gen. of W. Pacific, Aug., 1879; clk. of exec. coun. and ch. elk. of crown lands in col. of Fiji, May, 1880; also acted as sec. to lands comm.; res. comsnr. and stip. mag. for the island of Rotumah, May, 1882; comsnr. to inquire into claims of Europeans to land, Nov., 1882; stip. mag., Fiji, Apr., 1886; priv. sec. to gov., Ceylon, June, 1887; ch. elk. col. sec.'s off., Leewards, and clk. of fed., exec. and legis. couns., and of the local legis. coun. of Antigua, Apr., 1889; ag. comsnr., Montserrat, Dec., 1891, to Mar., 1892, Mar., 1894, to June, 1895, and July to Dec., 1899; ag. col. sec., Lee-ward Islds., Aug. to Nov., 1900; asst. col. sec., Trinidad, July, 1901; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, Aug.-Sept., 1903, Mar.-Aug., 1904, Apr.-Sept., 1905, Apr.-Sept., 1906, and Feb.-Sept., 1907; admstd. govt., 25th-28th Aug., 1905, and 30th Apr. to 8th May, 1907; ag. aud.-gen., Mar. to May, 1908; ag. col. sec., June to Nov., 1909.

GORGES, EDMOND HOWARD LACAM, M.V.O. (1911).—B. 1872; clk., col.-sec.'s off., Cape, 31st Dec., 1889; parly. clk., 1898; prin. elk., 1899-1901; also sec. to med. coun., 1896-1901; priv. sec. to col. sec., 1898 and 1900-01; prin. elk., convict dept., 1901; transfd. to Transvaal civ. ser. as ch. elk., col. sec.'s off., 14th Mar., 1901; under-secr., May, 1903; sec. to prim. min. and elk. to exec. coun., Mar., 1907; asst. col. sec., Aug., 1907; also chmn. of pub. ser. bd., Jan., 1909; on estabm't of Union, apptd. sec. for the Interior, 31st May, 1910.
GOSLING, JOHN THOMAS.—Served in G.P.O., London, 1887 to 1897; ag. P.M.G., B. C. Africa, 1897 to 1898; P.M.G., B. C. Africa, 1898-1904; P.M.G., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 9th Apr., 1904.
GOSLING, SAMUEL BUCKNELL.—Served in G.P.O., London, 1889; asst. P.M.G., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 1904; asst. P.M.G., G. Coast, 1909; P.M.G., S. Nigeria, 1912; S. African medal (four clasps).

GOTTLIER, F. H. VALENTINE.—Entered govt. service, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1880; ch. elk. G.P.O., Singapore, 1888; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1892; dep. registr., sup. ct., Singapore, Aug., 1895; ag. ast. registr., sup. ct., and mag., Malacca, Mar., 1898; also ag. sheriff, Sept., 1898, to Nov., 1899; asst. registr., sup. ct., Penang, July, 1904; registr., sup. ct., Singapore, Jan., 1908.

GOUGH, FREDERIC HARRISON.—B. 1863; ed. at Durham schl. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class, mods., 1884; B.A. (2nd cls. final class schl.), 1886; M.A., 1889; barrister-at-law, Inner Tem., 1894; N.E. Circuit; pol. mag. and coroner, Free-town, S. Leone, July, 1903; solr.-gen., Oct., 1903; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1903-1908; ag. ch. just., May-June, 1908; solr.-gen. and ag. atty.-gen., G. Coast, June, 1908; puisne judge, Nov., 1908.

GOUIN, Sir LOMER, K.C.M.G. (1913), KT. BACH. (1908).—B. 1861; ed. Sorel and Levis Coll., and Laval Univ.; called to the bar, 1884; K.C., 1898; D.C.L., 1902; elec. to the Quebec legis. for St. James div., Montreal, 1897, 1900, 1904 and 1905; for Portneuf, 1908; elec. to the City coun., Montreal, 1899, resig., 1900; min. of P.W., 1900; min. of colonization and P.W., 1901, resig., 1905; premier and atty.-gen., Quebec, since 1905; mem. of the coun. of pub. instr., since 1898; offr. of La Legion d'honneur, 1907.

GOULBURN, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. CHRISTOPHER GEORGE BARLOW.—D.D., 1891; ordained by Bishop of N. Queensland, 1881; curate of Mackay, 1881-2; vicar of St. Paul's, Charters Towers, 1882-5; mission chaplain to the bishop, 1885-6; vicar of St. James's pro-cathedral, Townsville, 1886-91; hon. canon, 1887-91; vicar-gen. 1887-8; consecrated 2nd bishop of N. Queensland, 1891; installed Bishop of Goulburn, 23rd Apr., 1902.

GOULD, Lieut.-Col. Hon. Sir ALBERT JOHN, KT. BACH. (1908).—B. 1847; solr.; M.L.A., N. S. Wales, 1881-98; M.L.C., 1899-1901; min. for just. in Reid ministry, 1894-8; returned to the first senate of Commonwealth parlmt., 1901, and re-elected at all subsequent elections; pres. of the Senate, Feb., 1907, to June, 1910.
GOULD, JULIAN BARING.—Cadet, Sarawak ser., Mar., 1897; asst. res., Nov., 1900; res., 2nd cls., Jan., 1902.

GOWDEY, CARLTON CRAGG, B.Sc., F.E.S., F.Z.S.—Ed. Harrison coll., and Massachusetts agric. coll.; Boston Univ., B.Sc., 1908; temporarily attached to Imp. dept. of agric. for W. Indies as asst. entomologist, Sept., 1908; economic entomologist, Uganda, Dec., 1908; ag. offr.-in-charge, scientific dept., Uganda, 1909.

GOWDEY, WM. WALLACE.—Solr., Barbados, 1881; mem. house of assem., 1883-93; mem. of comttee. of inquiry into Dodd's reformatory and industrial schl., 1884; mem. bd. of health, 1884-92; J.P., 1885; mem. of financial inquiry comsn., 1885; of general hosp. bd., 1889-92; pol. mag. and judge petty debt et., Dist. E. Barbatos, 1892; receiver of wrecks, Dist. E. July, 1897; pol. mag., Dist. A., Oct., 1901; visiting just., Lazaretto, Jan., 1902; receiver of wrecks, Dist. A., May, 1902; comsnr. of probates, Aug., 1902; judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., Dec., 1902; has acted on numerous occasions as inspr. of prisons, regar. of friendly societies, coroner, judge of the asst. ct. of appeal and master-in-chancery; visiting just., Glendairy prison, Jan., 1913.

GOWERS, WILLIAM FREDERICK.—B. 1875; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1898 (1st cls. class. tripos); entd. service of B.S.A. Co., Jan., 1899; asst. nativ. commr., Matabeleland, 1900; resigned, 1902; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1902; 2nd cls. res., 1906; 1st cls. res., 1910; ag. chief sec., Sept., 1912.

GRAAFF, HON. SIR D. P. DE VILLIERS, BART. (1911).—Formerly cabinet min in Cape Colony; min. of pub. wks., and min. of posts and telegraphs, Union of S. Africa, 1910; attended Imp. Conf., 1911; min. without portfolio, 1912; resigned, 23rd Sept., 1913.

GRABHAM, MICHAEL, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P.(Lond.), M.A., M.B., B.C.(Camb.), 1891.—B. 1866; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; St Thomas's Hosp., Lon.; B.A. Camb., 1887; 1st cl. honours nat. sciences tripes and exhibitioner St. John's Coll.; asst. med. offr., city asylum, Birmingham, 3rd Nov., 1891; surg. to the living-in hospital and general penitentiary, Kingston, Jamaica, 1st Apr., 1892; re-apptd., 1st July, 1899; served on cattle diseases commn., 1894; late sec. med. council of Jamaica, 1897; mem. bd. of governors, Institute of Jamaica, 1902; joint author, with F. V. Theobald (S.E. Agric. Coll.), "A Monograph on the Mosquitoes of Jamaica," 1905; author of various papers on same subject, 1906 and 1907.
GRAHAM, EDWARD NAGGAR.—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), house surg., S. Sttlmts., Sept., 1906; med. offr., gen. hosp., Penang, 1908.

GRAHAM, ERNEST WILLIAM, M.B., C.M., Glasgow (Cert. of London S.T.M.)—B. 1871; med. offr., S. Nigeria (Niger Coast Prot.), 16th Dec., 1899; senr. med. offr. and transf. to N. Nigeria, 28th June, 1910; senr. med. offr. (grade iii.), Gold Coast, 30th Oct., 1912.

GRAHAM, SIR FREDK., K.C.B. (1907), C.B. (1899).—B. 1848; apptd. clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., after compet. exam., 30th May, 1870; 2nd class clk., 30th Sept., 1872; priv. sec. to Mr. Herbert, 1st Oct., 1872; to the Rt. Hon. M. E. Grant-Duff, M.P., 8th Jan., 1881, to 5th Aug., 1881; to Mr. L. H. Courtney, M.P., 6th Aug., 1881; to the Hon. Evelyn Ashley, 12th May, 1882; and to the Earl of Dunraven, 24th June, 1885; to Sir R. Herbert, 3rd Feb., 1886; and again to the Earl of Dunraven, 3rd Aug., 1886, to Feb., 1887; 1st class clk., Dec., 1887; ag. prin. clk., Feb., 1892; prin. clk., 11th Mar., 1896; asst. under sec. of state, 1st Mar., 1897; ret., 1907.

GRAHAM, HON. GEORGE PERRY, LL.D.—B. 1859; ed. High schs., Iroquois and Morrisburg, Ontario; journalist; Morrisburg Herald, Ottawa Free Press, Brockville Recorder; elected to Ontario legislature for Brockville, 1898, 1902 and 1905; provcl. sec. of Ontario, 1904; resign. with the govt., 1905; leader of the opposition in the Ontario legislature, 1907; sworn of the privy coun. for Canada and min. of rllws. and canals, 1907; elected to the H. of C. for Brockville, by acclamation, 1907; defeated at g. e., 1911; re-elec. for S. Renfrew, bye-elec., 1912.

GRAHAM, SIR JOHN JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1847; clk. to C.C. and R.M., Albany, 1864; clk. to reglar. E. dists., ct., May, 1865; asst. registr., Jan., 1868; asst. registr., sup. ct., Mar., 1872; registr. and master, E. dists. ct., Feb., 1875; ch. clk. to atty.-gen. and clk. of peace, Capetown Jan., 1878; sec., law dept., July, 1882; high sheriff and taxing offr., sup. ct., May, 1884; mem. of the col. tender bd.; a J.P. for the col.; sec. to law dept., 1889; ret., Oct., 1908.

GRAHAM, HON. THOMAS LYNDPOCH, K.C.—B. 1860; ed. at St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, and Clare Coll., Camb. (B.A.), barrister-at-law, Inner Tem., 1883; advoc. of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope; M.L.C., 1898; atty.-gen., May, Oct., 1898; col. sec., 1900-1902; atty.-gen., 1902; ag. prime min., June to Aug., 1902; judge of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, July, 1904; judge pres., Eastern Districts local div., 1st Apl., 1913.

GRAHAM, HON. WALLACE, B.A.—B. 1848; ed. Acadia Coll., Nova Scotia; called to the bar, 1871; Q.C., 1881; standing counsel in Nova Scotia for govt. of Canada, 8 yrs.; commr. to revise statutes of Canada, 1883; commr. to revise statutes of Nova Scotia, 1898; judge in equity of sup. ct. and of et. of divorce in Nova Scotia, since 1889; is gov. of Dalhousie Coll., Halifax, Nova Scotia.
GRAINGER, HENRY ALLERDALE.—Mem. H. of Ass., S. Austr., 1884, 1890 and 1901; state agent in London for S. Austr., 1901; ag.-gen., 1905; trustee of S. Austr. sav. bank, 1907.
GRANGE, HERBERT CHARLES.—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to bd. of educ., Aug., 1901; transf. to C.O., Feb., 1902.

GRANNUM, EDWARD ALLAN, (C.M.G. 1931)—Passed Barbados, W.I., civ. ser. exam., 1881; 3rd clk. regist.'s off., Jan. to Oct., 1885; clerk, Oct., 1885, to Nov., 1887; clk. master-in-charges off., Nov., 1887, to June, 1888; honours graduate diploma, Ontario Com. Coll., Canada, affiliated institute chartered accountants, Canada, Dec., 1889; practised public accountant, Barbados, W.I., to Aug., 1897; asst. auditor, Sierra Leone and Gambia, 25th Sept., 1897, to 15th Jan., 1901; acted auditor May to Nov., 1898; asst. audite, Gold Coast and Lagos, Jan. to March, 1901; auditor, March, 1900; introduced system double entry accounts Gold Coast P.O., July, 1901; prepared scheme for introduction of double entry system of accounts for Accra town council, Aug., 1901; local auditor, Cyprus, 1902; auditor-gen., Mauritius, 23rd Apl., 1909; recvr.-gen., Mauritius, 1912.

GRANNUM, EDWARD T., C.M.G. (1911)—Reptv. of Bridgetown, Barbados H. of Assem., 1883-95; mem. of finance commn., 1885; J.P., 1886; mem. of exec. comttee, 1885-89, and 1900-1904; mem. of gen. bd. of health, 1885; repd. Barbados in negotiations at Washington in connection with McKinley tariff act, 1891; mem. of tech. educn. comttee, 1892; mem. of emigr. commn., 1893; aud.-gen., 1904; mem. of educn. commn., 1894; M.L.C., 1900; dir. of gen. hosp., and mem. of house comttee, 1906; mem. of educn. commn., 1907; chmn. of comttee at Barbados to consider trade relations with Canada, 1908; mem. of cust. commn., 1908; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. comn. in 1895, 1896, 1897, 1905, 1906, 1907, 1908, and from May to Nov., 1909; admstl. govt. of Barbados for a short time in 1907.

GRANNUM, REGINALD CLIFTON.—B. 1872; 2nd clk. to gov., Windward Is., Sept., 1891; div. St. Vincent, Apr., 1892; supervisor of customs, G. Coast Col., Oct., 1893; asst. local audr., G. Coast and Lagos, May, 1895; local audr., S. Leon, June, 1896; J.P., S. Leon, 1897; ch. asst. treas. G. Coast, Apl., 1903; ag. treas. and mem. exec. leg. couns., mem. bd. of educ., July, 1903, to June, 1904, Dec., 1904, to Dec., 1905, June, 1906, to May, 1907; Oct., 1907, to Sept., 1908; admitted student of Gray's Inn, Aug., 1907; recvr.-gen., B. Guianas, Sept., 1908; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Dec., 1908; ag. gov't sec., 22nd July to 22nd Oct., 1913; deputy gov't, 2nd Sept. to 22nd Oct., 1913.

GRANT, A. W.—Ed. at Aberdeen Gram. Schl., and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; B.A.; Senior Optime Math. Tripos, 1894; mem. of Lincoln's Inn, 1892; asst. mast., Ware Gram. Schl., 1896; army coach, Aldershot, 1897; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1899; senior grade, 1901; ag. second master, July-Nov., 1909, and from 11th May, 1910, to Oct. 1911; mast. in charge of pol. schl., 1906; examiner, Tech. Inst., 1909-1911; J.P., 1911; dir., Tech. Inst., 1912; hon. sec., R. Sanitary Inst. (Hong Kong and S. China branch), 1912; hon. sec., St. John Ambulance Association (local branch), 1912; ag. 2nd master, Queen's Coll., 1912; hon. examnr. in Mathematics and German, matri. exam., Univ. of Hong Kong, 1912.

GRANT, CHARLES CAMERON.—King's student, Aberdeen Univ., 1897-8; M.A., ditto, 1899; 2nd asst., Fraserburgh pub. schl., Aberdeenshire, 1899-1901; 1st asst., Aberlour pub. schl., Banffshire, 1901-2; priv. coun. certif., Scotch educn. dept., 1901; teacher, refugee camp, Springfontein, O.R.C., Nov., 1902; prin., govt. schl., Winburg, Jan., 1903; corrpdce. clk., educn. dept., Sept., 1904; admstve. clk., 1905; sec., Grey Coll. coun., 1906; junr. prin. clk., educn. dept., 1912.
GRANT, COLIN.—Apptd. 3rd asst., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1897; 2nd cls. dist. res., Oct., 1905; 1st grade res., Feb., 1909.
GRANT, D. K. S.—Asst. conservator of forests, E.A.P., Dec., 1912.

GRANT, HENRY EUGENE WALTER, C.M.G. (1911).—B. 1855; sec. to the educn. bd., Barbados, 1882; priv. sec. to Sir W. Robinson, 1883; and at Trinidad, 1885 to 1891; went on mission to Venezuela, 1887; sec. to special pub. wks. and road loan bd., Trinidad, 1890-1; priv. sec. to Sir W. B. Griffith, gov. of G. Coast, 1891-2; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1896; res. just., Harbour Is., Bahamas Apr., 1897; ag. ch. clk. coll. sec.'s office, and ag. clk. of councils, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1899; appt. confirmed, 1901; ag. atty.-gen., B. Hond., Mar., 1902, to Oct., 1903; ag. col. sec., 14th Dec., 1904, to 21st July, 1905; admstd. govt., 22nd July to 6th Aug., 1905; ag. col. sec., 7th Aug. to 9th Nov., 1905; col. sec., Falklands, 10th Nov., 1905; assumed office, 14th Feb., 1906; admstd. govt., Apr. to Sept., 1907; col. sec., Leeward Is., Nov., 1909; British agent, Tonga, 1912.

GRANT, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Henry Fane, G.C.V.O. (1909), K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1885).—B. 1848; ed. at Eton; entd. Army, 1868; col., 1888; served in Egyptian army, 1884 (brevet lieut.-col. 3rd cls. Medjidie); with Nile expdt., 1884-85 (desps., C.B., medal with two clasps, Khedive star); commd. 5th div., 2nd army corps, 1903-7; gov. of Malta, 9th Aug., 1907; lieut., Tower of London, 1909.

GRANT, R. W. LYALL.—M.A. (Aberdeen); 1895; studied at Gottingen and Marburg Univs., LL.B. (Edin.); Vans Dunlop Scholar in Law, Edin. Univ.; mem. Faculty of Advocs. (Scottish Bar), 1903; lectured on law of domicile, Edin. Univ.; one of the compilers of the "Digest of Scottish Cases from 1800"; in practice at Scottish Bar till 1909; atty.-gen., Nyassaland, Apr., 1909; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. judge, Sept., 1909 to June, 1910, and from Aug., 1912, to May, 1913; judge of the high ct., Aug., 1914, and mem. of H.B.M. ct. of appeal for Eastern Africa; mem. of various couns. in the protectorate.

GRANT, THOS. ST. JOHN.—B.A., Dublin Univ., 1896; clk. to C. C. and R. M., Komgha, Cape Colony, Dec., 1897; ditto, East London, June, 1898; ditto, Port Elizabeth, Jan., 1899; passed Cape civ. serv. law exam., Feb., 1899; clk., atty.-gen.'s off., Cape Town, July, 1899; clk. to R. M., Cape, Oct., 1899; registr. to judge pres. of high ct., Griqualand West, Sept., 1901; transf. to O.R.C. civ. serv. as asst. res. mag., Winburg, May, 1902; ditto, Bloemfontein, July, 1904; ag. res. mag., Bloemfontein, Nov., 1905, to Mar., 1906; res. mag., Hoopstad, 1907; mag., Bethulie dist., 1913.

GRAVES, FRANK GEORGE HELY-HUTCHINSONSON.—B. 1875; cadet, H.M.S. "Conway," 1889; clk., Imperial Ottoman Bank, Alexandria, 1894; clk., Mersey Dock and Harbour Board, 1898; clk. in crown agent's off., 1899 to 1906; acctnt., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, 18th Nov., 1906; served on various bds. of survey; ag. ast. treas., Oct., 1911; acctnt. treas., Cyprus, 26th March, 1913.
GRAY, E. A. S.—Demy, Magd. Coll., Oxon, B.A., 1892; asst. mast. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1896; ast. dist. comsnr., Niger Coast prot., Sept., 1899; dist. comsnr., Feb., 1902.
GRAY, H. W.—Office supt., lieut.-gov.'s off., E.A.P., May, 1908; clk. of the couns., Oct., 1908; ast. dist. comsnr., 1911.
GRAY, J. H.—Chief offr., Uganda rlyw. lake steamers, Feb., 1904; commdr., Mar., 1908.

GRAY, JOHN.—B. 1874; ed. Glasgow Univ. and F.C. Training Coll., Glasgow; held several educational appts. in Scotland and England; served in S. African war; supt., Brandport refugee camp, 1902-3; prin., Hoopstad schl., O.F.S., 1903; 1st cls. clk., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1904; senr. clk., educn. dept., 1912.

GRAY, REGINALD, K.C. (1908).—B. 1851; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1875; compiled, in 1894, Bermuda laws from 1690-1883; revising offr., Bermuda, 1889-93; compiled, in 1903, Bermuda laws from 1690-1902; counsel to statute law consolidation comte. since 1900; atty.-gen., Bermuda, 28th May, 1900; ex-officio mem. of exec. coun.; M.L.A., 1895-1897, and 1900-1906.

GRAY, THOS. ALEXANDER.—B. 1864; served in R.I.C., June, 1882, to Jan., 1899; in charge of div., Leeward Is. pol. and gaol, Montserrat, Jan., 1899; mem. of hurricane distress relief comte., and of roads, Northern dist., 1900; inspr. of pol., survr., Dominica, Feb., 1902; J.P. and mem. of telephone bd., Dominica, May, 1902; lieut., Roseau volr. fire brigade, July, 1903; ag. sub-inspr. of schls., Dominica, June to Nov., 1903, and again from Apr. to Oct., 1904; mil. instr., Dominica defence reserve, 1st Apr., 1904; mem. of Roseau town bd., Jan., 1906; dep. supt. of jail at Mombasa, Br. E. Africa, Jan., 1907; inspr. of prisons, E.A.P., 1st Apr., 1911.
GREAVES, CHARLES C.—Clk., water comanrs., Nevis, Jan., 1871; ast. supt. pub. wks., Nevis, Jan., 1899; mag., Nevis, Jan., 1911; mem. exec. and legis. couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1911.

GREAVES, Lieut.-Gen. Sir GEORGE RICHARD, K.C.B. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1881), C.B.—B. 1831; has held several important posts; ch. sec. to govt., in Cyprus, July, 1878, to July, 1879; adjt.-gen. in India, Oct., 1879, to Oct., 1884; ch. of the staff at Suakin, 1885; in command of Meerut div. of Indian army to 1892.

GREAVES, KENNETH JAMES.—B. 1870; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to bar, Mid. Temple, 26th Jan., 1900; ag. deputy clk., house of assem., Barbados, May-Sep., 1900; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown, Oct., 1900, to Feb., 1901; ditto, April, 1901; ditto, St. Thomas and St. James, Nov., 1901, to Aug., 1902; apptd. pol. mag., 1st Sept., 1902; ag. pol. mag., judge of petty debt ct., and recvr. of wrecks for Dist. "B" from 5th Oct., 1907, to 28th Feb., 1908.

GREAVES, Sir William Herbert, Kt. Bach. (1904), K.C.—B. 1857; B.A., Oxon, honours, juris., 1879; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Barbados, Aug., 1884, to July, 1885, and July, 1886, to Jan., 1887, when confirmed; J.P., Sept., 1888; escheator-gen., 1887; Q.C. for Barbados, 1890; mem. exec. coun., 1890; acted as atty.-gen., July to Dec., 1887, and June to Dec., 1891; mem. of house of assem. and of bd. of educn., 1888-1902; atty.-gen., Mar., 1896; ch. just., Apr., 1902.

GREEN, AMAZIAH.—Entd. audit dept., N.S. Wales, 1877; regis. of adjustments, audit dept., for two years; bookkeeper, post and telegraph dept., for eight years; acctnt., dept. of justice, 1887; senior inspr., treasy. dept., 1890; military staff paymr., 1900; Comwth. pub. serv. inspr. for W. Australia, 1902; is also comwth. electoral offr., and registr. for comwth. pub. wks., for W. Australia; J.P. for W. Australia and N.S. Wales.

GREEN, Alfred Pierce.—B. 1856; surg., A.M.D., 1878; med. offr., col. forces in Basute war, 1881; regtl. surg., left wing, C.M.R., 1881-4; med. offr., Cape rly., 1889; ditto, Bechuanaland border pol., 1889; J.P., Mafeking, 1892; surg.-capt., Johannesburg mtd. rifles, 1901; sen. med. offr., concentration camp, Wynburg, O.R.C., 1902; Basutoland war medal, 1901; S. African war medal and clasp, 1901.

GREEN, Charles Francis J.—Cadet, S. Stltmts., Nov., 1898; ag. dist.offr., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1900; ag. 4th mag., Sing., June, 1901; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1901; ag. dist. offr. Malacca, Feb., 1902; confirmed, July, 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., July, 1906.
GREEN, George C.—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 26th Aug., 1901.
GREEN, Harold.—B. 1887; ed. at Queen Mary's Gram. Schl., Hants.; entd. Royal botanic gardens, Kew, 1907; asst. supt., botanical and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1911.

GREEN, James Ernest.—B. 1860; ed. at Cheltenham Coll., and Trin. Hall, Camb., 1879; B.A., LL.B., Law Tripos, 1882; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Mar., 1884; admitted a solr., Mar., 1895; restored to the bar, 1905; solr.-gen. (to act as atty.-gen.), S. Nigeria Prot., May, 1905; solr.-gen. of Colony and Prot. of S. Nigeria, May, 1906; ag. chief just. of Prot., 1907; ag. pulse judge of Prot., 1907, and of Colony and Prot., 1908; ag. atty.-gen. of Colony and Prot., 1907 and 1908; asstd. to edit Laws of S. Nigeria, 1908.

GREEN, John Frederick Norman, B.A.—B. 1873; scholar, Emman. Coll., Camb., 1890; bracketed 15th wrangler, 1893; B.A., 1893; 1st class, Part II., nat. science tripos, 1894; 2nd class elk., C.O., after compet. exam., Feb., 1896; priv. sec. to Sir E. Wingfield, Dec., 1898; to Sir M. Ommannay, 1900; 1st class elk., 16th June, 1902; sec. to col. regns. comtees, 1907-8.
GREEN, William Kirby.—Ed. Eton and Bruges; served in 10th regt. I.Y., S. Africa, 1900-01 (medal with 4 clasps); 3rd grade res., Nyasaland Prot., 1901; 2nd grade res., Feb., 1909.

GREENE, Hon. Col. Edward Mackenzie, C.M.G. (1909), K.C., V.D.—Hon. colonel in army; served in Zulu war, 1878-1879 (medal, with clasp); S. African war, 1899-1902, in command of Natal Carbineers (Queen's medal, with three clasps; twice mentioned in despatches); mem. of Natal legis. assem., 1893 to date of Union; min. for rly. and harbours, 13th July, 1908; Natal deleg. to S. African National Convention, Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; represented Natal at Imp. Conf., on defence, 1909; mem. rly. and harbours bd., Union of S. Africa, 1910.
GREENE, Groffret Philip.—B. 1888; gen. mag., Ceylon rlyws., 11th Oct., 1901.

GREENE, George Ball.—B. 1873; ed. in England; jun. elk., ld. dept., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1887; 5th class elk., Feb., 1888; 4th elk., secretariat, 1891; sec. to compt. for inquiring into post-office, 1893; sec. pilotage comsn., and 3rd class, 1895; 2nd class, and despatch elk., 1899; 1st class elk., 1901; ag. prin. elk., 1902, 1903, 1904, 1906, 1907 and 1908; priv. sec. to gov. and ag. gov., 1905, 1906, 1907 and 1908; ag. elk. of legis. and exec. comsn., 1905 to 1908; prin. elk. and elk. of exec. and legis. couns., 1911.

GREENE, Maurice Cherry.—B. 1881; ed. at Rathmine's schl. and St. Stephen's Grea schl., Dublin, and Dublin Univ.; B.A. 1905; student, King's Inns, Dublin, 1910-1911; ass. res., N. Nigeria, 24th July, 1912.

GREENE, Roger.—B. 1877; ed. at Dublin; elk., atty.-gen.'s dept., Fiji; ag. regiar., mp. ct. and curator of intestate estates, Mch., to Jul., 1907; regiar., high comsnr.'s ct. for the Western Pacific, May, 1907; regiar.-gen. under Pacific civil marriages order-in-council, 1907; regiar.-gen. regiar. of titles and regiar. of supreme ct., Apr., 1913.

GREENE, Rt. Hon. Sir William Contingham, P.C. (1912), G.C.M.G. (1914), K.C.B. (1916), C.B. (1897).—B. 1854; ed. Harrow, and Pemb Coll., Ox.; M.A. 1880; entrd. F.O. 1877; ag. 3rd sec. legation at Athens, 1880; ditto Stuttgart, 1883; ag. chargé d'affaires Stuttgart and Darmstadt at various times, 1883-9; 2nd sec. diplomatic service, 1887; 2nd sec. Hague, 1889-91; Brussels, 1891-3; sec. of legation and chargé d'affaires, Teheran, 1893-6; H.M. agt. at Pretoria S.A. Republic, with rank of chargé d'affaires, 25th Aug., 1896, to 1899; envoy extraordinary, Switzerland, 1901; ditto, Roumania, 1905; ditto, Denmark, 1910; H.M.'s Ambassador, Japan, 1912.

GREENER, Herbert.—D.S.O., elk., sec.'s dept., G.P.O., Cape, 1884; in charge of money order dept., 1886; 1st elk., admstr.'s office, B. Bechuanaland, 1888; recvr. of revenue, Vrburg, and P.M.G., Bechuanaland, 1889; examnr. of accts., acctnt. to high comsnr., (capetown, 1890; paymr. (rank captain), Bech. hr. pol., 1891, combined with prin. cust. offr., for Bech. Prot., 1895; specially employed in charge of finances, Mafeking siege, and financial ast. to mil. govt., Bloemfontein, 1899-1900; transf. to S.A.C. as ch. paymtr. and acctnt.-gen. (rank major), Oct., 1900; promoted lt.-col., 1901.

GREENHOUGH, Frederick Harb., M.I.C.E.—B. 1871; engineering student, Bradford Tech. Coll., 1886 to 1890; asst. engrnr., S. Devon Doubling G.W.R., L.D. and E.C. riv. constn., Port Talbot rly. and docks, 1890 to 1896; engrnr.-in-charge quay wall constn. and reclamntion, Vera Cruz harb., Mexico, 1896-97; chief engrnr., Eastern section, Admiralty harb., Dover, 1897 to 1903; chief engrnr., Admiralty breakwaters, Grand harb., Malta, 1903 to 1907; dist. engrnr., Nigerian rly., 1908; engrnr. in charge open lines, constn. and survey, Lagos terminus, 1909 and 1910; ag. suptdg. engrnr., 1911; asst. chief engrnr., 1914.

GREENWOOD, ALFRED CRAVEN.—Exce. staff, inventions exhibn., 1885, and Col. and Ind. exhibn., 1886, and Royal Jubilee exhibn., Manchester, 1887; sec. to various charitable institns., 1887 to 1890; priv. sec. to Sir A. Shea, gov. Bahamas, and clk. to coun., 1891-4; treas. of Gibraltar, 1897; J.P. and mem. of sanitary coman., chrmn. at various times, also contractg. offr., 1897; ag. capt. of port on various occasions; hosp. comsrr., 1903; chrmn. of comtees on wharfage tolls and storage waterport wharf, 1902; chrmn. of comte. on management, working and revenue of new comml. mole, 1904; ag. pol. mag., inspr. of schls., and coroner on various occasions; ag. col. sec., 1914.

GREENWOOD, ERNEST.—B. 1876; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1899; N.E. Cire., Leeds, Bradford, West Riding and N. London Sess.; ast. dist. comsrr., S. Nigeria, 23rd Mar., 1907; ast. col. sec., 1st July, 1908; ast. pol. mag., 1st Jan., 1909; pol. mag., 23rd Jan., 1911; ag. solr.-gen., 1912; mem. of special comsrr., Ogoja, December, 1912; ag. pulsh judge, Dec., 1913; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, 29th Jan., 1914.

GREENWOOD, THOMAS JAMES.—B. 1864; supt. of transport, Egyptian campaign, 1882; awarded Queen's medal and Khedive's star; inspr. Cyprus pol., Feb., 1887; ag. local comdts., Limasol, Oct. to Dec., 1894, and Apr. to July, 1895, bronze medal, roy. hum. soc., for saving and attempting to save life during Limasol floods, Nov. 12th, 1894; local comdts., Cyprus pol., 1896; ag. comsrr., Kyrenia, May to Nov., 1900; ditto, Larnaca, Oct. to Dec., 1901.

GREGOROWSKI, REGINALD ADDINGTON.—2nd clk., gov't. sec.'s office, O.F.S., Dec., 1895; priv. sec. to state pres., 5th Mar., 1896; landdrost clk., Bethlehem, Dec., 1897; priv. sec. to state pres., July, 1898; landdrost clk., Wepener, Jan., 1899; prin. govt. schl., Reddersburg, O.R.C., 1st Oct., 1900; ast. clk., educn. dept., 1st Mar., 1901; oh. clk. and registr., educn. dept., 1st July, 1901; prin. clk., col. sec.'s off., June, 1909; oh. clk. to admstr., O.F.S. prov., 10th Aug., 1910.

GREGORY, CHARLES WILLIAM.—Natal Mounted pol. (Zululand), 1890-1901; attached to Army Service Corps, 1901-1902; chief storekeeper, central stores, Pretoria, Transvaal repatriation department, 1902-1904; Transvaal civ. ser., 1906; chief storekeeper, P.W.D., East Africa Prot., Sept., 1906.

GREGORY, HON. FRANCIS BROOKE, LL.B.—B. 1862; ed. at pub. schls. and Harvard Univ.; admtd. an atty., New Brunswick, 1884, and called to the bar, 1885; called to the bar, B. Columbia, 1890; registr. of probate ct., York Co., New Brunswick, 1885-1890; French cons. agt. at Victoria, B. Columbia, 1904-1909; commd. 5th regt. Canadian garr. artil. at Victoria, B. Columbia, 1898-1903; lieut.-col., reserve of offrs. (Canadian Militia); mem. of Diamond Jubilee contingent to England, 1897; on spec. ser. to Alaska in connection with Behring sea fisheries dispute, 1891-2; judge of sup. ct., B. Columbia, Nov., 1909.

GREGORY, HON. HENRY.—B. 1860; M.L.A., for N. Coolgardie, W. Australia, 1897; ditto for Menzies since 1901; min. for mines, 1901-2, and 1902-4; min. for mines and rlys., Aug., 1905; ditto, 16th Sept., 1910, to Oct., 1911; mem. H. of R., C. of A., 1913.

GREENFELL, 1ST BARON OF KILVEY (creat. 1902). FIELD-MARSHAL SIR FRANCIS WALLACE, G.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1841; gov. and comdr.-in-chief of Malta, 1898; A.D.C. to Sir A. Cunynghame; also staff offr. to Col. Glyn in the Transkei, 1887-8; dep. ast. adjt. and qrtmr.-gen. in the Kaffir war, 1878; the same in the Zulu war, 1879; ast. qrtmr.-gen. in the Boer war, 1881; served in the Egyptian campaign of 1882, and the Nile expdn. of 1884; was Sirdar of the Egyptian army from 1885 to 1892; commanded the troops employed at Suakim against the Dervishes; after filling various posts at home, went again to Egypt in 1897 to command the British troops; with Lord Kitchener in the Nile campaign, 1898; gov. of Malta, 1899 to 1903; commd. 4th Army Corps, 1903-4; G.O.C. forces in Ireland, 1904.

GREY, 4TH EARL OF (creat. 1806). RT. HON. VISCOUNT HOWICK, BARON GREY, BART.—P.C. (1908); G.C.B. (1911); G.C.M.G. (1904); G.C.V.O.; ed. Harrow, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.P., 1880-6; dir. B.S.A. Co.; admstr. of Rhodesia, 1896 to 1898; gov.-gen. of Canada, 1904 to 1911.

GREY-WILSON, SIR WM., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1832; ed. at Chelt. Coll.; priv. sec. to Sir William Grey, gov. of Jamaica, 1874; to Lieut.-Gov. Edwd. E. Rushworth, Mar., 1877; admstr., Major-Gen. J. R. Mann, R.E., Aug., 1877; to Lieut.-Gov. F. P. Barlee, Br. Honduras, Sept., 1877; and clk. of the exec. and legis. couns., June, 1878; ag. mag., Orange Walk, in charge of frontier scouts, Dec., 1879, to Apr., 1880, and June, 1880, to Mar., 1881; ast. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, 1883; special comsrr., Sulymah, Dec., 1883, to June, 1884; 4th ast. col. sec., G. Coast Coll., 1884; col. sec., St. Helena, July, 1886; ag. gov., 1887; gov., 1890; gov., Falklands, 1897; gov., Bahamas, 5th May, 1904; retired, 1912.

GRIER, SELWYN MACGREGOR.—B. 1878; ed. at Marlboro' Coll. (classical scholar) and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (classical scholar); 2nd class tripos, 1900; 1st class French and English special, 1901; B.A. 1900; called to the bar, Nov., 1910; schoolmaster at Berkhamstead, Herts., 1901-1902; ditto, Cheam, Surrey, 1902-1903; ast. res., Northern Nigeria, 1906; passed in Hausa, 1907; 3rd class res., 1st Oct., 1908; in charge of Zari prov., April, 1910; and from May, to Nov., 1911.
GRIESS, WILLIAM MASON.—Ed. at Repton Schl. and Cooper's Hill; senr. ast. engrnr., Uganda Rly., 20th Dec., 1895; dist. engnr., Apr., 1909.

GRIFFIN, CHARLES JAMES.—B. 1875; B.A. (hons.) Royal Univ., Ireland; 1st scholar in modern literature, exhibitioner; Chancellor's gold medallist; called to the Irish Bar, June, 1898; went Connaught circuit; apptd. Crown prosecutor, B.C.A. Protectorate (now Nyasaland), July, 1901; ag. chief judicial offr. and H.B.M. vice-consul, Nov., 1901, to June, 1902; ag. judge of High Court, Feb. to Oct., 1904, and Nov., 1905, to Feb., 1906; atty.-gen., Nov., 1905; judge of High Court, Feb., 1906, and member of H.B.M. Court of Appeal for Eastern Africa; chairman of comsrr. on trading and licensing, May to Sept., 1909 (reed. thanks of Prot. gov't.); comsrr. for revision of Nyasaland laws, 1913; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, Oct., 1914; King's proctor for prize ct. proceedings; K.C. for the colony, Dec., 1914.

GRIFFIN, C. T., I.S.O. (1911).—M.R.C.S., Eng.; L.R.C.P., Edin.; L.S.A., Lond.; suptlg. med. offr., Dikoya, Ceylon, 1st Jan., 1883; dist. med. offr., 17th Jan., 1888; govt. med. offr., 1st Sept., 1892; col. surg., prov. of Uva, 21st Sept., 1898; ast. prin. civ. med. offr. and insp. gen. of hospitals, 17th Jan., 1901. GRiffin, Eugene Patrick.—Third clk., treas., Gibraltar (after compet. exam.), Feb., 1883; pol. clk., 1883; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, 1886; 2nd class clk., Jan., 1890; 1st class clk., Jan., 1893; ch. clk. and cashier, P.O., Nov., 1893; transferred to col. sec.'s office, July, 1895; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, and regis. of births, marriages, and deaths, 1913.

GRIMSHAW, Herbert C. W.—Ed. private schl. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A., 1885; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1893; dist. comnsh. Gold Coast, Sept., 1902; ag. solr.-gen., 1907; cantonment mag., Coomassie, July, 1909; prov. comnsh., 25th Feb., 1911.

GRINDLE, G. E. A. C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1869; scholar of Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 1st cl. classical mods., 1889; 1st class in final classical schl., 1891; B.A., 1891; elected fellow of C.C.C. 1891; Chancellor's English Essay, 1892; M.A., 1894; 2nd class clk. in local govt. board, Feb., 1893; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1895; 2nd class clk., after compet. exam., in office of S. of S. for Cola., 10th Feb., 1896; ass. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Nov., 1898; 1st class clk., 30th June, 1900; prin. clk., 30th June, 1909.

GRINLINTON, F. H., C.M.G. (1903), B. 1853; F.R.G.S., A.I.C.E., F.R. Met. S.—aptd. to Ceylon survey dept., Apr., 1870; dist. survr., Jan., 1877; ch. survr., Nov., 1881; ag. survr.-gen., Aug., 1894; survr.-gen., Dec., 1896; ret. June, 1904; proceeded to F.M.S. to advise govt. on reorganizn. of survey dept., Oct., 1904.

GROOM, HON. LITTLETON ERNEST, M.A., LL.M.—B. 1867; M.P. for Darling Downs, Queensland; elected to 1st lse. of rep., C. of Aust., 1901; re-elected, Dec., 1903; mem. of royal comsns. on Iron Bonus, 1902, and Navigation Bills, 1904-5; several times Crown prosecutor, and twice ag. judge of dist. ct., Queensland; min. for home affairs, C. of Aust., 5th July, 1905; atty.-gen., 13th Oct., 1906; min. of external affairs, June, 1909, to Apr., 1910; min. of trade and customs, June, 1913-Sept., 1914.

ROSS, D.—aptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and temporarily employed in the bd. of agric. and fisheries, Nov., 1906, to Apr., 1907; in the bd. of educn., Apr. to June, 1907; in the Scotch educn. dept., June to Aug., 1907; at general post office, Sept., 1907, to Jan., 1908; assigned to the C.O., Jan., 1908.

GUPPY, ROBERT.—Imperial postal serv., 31st Mar., 1892; ch. clk. and acctnt., post and tel. dept., G. Coast, 1900; ag. dir. of telegraphs, 2nd June to 26th July, 1901; acctnt., 1902; prepared system of accounting passed in Council and brought into force 1st Sept., 1902.
GURNEY, JOHN AUGUSTUS.—B. 1855; ed. at Chelt. Coll., and Jesus Coll. (Camb.), LL.B.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1877; parly. draughtsman, Victoria, May, 1882; Crown prosecutor, Mar., 1889.

GURNEY, WALTER EDWIN.—Temporarily employed in audit and control off., Cape Colony, June, 1880; apptd. book-keeper, Feb., 1886; aspr. and acctnt., Dec., 1889; asst. cont. and auditor-gen., July, 1896; ag. cont. and auditor-gen., June, 1903; cont. and auditor-gen. and shrmn. of tender bd., Nov., 1903; cont. and auditor-gen., Union of S. Africa, 1910.
GUSHUE, GEO. WILLIAM.—B. 1854; M.H.A. or Trinity Dist., Newfoundland, 1894; again elected, 1900; re-elected, 1904; min. of pub. wks., 900.

GWYN, CECIL.—B. 1867; apptd. to pay and accounts branch, W.A.F.F., Oct., 1898; asst. reasr., N. Nigeria, 1901; chief asst. treasr., 1908; reasr., Gambia, Oct., 1909; ag. gov., Oct., 1909; g. col. sec., Oct., 1909, to Jan., 1910, July to Oct., 1910, and from July to Nov., 1912; ag. chief mag., July to Nov., 1911; ag. gov., Oct. to Dec., 1912; senior J.P. and comar. of ct. of equests.
HAANEL, EUGENE EMIL FELIX RICHARD, Ph.D.(Brd.), Mem. A.I.C.E., A.Ph.S., A.E.C.S., tc.—B. 1841; entered civ. ser., Canada, 1901; apptd. dir. of mines, 1907.
HADDON, ERNEST B., M.A., F.R.A.I.—B. 1882; ed. Christ's Coll., Camb.; nat. science ripos., 1904; dip. anthropol., Camb., 1912; asst. ollr., Uganda, 1905; dist. comsnr., 1912.

HADDON-SMITH, SIR GEO. BASIL, K.C.M.G. (1915); C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1861; lieut. Royal Guernsey militia, 1879; attached 32nd (D.C.L.I.) regt., 1880; attached 107th (Royal Sussex) regt., 1881; asst. inspr., Lagos constab., 1886; adjt., 1887, 1889, and 1893; ag. inspr.-gen. and sheriff of Lagos, 1888, 1890, 1891, and 1893; ag. dist. comsnr., 1887, 1888, 1889, and 1891; took part in Jebu expedition, 1892, mentioned in despatches (medal and clasp); political offr. on Sir G. Carter's mission to Yorubaland, 1893; ast. col. sec., Lagos, 1894; ag. col. sec., 1895; priv. sec. to Sir F. Scott in Ashanti expdn., 1896; ch. ass. col. sec., G. Coast, 1896; ag. col. sec., 1896-7; mem. of exec. and legis. coun., G. Coast; political offr. to Col. Sir J. Willcocks in the Ashanti expdn., 1900; took part in relief of Kumasi, spec. ment. in desps. (Ashanti medal and clasp); ag. gov. Gambia, July to Oct., 1901; col. sec., S. Leone, 1901-1911; ag. gov. of S. Leone on many occasions; gov., Bahamas, 1912; gov., Windward Is., 1914.

HADEN, FRANCIS SEYMOUR, B.A., C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1850; ed. at Westminster schl., and at Chr. Ch., Oxford; priv. sec. to Sir Henry Bulwer, K.C.M.G., lieut.-gov. of Natal, Mar., 1877; ag. ch. clk., col. ass. of office, Mar., 1880; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1881; acted as col. treas. and also as col. sec., 1885; col. sec., 1887; was chmn. of Natal trade coms., 1885-6; deleg. to customs and rly. confce., Cape Town, 1888; dep. gov., Natal and Zululand in Dec., 1890; dep. gov. in Aug., 1891; ret., 1893, on introduction of responsible gov't.
HAGETTHORN, HON. F.—Comsnr. of works, Victoria, Dec., 1913.

HAHN, D. M., M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1867; ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, Real school, Hamburg, and Technological Inst., Hamburg; in charge of govt. saw mill, Trinidad, Jan., 1887; foreman, P.W.D., Jan., 1888; ag. asst. engr., 1891; ast. engr., in charge of wks. and bldgs., Feb., 1894; ast. engr., office and wks., Nov., 1899; engr., office and wks., Apr., 1905; ag. div. engr., N. Div., 1903 and 1907, in addition to substantive duties; ag. inspr. of mines, 1907 to June 1908; and dep. inspr. of mines, July, 1908, in addition to substantive duties; mem. bd. of industrial training.

HAINES, REV. FRANK WM., B.A. (Oxon).—Ed. Christ's Coll., Brecknock, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; govt. tutor and chaplain, Selangor, 1890; inspr. of schls., Selangor, 1891; col. chaplain, Malacca, 1899; also ag. headmr., High schl., Malacca, 1899-1900; col. chap., Penang, Apr., 1901; (Surrogate), hon. chaplain, Penang volunteer corps.

HALCOMB, FRED., M.A. (Wadham Coll., Oxon).—B. 1836; parly. librarian, S. Australia, Jan., 1870; clk., ast. and sergt.-at-arms, legis. coun., Apr., 1874; clk. house of assem., May, 1887; clk., legis. coun., and clk. of parlaments, 1901.
HALDANE, J. O.—Ast. collr., Uganda Prot., 6th May, 1904; dist. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1908.

HALL, HON. D. R.—Barrister-at-law; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1902-1905; mem. for Werriwa, N.S.W., in federal parlmt., 1906 to 1st Apr., 1912, when he resigned; apptd. to legis. coun., N.S.W., and took office as solr.-gen. and min. of justice, 2nd Apr., 1912; atty.-gen. and min. of justice, 1914.

HALL, GILBERT AMOS.—Ed. at Harrow schl.; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final exam. in Malay, Apr., 1891; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Julv., 1891; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July to Sept., 1893; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Mar., 1894; supt. of educn., Penang, Oct., 1894; ag. dist. offr. Malacca, Nov., 1896; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, May, 1897; sheriff and dep. regist., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1897; ag. collr. id. rev., Penang, Mar., 1898; 2nd ast. col. sec., S. Stltmts., Aug., 1898, but continued to act as collr. id. rev., Penang, till Mar., 1900; ag. collr. id. rev. and offr. in charge of treasy., Malacca, Sept., 1900, and confirmed, Aug., 1902; sen. dist. offr., July, 1904; ag. P.M.G., Aug., 1904; ag. inspr. of prisons, S. Sttlmtns., Oct., 1905; ag. asst.-gen., S. Sttlmtns., Aug. to Dec., 1907; ag. dist. judge, and 1st mag., Penang, 1909; dist. judge and 1st mag., Singapore, 3rd Apr., 1911, but continued to act in Penang; ag. dir. of educntn., Feb., 1912.
HALL, ROGER EVANS.—B. 1883; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; 3rd cl. hons. mods.; 2nd cl. hons. final jurisprudence; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1908; asst. dist. comm-r., Gold Coast, 1910.

HALL, MAJOR W. DURHAM.—Asst. commnr. of pol., G. Coast, 1894; served in Ashanti expdt., 1895-6 (received the "Ashanti Star"); attached to London Met. Pol. for course of instr., and to L.C.C. for instr. in weights and measures (received certif. from Council), 1896; ag. commnr. of pol., sheriff, mag. and gov. of pris., G. Coast, 1898; ag. dist. commnr., 1898; attached to 1st batt. Royal Sussex regt., 1898-99; passed school of instr., 1900; served with Ashanti expdt., 1900 (specially selected to raise levies, ment. in desps., medal and clasp); attached to 1st batt. Royal Rifle Reserve regt., 1900-01; offr.'s musketry certif., Hythe, 1901; apptd. to a company, W. African regt., May, 1901; ag. adjut., June to Dec., 1901; garrison adjut. and intell. offr., S. Leone, Jan., 1902, to May, 1903; A.D.C. to ag. govrs., S. Leone, 1902-3; asst. commnr., N. Nigeria constab., 1903, and assisted in raising and organising the force; served in Bassa expdt., N. Nigeria, 1904 (W. African general service medal and clasp); attached to Brighton pol. for course of instr., 1905; local commit. of pol. and asst. commnr., Cyprus, Dee., 1905; chief commandant, 1913.

HALLETT, WM. CARDY.—B. 1859; ed. at Mt. Allison Univ., New Brunswick; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. to col. survr., Bermuda, 1st Aug., 1881; asst. supt. of pub. wks., 1st Jan., 1887; supt. of pub. wks., 1st July, 1887; col. survr., 1st Oct., 1887; has also served as suptdg. engr. of channel improvement wks., 1894-96, for which received thanks of gov. and exec. coun.; mem. of parish boundaries commn., 1899; spec. employed on survey of Jenkins boiler channel, 1902; visited London in connection with St. George's harbour improvements, Feb. to Apr., 1903; mem. of St. George's harbour commn., 1903; seconded for spec. work preparing plans and documents for St. George's channel wks., 1st Dec., 1903, to 31st Mar., 1904; mem. of Sandys parish road commn., 1904.

HALLEY, JOHN.—B. 1873; ed. Gordon's Coll., Marischal Coll., and Univ. of Aberdeen, M.B., C.M., D.Ph.; civ. surg. attached to R.A.M.C., Sierra Leone, 1900-2; ditto, S. Africa, 1902; dist. med. offr., Fiji, 1903; health offr. and local authority, Suva, 1903; supt. of Muana leper asylum, Beqa, 1904; dist. med. offr., Navua, 1905; stip. mag. of the colony, 1905; govt. med. offr., Levuka, 1906; ag. rec. commnr., Rotumah, 1908.
HALLECK, ROBT. HENRY.—B. 1878; ed. Dollar Academy, Scotland; clk., central repatriation bd., Bloemfontein, Apr., 1902, to May, 1903; clk. to col. sec., O.R.C., May, 1903.

HALLIFAX, EDWIN RICHARD.—Ed. Blundell's schl.; Blundell's scholar, Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1896; cadet, Hong Kong, 1897; passed cadet, 1900; ag. police mag., New Territory, 1899; pol. mag., Hong Kong, 1899; J.P., 1900; asst. supt. of pol., and pol. mag., N.T., 1900; ag. registr.-gen., Mar.-June, 1902; acted as dep. supt. of pol., asst. supt. of fire brigade, capt. supt. of pol., etc., on several occasions, 1902-04; seconded as Transvaal emigr. agt. at Chinwangtao, Mar., 1905; dist. offr., N.T., Sept., 1907; in addition acted as asst. land offr., N.T., May-Aug., 1908; Aug.-Sept., 1908; ag. 1st pol. mag., Oct., 1909, to May, 1911; ag. dep. supt. of police and asst. supt. of fire brig., May, 1911; ag. registr.-gen. and M.L.C., 30th Oct., 1911; M.E.C., 1st Mar., 1912; registr.-gen., 29th Nov., 1912; till altered to sec. for Chinese affairs, 25th July, 1913.

HALLIFAX, F. J.—Cadet, S. Sttlmtns., Mar., 1893; passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1894; dist. offr., Penang, Apr., 1896, and Malacca, Nov., 1896; supt. of educn., Penang, July, 1897; dist. offr., June, 1898; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, June, 1901; ag. coll. of land revenue, Penang, Apr., 1902; also ag. registr. of deeds and regist. of rec. sale, Penang, May, 1902; asst. P.M.G., Penang, Aug., 1902, but continued to act as coll. of land rev.; ag. collr. of land rev., and offr. in charge of Treasury, Malacca, Apr., 1903; 2nd mag. Penang, Jan., 1905; ag. sen. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1906; pres. mun. commnr., Penang, 1st Oct., 1906; collr. of land revenue, Simpang, 1st Oct., 1908, but remained seconded as pres. mun. commnr., Penang; pres., mun. commnr., Singapore, 1st Jan., 1911; promoted to C.O., 1st Jan., 1912, but remained seconded.

HALL-JONES, HON. SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1910).—B. 1851; ed. at public schls.; arrived in New Zealand, 1875; mem. of house of representatives for Timaru, New Zealand, since 1890; min. of pub. wks. and marine, 1896; premier, treas., min. for educn. and labour, June, 1906; resig. premiership, and assumed portfolio of pub. wks. and rly. in Ward ministry, Aug., 1906; asst. commnr. in Lond., 1908-1912.

HAMBLIN, R.A.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the col. ser. and temporarily employed in the science div. dept. from Mar. 21st to May 26th, 1898; assigned to C.O., July, 1899.

HAMILTON, CHARLES BOUGHTON, C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1859; entered the service, 1885; clk., rec.-gen.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1873, 1877; book-keeper, 1879; ag. rec.-gen., 1881; and ag. gen., 1883 to 1886; received thanks of coun. for leaving post of rec.-gen., &c., Trinidad, 1886; J.P.; ag. audr.-gen., M.E.C., and M.L.C., 1886; mem. of bd. of educn., 1887; of bd. of health, 1888; M.L.C., 1889; chmn. of comtee. for widows fund, 1889; ag. col. sec., 1890; chmn. of improvement comtee., and ag. audr.-gen. and col. sec., 1891; mem. coll. coun., 1891; rec.-gen. Br. Guiana, Dec., 1892; ag. aud.-gen. and mem. of policy, May, 1893, to May, 1895, and ag. Oct., 1895; sen. commnr., Vlissingen estate; chmn. spirits commn.; "Trotman," trust comtee. for widows and orphans fund, &c.; mem. exec. comtee., Dec., 1895; off. mem. of policy, July, 1898; chrmn., excise bd., 1901; chrmn., educn. comtee., 1902; vote of thanks of comb. ct. for spec. ser. gratuitously rendered in public roads and bridges adminstr., 1894 to 1905; chmn. of comtee. for sub-dividing colony into districts, 1907; received thanks of S. of S. for "valuable services rendered the Crown and Colony during long and honourable term of office," Aug., 1908, govt. director Demerara Rly., July, 1910.

HAMILTON, ERIC WILLIAM, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Blundell's; exhibtn., Sid. Suss. Coll., Camb., 1906; scholar, 1907; B.A., 1909; cadet, Hong Kong civ. ser., 17th Nov., 1911; ag. sec., sanitar. bd., 1914; J.P., 1914.
HAMILTON, FREDERICK GEARY.—Ed. at St. Paul's schl., Lond., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; ass't collr., E. Africa Prot., 17th Dec., 1906; list consmr., E.A.P., Apr., 1912.
HAMILTON, KENNETH.—B. 1884; ed. Edin. Acad. and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; 3rd cls. economics trip; ass't res., N. Nigeria, 6th Apr., 1910.
HAMILTON, R.—Medical offr., E.A.P., Mar., 1911.

HAMILTON, ROBERT WILLIAM.—Ed. at St. Paul's schl. and Trin. Hall, Camb.; class scholar, 3.A., 1889 (honours); M.A., 1892; student Inner Temple, 1893; called to the bar, 1895; dist. omsnr., Lagos, 1895 to 1897; registr., E. Africa Prot., 1897; town mag., 1899; ass't judge and admistr.-gen., 1900; judge of high court of E. Africa, 11th Aug., 1902; apptd. to serve on land comm., 31st Oct., 1904; prin. judge, E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1905.

HAMILTON, SIR W. A. B., K.C.M.G. (1897), C.B. (1892), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1844; ed. at Harrow; apptd. a jun. clk. in the C.O., May, 1864; 1st compet. exam.; sent on secret service to N. America, 1867; 2nd cls. clk., 30th Sept., 1st cls. lk., July, 1879; and priv. sec. to Mr. Knatchbull-Hugessen (afterwards Lord Brabourne), M.P., 26th Oct., 1872; called to the bar (Inner Tem.), 8th Nov., 1872; priv. sec. to Mr. James Lowther, I.P., under sec. of state for the cols., 25th Feb., 1874, to 14th Feb., 1878; priv. sec. to the Right Hon. James Lowther, M.P., ch. sec. for Ireland, 5th Feb., 1878; capt. in the E. Lothian yeomanry cavalry, 5th June, 1878; major, 18th June, 1883; on lieut.-col. and comdg. regt., 1894; comdg. 2nd yeomanry brigade, 1895; author of "Mr. Fontenelle: a Romance of the Civil Service"; priv. sec. to Mr. Stanhope, 3rd Aug., 1886; and Sir H. Holland (afterwards Lord Knutsford), Jan., 1887, to Aug., 1892; sec. to col. confere, 1887; priv. clk., 1st Jan., 1894; ch. clk., Mar., 1896; et., Jan., 1909; offr. of arms of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1901-1911; Gentleman Jerer of the Blue Rod, 1911.
HAMLIN, ANDREW DOUGLAS.—R. 1886; unr. asst. treas'r., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; asst. rear., Nov., 1910; asst. sec., Oct., 1912.

HANCOCK, HERBERT HENRY, M.A.—Scholar & John's Coll., Oxon; 2nd class classical mods., 1876; 2nd class classical finals, 1878; B.A., 1879; L.A., 1883; asst. master Cheltenham Coll., 1879; nd master Bosworth schl., 1880; prof. classics and moral philos., Codrington Coll., Barbados, 1883; ag. prin., 1889-91; 2nd master, Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, 1892; ag. prin., Apr., to Dec., 1894, June to Aug., 1898, Sept. to Dec., 1902, and an. to Dec., 1908.

HANCOCK, WM. JOHN, M. INST. C.E., I.I.E.E.—B. 1865; hon. loc. sec., I.I.E.E., Lond.; d. at Univ. of Glasgow; supt. of telephones, V. Aust., 1885; supt. of telegraphs, 1890; govt. electrical engnr., 1894.

HANCOX, STANLEY EWART.—B. 1890; B.A., Iron.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1914; office sett. to gov't. agt., S. Prov., June, 1914; extra ito, Central Prov., June, 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Valle, in addition to his own duties, July, 1914; attached to Colombo customs, Sept., 1914.

HAND, JOHN ST. VINCENT.—lst cls. clk., exec.'s off., Lagos, Oct., 1898; ch. clk., gen. man.'s off., Lagos gov't. rlyw., Apr., 1901; asst. sec., exec.'s off., S. Nigeria, Feb., 1904; priv. sec. to J. Thorburn, Esq., ag. gov. of Lagos in 1905, 1906, 1907, 1908, 1909 and 1910; and clk. to exec. coun.; asst. col. sec., S. Nigeria, 31st July, 1907.
HANDS, ARTHUR L. C.—B. 1880; clk. in P.O., St. Helena, Sept., 1896; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s off., Apr., 1898; ch. clk. and clk. to exec. coun., Jan., 1907; J.P., 1910.
HANITSCH, KARL RICHARD, PH.D. (Jena).—B. 1860; demonstrator of Zoology, Univ. Coll., Liverpool, 1887; curator and librarian, Raffles library and museum, Singapore, 1895.

HANKEY, CAPT. M. P. A., C.B. (1912).—B. 1877; ed. Rugby; joined R.M.A., 1893; served in H.M.S. "Ranillies," flagship and 2nd flagship Mediterranean, 1899-1901; naval intell. dept., 1902-6; granted temporary rank of Major whilst serving on a special comtee., 1906; intell. offr., Mediterranean, 1907; asst. sec., comtee. of Imp. defence, 1908; apptd. sec., 1912.

HANLEY, ALLAN HASTINGS, C.M.G. (1903); F.R.C.S.—Med. offr., Niger Coast Prot. (now S. Nigeria), May, 1893; sen. med. offr., Apr., 1901; dep. prin. med. offr., May, 1903; ag. prin. med. offr., Dec., 1903, to Apr., 1904.

HANN, HUGH.—B. 1862; joined R.A. 1881; served in India, 1882 to 1900; ret. from serv., 1890; joined home prison serv., 1891; promoted clk. and schoolmaster, 1896; supt. of pris., S. Leone, 1891; sheriff and ag. supt. of pol., S. Leone, July to Sept., 1902; J.P. for colony, 1902; formed S. Leone fire brigade and first supt., 1893; 1st asst. supt. of pris., Ceylon, 1906; supt. of pris., Kandy, 1st Nov., 1906, to Sept., 1908; asst. supt., convict establishment, Colombo, Oct., 1908, to Dec., 1909; ag. insp.-gen. of prisons, Ceylon, and supt. of convict establishment, Sept. to Dec., 1909; supt., Mutwal and Hulftsdorp prisons, Jan. to Aug., 1910; supt., Mahara prison, Sept., 1910; ag. insp.-gen. of prisons, Sept. to Dec., 1911; supt., Fremantle prison, W. Aust., 1st Jan., 1912.

HANNA, HON. WM. JOHN, K.C.—Prov. sec. and regisr.-gen. of Prov. of Ontario, Canada; B. 1862; ed. at pub. schl., Brooke Tp., Lambton Co., Ontario; barrister-at-law; an unsuccessful candidate for H. of C. for W. Lambton at g.e., 1896 and 1900; first elected to legislature at g.e., 1902; re-elec. at g.e., 1905; prov. sec. in Whitney adminstr., 8th Feb., 1905; re-elec. by acclam. at bye-elec., 21st Feb., 1905; re-elec. at g.e., 1908, 1911 and 1914.
HANNAY, HENRY DESMOND.—Sub-inspr., Bechuana land Prot. police, 1901; ag. mag., N'Gami, 1907-08; inspr., Feb., 1914; ag. res. mag., 1914.

HANNYNGTON, JOHN ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1911).—Capt., Indian army; temp. maj., King's African Rifles; ed. at Unit. Serv. Coll., Westward Ho!; passed in Hindustani, higher standard, Pushu, higher standard, Kiswahili, govt. test. exam. for promotion to field offr. and staff employ; gazetted 8th June, 1889; apptd. comdt., Jubaland Camel Corps, 15th Dec., 1901; comdt., 6th King's African Rifles, Somaliland Prot., 28th Feb., 1905.

HANSON, B. E.—Ed. Clifton Coll.; clk., exchequer and audit dept., 19th Apr., 1899; clk., audit off., Hong Kong, 1st June, 1900; ag. local aud., 31st Mar., 1901, to 1st Aug., 1902; returned to head office, July, 1903; asst. aud., Uganda, 9th June, 1904; ag. local aud., May to Nov., 1904, and July to Nov., 1906; local aud., N. Nigeria, 3rd Mar., 1908; local aud., Sierra Leone, 23rd July, 1909.
HARAN, JAMES AUGUSTINE, C.M.G. (1909); M.D., M.A.—Med. offr., E. Africa Prot., 1st Feb., 1898; med. offr. of health, Mombasa, Nov., 1908; sen. med. offr., 1910; dep. P.M.O., Apr., 1913.

HARBIN, JOHN ALPHONSO.—Ch. clk. to regis., Grenada, July, 1884; ditto to regis. sup. ct., July, 1886; marshal V.-A. ct., Mar., 1887; ag. regis. sup. ct., and ag. regis. in V.-A. ct., May, 1888; to Apr., 1890; acctnt., treasv., July, 1890; sec. to commn. pub. wks. dept. inquiry, Dec., 1890; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1892, to Feb., 1893; ag. inspr. of schools, Feb., 1893, to Mar., 1894; inspr. of schools, Mar., 1894; ag. col. postmaster, Aug.-Sept., 1894; mem. bd. of ed., Apr., 1895; ag. regis. sup. ct., Aug.-Sept., 1901; ag. inspr. of schools, St. Vincent, Sept.-Nov., 1901; mem. bd. of ed., St. Vincent, Sept.-Nov., 1901; ag. pol. mag. and asst. treas., Carriacou, Dec., 1901, to Jan., 1902.

HARCOURT, ALGERNON BERNARD, I.S.O. (1912).—B. 1863; apptd. cons. agt. in Oil R. Protect., Oct., 1892; ag. v.-cons., Bonny dist., June, 1894, to July, 1895; dist. comsnr., Bonny, 1st Apr., 1896; ag. v.-cons. cent. div. on various occasions; transf. to Cross Riv. div., Dec., 1899; travelling comsnr., S. Nig., 1st Apr., 1900; ag. div. comsnr., Cross Riv. div., May-Dec., 1900; ag. res. Benin City Territories, 1901 and 1902; divl. comsnr., Cross River, 17th Jan., 1903; polit. offr., M'Kpani exped., 1903 (medal and clasp); ag. sec. and dep. high comsnr., 24th Feb. to 3rd Apr., 1905; asst. prov. comsnr., E. Prov., 1st May, 1906; ag. prov. comsnr., E. Prov., June to Dec., 1906; ditto, W. Prov., 1st Jan. to 31st Mar., 1907; ditto, W. and C. Provs., from 9th Sept., 1907, to 14th Sept., 1908; ditto, E. Prov., from 23rd June to Dec., 1912.

HARCOURT, Rt. Hon. LEWIS, P.C. (1905).—B. 1863; ed. at Eton; Hon. D.C.L., Oxford; a trustee of the British Museum, Wallace Collection and London Museum; ecclesiastical comsnr.; comsnr. of exhibition of 1851; hon. F.R.I.B.A.; mem. of com. and executive of British schl. at Rome; M.P. for Rossendale, Lancashire, since 1904; first comsnr. of wks., 1905; sec. of state for the colonies, Nov., 1910.

HARDING, ALFRED J.—B. 1878; ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and St. John's, Camb.; foundtn. schlr., 1889; 1st class nat. sci. trip part I., 1900; B.A., 1900; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd el. clk., Oct., 1901; sec. to comtce. to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1908; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 1st Feb., 1909; sec. to Royal comsnr. on Mauritius, 1909; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, Nov., 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., Sept., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from 1st Apl., 1912.

HARDING, COLONEL COLIN, C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1863; formerly ch. inspr. of native police, Mashonaland; served in Mashonaland rebellion, 1896-7; comdt. of Barotse native pol., 1901-1906; dist. comsnr., Northern territories, G. Coast, 1910.

HARDING, EDWARD J., M.A. (1907).—B. 1880; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1899); 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1903; B.A., 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., marine dept., bd. of trade, 30th Nov., 1903; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 31st May, 1904; sec. to distressed colonial seamen comtce., 1909; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 14th Oct., 1912; sec. to Dominions Royal Comsn., Dec., 1912.

HARE, REGINALD CHARLES.—B. 1853; clk. treasy., W. Australia, Apr., 1881; pol. inspr. S. dista., June, 1886; ag. gov't. res., Wyndham, May, 1888; ditto, Roebourne, June, 1889; sec. W. Australian govt. agency, London, May, 1891; ag. agent-gen., 1909.
HARLEY, JOHN A.—M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), certif. Lond. S.T.M. (with distinction), Jul., 1899; apptd. to W.A.M.S., Gambia, 16th Apr., 1910; ag. protectorate med. offr., 2nd Jan. to 11th May, 1911.

HARMAN, WM. HENRY.—B. 1882; appd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of civ. serv. and assigned to science and art dept., 10th Apr., 1900; and to bd. of agric., 6th July, 1901; transf. to C.O., 27th Jan., 1902.

HARPER, CHARLES HENRY.—Ed. at Blundell schl., Devon, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; R.I. 1899; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 5th May, 1909; cadet, G. Coast, 24th Jan., 1909; attached Ashanti field force, 1st June to 1st Sept., 1910; asst. dist. comsnr., 6th Sept., 1901; ag. ast. cd. sec., 26th Oct., 1904; ast. col. sec., 8th Feb., 1906; ag. chief ast. col. sec. and clk. of occas., 14th Apr. to 25th June, 1908; cantonment mg., Coomassie, 16th Oct., 1908; prov. comsnr., 23rd July, 1909; census offr., 1911; ag. chief ast. clk. of couns., 15th May to 29th June, 1912; ag. col. sec., June to Oct., 1912.
HARPER, FRANCIS HENRY.—B. 1877; transferred from the service of the Royal Navy to supplementary clk. to C.O., 1st Jan., 1906; ast. clk., Nov., 1912.

HARRIGAN, SAMUEL SIMONS.—Headmaster Wesleyan schl., Virgin Is., 1878; reng. ast. clerical ast., govt. offices, Road Town, 1880; ag. govt. offr., West End, Nov., 1894, Feb., 1895 and Feb. to Apr., 1907; govt. offr., Road Town, 1897.

HARRIOTT, EDMUND COWLES.—B. 1866; di. to comsnr., and to legis. bd., Turks and Caicos Is., Nov., 1887; inspected govt. schls., 1890, 1891 and 1902; ag. regist. sup. et., and of deeds, 1891 and 1894; ag. ast. comsnr. (dist. mag. resp. sup. ct. and regist.-gen., man. savings bank, inspr. of pol. and prisons), G. Turk, May to Jul., 1896, Aug. to Nov., 1901, and May to Aug., 1906; J.F., 1901; ag. aud., Aug. and Sept., 1901, and June to Aug., 1906; ag. dist. mag., visiting Caicos Is., July, 1902; ag.acctnt. and clk. (coll. of cost. postmtr., cashier and regist. shipping), comsnr. office, Apr. to June, 1903; confirmed July, 1903; ag. off. mem., legis. bd., 1906; 1st cls. clk. rev. dept., Jamaica, Jan., 1907; ag. ast. comsnr., Salt Cay., May to July, 1909; ag. depurt. comsnr., Aug. to Nov., 1909.

HARRIS, CHARLES ALEXANDER, C.B. (1904), C.M.G. (1900); M.V.O. (1911), B.A.—B. 1857; scholar, prizeman, and Porteau medalist of Christ Coll., Camb.; Tandred student in com. law, Linc. Inn, 1877; 12th classic, 1878; apptd. 2nd cls. clk. C.O., after open competn., 1st July, 1879; sec. W. Indies Finance Comms., Dec., 1882; ast. sec. Sugar Bounties Confce., 1889; priv. sec. to Mr. Buxton, M.P., Jan., 1894, to June, 1895; and to the Earl of Selborne, June, 1895; 1st class cls., 1896; specially engaged in work connected with Venezuelan bdry. arbitn., 1896-8; prin. clk., Nov., 1898; sent to Paris, 1899, in connection with Venez. bdry. arbitn.; conducted British case in Brazilian-B. Guiana boundary arbitn., 1901; mem. of comtce. to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1908; ch. clk., 1st Feb., 1909; represents C.O. on advisory comtce. of comm. intell., B. of T.; mem. of managing comtce. of Imp. Inst.; author of numerous articles in Doc. of Pol. Econ. on economic and statistical subject and of lives of various colonial govs. and states in Dict. of Nat. Biog.

HARRIS, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1884; entd. ser. of Imperial Post Office, 1st Jan., 1900; transf. to dept. of posts and telegraphs, Cape of Good Hope, 13th Nov., 1902; resig., Dec., 1907, and entd. S. Rhodesia telegraph serv., 1st Jan., 1908; seconded for serv. with African Trans-Continental Telegraph Co., 12th April, 1908; resig., 31st Jan., 1910; provincial postmaster, S. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1910; asst. supt. of posts and telegraphs, Fed. Malay States, 14th June, 1913.
HARRIS, REGINALD ROBERT.—B. 1890; clk., govt. sec.'s office, Nyasaland Prot., 18th June, 1913.

HARRISON, CUTHBERT WOODVILLE.—B. 1874; ed. at Sherborne Schl., Clifton Coll., Balliol Coll., Oxford, and with Walter Wren, Esq.; 3rd cl. class. moda., 1894; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1897; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, May, 1899; also ag. harb. mast., Klang, Feb., 1901; passed cadet, Apr., 1901; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, continued also as ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, and harb. mast. at Port Swettenham, Oct., 1901; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., 1903; ditto, Tampin, Mar., 1904; also asst. dist. offr., Matang, Oct., 1904; ag. asst. sec. to res., Mar., 1905; ag. sec. to res., Aug., 1906; ag. asst. sec. to res., Dec., 1906; ag. regiar., sup. ct., Nov., 1907; ag. asst. dist. offr., Larut, Feb., 1908; also ag. dist. offr., Larut, and regiar. of titles, Perak North, Aug., 1908; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Mar., 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., Larut and Krian, and regiar. of titles, Perak North in addition, Feb., 1911; ag. sec. to res., Jan., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Larut, May, 1912; editor of "Illustrated Guide to F.M.S."
HARRISON, D. H.—Asst. traffic manager, Uganda Rly., Aug., 1912.

HARRISON, JAMES HERBERT HUGH.—Ed. Regent's Park Coll., and New College (Lond.), and at University Coll. and Hospital; F.R.C.P. (Lond.); M.R.C.S. (Eng.); dist. mag. and dist. surg., Cayo, Br. Honduras, 1890; ag. dist. surg., Orange Walk, 1891, 1892; dist. commnr. and dist. surg., Orange Walk, June, 1892; asst. col. surg., Belize, 12th May, 1897; col. surg., 1st Sept., 1906.

HARRISON, JOHN BURCHMORE, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1836; Nat. Sc. scholar, Christ's Coll., Camb., B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripos), 1873; M.A., 1887; prof. of chemistry and agricul. science, Barbados, Sept., 1879; govt. analyst and prof. of chem., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1889; director of dept. of science and agric., B. Guiana, Jan., 1905; is F.I.C., F.G.S., F.C.S., and F.G.S. of America; author of numerous papers relating to the agric. and geology of the W. Indies and B. Guiana; on spec. serv. to U.S.A. in connection with questions relating to sugar analysis, recd. thanks of U.S. govt.; specially thanked on several occasions by S. of S. for services in connection with tropical agriculture, etc.

HARRISON, NORMAN, A.M.I.E.E.—Junn. clk., dept. of posts and telegraphs, Pietermaritzburg, 1st Feb., 1890; 4th cls. clk., 27th Feb., 1894; 3rd cls. clk., 1st Sept., 1894; 2nd cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1898; 2nd asst. engrnr., 1st Mch., 1898; 1st asst. engrnr., 1st Nov., 1901; ag. chief engrnr., from 6th June to 13th Sept., 1903; transf'd to Transvaal asst. engrnr. of telegraphs, 1st Dec., 1903; engrnr., 1st Apr., 1905; acted as chief engrnr., 5th Feb. to 4th Aug., 1908; ag. chief engrnr., 1st Nov., 1908; chief engrnr., 1st July, 1909; engrnr.-in-chief, Union of S. Africa, 19th Oct., 1910.

HARRISSON, SYDNEY THIRLWALL, C.M.G. (1908).—Ed. Blackheath; apptd. asst. acct., W.D., G.C. Col., 9th Nov., 1894; ch. acct., mel June, 1897; asst. acct., W.A.F.F., 26th Feb., 1898; ch. acct., 8th Nov., 1899; treas., N. Nig., 1st an., 1900; comptroller of customs, Barbados, 1908.
HART, WALTER EDWARD.—B. 1847; librarian, public library and museum, Mauritius, 1st Oct., 1903.

HART-DAVIS, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1874; ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford, B.A.; served in 13th I.Y. in South Africa, 1900 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); priv. sec. to Sir W. Haynes Smith, Cyprus, 1901; asst. insp. of schools, Cyprus, 1901 to 1906; passed exams. in Turkish and Greek, 1903; insp. of schools, Gold Coast, 1906; dis. comnr., 1906 to 1910; sec. to high comnr. for the Western Pacific, 1910.

HARTE, LOVELACE FRED. RALPH.—First class certif. at I.C. Coll. in Dec., 1875; articled law student, 3rd Jan., 1876; ag. clk. of the peace the same year; Jan., 1877, clk. in customs dept., Trinidad; clk. in audit office, Sept., 1878; 7th clk., Jan., 1883, June, 1887; 2nd clk., registr., sup. ct., 1887; ch. clk., 1897; also comnr. of oaths for whole isld.; ag. dep. registr. on occasions in 1896-7-8.
HARTLAND, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1882; ed. Brighton House Schl., Clifton, Cork Gram. Schl., Presentation Brothers Coll., Queen's Coll.; insp. of plantations, agricultural dept., Zanzibar, Nov., 1913.
HARTLEY, CHAS.—M.A., Cantab.; lecturer in English and modern languages, Royal Coll., Ceylon, 11th Dec., 1896; principal, Royal Coll., 23rd June, 1903.

HARTLEY, E. B., C.M.G. (1901).—Surg.-Lieut.-Col., C. M. R.; created V.C. for gallantry in the operations against the stronghold of the Basuto chief Morosi; prin. med. offr., Cape col. forces, 1878; served through the Gaika, Morosi, and Basuto wars, 1878-81; govt. med. offr., Basuto-land, 1874-7; ret., 1904.
HARVEY, DAVID HAROLD.—Entd. Natal civ. serv., Apl., 1898; transf'd, to Swaziland adminstr., as clk., Mar., 1903; sub-inspr., Swaziland police, Apl., 1907; dep. asst. comnr., Apl., 1911.

HARVEY, GEORGE LEONARD, C.E.—Asst. dir. of wks., So. Nigeria, June, 1900; asst. dir. of pub. wks., 1901; deputy dir. of pub. wks., 1901; ag. dir. of pub. wks., Mar. to Nov., 1902, and Apr. to Nov., 1904.

HARVEY, HON. HORACE, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1863; ed. Univ. Coll., Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1889; admitted to practice in N.W.T., 1893; regiar. of land titles for South Alberta land regist. dist., 1896; dep. atty.-gen. of N.W.T., 1900; puisne judge, sup. ct., N.W.T., June, 1904; puisne judge, sup. ct. of Alberta, 1907; ch. just. of Alberta, Oct., 1910.
HARWARD, JOHN.—Principal, Royal Coll., Ceylon, 1st Apr., 1892; dir. of pub. instrn., 23rd June, 1903; designation of office changed to dir. of educn., Aug., 1913.
HASKINS, WILFRED J., A.M.C.E.A.—Asst. supt. of wks., S. Sttlnts., Mar., 1905.

HASTINGS, A. C. G.—Served in 43rd Corp. I.Y. in S. African war, Jan., 1900, to Aug., 1901; attached to vice-consulate, Fez, Dec., 1901, to Sept., 1905; ag., vice-consul, Nov., 1903, to May, 1904; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Apl., 1906; 3rd cls. res., 1911.

HASZARD, HON. FRANCIS LONGWORTH.—B. 1849; ed. at Prince of Wales's Coll., Prince Edward Is.; called to the bar, 1872; K.C., 1894; elected to legis., P.E. Is., 1904 and 1905; mem. exec. coun., 1905; prem. and atty.-gen., P.E. Is., 1908; judge, supreme ct., and master of the rolls, P.E.I., 1911.

HATCHER, C. M.—B. 1873; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 11th Sept., 1893; transf. to C.O., 18th Mar., 1896; minor staff offr., Feb., 1902; staff clk., first grade, July, 1913.
HATHERTON, BARON.—COL. THE HON. EDWARD GEORGE PERCY LITTLETON, C.M.G. (1880); B. 1842; late mil. sec. to the gov.-gen. of Canada.
HATHORN, HON. KENNETH HOWARD, K.C.—B. 1849; puisne judge of supreme ct. of S. Africa (Natal prov. division), May, 1910.
HATTON, EDWIN FULLARTON, M.D., M.R.C.S.—Ed. at Upper Canada Coll. and Toronto Univ.; entd. col. serv., 1882; is now med. supt. of lunatic asylum and poor asylum, and dist. med. offr., Grenada; M.L.C.

HAUGHTON, T. H.—B. 1881; served in S. African Constab., Mar., 1901, to Dec., 1907; held rank of lieut. and sub-instr. (Queen's medal with five clasps); asst. res., Muri Prov., N. Nigeria, 9th May, 1908.

HAWKINS, ISAAC THOMAS, M.I.C.E.—B. 1858; mem. of incorp. assoc. of mun. and county engrs.; diplomas in engineering science and construction, sanitary science and pub. health law; articled and served through shops, 1875-1881; held two chief mun. apptmts., 1882-1888, and 1888-1897 (including the designing and carrying out of an extensive scheme of water supply); col. surv., Lagos, 19th June, 1897; dep. dir. of pub. wks., 1st Jan., 1898; prov. engnr., S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906; ag. D.P.W. for an aggregate period of 43 years, between 1897 and 1906.

HAY, SIR JAMES SHAW, K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1839; joined 89th (Princess Victoria's) regt. in 1858; served in India during the latter part of the Mutiny; was adjt. for some time, and afterwards instructor of musketry; ag. adjt. to the provisional depot batt. at Dum Dum in 1859; adjt. to the Glasgow Highland volrs. in 1868 at their formation; asst. inspr., G. Coast armed native pol., 1875; dist. comsnr. of Acora, Oct., 1875; inspr., G. Coast constab., Apr., 1877; astt. col. sec., G. Coast col., 1878; had charge of several special missions to the tribes in the interior; ag. col. sec. from Oct., 1878, to Jan., 1880, and again, Aug., 1880; inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius; admstr. of the Gambia, 1885; adminstd. the govt. of the W. Africa Sttlmts., from July, 1886, to Dec., 1887; gov., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1888; served in the Yonnie expdn., 1887-8 (medal and clasp); ag. admstr., Barbados, 1891; gov., Barbados, 1892 to 1900.

HAYCRAFT, THOS. WAGSTAFFE.—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1885; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; examr. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lond. chan. of arbitn., 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1899; ag. Queen's advocate, Oct., 1900, to Feb., 1901; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1911; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1913; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."
HAYES-WILLIAMS, WILLIAM GORDON.—B. 1862; registr.-gen., New South Wales, 1898.

HAYNES, ALWYN SIDNEY.—B. 1878; ed. at Haileybury Coll.; cadet, F.M.S. civ. ser., Nov., 1901; attached to dist. office, Batang Padang, Jan., 1902; ag. financial asst., Krian, Oct., 1902, to Apl., 1903; passed final exam. in law, Oct., 1902; mag. for Perak, 1902; passed final exam. in Tamil, Aug., 1903; ag. financial asst., Krian, Sept., 1903; administered estate of late Datoh Panglima Kinta in Perak, 1904-1906; passed final exam. in Malay, Nov., 1905; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Mar., 1906; ag. inspr. of schls., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1906; 1st cls. mag. Negri Sembilan, 1906; ag. ast. supt. of Indian immig., Penang, Nov., 1906; ast. supt., ditto, Nov., 1906; examiner in Tamil, Perak and Penang; ag. sup. of Indian immig., F.M.S. and S. Sttlmts., Feb. to May, 1910; ag. ast. supt. of immigts., Klang Jan., 1911; passed final exam. in Siamese, Aug., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Sept., 1912.

HAYNES, THOS. BENJAMIN.—Apptd. dispense penal settlement, Massaruni, B. Guiana, 6th June, 1870; 1st cls. prison warder, 12th July, 1877; smstrmr. to penal settmts., 1st Sept., 1878; dispenser in charge of leper asylum, Kaolins, 27th Oct., 1879; clk. to penal settmts., 27th Oct., 1883; keeper, New Amsterdam prison, Berbice, 5th July, 1887; supt., Georgetown prison, 15th June, 1894.
HAYNES, W. LINDSAY H.—Clk. to ch. just. Barbados, 1874-8; clk. to comsnrs. apptd. to investigate offences against rioters, 1876; int. offr., dist. B, 1878; now inspr. of int. rev. offr. reglar.

HAZELAND, FRANCIS ARTHUR.—B. 1861; ag. puisne judge, Hong Kong, Nov., 1878; ag. registrar, Mar. to Nov., 1882; clk. to ch. just., 1883; ag. ch. clk. col. sec. office, Mar., 1890; ag. clk. of coun., Mar. to Dec., 1890; registrar, and acctnt., Jan., 1896; J.P., 1894; reg. sup. et., offl. trustee, regiar. of compacts and land offr., Oct., 1895, to Feb., 1896; has passed in Chinese (higher standard); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Hilary, 1900; ag. pol. mag., Jun., 1900, to June, 1901; 2nd pol. mag., 7th June, 1901; ag. 1st pol. mag. and coroner on several occasions, 1902-7; 1st pol. mag. and coroner, 21st Mar., 1908; ag. atty.-gen., 28th Oct., 1909, to 1st May, 1910; ag. puisne judge, from 1st May, 1910, to 13th Nov., 1911, and from 16th Apr. to 3rd Sept., 1914.

HAZEN, HON. JOHN DOUGLAS, K.C., LL.D.—B. 1860; ed. at Collegiate Schl., Fredericton, and Univ. of New Brunswick; called to the bar, 1883; K.C., 1894; elec. to H. of C., Canada for St. John City and County, 1891; defeated, 1896; elec. to New Brunswick legis., for Sunbury, 1898, 1903, 1908; leader of the opposition in the New Brunswick legis. during that period; prem. and atty.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1908-1911; has been pres. of St. John law soc. and barristers' soc. of New Brunswick; mem. of the P.C. for Canada, and min. of marine and fisheries in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911.

HAZLERRIGG, GREY.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Camb., B.A., LL.B. (class. tripes, 1900, law tripes, 1905); dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Dec., 1908; ag. ast. col. sec., May, 1909; regiar. sup. et., Ceylon, Oct., 1910; sec. to comtece. on revision of code of civil procedure, Mar., 1912; called to Ceylon bar, July, 1914; sec., coun. of legal educn., July, 1914.
HAZLERRIGG, G. M.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1913.

HEAN, HON. ALEXANDER, C.M.G. (1912).—B. 1859; warden and coun. of Sorrell mun., Tasmania, since 1886; M.L.A. for Sorrell, 1906; min. of lands and wks., agric. and rlyw., and comsnr. of main roads, July, 1904; ag. premier, 1911.
HEARSEY, HERBERT HYDE YOUNG.—M.B., C.M. (Edin.), 1890; med. offr., Nyasaland Prot. Div., 1896; prin. med. offr., Apr. 1902.

HEARST, HON. WILLIAM HOWARD.—B. 1854; ed. pub. schls., Collingwood Coll. Inst., and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1888; K.C., 1908; prominent legal practitioner at Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario; now head of firm Hearst, Rowland and Brown; has been counsel in all mat.

HEATH, ASHTON MARLER, M.I.C.E. (1898); M.I.M.E. (1898).—B. 1859; ed. at Manchester gram. schl.; served at Messrs. Sharp, Stewart & Co.'s loco. wks., Manchester, 1877-1882; asst. under loco. supt., L. & S. W. Rly., 1882-1887; inspr. under Sir A. M. Rendel, K.C.I.E., 1887-1897; chief asst. engrnr. to J. Carruthers, Esq., M.I.C.E., consulting engrnr. to W. Aust. and N. Zealand govt., 1897-1900; entd. Crown Agents' office as head of engineering and wks. dept., 1900; head of engineering inspnr. dept., and chief inspecting engrnr. since 1904; mem. of engineering standards comtce., 1905; assoc. mem. of mech. transport comtce. of War Office, 1907; mem. of deptl. conference on deadlocks glaze, 1908.

HEATH, W. G.—M.B., Ch.B., Edin. Univ.; qualified 1897; med. offr., Windward dist., Dominica, 1898; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, 1901; ag. med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, 1903; litto. dist. "A," 1904; ag. sen. med. offr., Montserrat, 1905; pres., defence reserve, 1909; ag. nem. legis. coun., 1911; ag. med. offr., Antigua, 1911.

HEIDENSTAM, EDGAR HENRY DR.—Ed. Rossall and private tutors; ag. priv. sec. to Sir W. F. Haynes Smith, high comsrr. of Cyprus, 1st Nov., 1901, to 13th July, 1902; clk., chief sec.'s office, 14th July, 1902; inspr. of Cyprus military police, 1st Feb., 1905; a.-d.c. and priv. sec. to Sir C. King-Harman, and clk. to exec. coun., 1st Mar., 1906; clk. to legis. coun. and ast. clk., chief sec.'s office, 1st Oct., 1907; as. chief clk., C.S.O., 13th July to 15th Oct., 1908.
