Oxford; 2nd class classical mode, 1878; 2nd class in final classical schl., 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. in the office of the S. of S. for cols., 17th Apr., 1882; honourably mentioned for Cobden prize essay, Oxford 1883; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1884; Apr., 1891; ag. auditor-gen., Leeward Is., Oct., 1895, to Feb., 1896; priv. sec. to the Earl of Selborne, Sept., 1896; sec. to the Roy. comm. apptd. to inquire into condition of the W. Indies, Dec., 1896; 1st class clk., June, 1897; sent to Washington on special service in connection with W. Indian reciprocity negotiations, 1888; col. sec., Jamaica, 1900-1904; ag. gov., 1900, 1902 and 1904; returned to C.O. as a priv. clk., Oct., 1904; gov. of Jamaica, 20th Apr., 1907; assumed govt., 16th May, 1907; represented West India Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; perm. sec., bd. of agriculture and fisheries, 1913.

O'LOUGHLIN, HON. LAURENCE.—Mem. House of Assembly, S. Australia, 1890; comanr. of crown lands, 1896-9; ditto, 1899-1902; comanr. of crown lands, min. of agric. and min. controlling Northern Territory, 1905-1909; comanr. of pub. wks. and min. for the N. Territory, 6th June to 22nd Dec., 1909; comanr. of pub. wks. and water supply, 22nd Dec., 1909, to 5th June, 1910; speaker H. A., S. Australia, 1912.

O'MALLEY, SIR EDWARD LOUGHLIN, K.T. BACH. (1891).—B. 1842; ed. Trin. Coll., Camb. grad. B.A., 1864; M.A., 1868; called to the bar (Mid. Term.), 1866; joined Norfolk circuit; atty. gen. of Jamaica, 1876; atty.-gen., Hong Kong Nov., 1879; ch. just., S. Sttlmte., 1899; ret. 1892; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1895; judge of H.M. sup. consular ct. for the Ottoman empire, 1897; ret. 1904; mem. of Mauritius royal comm., 1909. O'Malley, Hon. King.—Formerly M.H.A., S. Australia; elec. to first H. of R., C. of A., 1901; re-elec., 1903 and 1906; min. for home affairs, C. of A., Apr., 1910 to June, 1913.

O'SHAUGHNESSY, JOHN J. F., M.Inst.E.E.—Major, Lon. Army troops, R.E.; served in S. Africa as capt. R.E., 1900-1902 (2 medals and 5 clasps); hon. capt. in the Army; asst. supt. telegraph construction, S. Nigeria, 1905; dep. director of telegraphs, S. Nigeria, 1907.
O'SULLIVAN, HON. THOS. K.C.—B.1856; solr., Queensland, 1878; called to the bar; sec. for pub. wks., Queensland, 1907; min. of agric., with a seat in legis. coun., Feb., 1908; is now atty.-gen.

OSWELL, WILLIAM ST. JOHN, F.R.G.S.—Ed. at Oswestry gram. schl. and by private tutor; lieut. 2nd batt. sharpshooters, Boer war, 1901-2 (medal and 5 clasps); asst. comsnr., S. Leone, 1903; comsnr., Karen dist., S. Leone Prot., 1906.
OTOOLE, W. F.—Entd. Bahamas civ. serv., 1893; 2nd clk., post office, 1904; Out Island comsnr. (2nd div.), 1909; 2nd cls. supervisor of customs, G. Coast; supervisor of customs, S. Nigeria, 1912.
OTTERTSON, HENRY, C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1846; apptd. to the staff of H. of Rep., N. Zealand, 1872; clk. of H. of Rep. since 1898.

OTTLEY, Rear-Admiral Sir CHARLES LANGLEY, K.C.M.G. (1907); C.B. (1911); M.V.O. (1903).—B. 1858; Peru, 1877; Egypt, 1882; has served as naval attache' to maritime cts. at Washington, Tokio, Rome, St. Petersburg, and Paris; dir. of naval intell., 1905-1907; sec. to commc. of Imp. defence, 1907; resig., 1912.

OVERY, CHAS. RAND.—Educ. at Yalding gram. schl.; ch. architectural draughtsman, Admiralty wks. dept., Keyham naval barracks, 1903-4; ch. draughtsman, survey dept., E.A.P., 1904-6; govt. architect, P.W.D., E.A.P., 16th July, 1908.

PACKER, STANLEY.—B. 1878; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford, B.A.; Middle Temple, 1902; asst. sec. Bankers' Institute, 1905-7; called to the bar, 1908; dist. mag., Kampala, Uganda Prot., July, 1910; ag. dist. mag., Entebbe, adminstr.-gen., principal regar. of documents, regar. of companies and offi. recr. in bankruptcy, Dec., 1910 to Sept., 1912, and from Apl. to Nov., 1913; ag. crown advoc., Uganda Prot., Dec., 1910, to Nov., 1911.
PADDLE, JAMES ISAAC.—F.R.C.S. (Eng.); M.D. (Lond.); asst. govt. med. offr. and comsnr. in lunacy, Mauritius 8th Sept., 1890; med. sup't, lunatic asylum, 15th June, 1898.

PAGDEN, A. S.—Ed. Wellington Coll. and King's Coll., Camb., scholar of King's Coll., 1st class, classical tripos, 1881; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1881; pol. mag., Panadura, June, 1887; office asst. to the govt. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1890; dist. judge, Badulla, 1896; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, Mar., 1896; ag. comsnr. of requests, Colombo, June, 1896; pol. mag., Colombo, June, 1900; chmn., municipal coun., and mayor of Colombo, June, 1901; prin. ass't. col. sec., Feb., 1905; postm-r-gen., Nov., 1906; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., Aug., 1911; postm-r-gen., Oct., 1911; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., Feb., 1913; ag. controller of revenue, Aug., 1913; controller of revenue, Nov., 1913.
PAGDEN, N. D.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1912.

PAGE, GEORGE WM.—B. 1861; copyist, science and art dept., S. Kensington museum, Apr., 1881, to Dec., 1884; 2nd cl. clk. prsn. dept., Jan., 1885, to Dec., 1896; keeper of the gaol Freetown, S. Leone, Jan., 1897, to Apr., 1901; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Leone Prot., May, 1901; dist. comsnr., Oct., 1903; dist. comsnr. of Sherbro dist., Aug., 1905, and in addition dist. comsnr. of N. Sherbro dist., Mar., 1906; ag. curator of intestate estates, Nov., 1900, to Apr., 1901; J.P. for the colony.

PAGET, ALFRED JAMES MEYRICK.—Ed. Edin. Camb. and Lond.; M.D.; diploma Med. Psychological Society of Great Britain, master of surgery; apptd. to foreign med. serv., Uganda Oct., 1899; senior med. offr., Somaliland, 1st Sept., 1904; fellow of Roy. Inst. of Pub. Health; general African service medal, Somaliland, 1904-10.

PAINE, JOHN JAMES.—B. 1863; served with 19th (Princess of Wales' Own) Hussars in Egypt; gained medal and 3 clasps and Khedive star for Sudan and Nile expdtions, 1884 and 1885; apptd. coal porter, C.O., 1st Jan., 1883; placed on estab. as office porter, 15th Apr., 1904.

PAIRAUDEAU, G. H.—Entd. P.W.D., R. Guiana, as jun. clk., Nov., 1879; served several capacities as clk., record clk., Mar., 1881; 3rd cl. offr., Apr., 1892; 2nd cl., Apr., 1902; sec. to the comsn. on values of govt. properties ex. 1894; ch. clk., P.W.D., 1st Apr., 1906.
PALLISER, CHARLES FREDERICK WALTON.—Entered civ. ser., New Zealand, 1874; sec. to high comsnr. for N.Z. in London, 1909; ag. high comsnr., May to Aug., 1912.

PALMER, ALFRED FELIX.—Auditor's div., Grenada, Sept., 1879; rev. offr., St. Lucia, 1882; ag. ch. rev. offr., 1882-83; ag. ch. clk., treas. 1883 and 1884; sub-coll. customs and imposts rev., and clk., 2nd dist. ct., Oct., 1884; acted as mag., and as coroner, dep. coroner, and J.P., ag. ch. clk., govt. office, 1893.

PALMER, CHAS. STUART RUSSELL, M.I.C.E.—Ed. at Roorkee Civil Eng. Coll., India; ed. P.W.D., India, Oct., 1878, as asst. engnr.; spec duty in England, 1885 to 1887; asst. to ch. engnr. and asst. sec. to ch. comsnr., cent. provs., up to 1889, when apptd. exec. engnr. of Hooghly dist., and in 1892 of Nagpur div.; ret. from India serv., May, 1894; asst. survr., W. Aust., Oct., 1894; engnr. in charge, roads and bridges, and harbours and rivers branches, Aug., 1897, of water supply, 1898, and sewerage, 1900; engnr.-in-chief, June, 1902; insp'tg. engnr. for W.A. in Lond., 1904.

PANZERA, LT.-COL. FRANCIS WM. C.M.G. (1911).—Govt. engnr. offr., 21st Jan., 1893; served for some years with R.A. and artillery militia ordnance dept., etc., insp'tg., science and arts dept.; apptd. as a cap't., reserve of officers as acting engnr. to staff of R.E. dist., 1888, to organise, raise and train the Harwich div. submarine miners, R.E. militia and 1st cl. army reserve; major, 1st Apr., 1890; commanded troops, Harwich and Land-guard, 1890-1; nominated by D.A.G., R.E., for service under the C.O., 1872; and apptd. engnr. offr. to govt. of Bech. and Bech. Prot., Jan., 1893, and to the Bech. border pol.; mem. of Bech. Prot. concessions act., 1890; mag., Macloutsie and dist., 1893; commandant Impl. base, Matabele border, and L. of C. Matabele war, 1893-4 (medal and promoted major, reserve of offra.); organised and in charge of pub. wks. dept., 10th Mar., 1893; sec., Khami So. boundy. comsnr. in 1894; govt. engnr. and Int. representative under rly. agreement (Bech. rlyws.) of Aug., 1894; expert to treasury for Janc. son trial, 1896, for preparation of maps, etc., mag. for Bech. Prot.; 2nd in command, Prot. div., B.S.A. pol.; British mem. and pres. British-Transvaal joint boundy. comsnr., 189 ag. asst. comnr., Palapye, June to Aug., 1897; spec. comnr., Ngamaland, to delimit and demarcate boundaries of the country, etc., Feb., 1898, to Mar., 1899; served in S. African War, 1899-1902; commanded artillery; brigade major; D.A.A.G., etc.; defence of Mafeking; afterwards D.A.A.G. on staff of an A.I.G.; Imp. mil. mem., war losses compensation comn.; commndt., N. Bech. Prot. (ment. in desps., Queen's medal with 2 clasps, King's medal, 2 bars); promtd. lieut.-col., reserve of offrs.; asst. comnr., Bech. Prot., 1st Aug., 1901; spec. comnr. for adminstrg. oaths of allegiance, 1901-2; ag. res. comsnr., Bech. Prot., in 1904 and 1906; res. comnr., ditto, 2nd Jan., 1907.
PARDOE, AVERN.—B. 1845; ed. Stratford-upon-Avon Collegiate gram. schl.; on staff of Toronto Globe, 1875-89; managing editor, 1883-9; librarian of Ontario legislature, 1898.

PARKER, FREDERICK HARDYMAN, B.L., M.A. (Edin., 1877), F.R.G.S., M.R.A.S.—Watson prize, hist. and pol. econ., Edin.; schlr. in property law, Mid. Tem., 1879; called to the bar, Trin., 1880; employed in Canada; admitted to the bar, Br. Honduras, 1881; dep. atty.-gen., Coroal circuit, 1882; notary and standing conveyancing counsel, 1883; regisr. of the sup. ct., keeper of the records, and provost-marshal, Sept., 1884; also regisr., Admty., lands, titles, a J.P., and prison justice; acted as dist. mag. and coroner, Belize, and as atty.-gen., 1883-7; ag. ch. just., 1886-7; recd. Jubilee medal, 1887; mem. exec. and legis. couns. on various occasions; sec. to crown lands bd., 1883; chrmn., comsnr. on land tenures and titles, 1884; sec. to comsnr. for consolidation and codification of laws of B. Honduras, 1884-1888; recd. thanks of govt.; pres., dist. ct., Papho, Cyprus, 1890; ag. pres., Limasol, 1892, and Larnaca, 1893-5; pres., Famagusta, 1892; passed in modern Greek, 1894; pres., Limasol, 1897; pres., Nicosia, 1902; ag. pres., Kyrenia; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., Cyprus, 1903, 1904 and 1905; has passed in Turkish; attached to Bow-st. pol. ct., London, 1905-6; ag. ch. just., Cyprus, 1906; puisne judge, Leeward Is., 1907; ag. ch. just. and keeper of the records, Leeward Is., 1909 and 1911; on reconstitution of supreme ct. of the Leeward Is., apptd. first puisne judge, 1911; chrmn. of comsnr. of enquiry on legal affairs in Montserrat, etc., 1911; ag. provisionally as chief just., Leeward Is., from Oct., 1911.

PARKER, HON. SIR STEPHEN HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1914). Kr. Bach. (1908).—Called to the bar, 1868; Q.C., 1890; mem. of legislature of W. Australia from 1878 to 1897; col. sec., 1892 to 1894; represented W. A. at the C.O., and gave evidence before sel. comte of H. of C. relative to the grant of self-govt. to the Colony, 1890; represented W. A. in London relative to the federation of Australia, 1900; justice of supreme ct., 1901; ch. just. of W. A., 1906-1913.

PARKER, WILLIAM ALSTIN.—B. 1865. ed. Queen's coll., B. Guiana; entd. B. Guiana natv. serv., 1883; qualified as notary public and apptd., 1892; admitted solr., 1897; chief elk., and acctnt., admistr.-genl.'s office, 1897; offr. revr. and pub. trustee, 1905; comsnr. of affidavits; has acted at various times as crown solr., stip. mag., admistr.-gen., and regisr. of sup. ct.; served on comen. of enquiry into working of regisr.'s office, 1905.

PARKINSON, A. C. C., M.A. (1910).—B. 1884; ed. at Epsom and Oxford; class demy, Magdalen, 1903-7; 1st cls. mods., 1905; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1907; apptd. after compet. exam., 1st cls. elk., acctnt.-gen.'s dept., Admiralty, Feb., 1908; transf. as 2nd cls. elk., C.O., 19th Apr., 1909; sec., man. comte, of imperial bureau of entomology; sec. to comte, apptd. to investigate relationship between wild animals and trypanosomiasis, 1913-1914; sec. trop. diseases research fund advisory comte.; sec. managing comte. of trop. diseases bureau; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 6th Oct., 1914.
PARKINSON, J. W. H.—Regisr. of high ct., E.A.P., 26th Mar., 1907; admstr.-gen., 1909.

PARODI, ERNEST VICTOR.—B. 1870; ed. Stonyhurst Coll., Lancs.; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., June, 1893; solr.-gen., N. Nigeria, 4th July, 1903; ag. atty.-gen., 2nd Aug. to 22nd Dec., 1903, 15th Apr. to 21st July, 1904, 1st Mar. to 31st Oct., 1906, 16th June to 20th Oct., 1906, 18th Nov., 1906, to 13th June, 1907, and 13th Feb. to 29th Apr., 1908; ag. chief justice, 30th Apr. to 15th Dec., 1908, and Sept., 1909; puisne judge, 1st Apr., 1910; ag. chief just., 28th Apr. to 25th Nov., 1910, and from 21st Feb. to 20th Sept., 1912; judge of the circuit ct. of the Prot. and puisne judge of the supreme ct. of the col. of Sierra Leone, Apl., 1913; ag. chief just., 22nd Oct. to 9th Nov., 1913, and from 27th Nov., 1914.

PARR, CECIL WILLIAM CHASE.—Jun. offr., Perak civ. ser., 1889; dist. offr., Tampin, Negri Sembilan, 1897; dist. offr., Klang, 1904; ag. comsnr. of trade and customs, F.M.S., 1909; ag. British res., Negri Sembilan, 1911; 2nd lieut. M.S.V.R., 1911; under sec., F.M.S., 1912; lieut. M.S.V.R., 1912; ag. agt., M.S. information agency, London, 1912; seconded as gov. of B. N. Borneo, Apr., 1913.

PARSONS, ALLAN CHILCOTT, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.); D.P.H. (Oxon.), 1910; certif. in trop. med., 1903; F.R.G.S., F.R.C.I.—Ed. Epsom, St. Thomas' hosp., and Kings' Coll., Lond.; late H.S. and H.P., Royal Free hosp., Lond.; senr. H.S., Victoria hosp. for children, Lond.; clin. asst., skin dept., St. Thomas' hosp.; clin. asst., St. John's hosp., Lond.; civil surg., S. African War (Queen's medal and four clasps); joined W.A.M.S. Aug., 1903; supt., Bornu freed slaves home, 1904; serv. with Chibbuk expdt., 1906 (medal and clasp, ment. in desps.); late lieut., R.A.M.C. (V.) ; contributed "Arrows and Arrow Wounds in N. Nigeria," Brit. Med. Jour., 1909; "Filaria Volvulus" to "Parasitology," 1909.

PARTINGTON, T. W. EDGE.—B. 1883; served in Royal Navy, 1899 to 1902; medal and clasp for active service in China; apptd. res. mag. at Gizo, Solomon Islands, 1903; deputy comsnr. for W. Pacific, Aug., 1905; ag. res. comsnr. for six months; dist. mag., Mala, Solomon Is., Sept. 1909.

PARTIDGE, CHARLES, M.A. (1900), F.S.A. (1904), F.R.G.S. (1903), Fell. of Roy. Anthropol. Inst. of Great Britain and Ireland.—B. 1872; ed. Christ's Coll., Camb., and in France; 1890-92, read law in solr.'s chmbrs.; 1892-5 at Camb.; 1897-1900, priv. tut. to inter alios, Sir S. Shuckburgh, Bart.; joined 2nd V.B. Suffolk R., Jan., 1900; founder and edtr. of East Anglian Miscellany, Mar.-June, 1901; elected mem. of coun. of Suffolk inst. of archaeology, Apr., 1901; apptd. asst. dist. comsnr. in Southern Nigeria, June, 1901; in treas. at Calabar, July, 1901; in charge of Idah sub-dist., Aug., 1901, to May, 1902; Calabar, June-Sept., 1902; ag. dist. comsnr., Obubura Hill, from May, 1903, to May, 1904; polit. offr., Ikot Ekpenye, Jan.-Dec., 1905; promoted dist. comsnr., 9th Jan., 1906; dist. comsnr., Ikot Ekpenye, Aug., 1906, to Aug., 1907; ag. prov. sec. and ag. asst. prov. comsnr., E. Prov., 16th-25th May, 1907; dist. comsnr., Meko, W. Prov., and offr. in charge of preventive service, 20th July to 27th Nov., 1908; ag. sen. asst. col. sec., Nov.-Dec., 1908; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., 1st Jan., 1909; apptd. to represent S. Nigeria in delimitation of Anglo-French (Nigeria-Dahomey) boundary, 13th Jan., 1909, and of Ekiti boundary, (with N. Nigeria), 24th Feb., 1909; has done lit. work of various kinds, including complete verbal cross-index (40,000 slips) to Yule's "Gloss. of Ang.-Indian Words"; article on "Igaraal" in "Blackwood's Mag.", Sept., 1904; "Cross River Natives", Jan., 1905.
PASCOE, THOMAS, JUNE.—M.L.C., S. Australia, 1900-1902, and from 1910; min. of agriculture, 1909-1910; min. of agric. and irrigation, 1912.

PASHLEY, EDWARD REYNOLD.—B. 1869; storekeeper, petroleum warehouse, customs, Bahamas, 1895; landing waiter, 1896; senr. clk. of check staff, 1897; landing waiter and preventive offr., 1899; attended impl. customs, London, 1900; customs offr.; excise supervisor, boarding offr., Tobago, 1901; landing waiter, customs, Trinidad, 1902; transf. to acc'tant's branch, treasy, 1906; 5th clk. and paymr., 1907; 4th clk., 1912; 3rd clk., 1913; compt'ry. of customs, reg'ry. of shipping, J.P., Bahamas, 1914.
PATRSON, THOMAS WILSON.—B. 1852; rly. contractor; mem. of legislature, British Columbia, 1902-1907; lieut.-gov'r. of British Columbia, 1909-1914.
PATRSON, WILLIAM.—Under representative govt., W. Australia, elected M.L.C., 1880-89, and M.L.A., 1890 to Dec., 1894, then resig.; man. agricul. bank, Jan., 1895; dir. of agriculture, 1902.
PATRSON, JOHN.—Ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin.; Scottish Horse, S. African War; asst. treas., E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1906.

PAUL, A. H. D.—Ed. at Bradfield and Bath colls.; trooper in Matabeleland mtd. pol., 1896; took part in the Jameson raid, 1896; planting in Ceylon, 1896 to 1904; trooper in Ceylon M.I.; dist. supt. of pol., N. Nigeria, 1905; asst. res., 1st Nov., 1908; ag. supt. of agric., Aug., 1911.

PEACE, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1893), F.S.S.—B. 1840; emigrn. agt. for Natal, Jan., 1881; London agt. for Natal harbour bd., Sept., 1881; author of "Our Colony of Natal"; chevalier de l'ordre de Léopold, for services as Belgian consul in Natal, 1870-9; agt.-gen. for Natal, 1893-1904.

PEACOCK, HON. SIR ALEXANDER JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1861; mem. of Austn. Federal Convention, 1897-8; premier of Victoria, 1901-2; later chief sec. and min. for labour, Victoria; min. for educn. and labour, Dec., 1913; premier, treas., and min. of justice, 1914.

PEACOCK, WALTER.—B.A., Trinity Hall, Camb.; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1898; passed fin. exam., Chinese, June, 1901; ag. 2nd ast. protect. Chinese, Penang, July, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Is., Dec., 1902; ag. 3rd mg., Singapore, June, 1903; apptd. to cls. V., Sept., 1903; ag. ast. prot. of Chinese, Penang, July, 1906; seconded for service in Burma, June, 1907; ag. ast. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Mar., 1909.

PEAKE, HON. ARCHIBALD HENRY.—M.H.A., South Australia, 1897; treas. and atty.-gen., 1905-1909; premier, treas., and min. of educ., 5th June to 22nd Dec., 1909; premier, comm. of crown lands and imigrn., and min. of educ., 22nd Dec., 1909, to 3rd June, 1910; premier, treas., and min. of educ., 1912.

PEARCE, MAJOR FRANCIS BARROW, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1866; ed. at Cheltenham, and Rct. Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; entd. W. York reg., 1886; capt., 1893; brevet-major 1900; major 1904; served with Ashanti exp'dn., 1886-9; in B.C. Africa, 1898 (deeps., medal with clasps); in comm'd. of British forces in operation in E. Nyassaland, 1899 (deeps., medal with clasps); brevet major); and in S. Africa, 1901 (medal with clasps); ast. dep. comsnr., B.C. Afr. Prot., 1st Nov., 1897; dep. comsnr., 1901; ast. as comsnr., and consul-gen., 1903, Nov., 1910 to Jan., 1906, and during 1907; ag. gov'r. Nyassaland, Apr.-July, 1910; and from Nov., 1912 to Sept., 1913; British res., Zanzibar Prot., Mar., 1914.

PEARCE, FRANK LEOPOLD.—Sorting clk. of sec.'s off., Jamaica, 19th Aug., 1884; seconded ast. sec. to Jamaica exhibtn., Mar., 1890; sec. winding-up comte., May, 1891; 3rd cl. clk., Apr., 1890; 2nd cl. clk., Sept., 1896; prot. immigta., 1st July, 1901.

PEARCE, HON. GEORGE FORSTER.—Senator for Western Australia in commonwealth parlmt.; min. of trades coun. at Perth, 1896; pres., 1899; returned to first Commonwealth Senate, 1901; re-elect., 1906, and elected chmn. of comtees; min. of defence, C. of A., Apr., 1910 to June, 1913; Imp. Conf., 1911; min. of defence since Sept., 1914.
PEARCE, G. H., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1846; clk. in penitentiary, Jamaica, 1860; in priv. sec. office, 1864; in P.O., 1865; ch. clk. P.O., 1872; ag. postmr., 1874 to 1875, and 1882; new postmr.

PEARCE, THOS. WM.—B. 1855; missionary in Lond. Miss. Soc., Canton, 1879-1890; Hong Kong, 1890-1902; ag. inspr. of schools, 5th Sep., 9th Oct., 1896, again in 1900; examiner, Queen's Coll., 1897 and 1898; mem. of comtees on interpretation, 1895-1900; apptd. mem. of bd. of examiners in Chinese, 14th Jan., 1897; mem. of govn'g body, Queen's Coll., 1905; mem. of course on continuation classes, 1907.
PEARCE, WM. PHILIP.—Clk. to postmr. St. Kitts, 1883; clk., P.O., 1887; ag. postmr., 1889; postmr., 1892.

PEARSE, WILFRID WILLIAM.—M.B., M.D., Aberdeen, 1893; D.P.H., Aberdeen, 1894; M.B., Aberdeen, 1906; 2nd ast. med. offr., Shrewsbury Infirmary, 1894-95; ast. med. offr. of health, Hong Kong, 1901; J.P., 1902; ag. med. offr. of health, 1903-6 and 1908; lecturer on publ. health, Univ. of Hong Kong.

PEARSON, ARTHUR A., C.M.G. (1902)—B. 1847; ed. at Rugby; apptd., 1867, after a comp't exam., clk. in the office of the S. of S. for colo., 2nd class clk., Sept., 1872; ast. priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, Mar., 1874, to Feb., 1878; ast. priv. sec. to Sir M. Hicks-Besch, Feb. to Mar., 1878; priv. sec. to Earl Cadogan, Jul'y, 1879, to Apr., 1880; priv. sec. to the Right Hon. M. B. Grant Duff, Apr., 1880, to Dec., 1890; 1st-cl. clk., Jan., 1881; principal clk., Apr., 1885; reg't crown cols. at postal confere., London, June and July, 1898; one of the Brit. Delegates at Brussels sugar conference, 1901-2; ast. deleg. permanent sugar commission at Brussels, 1902; ret. from C.O., 31st Dec., 1906; accompanied C. Lucas on his visit to Australia and New Zealand, 1909.

PEARSON, AYLMER CAVENDISH.—B. 1876; ed. at Felsted schl., Essex, and Trinity coll., Dublin; cadet, N. Borneo serv., 1897; asst. treas., 1899; res., Kudat, 1901; sec. to gov., 1904; sessions judge, 1905; mem. of coun., 1906; ag. jud. comnr., 1908; ag. comnr. of lands, 1908; govt. sec. and judge of high ct., 1909; ag. gov., N. Borneo, 1910 and 1911; temporarily attached to C.O. for a short period in 1912, after which he returned to Borneo as dep. gov. for 3 months; attached to various colonial services for short periods.

PEARSON, EDWARD LEE.—Ed. at Foster's, Stubbington House, and at Avranche Coll., France; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 14th June, 1900; collr., 1st July, 1904; Africa gen. serv. medall and clasp, 1901.
PEARSON, J. M.—Asst. dist. comnr., E.A.P., 4th Apr., 1909.
PEARSON, JOSEPH, D.Sc. (Liverpool), B.Sc. (Victoria), F.L.S.—Dir. of the Colombo Museum, and marine biologist to Ceylon Govt., 11th Aug., 1910.

PEARSON, TURNER.—Clk. to govt. reformatory, Stony Hill, Jamaica, 1869; transf. to Queen's Coll., Spanish Town, Aug., 1873; ch. clk. and purveyor, pub. hosp.; and clk. to the Look keep, Apr., 1875; ch. clk., island med. dept.; cc. cent. bd. of hth., and to quarantine bds., 1897.
PEASE, J. W. K.—Asst. dist. comnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914.
PEDRAZA, R.—Asst. dist. comnr., E.A.P., Aug., 1914.

PEEBLES, CAPT. H. W.—Joined Canadian L.I., Dec., 1899; 2nd lieut. A.S.C., Sept., 1900; leut., Nov., 1901; S. African War, Jan., 1900, to May, 1902 (severely wounded, Queen's medal and our clasp, King's medal and two clasps); reserve officers, June, 1902; A.D.C. to Brig.-Gen. Sir V. H. Manning, K.C.M.G., C.B., Feb., 1903, to June, 1904; Somaliland campaign, Jan., 1903, to June, 1904 (twice men. in deep, medal and two clasps); A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Sir B. Sweet-Escott, K.C.M.G., govr. of B. Hond., Oct., 1904; local capt., Nov., 1904; commndg. Belize L.I. coln., Dec., 1904; local major, 1905; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1905 to Jan., 1907; ag. priv. sec. to Sir B. Sweet-Escott, K.C.M.G., govr. of Leeward Is., Oct., 1907; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1908; 3rd class res., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1909; resigned on account of ill-health, June, 1909; clk. and priv. sec. to administrator, Dominica, Mar., 1913.

PEEL, W.—B.A., Queen's Coll., Cambridge; 18th wrangler, math. tripos, 1896; cadet, S. Iltlimts., Oct., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Aug.-Dec., 1898; and from Mar., 1899; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Dec., 1899; dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1901; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., May, 1902; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Nov., 1902, to Jan., 1903; and in Aug., 1903; ag. asst. supt., Indian immigrts., Sing., in addition, Aug., 1903; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Sept., 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Feb., 1905; ag. collr. of land rev., Penang, Jan., 1906; dist. offr., Tampin, F.M.S., June, 1908; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, 22nd Oct., 1909; ag. dist. offr., Lower Perak, 21st Feb., 1910; ag. supt. of Indian immigrts., S.S. and F.M.S., 2nd May, 1910; ag. chief asst. dist. offr., Kinta, and reg. of titles, South Perak, 21st Oct., 1910; seconded as pres. mun. comnr., Penang, 1st Jan., 1911; promoted to Class III., but remained seconded, 1st Jan., 1912.

PEET, JAMES HERBERT.—B. 1882; clk., audit office, Bahamas, 1904; 4th clk., P.O., 1907; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1909; 3rd clk., treasury dept., 1910; ch. clk., P.O., 1913; ch. clk. and asst. examg. offr., customs, 1914; has held numerous ag. appts.

PELLETIER, HON. LOUIS PHILIPPE, K.C.—B. 1857; ed. St. Anne Coll., Laval Univ., Quebec; pres. of Nat. conservative assocn. of prov. of Quebec; founded newspaper "La Justice"; leg. coun., Quebec, 1888; res., and ret. by acclam. to legis. assembly for Dorchester; prov. sec., 1891; atty.-gen., 1896-7; sat for Dorchester until 1904; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1911; mem. of the P.C. for Canada, and postmaster-gen. in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; judge, superior court, Quebec, 1914.

PELLETIER, PHILLIPE.—B. 1849; called to the bar, Quebec, 1875; mem. of the coun. of the bar of Montreal, and sec., 1878; a fire comnr. for city of Montreal, 1879 to 1887; unsuccessfully contested St. John's in provincial elections, 1886, and Beauharnois in Dominion elections, 1887; ch. clk. to sec. of state, Canada, 1888; asst.-under-secr. of state of Canada, 1909.

PEMBERTON, SHOLTO RAWLINS.—Ed. Downing Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1882; called to the bar (Inner Tem.), 1883; admitted to the bar, Leeward Is., 1883; elected mem. legis. assem., Dominica, and of gen. legis. coun., Leewards, 1885 to 1895; mem. educn. comnr., 1886; of road bd., Dominica, 1889; nominated mem. legis. assem., Dominica, and mem. exec. coun., 1896; comnr. of valuation, 1895; comnr. of assessed taxes, 1896; J.P., 1896; chmn. bd. of guardians, 1897; vis. just. of gaol, 1897; mem. quarantine bd., 1898; nom. mem. legis. coun., Dominica, and of gen. legis. coun., Leewards, 1898; ag. 1st puisane judge, Leewards, 1898; ag. asst. atty.-gen., Dominica, 1899-1900; ag. 2nd puisane judge, 1901 and 1902; asst. atty.-gen., 1903; ag. puisane judge, 1903-4-5-7-8-9 and 1910; ag. atty.-gen., 1905, 1908, 1909 and 1910; ag. chief just., 1911; ag. admstr. of Dominica, 1911; 2nd puisane judge, Leeward Is., Apl., 1911.
PENDLETON, ALAN GEORGE, C.M.G. (1906).—Gen. traffic man., S. Aust. rly. dept., Nov., 1876; comnr. of rly., 1895; ret., 1909.
PENNEY, ALFRED.—M.H.A., Carboner, Newfd., 1885-9; mem. exec. coun., and surrv.-gen., 1885-9; judge dist. at Harbour Grace, 1898; stip. mag., Carboner, 1900.

PENNEY, WILLIAM HUGHES.—Ed. Hymer's Coll., Yorkshire; apptd. to treasy. dept., B. N. Borneo, July, 1888; ag. treasy. genl., 1891; P.M.G., 1895; dis. mag., 1896; ag. res., Prov. Alcock, 1898; prot. of Chinese and mem. of coun., 1901; supt. of customs, 1902; resident and dep. gov., Labuan, Jan., 1903; supt. of customs, Sandakan, 1904; mag., Sandakan, 1907; protector of labour, 1908; excise comnr., 1912.
PENRUDDOCKE, J. H.—Asst. loco., supt., Uganda rlyw., July, 1899.

PERCEVAL, SIR WESTBY BROOK, K.C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1854; was mem. for Christchurch City in legis. assem., of N. Zealand; chmn. of comtees. in 1890; agt.-gen. for the col. in England, 1891-96; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1878; Roy. comnr. for Chicago Exhibn., 1893; agt.-gen. for Tasmania, 1896 to 1899.
PERCIVAL, ARTHUR BLAYNEY.—Asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 14th June, 1900; ranger of game preserves, 10th May, 1901.

PERCY, FREDERICK WM.—B. 1857; ed. at private schl., Dublin; writer, Royal Navy, 1872; passed English civ. serv. (exam.), 2nd cls. clk., 1874; clk., wks. and rlyws. depts., W. Australia, 1892; minister's clk., wks. dept., 1896; ag. chf. clk., Jan., 1898; statistical clk., July, 1898; ag. chf. clk., Jan. to Dec., 1902; chf. correspondence clk., Mar., 1903; ag. chf. clk. for pub. wks., 14th Nov., 1908.

PERDUE, HON. WM. EGERTON, B.A.—B. 1850; ed. Brampton high schl. and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1879; called to the bar, Manitoba, 1882; judge, ct. of King's Bench, Manitoba, 1903; judge, ct. of appeal, Manitoba, 1906.

PEREIRA, JAMES CECIL WALTER, K.C.—B. 1856; proctor of dist. ct. of Colombo, 1873; proctor of sup. ct. of Ceylon, and notary public, 1881; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1887; advocate of sup. ct., Ceylon, July, 1887; mem. of Ceylon incorporated coun. of legal educn., 1898; ag. solr-gen., 1900 and 1901; advocate of high ct. of judicature at Madras, 1901; comsnr. of assize, Southern circ., 1901; ditto, Western circ., 1901; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1903; K.C., 1904; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1905; solr-gen., 1906; ag. atty-gen., 1907 and 1910; puisne judge, 1912; author of "The Laws of Ceylon" in two vols., published in 1901 and 1904; 2nd edition (in one volume), 1913.

PERIES, FRANCIS DANIEL.—B. 1864; ed. Royal coll., Colombo; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., 1909; pol. mag., S. Prov., Jan., 1904, Apr., 1905; Mar., 1907; ag. comnr. of requests and pol. mag., Galle, Mar., 1905; ag. pol. mag., Balapitiya, Oct., 1906; pol. mag., Chilaw, July, 1910; pol. mag., Panadura, Feb., 1912; ag. dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1913.

PERKINS, ARTHUR JAMES.—B. 1871; Diplômé de l'école Nationale d'Agriculture de Montpellier, 1890; management of farm estates, Tunis, 1890-2; prof. of viticulture and oenology, S. Aust., 1892; sec. for agriculture, 1902-1904; prin. of agric. coll. and prof. of viticulture and oenology, 1904; dir. of agric., 1914.

PERKINS, H. INNES, I.S.O. (1904), F.R.G.S.—Clk. to dir. of surveys, Trinidad, 1880-81; 3rd asst. Crown survr., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1881; 2nd ditto, Sept., 1884; accompanied E. F. in Thurn on an expdn. to ascend Mount Roraima in 1884-5, and furnished from actual survey a map of the mountain and the country in its vicinity to the Roy. Geog. Soc.; isa J.P.; author of papers on natural history, geography, and mining industries of Br. Guiana; asst. Crown survr., July, 1889; ag. Crown survr., 1890, 1892, and 1893; apptd. a comsnr. on scheme for settlement of Indian immigrants, Apr., 1895, and mem. of comtece to settle immigrants; ag. comsnr. of mines, July, 1895, and ag. Crown survr. in addn., Sept. to Dec., 1896; joint author with Prof. Harrison, in reports of geology of Barima, Barama Rivers, 1897, Essequibo, Potaro, and Demerara Rivers, 1898, and Mazaruni River, 1899; F.G.S., 1898; comsnr. for delimitn. of Br. Guiana boundary, Nov., 1900; survr.-gen., B. Honduras, 1905; mem. of exec. coun., 1908; sometime mem. of institution of mining and metallurgy, London.
PERKINS, ROBT. CLERK, D.S.O., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Med. offr., S.A.C., and ag. dist. surg., Swaziland, 1902 to 1907; is now med. offr. to Swaziland adminstr.
PERKINS, THOS. LUFT.—Divnln. survr. and engrn. for county of Middlesex; dist. engrn. for City dist. of Bristol; ditto for Clifton dist., Bristol; exec. engrn., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1904.

PERLEY, HON. SIR GEORGE HALSEY, K.C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1857; ed. Ottawa grain, schl., St. Paul's schl., Concord, N.H., and Harvard Univ., B.A., 1878; elec. to H. of C., Canada (Argenteuil), 1904; re-elec., 1908 and 1911; chief conserv. whip, 1911; mem. of privy coun. for Canada min. without portfolio in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; ag. high commnr. in London, 1914.

PERRY, SIR ALLAN, KT. BACH. (1904).—M.D. (Durham); D.P.H.(Lon.); M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.S. Lond.—B.1860; prin. civ. med.offr.and inspr. gen. of hosps., Ceylon; prin. of Ceylon med. coll., 10th Dec., 1897; major R.A.M.C. (retired); mem. municipal coun., Colombo; late pres. Ceylon branch Br. med. assoc.; del. for Ceylon Internat. Leprosy Conf., Bergen, 1909.
PERRY, B. C. G.—B.1886; apptd.after comp. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., assigned to C.O., 9th Apr., 1906.

PERRY, GEORGE McKEDDIE, M.B., C.M., Glasgow, and St. Mary's hosp., London, F.R.G.S.—Asst. col. surg., Lagos, Oct., 1893; ag. col. surg., Feb.-Mar., 1894; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1894; med. offr. to expedn. to Idanai mountain, sen. asst. col. surg., Aug., 1895; ag. col. surg., 1895.
PERRY, HON. JOHN.—B. 1845; M.L.A. Elect. dist., N.S.W., since 1889; min. of pti. instr., 1899; ch. sec., 1904; min. of agric., 1907.
PERTH, ARCHBISHOP OF, MOST REV. CUS. OWEN LEAVER RILEY, D.D., Cantab.—B.1854; vicar of St. Paul's, Preston, Lancs., 1883-1894; bishop of Perth, W. Aust., 1894; created archbishop, 1914.

PETERS, JAMES EDWARDS.—B. 1888; ed. Antigua high schl.; apptd. 2nd clk. regist. off. Antigua; performed duties of 1st clk. in 1878 and 1880; ag. clk. of mag.'s ct., dist. "A," in 1881 and 1884; sec. to record comsnr., in 1885; ag. comsnr. to administer oaths in 1894; ag. dep. coroner for dist. "A" in 1894; clk. mag.'s ct., and dep. coroner, dist. "A," 1895; ag. clk., regist.'s off., in 1897, 1898, 1899 and 1900; performed duties of regist. and prov. marshal in 1901; clk., mag.'s ct., dista. "A" and "B"; ag. dep. coroner, dist. "B," July, 1907.

PETHERICK, EDWARD AUGUSTUS.—B.1847; F.L.S., F.R.G.S.; emigrated to Melbourne in 1852; educ. privately, and in municipal office Collingwood; entd. publishing house of George Robertson, Melbourne, 1862; agent for colonial booksellers in London, 1870 to 1894; devoted his attention to bibliography and research in English and European libraries, and collected an extensive library relating to Australasia and Tasmania, which he offered to the commonwealth; part of the nucleus of an Australasian Historical Museum. The collection was taken over and the donor apptd. archivist to the Commonwealth; 1993; is hon. corres. sec. to Royal Col. Inst. and mem. of the Library Assocnt., U.K.

PHILIPS, HUGH RICHARD.—Ed. Weymouth Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; local audtr. Niger Coast Prot., 27th Oct., 1894; asst. audtr. E. Africa Prot., 9th Dec., 1896; local audtr. Uganda, 29th Apr., 1897; served in Uganda mutiny (medal and clasp); local audtr. E. Africa and Uganda rly., 26th Dec., 1901; audtr. Hong Kong, 1st Nov., 1904; J.P., 1906; hon. audtr. Hong Kong Univ., 2nd May, 1911.

PHILBRICK, ARTHUR JAMES.—Ed. Rugby, 1879-1884, Trin. Coll., Camb., 1884-1887; B.A., LL.B., Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1889; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 16th Nov., 1901; prov. comsnr., 14th Apr., 1902; prov. comsnr., Ashanti, 1900; ag. chief comsnr. Ashanti, 1911, 1912 and 1914.

PHILLIPS, HAROLD ERNEST.—B. 1877; temporary clk. to auditor, B. Honduras, 6th Oct., 1892; 5th clk., treasy., customs and P.O. depts., 3rd June, 1893; 4th clk., 1st Feb., 1894; 3rd k., treasy. and cust. dept., 16th May, 1897; 2nd k., 7th June, 1898; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1st Sept., 1900; ag. chief clk., C.S.O., and clk. of councils, 24th Apl. to 30th Sept., 1901, 15th Mch., 1902, to 17th July, 1904, and from 14th Dec., 1904, to 28th June, 1905; ag. dist. comsnr., Stann Creek, 2nd July, 1905, to 14th Jan., 1906; ch. clk., C.S.O., ad clk. of couns., 17th Jan., 1906; priv. sec. to Sir E. Swayne, 16th Mch., 1909, to 31st July, 1910; ag. comsnr. of currency, 18th Feb., 1911; treas., collr. of cust., harb. mast., reg. of pilgrims, chrmn. of bd. for examtn. of pilots, pres. quarantine bd., chrmn. of poor relief committe, dir. of P.O.G. fund, mem. of bd. of educn., mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 9th Apl. to 13th Aug., 1911; J.P. for the Colony, 8th May, 1911, riv. sec. to ag. gov., 5th Aug., 1911.

PHILLIPS, HENRY DENBIGH.—B. 1872; superum. clk., col. engnr.'s office, Br. Honduras, 1887; clk., col. engnr.'s office, 1888; ag. rev. offr., July, 1888, to Feb., 1889; clk. to att.-gen., May, 1889; ag. clk. to reg. of sup. ct. and keeper of records, 1889, 1890, 1891, 1892, 1893 and 1894; k. to reg. sup., 1894; comsnr. of sup. ct. nd a comsnr. of deeds, Aug., 1894; ag. regist. en., Feb. to Sept., 1898; ag. dist. comsnr., Stann creek, Apr. to Oct., 1899; dist. Toledo, Mar. to rec., 1900; dist. comsnr., Toledo, 1st Jan., 1901; ist. comsnr., Stann Creek, 28th Aug., 1905; ag. ist. comsnr., Belize; offl. recvr. in bankruptcy dept., admtr., 11th Mar., 1907; ag. regist. en. in conjunction with duties as dist. comsnr., pr. to Dec., 1907; and again from 5th Apr. to 2nd Oct., 1908; a comsnr. of currency, 24th Sep., 1908; ag. treas. and collr. of cust., harb. master, treas., quarantin bd., chrmn. of pauper relief comttee., 23rd Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; ag. ist. comsnr., Belize, etc., and regist. gen., 1st May to 12th Aug., 1909, and from 22nd an., 1910, to 3rd Dec., 1911; in charge of audit opt., 4th Dec., 1911.

PHILLIPS, J. E. T.—Ed. Marlborough Coll., Pembroke Coll., Oxford, and Durham Univ.; representative undergrad. of Univ. of Oxford, Dublin and Durham at Univs. Congress, London, 912; assist. dist. comsnr., Uganda, Mar., 1913.

PHILLIPS, THOMAS B.—Served in treasy., secretariat, customs, and post office, British Honduras, 1891 to 1900; assist. treas., N. Nigeria, 2nd Aug., 1900; has acted on several occasions as chief ass't. treas. and treas.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM LAMBERT COLLYER, 3. 1838; B.A. (Dunelm); dep. clk., gen. assembly, Barbados, 1879; comsnr. of probate, 1882; clk. gen. assem., 1891; col. treas., 1899; ag. col. sec., July to Sept., 1911, May to Aug., 1912, and from May to Dec., 1913.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM NATHANIEL.—B. 1861; entd. civ. serv., Barbados, Dec., 1883; chief clk., savings bank, Dec., 1886; offr. of customs, Sept., 1890; ag. actuary, savings bank, Apl. to July, 1892; 2nd clk., treasy., Aug., 1892; chief clk., treasy., Sept., 1907; ag. col. treas., Feb. to Nov., 1910, and July to Sept., 1911.

PHILP, HON. SIR ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1851; M.L.A. of Queensland, since 1886; sec. for pub. wks., 1893-6; sec. for pub. instr., 1894-5; for rlys., 1895-7; for mines, 1893-9; treasur., Mar., 1898, to Dec., 1899; premier, Dec., 1899, to Sept., 1903; and Nov., 1907, to Feb., 1908.
PICKERING, C. H.—Town mag., E.A.P., 1910.
PICKERING, WM.—Clk of wks., P.W.D., Gambia, 22nd Mar., 1899.
PICKFORD, W.—Dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 21st Aug., 1909.

PICKWOAD, CECIL ARTHUR.—B. 1876; ed. Bedford Modern schl.; 3rd customsoffr., Br. Hond., June, 1895; 2nd ditto, Nov., 1895; several times acted as clk. in treasy., cust. and post off.; 3rd clk., treasy., and cust. and clk. to quarantine bd., May, 1897; 2nd clk., Sept., 1900; asst. auditor, N. and S. Nigeria, Dec., 1901; local auditor, G. Coast, Dec., 1902; local auditor, Lagos, S. Nigeria, May, 1905; ditto, new col. and prot. of S. Nigeria, 1906; ditto, Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1914.

PICKWOOD, HOWELL.—Ed. at Bedford Mod. achl.; clk., atty.gen.'s off., Br. Hond., Nov., 1895; 3rd cust. offr., 12th Aug., 1898; 4th clk., treasy., and cust., and clk. to comsrs. of currency, 27th Dec., 1901; 2nd grade cust. and excise offr., Aug., 1903; col. branch of exchequer and audit dept., 21st Oct., 1904; asst. treas., E. Africa, Prot., 26th June, 1905.
PICKWOOD, HUGH FREDERICK.—B. 1878; comsnr. (4th div.), Bahamas, 1909; ditto (3rd div.), 1914.

PIELOCK, HENRY.—Ed. at Royal Naval schl., New Cross, London; Imperial Brit. E. Africa Co., 1892; comdr., E. Africa Prot., ss. "Juba," 1st July, 1895; asst. collr., Nov., 1896; port offr., 1st Apr., 1897; holds nominal appt. of asst. dist. supt. of police.

PIERIS, PAULUS EDWARD, Samarasinha Sriwardhana, M.A., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1895.—Barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1896; asst. to govt. ag., Colombo, 1898; pol. mag., Panadura, 1899; dist. judge, Ratnapura, 1899; asst. to govt. ag., Galle, 1901; ag. dist. judge, Matara, Mar., 1903; office asst. to govt. ag., Galle, June, 1903; asst. comsnr., St. Louis exhibn., Feb., 1904; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1905; ditto, Kalutara, Mar., 1906; ag. regist.-gen., Dec., 1910; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, May, 1912; comsnr. of requests, Colombo, Nov., 1912; ag. dist. judge, Kandy, Nov., 1913; dist. judge, Galle, June, 1914.

PIGGOTT, SIR FRANCIS TAYLOR, KT. BACH. (1905).—Barrister-at-law; employed by F.O. on special service in 1887; legal adv. to prim. min., Japan, Nov., 1887; sec. to att.-gen. Sir C. Russell (afterwards Lord Russell of Killowen) during Behring Sea arbitrn., 1893-4; proo.-gen., Mauritius, 1894; ag. ch. just., 1895 to 1897; ch. just., Hong Kong, 27th Apr., 1905; ret., 1912; read. thanks of coun. of govt. for ser. in connection with planters' loans, 1905; author of "Nationality and Nationalization," "Exterritoriality," "Extradition," "Foreign Judgments and Jurisdiction," "Law of Torts," and "Imperial Statutes applicable to the Colonies."

PIGOTT, FRANCIS JOSEPH, M.I.C.E.—Dist. engrn., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1887; ag. mun. engrn., Colombo, 1897; prov. engrn., S. Prov., 1902; ditto, E. Prov., 1903; asst. D.P.W., 1904; dep. col. engrn. and survr.-gen., Penang, S.S. Sttltns., 1905; ag. col. engrn. and survr.-gen., S.S., Jan., 1907, to Apr., 1908; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Singapore, 1907; ag. col. engrn. and survr.-gen., Sept., 1909; confirmed Dec., 1909.

PIKE, RICHARD NICHOLSON.—B. 1880; midshipman and ag. sub-lieut. R.N., 1894-1900; lieut., I.Y., S. Africa, Jan., 1901 to Sept., 1902 (Queen's medal and five clasps); lieut., 8th Batt. Rifle Brigade, Dec., 1903; seconded for serv. with W.A.F.F., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1904; ag. deputy marine supt. and marine supt., Jan., 1906 to Jan., 1907; asst. res., Jan., 1907.

PINDER, ALBERT HUMPHREY, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 12th Nov., 1910; attached to Kandy Kachcheri, Dec., 1910; ditto, Puttalam, Mar., 1911; ditto, Kandy, Oct., 1911; ag. pol. mag., Puttalam, Jan., 1912; ditto, Gampola, May, 1912; ditto, Chilaw, Jan., 1913; office asst. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, May, 1913; office asst. to prin. collr. of customs and second landing survr., Colombo, Feb., 1914.

PINTO, MAURICE SALVADOR.—B. 1873; B.A., Madras; Barrister-at-law; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1896; off. asst. to govt. agt., E. Prov., Jan., 1898; Sabaragamuwa, Dec., 1898; addl. pol. mag., Tangalla, Sept., 1900; pol. mag., Panadure, Dec., 1900; Avisawella, Dec., 1901; Galle, Sept., 1906; commr. of requests, Colombo, Nov., 1907; dist. judge, Jaffna, Jan., 1911.
PIRIE, J. H. H., M.B., Bac. Surg., M.D., M.R.C.P.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1913.

PITOT, LEON EMILE.—B. 1866; govt. survr. and supt. of pub. wks., Seychelles, 11th Apr., 1889; govt. engr. and architect, Mauritius, Apr., 1891; gen. man. of rlyws. and engineer, Mauritius, 14th Sept., 1896.
PITT, C. G.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Mar., 1913.

PITTMAN, EDWARD FISHER, A.R.S.M.—B. 1849; entd. mines dept., N.S. Wales, 1877; held positions of mining survr., geological survr., and chief mining survr.; lecr. in mining at Sydney Univ., 1893-1902; under-sec. for mines, N.S. Wales since Sept., 1902, and govt. geologist since Sept., 1901; author of "The Mineral Resources of New South Wales," 1901, and many geological reports and papers.

PLANT, GEORGE FREDERIC.—B.A., (Oxon); graduated, class. hona., 1900; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1901; off. asst., Kurunegala, 1902; off. asst., Galle, 1904; pol. mag., Avisawella, 1905; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Apr., 1906; ditto, Matare, Dec., 1906; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1909.
PLATTS, W.A.F.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., 15th Feb., 1907; dist. commr., Apr., 1913.

PLAYFORD, THE HON. THOMAS.—Mem. house of assem., S. Australia; held office as commr. of Crown lds. and immigrn. at various periods between Feb., 1876, and June, 1881; commr. pub. wks., June, 1884, to Feb., 1885; commr. of Crown lds. and immigrn., Feb., 1885, to June, 1885; treas. and premier, 1887 to 1889, and again 1890 to 1892; mem. of fed. convention, 1891; mem. of Kingston ministry, 1893; agt.-gen. for S. Australia in London, 1894 to 1898, and representative of col. at col. conf., Ottawa, 1894; mem. H. of Assem., S. Australia, 1899 to 1901; mem. of Senate, Australian parliament, 1901-1906; min. of defence, Commonwealth, 1905-1906; has ret. from politics.
PLOWMAN, C. H. F.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912.

PLOWMAN, GEORGE THOMAS, C.M.G. (1908).—Clerk, and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Natal, 1890; 1st clk., 1891; ch. clk., 1894; sec. to civ. ser. bd.; asst. under-sec., 1898; sec. to prime min., July, 1901; sec., lands and works (now rlyws. and harbrs.), Nov., 1901; mem., civ. serv. bd., 1901; sec. to prime min.; acting, offr., rlyws. and harbrs., 1st July, 1906; prov. sec., Natal Prov., 1910.

PLUNKET, 6TH BARON (created 1827), WM. LEE PLUNKET.—B. 1864; G.C.M.G. (1910); K.C.V.O., J.P., Dublin and Wicklow; honorary attaché to the embassy at Rome, 1889-92; Constantinople, 1892-4; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland 1900-1904; govt. and commdr-in-chief, New Zealand, 1904-1910.

POMARE, HON. MAUI, M.D., Chicago, U.S.A.—B. 1876; educ. Boys' High Schol., Christchurch; govt. health offr. to maoris, 1901; entd. New Zealand parlmt., 1911; mem. of exec. com. representing native race, 1912.

POND, JOSEPH EDMUND.—Copyist, commr's office, Montserrat, 1st June, 1907; 3rd clk. board of offr., treasy. and customs dept., 16th July, 1910; ag. 2nd clk. and post office clk., Apr. to July, 1911; 4th clk. and clk. to valuation commrs., Apl., 1912; ag. commr's clk. and educational dist. offr., from 23rd June, 1912 to Jan., 1913; 3rd treasy. clk. and post office clk., Nov., 1912; ag. 1st clk., from 26th May, 1913.

PONTIFEX, SYDNEY CLAUDE OVERTON.—B. 1871; apptd. to cust., Br. Guiana, 1891; 5th class offr., 1892; 4th ditto, 1894; 3rd ditto, 1896; transf. to cust., So. Nig., 1899; ag. P.M.G., Apr., 1900; asst. collr. cust., Feb., 1901; asst. collr. and ag. P.M.G., July, 1902, to Feb., 1903, and Apr. to Nov., 1904; prov. collr. of cust., Oct., 1906.
POOLE, H.—B. 1887; apptd., after compt. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., May, 1907.

POPE, FREDERICK SIDNEY.—B. 1869; educ. Wellington Boys' Coll., New Zealand; served in govt. rlyw. dept., N. Zealand, 1886-1900; dep. of agric., 1900-1909; sec. for agriculture, industries and commerce since 1909.

POPE, SIR JOSEPH, K.C.M.G., (1912), C.T.O. (1908), C.M.G. (1901), I.S.O. (1906)—B. 1854; entered civ. ser. of Canada in 1878 as priv. sec. to min. of marine and fisheries priv. sec. to the late Sir John A. Macdonald, priv. sec. min. from 1882 till his death in 1891; asst. of the Queen's Privy Coun. for Canada, 1891; under-sec. of state and dep. reg. gen. of Canada, 1896; author of the Memoirs of Sir John A. Macdonald; Royal Tour in Canada, 1901, and other works; attached to the staff of the Br. att. on the Behring Sea arbitration at Paris, 1885; agt. of Canadian govt., at joint high comm. Quebec and Washington, 1898-99; deputies to govt. to arrange Royal tour in Canada, 1901; assoc. sec. to Alaska boundary tribunal, London, 1903; apptd., in association with Mr. Bryan, H.M.'s plenipotentiary at the International Conference on pelagic sealing, 1911; recd. C.T.O. for services on the occasion of the Quebec tercentenary celebrations, 1908; holds 2nd cl. of Japanese Order of the Sacred Treasure; under-sec. of state for external affairs, 1909.
POPE, PERCY W. T.—B. 1856; ed. of Wales Coll., Charlottetown, P. E. I.; Dominion auditor at Charlottetown, 1883; rec. gen., 1st Jan., 1887.

POPHAM, HENRY BRADSHAW.—B. 1858; educ. Tonbridge Sch.; commr., 13th Light Infantry, 1900; served in S. African War (2 medals); seconded G. Coast Regt., 1906; priv. sec. A.D.C. to agt. govt., G. Coast, Mar. to Aug., 1909; col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1910; priv. sec. agt. govt., Feb. to June, 1911; ag. chief ass't sec. and clk. of councils, July-Aug., 1913.
POPLEWELL, H. B.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., 1910.

PORCH, MONTAGU PIPPEN.—B. 1877; at Bath Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford, 1902; M.A., 1904; served with Mil. Yeomanry in S. African war, 1900-1902. medal and three clasps); Egyptian exploration fund with Prof. Flinders Petrie, 1904 and 1905; asst. res., Northern Nigeria, 14th July, 1906; 3rd cls. res., 1st Apl., 1912.

PORRAL, ALBERT, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1846; supernum., Crown land office, Gibraltar, Nov., 1882; 2nd class clk., Jan., 1875; 1st class clk., Jan., 1883; sec. to oomsn. to inquire into the organisation of the pol. force, 1887; contracting offr. in 1890; sec. to the Crown lands bd., with a seat at that bd., Jan., 1891; ag. contracting offr., June to Oct., 1892; oomsn. of Crown lands, 1894; offl. trustee in bankruptcy, 1907; is also Imp. Russian consul, and manager of the Anglo-Egyptian bank.

PORRAL, JOHN.—Ck., port office, Gibraltar, Aug. to Sept., 1874, and July, 1877, to May, 1878; writer in H.M.'s dockyard, Sept., 1874, to July, 1877; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, June, 1878; 2nd clk., 1883; 1st class clk., Jan., 1890; ch. clk., Dec., 1891; asst. col. sec. and sec. to bd. of health, 1913.
PORTAL, E.—Asst. govt. med. officer, Seychelles, July, 1892; govt. med. officer, Mauritius (Rodrigues), 1901; asst. med. supt., larkly, and lunatic asylums, 1st July, 1905.

PORTELLI-CARBONE, ARFONSO, M.D. (Malta Univ., 1883).—L. M. (Rot. Hosp., Dub.), 1890; police physician for Zurrico, Safi, Crendi, and act. ol. phys. for Micababa and Chireop, and sanitary inspr. for the 6th country dist., Aug., 1884; dist. med. offr. for Valetta, Sept., 1885; is a mem. of the Br. Med. Assoc. (Mediterranean branch), 1888; ed. Br. Gynaecol. Soc., and mem. Intern. Med. cong., Berlin, 1890, and Rome, 1894 (obst. sec.).

PORTER, W. HENRY, I.S.O. (1911).—Ck., res.'s office, Dominica, Nov., 1874; treas. k. and excise offr., Nevis, 1878; 1st land agent after and quarantine offr., St. Kitts, May, 81; ag. treas., Dominica, Feb., 1884, to Oct., 87; ag. mag., dist. F., Nov., 1887, to Feb., 88; treas., Dominica, July, 1890; adminstr. rt. of Dominica, Apr. to Sept., 1904, June, 03, to Apr., 1906, Sept., 1910 to June, 1911; is mem. of exec. and legis. couns. and chrmn. of quarantine board.

PORTER, W. T.—Ed. at Univ. Schl. and aus Coll., Camb., B.A. (law), 1901; called the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1901; joined E. circuit, and pract. at York, Leeds, Bradford and W. Riding of Yorks sessions; mag., Africa Prot., 10th May, 1907; ag. adminstr., July to Nov., 1907; town mag., Mombasa, ig., 1907, to Sept., 1908; 1st cls. mag. for winces of Kisumu and Naivasha, Sept., 1908, Feb., 1909, town mag., Nairobi, 23rd Feb., 9; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 26th Oct., 1911.

POTT, P. A. H.—B. 1877; asst. transport rt., G. Coasts, Sep., 1901; dist. comsnr., Ashanti, Jan., 1907; ag. cantonment mag., massie, Oct. to Dec., 1907, and Apl. and y., 1908; ag. prov. comsnr., S. Prov., Ashanti, y and June, 1909; ditto, W. Prov., Ashanti, 2, 1910, to June, 1911, and from June to Dec., 2.

OTTER, HENRY YARLEY DEIGHTON.—B. 1875; at Edinburgh Acad.; 2nd lieut., Edinburgh y Artil, July, 1894; lieut., Feb., 1896; passed "Arillery," July, 1896; asst. engrnr., with srs. Blyth and Westland consulting engrns. North British Rly. Co., 1895; asst. engrnr. to John Strain, Glasgow, 1896; asst. engrnr., edonian Bly., 1897; draftsman, dir. of wks. t. Admiralty, 1898; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., I.S., 21st Nov., 1901; state engrnr.'s asst., ak, 14th Feb., 1902; asst. engrnr., Larut and Matang, Perak, 1st Jan., 1905; exec. engrnr., 3rd grade, 1st Jan., 1906; ag. exec. engrnr., 2nd grade, Perak, June, 1906; ditto, Mar., 1909; ditto, Pahang, Jan., 1910; exec. engrnr., grade II., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1913.

POTTER, THOMAS IRWIN.—Ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; ag. asst. clk., col. hosp., 18th Dec., 1882; clerical asst., Crown lands off., 10th Apr., 1883; clk., survey dept. (in addition to former post), 1st Dec., 1884; ch. clk., Crown lands off., 16th Aug., 1897; ch. clk. and asst. inspr. of schls., 1st Nov., 1898; warden, La Brea and Oropuche (owing to spec. knowledge of asphalt question); ch. offr. of customs, Brighton, La Brea, 1st Jan., 1899; harb.-master, Brighton harbour, La Brea, in addition to wardenship, 1st July, 1899; J.P. for county of St. Patrick; mem. of society of arts, and dormant mem. Zoological society of Lond.; ag. sub. int., crown lands, 1st Sept. to 31st Oct., 1901, and from 10th Oct., 1905, to Jan., 1906; ag. harb.-mast., June to Dec., 1906; stip. justice, La Brea, and Oropuche in conjunction with wardenship, 5th June, 1907; ag. harb.-mast., May to Nov., 1908; ag. sub-intendant, Nov., 1908, to April, 1909; ag. adminstr.-gen., 28th Apl., 1909; ag. offl. recvr. and adminstr.-gen., 1st Feb. to 13th Dec., 1910; ag. registr.-gen., 1st Apl. to 30th June, 1911; registr.-gen. (provisionally), 1st July, 1911.
POTTS, HENRY WILLIAM, F.L.S., F.C.S.—Prin. of Hawkesbury Agric. Coll., Richmond, N.S. Wales, since May, 1902.

POTTS, MOSES A.—Ed. S. Leone Gram. schl.; served in H.M.'s commissariat, S. Leone, June, 1853, till Jan., 1860; ag. 2nd clk., treas., July, 1860, to Jan., 1866; extra clk., Jan., 1866; house and land tax clk., Aug., 1869; 2nd clk., treas., after a compet. exam., Sept., 1870; census master, 1871; ch. clk. and cashier, 1877; ag. aud.-gen., Dec., 1886, to Feb., 1887; charity comsn., 1890.

POUGNET, EDOUARD DESBARRIERES, A.C.G.L., A.M.I.C.E.—Pub. wks. dept., Mauritius, Jan., 1888; asst. water authority, Mch., 1898; inspr. of water wks., July, 1903; govt. survr., Mch., 1911; supt. of pub. wks. and survr. of roads, St. Kitts, Apl., 1914.

POWELL, HENRY.—Student, Royal Gardens, Kew, June, 1888, to Apr., 1890; curator, Botanic Gardens, St. Vincent, May, 1890, to Nov., 1903; asst. to dir. of agric., E. Africa Prot., 7th Nov., 1903; ch. of economic plants div., Apr., 1907.

POWELL, HENRY TREVOR.—Ed. Monmouth schl.; apptd. 3rd asst. in treasy., Brit. E. Africa Prot., 1st Dec., 1901; 2nd asst., 1st Apl., 1903; offl. mem., plague arbitn. bd., 1903; treas., Somaliland Prot., 28th Nov., 1903; supt. of post office, Nov., 1906; G.A.S. medal and 2 clasps Somaliland, 1902-1904 and 1908-1910.
POWELL, ROBERT ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E., assoe. Roy. Coll. of Science, Dub.—Dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Ceylon; ag. 2nd financial and office asst., Nov., 1892; dist. engrnr., Oct., 1893.

POWELL, ROBERT BRANKS.—Priv. sec. to lieut.-gov. of B. Columbia, 1900-1904; in charge of arrangements in connection with visit of Duke and Duchess of Cornwall and York to B. Columbia, 1901; cadet, Fiji civ. serv., Oct., 1904; employed in native dept., Nov., 1904; attached to comsnr., Colo N. and E., Feb., 1905; employed in govr.'s off. and on Western Pacific high comsn., May, 1905, to Oct., 1906.
POWER, EDMUND LE POER.—Dist. offr., Somaliland Prot., 1st Nov., 1902; dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., 1912.

PRAIN, Lt.-Col. Sir David.—Kt. Bach, (1912); C.M.G. (1912); C.I.E. (1906); M.A., M.B., LL.D., F.R.S.E., F.L.S., F.R.S.; b. 1857; ed. Aberdeen gram. schl. and Univs. of Aberdeen and Edinburgh; demonstrator of anatomy, coll. of surgeons, Edin., 1882-3; and univ. of Aberdeen, 1883-1884; entd. Indian med. serv., 1884; curator, Calcutta herbarium, 1887-1898; prof. of botany, med. coll., Calcutta, 1895-1905; director, botanical survey of India, and supt. of royal botanic garden, Calcutta, 1898-1905; trustee of the Indian Museum, 1898-1904; sec. bd. of scientific advice for India, 1903-1904; mem. of coun. of various scientific societies; director of royal botanic gardens, Kew, since 1905.

PRANKERD, Horace Arthur.—B. 1883; ed. at Ipswich and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (scholar, 2nd class Tripos), B.A., 1906; asst. master, Loretto, to July, 1907; then 2½ years at Lausanne and Freiburg-in-Baden Univ.; asst. sec., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; asst. res., 1911.

PRASAD, Murari.—B. 1888; Cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1912; attached to Galle Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Point Pedro, Sep., 1913; addtl. office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1914.

PRATT, Charles E. J. Blyth.—Ed. Trent Coll.; 2nd lieut., 4th batt. Sherwood Foresters Derbyshire regt., Jan., 1887; lieut., 4th batt. Prince of Wales North Stafford regt., May, 1888; instructor of musketry, Jan., 1890; lieut., Falmouth div. submarine miners, R.E., Apr., 1893; asst. inspr. Sierra Leone frontier pol., Aug., 1895; inspr., Apr., 1896; J.P. for S. Leone.

PRATT, Edward.—Ed. at Shrewsbury; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1898; ag. asst. dist. offr., Raub, May, 1899; ag. coll. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1901; passed cadet, May, 1902; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Jan., 1903; ag. asst. dist. offr., Tanjong Malim, Sept., 1903; ag. dist. treas., Batu Gajah, Jan., 1904; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Nov., 1904; ditto, Tanjong Malim, Dec., 1904; ditto, Kuala Lipis, Aug., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1908; ag. mag., Taiping, Oct., 1910; asst. regis., supreme ct., Kinta, May, 1911.

PRATT, F. G., A.M.I.C.E.; asst. engr., Lagos govt. rlys., Mar., 1907; asst. engr., P.W.D., Uganda, Sept., 1908; ditto, P.W.D., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1911; represented N. Nigeria at 3rd International Road Congress held in London in June, 1913.

PRENDERGAST, Hon. Sir James, Kt. Bach. (1881).—B. 1828; is grad. of Univ. of Camb.; called to bar (Mid. Tem.), 1857; admitted a barrister of the sup. ct. in N. Zealand, Dec., 1862; atty.-gen., 1865; ch. just. of N.Z., 1875-1899.

PRENDERGAST, Hon. James Emile Pierre.—Ed. at Seminary of Quebec, and Laval Univ., Quebec; B.A., 1878; I.L.B., 1881; returned for La Verandrye, Manitoba, Aug., 1888; again Dec., 1886; on formation of cabinet by Hon. Thomas Greenway, Jan., 1888, was given portfolio of prov. sec.; re-elected in said constituency; elected for Woodlands, 1888; judge of sup. ct., N.W.T., 1902; ditto, Saskatchewan, 1907; puisne judge, court of King's Bench of Manitoba, 1910.
PRESCOTT, Arthur Stanley.—Apptd. after compet. exam., supplementary clk., supreme ct., Gibraltar, 1895; 3rd cls. clk., 1899; asst. regist. and deputy clk. of arraigns, 1911.

PRICE, G. F.—Dist. acont., S. African govt. rlys. (construction), Natal, 1904-1907; acont., Baro-Kano rly., N. Nigeria, Feb. to July, 1908; acont., P.W.D., 1st Aug., 1908; asst. treas., Nigeria, Oct., 1910; ag. chief acont. treas., Afr., to Nov., 1912.
PRICE, Herbert Henry.—2nd clk. to asst. comanr., Northern Div., Bech. Prot., 1910; 2nd clk., res. comanr.'s office, 1st May, 1912; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1910.

PRICE, J. F. N.—Served in Bechuanaland Border police, S. Africa, 1895; 2nd lieut., Bedfordshire regt., 2nd Dec., 1896; A.D.C. to priv. sec. to Sir F. Cardew, K.C.M.G., gov. S. Leone, 23rd Oct., 1897; clk. of exec. comm., S. Leone; in S. Leone during Mendi and Timbuktu rebellion, 1897 to 1899 (awarded medal); spec. serv. with W.A.F.F., Lojoka, May to Oct., 1899, when invalided; served with 2nd Batt., Bedfordshire regt., Dublin, Sligo, cliffe and Colchester; passed 4th in nat. test at Hythe, 1900; captain, 20th June, 1900; adjut., and quarter-master, 22nd Dec., 1900; served with 3rd Batt. King's Own Yorkshire L.I., Chatham, Malta and Gozo, June, 1901; Mar., 1902 (awarded medal); asst. adjut. instr. of musk., 1902-1903; dist. asst. of police instr., N. Nigeria, 3rd Oct., 1903; staff offr., 1st May, 1905; passed lower standard in Hausa, Feb., 1908; passed in criminal law at R.I.C. dep., Dublin; passed in internat. law and Mohametan law at Impl. Inst.; passed in C. I., 2, 3, 4, 5, Preston, Lancs., 1911.

PRICE, Sir Thos. Rees, K.C.M.G. (1891); C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1848; served in various positions in the Vale of Neath and G.W. rly. company, England, 1863 to 1880; dist. traffic sup., Grahamstown, Cape govt. rlys., July, 1880; traffic man., West system, C.G.R., Dec., 1881; ditto, Port Elizabeth, Mar., 1881; traffic man., East system, 1st July, 1882; ag. ch. traffic man., 12th Nov., 1891; traffic man., North system, C.G.R. agent in Transvaal and O.F.S., 7th Oct., 1892; ch. traffic man., 1st Nov., 1893; ag. gen. man., 1896; gen. man., C.G.R., 16th May, 1897; to 11th Aug., 1902; gen. man., C.S.A. rly., mem. of rlys. and harb. bd., Union of S. Africa, 1910; pub. dist. comanr., 1911; is a mem. of geog. soc. of Lisbon.

PRICE, Walter Cromwell, B.A.—Ed. Rathmines Schol., Dublin, and at Dublin Univ. and Schol. of Engineering, Queen's Coll., Galway; asst. to James Price, A.M.I.C.E.; engaged at Galway harbour works, 1880; Lock Erin drainage, 1883; Irish light rlys., 1883-1884; Bandon water rlys., 1886-1886; dist. engr., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1887; in charge of irrigtn. wks., 1889-1900; ag. prov. engr., North-Central Prov., 1896; ditto, Uva Prov., 1902; ditto, Northern prov., 1902; prov. engr., North-Western Prov., 1904; ditto, Central Prov., 1907; mem. of mun. coun., Kandy, and also of san. bd., Kandy, 1910; prov. engr., W. Prov., Mar., 1914.

PRINCE, Prof. Edward Ernest, F.R.S.C. &c.—B. 1858; ed. Modern Schol., Leeds, and St. Andrews, Camb., and Edin. Univ.; appd. demonstr., zool., Edin. Univ., 1885; naturalist, Scotch fishery bd. laboratory, St. Ands.; prof. zool. and comp. anat., Royal Infirmary Coll., Glasgow, 1890; fishery expert, Irish Fish Survey, 1893; vice-pres. Intern. Fisheries Congress, Washington, Paris, and St. Petersburg, 1903-06-09; vice-pres., biol. sect., Brit. Assoc., 1909; ditto, Royal Soc. of Canada, 1908; mem. of internat. relations coun., American fish. assoc., Rome, 1911; life mem. of British Science Guild; mem. of internat. fisheries comanr.; repres. Canada...
PRING, ROBERT DARLOW.—Puisne judge, N.S. alea, since 1902.

PRINSEP, HENRY CHARLES.—Ed. Cheltenham ll.; went to W. Australia, 1866; apptd. J. 1867; agr. clk. and draftsman in lands dept., 4; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to admstr., Nov., 1884, June, 1885; ch. draftsman, lands dept., 1890; olk., 1892; under-secr. for mines, 1894; ch. prot. aborigines, May, 1898.

PRIOR, WM. BURGESS.—Mem. provisional N. neo Assoc.; res. E. Coast dist. Br. N. Borneo, g., 1881; prot. of coolies, E. Coast, 1884; m. E. Coast couns., 1888; Br. consular agt., 1880; and mag., Gold Field dist., Feb., 1887.
PRITCHARD, MAJOR C. G.—1st commn. R.A., d Nov., 1891; promoted capt., 4th Dec., 1899; Hong Kong volunteers, Jan. to July, 1901; ndt. and adj. (local major), 2nd Apr., 1902.
PRITCHARD, WILLIAM OWEN.—Med. offr., Africa Prot., 15th Feb., 1904.

PRITCHETT, CECIL JOHN SUCKLING, B.A. (ton).—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civil serv., Dec., 2; attached to Jaffna Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; pol. mag., Trincomalee, July, 1913; ag. asst. limt. offr., Nov., 1913; attached to Jaffna kacheri, Aug., 1914.

PROBYN, SIR LESLIE, K.C.M.G. (1909); M.G. (1903).—Ed. at Charterhouse, and in France and Germany, called to the bar, d. Tem., 1884; joint author of "The jurisdiction and Practice of the Mayor's Court," other law books; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 18; atty.-gen. of Grenada, May, 1896; also ag. sec. in 1897-8; ag. admstr. on several occasions; secretary, S. Nigeria, 12th Apr., 1901; gov., Sierra Leone, 14th July, 1904; gov., Barbados, Nov., 1910; assumed govt., 13th Feb., 1911.

PROUT, W. T., C.M.G. (1905), M.B., C.M. din.)—Aast. poor law med. offr., Mauritius, 36; surg. supt., immigrant ship "Dundee," 37; aast. col. surg., Gold Coast, 1888; dist. nmr., Gold Coast, 1890; col. surg., Gambia, 33; ag. chief mag., Gambia, 1893; med. in charge of base hosp. during campaign against Foday Sila; col. surg., S. Leone, 1895; title to prin. med. offr., 1901; retired, 1906; low of roy. med. soc., Edin., and correspndg. mem. of the epidemiological soc. of Lond.; lect. il. of trop. med., Univ. of Liverpool; med. adviser to C.O.; mem. of advisory, med. and military comte. for Tropical Africa, C.O.; thor. of article "Yaws" in "Diseases of Warm mates" (Davidson); Fil. Volvulus (Archives Parasitologique, May, 1901); "Filariasis in S. zone" (Brit. Med. Journal, 1902); and other pers.
PRYCE, HOWARD LLOYD, C.M.G. (1911).—av. comsnr., Gambia Prot., Oct., 1896; attached Gambia field force, 1901 (medal and clasp); ch. mag., Gambia, July to Oct., 1903; 1st cls. iv. comsnr., Jan., 1904.

PTOLEMY, WM. JOHN, I.S.O., J.P.—B. 1850; at Smithville gram. schl., Ontario; served ith G.W. Rly. Co. of Canada, 1873-1875; P.R. Dominion govt. telegraph construction, 75; mercantile life, 1876-1882; entd. govt. rv. as acctnt., treasy. dept., Manitoba, 1883 to 85; municipal comsnr., 1886; dep. prov. ear., 1886 to date.
PUGH, J.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Jan., 1910.

PUGHE, S. R.—B. 1877; appd., after compet. exam., olk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1897; promoted, with gratuity, Aug., 1904; confi. olk., Dec., 1907; minor staff clerk, 1911.

PUGSLEY, HON. WILLIAM, K.C., D.C.L.—B. 1850; educ. schls., Sussex, N.B., Univ. of New Brunswick, B.A. (1868), and Univ. of Lond., B.C.L., D.C.L.; called to the bar, N.B., 1872; reporter of the supreme ct., 1873-83; Q.C. by Dom. govt., 1891; elec. to legis., N.B., 1885; speaker, 1887; solr.-gen., 1890; resigned, 1892; re-entrd. legis., 1899; attorney-gen., 1900; premier, 1901; sworn of the priv. council for Canada and min. of pub. wks., 1907; re-elec. to H. of C., Sept., 1911; resig. portfolio on defeat of Laurier govt., Oct., 1911.

PURCELL, GILBERT KENHELM TREFFRY.—Ed. Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1888; M.A. 1893; called to bar, Linc.'s Inn, Nov., 1890; mem. of western circ., also practised at cent. crim. ct.; appd. asst. to atty.gen. Leeward Islda. (St. Kitts), Mar., 1898; official mem. of exec. and legis. couns. of the presidency of St. Christopher and Nevis; ag. att.-gen. Leewards, Aug.-Oct., 1900; appd. ag. 1st puisne judge, June, 1901; puisne judge, G. Coast, 11th June, 1901; chief justice, Sierra Leone, 1911.

PURDON, ANDREW.—B. 1875; ed. at Ebury and Conway colls., N. Wales; cadet, H.M.S. "Conway"; sub-lieut., R.N.R., Apr., 1905; lieut., 1908; qualified at Whale Island and H.M.S. "Vernon"; aast. marine supt., N. Nigeria Marine, 11th Sept., 1909; whilst on leave took special course of instruction in hydrographical survey at R.N. coll., Greenwich, 1911.
PURVES, JAMES McLENNAN.—Govt. forester, Nyasaland, May, 1900; ag. head, forestry and botanic dept., 1906; ch. forest offr., 1910; ag. dir. of agric., Aug., 1911, and May, 1914.

QUARTEY, ROBERT EMMANUEL.—B. 1863; outdoor offr., customs, Accra, 1881; 4th clk., customs and treasy., 1883; 2nd clk., audit office, 1885; 1st clk., 1887; audit clk. to local auditor, Jan., 1889; has several times acted as local auditor; transfd. to post and tel. dept., 1902; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., 1903; in charge of post and tel. dept., 1903; sent to Axim on spec. P.O. duty, 1903; on spec. duty at Pram Pram, 1904; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., 1905 and 1906; dist. postmnr., Jan., 1907; taking spec. course of instr. at G.P.O., London, 1907.
QUENTRALL, THOMAS.—Mining engrn., Kimberley, Cape, July, 1889; inspr. of mines, Kimberley, July, 1891.

QUICK, HON. SIR JOHN, KT. BACH. (1901); LL.D.—B. 1852; elec. to first H. of R., Commonwealth of Australia, 1901; re-elec., in 1903 and 1906; postmnr.-gen., C. of A., June, 1909; retired from politics, 1913.

RADCLIFFE, JOHN.—B. 1879; ed. Denatone and Royal Schl., Armagh; lieut., I.Y., S. African war, 1900-1901 (Queen's medal with 5 clasps); lieut., 5th Batt. Leinster Regt., 1903; D.S.C., N. Nigeria, 1906; senr. transport offr., 1908; ag. chief transport offr., Aug., 1909, to Feb., 1910; ag. cantonment mag., Zungeru, Jan., 1911 to July, 1912.

RADCLIFFE, CHAS. AMYAS.—B. 1861; Comdr., R.N., ret.; naval cadet, 1875; lieut., 1884; comdr., 1905; served in Soudan war, 1884 (promoted, medal and clasp "Suakin," Khedive bronze star); served in Mediterranean, E. Indian, Pacific, and Channel Is. stations, 1877-93; dep. mast. attendant, S. Sttlmts., Jan., 1903; ag. harbmr., Penang, Apr., 1903; ag. mast. attendant, S.S., Dec., 1903; mast. attendant, Dec., 1906.

RADFORD, WILLIAM JOHN.—Charity Cross hosp.; M.R.C.S. (England), 1887; L.R.C.P. (London), 1890; F.R.G.S., 1900; hon. mem., St. John's ambulance soc., 1896; med. offr., E. Afric. Prot., 1st June, 1898; sen. med. offr., 1910; chief sanitary offr., Apl., 1913.

RAE, JAMES MACLURE.—B. 1852; admitted to Bahamas bar, 1877; notary public same year; J.P., 1878; res. just., Crooked Is. dist., Oct., 1878; asst. comnwr., at Cockburn harbour, Turks and Caicos Islds., July, 1880; ag. judge of the sup. ct., and ex-officio auditor of the Turks and Caicos Islds., Aug., 1882, to May, 1883, and Mar., 1884, to Oct., 1886; mast. comnwr., Grand Turk, 1885; stip. mag., Bahamas Nov., 1887; ag. atty.-gen., July, 1893, to Feb., 1894; ag. ch. just., Feb. and Mar., 1894; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1896, to Jan., 1897; June, 1897, to Feb., 1898; Sept. and Oct., 1899; June to Oct., 1900; 11th to 24th June, 1901; 15th May to 3rd Aug., 1902; ag. ch. just., 4th Aug. to 9th Nov., 1902; ag. atty.-gen., 15th June to 31st Oct., 1904; ag. ch. just., 21st Nov., to 4th Dec., 1906, and 2nd to 16th Nov., 1908; ag. atty.-gen., 21st May to 25th Aug., 1908; ag. ch. just., 8th to 13th Feb., 1909; ag. atty.-gen., Sept.-Oct., 1909; ag. ch. justice, from 17th Aug., to 20th Nov., 1911.
RAINSFORD, R. F.—Asst. supt. of police, E.A.P., apr., 1914.

RALPHS, EDWIN, F.R.G.S., F.C.S., F.E.I.S., M.R. San. Inst.—B. 1872; ed. St. Mark's Coll., Chelsea; 1st cls. in Queen's scholarship, in final teachers' exams. (education dept.), and in archbishop's divinity exam.; honours in science, South Kens. exams.; asst. master (junior) Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1898; senior grade, 1901; Normal mast., 1906; ag. second master, Apr., 1903, to Mar., 1904, and Apr., 1908 to Apl., 1909; 2nd mast., 8th Apr., 1909; ag. head mast. and mem. of advisory comttee., Tech. Inst., 5th July to 5th Nov., 1909, and from 11th May, 1910, to 25th Oct., 1911; ag. mast. pol. schl., Hong Kong, May, 1902, to Mar., 1904; hon. examnr. in chemistry to Hong Kong Coll. of Med., 1901, 1902 and 1903; dir., Hong Kong Technical Inst., from 1907; also mem. of exam. comttee. for "Teachers' Classes," Tech. Inst., 1909-1911; J.P., 1910; inspr. of English schls., 1913.

RAM, GEORGE EDWARD—Lieut.-4th batt., Prince of Wales's, North Staffordshire Regt.; served in S. Africa, Oct., 1902, to July, 1903 (medal, with 3 clasps); ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov. of Newfoundland, 9th Sept., 1903.

RAMA-NATHAN, PONNAMBALAM, C.M.G. (1889), K.C.—B. 1851; ed. at the Acad., Colombo, and Pres. Coll., Madras; barr.-at-law, Inn. Tem.; advoc., sup. et., Ceylon, 1873; mem. of the legis. coun., 1879; mem. of the lawcoms. apptd. to report upon certain codes dealing with the laws of Ceylon, 1879; served also on the coman. apptd. at his instance to report upon the Thoroughfares Ordinance, 1881; obtained the introduction of P.O. savings bank in Ceylon, 1881-83; was one of the select comttee. of the legis. coun. apptd. to report upon retrenching the public expenditure of the island, 1882-83; a mem. of the coun. of legal educn., 1884; mem. of the Ceylon coman. of the Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; mem. of the Cent. Irrign. Bd., 1888; and of the select comttee. to report upon the incidence of the grain taxes, 1889; mem. of the coman. to inquire into the extension of the rly. to the N. parts of the island, 1890; solr.-gen. of Ceylon, 1892; ag. atty.-gen., 1894; ret., 1905; elected M.L.C., Ceylon, 1911.
RAMSBOTTOM, HON. A. E. W.—B. 1869; formerly col. treas., O.R.C.; prov. admstr. O.F.S., Union of S. Africa, 1910.

RANFURLY, 6TH EARL OF (SIR UCHTER JOHNSON MARK KNOX, G.C.M.G.), Viscount Northall; and Baron Welles of Dungannon, co. Tyrone, Ireland, Baron Ranfurly of Rampforlie, co. Brefrew, United Kingdom.—Born 1856; succeeded his brother 1875; ed. Trinity Coll., Cambridge; J.P., D.L. for co. Tyrone; Knight of Justice of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; lord-in-waiting to the Queen, 1895-7; governor of New Zealand, 1897-1904.

RANKIN, E. B. W.—Entd. service, Br. Guiana, 1885; 5th asst. survr. clk., regist.'s off., 1892; passed exam. as sworn clk. and notary pub., 1895, and acted as such on various occasions; a charge of Berbice off., Aug. to Dec., 1895; asst. clk., 1896; clk. to atty.-gen., 1888; spec. comm. of legislature on Demerara-Essequibo rly. tariff, 1899.

RANKINE, RICHARD SIMS DONKIN.—B. 1853; clk. treasury, Fiji, Feb., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir George O'Brien, Sept., 1897, to 1901; acted clk. ex. coun. Sept., 1899, to Mar., 1900; appd. nat. native taxes, treasury, 1901; priv. sec. to Sir Henry Jackson, July, 1902; priv. sec. to Sir E. im Thurn, 10th Oct., 1904; conf. sec. to Jan., 1905; sec. to consul-gen. for W. Pacific; clk. of exec. coun.; ag. sec., W. Pacific int. coman., June, 1906, and from Nov., 1907, to 31st Dec., 1908; asst. col. sec. and astt. recrv.-gen. to Jan., 1909; recrv.-gen. and coman. of stamp pres. of marine bd., mem. exec. and legis. com., 1910; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1913.

RANNIE, CLARENCE RUPERT.—B. 1872; clerical assist., audit dept., Antigua, May, 1889; clk. to col. engnr., Antigua, Mar., 1891; ag. cd. to audr.-gen., Sept., 1894; 2nd clk. to audr.-gen. Leeward Is., Jan., 1895; audit clk., Dominica, Mar., 1897, to Aug., 1900; cashier, treasy., Antigua, Sept., 1900; ag. 2nd outdoor offr., treasy., Aug. to 31st Oct., 1902; 2nd indoor offr., treasy., 14th Nov., 1904; ag. 1st indoor offr., 23rd Nov., 1904, to 20th July, 1905, 16th May to 15th Aug., 1908, 1st May to 31st Oct., 1909, and from 1st 31st Aug., 1910.

RASON, HON. SIR CORINTHWAITHE HERBERT BACH. (1909).—B. 1859; ed. at Brighton, Eastbourne and Reading; M.L.C. for Swan W. Aust., 1889; M.L.A. for S. Murdoch, 1897-1901; ditto for Guildford since 1901; min. of works, 1901; min. of works and rly., 1902-4; prem. of W. Aust., 1906; pres. of Royal coman. on immigr., 1888; and of Royal coman. on immigr., 1905; agt.-gen. in London for W. Aust., 1906-1909.

RAT, JOSEPH NUMA.—B. 1848; colonial scholarship, Trinidad, 1866; Warnefords scholarship King Coll., Lond., 1867; seven times prizeman, literature, divinity and medicine in King's Coll., 1867-1871; matric., Lond. Univ., 1868; prel. science, M.R. Lond. Univ., 1869; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1873; med. col. surg., G. Coast, Feb., 1887; dist. med. ed., Dominica, Aug., 1889; ag. med. offr. public institutions, Dominica, 1890; dist. med. offr. St. Kitts, Oct., 1892; mag. and med. offr. Anguilla, Dec., 1897; dep. judge, court of summary juris., Leeward Is., 1899; dist. med. offr., Nevis Jan., 1905; med. offr., dist. 3, St. Kttna Jan., 1913; M.L.C., Dominica, 1888-1889; chrmn., town improvement coman., St. Kitts, 1896-1897; mem. poor law bd., St. Kitts, 1896-1897. 1897; has translated several military drill books into the Hausa language for the G. Coast govt.; has also written numerous papers on Leprosy, Yaws, etc., grammars of the Carib and Hausa languages, and a translation of the Gospel of St. Mark into the French patois of the W. Indies.
RAYMOND, WILLIAM HENRY.—Sub-instr. of pol., S. Aust., 1st Jan., 1904; instr. of pol., 1st July, 1904; commr. of pol., 1st Jan., 1910.

READ, HERBERT JAMES, C.B. (1914), C.M.G. (1907).—B. 1863; M.A., Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 1st class math. mods., and 1st class math. finals; 2nd class math., 1885; 2nd class math., 1886; 2nd class math., 1887; 2nd class math., 1888; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Sept., 1896; priv. sec. to Mr. Afterwards Sir E.) Wingfield, Feb., 1898; 1st cls. lk., Oct., 1898; principal clk., 1st Apr., 1905; British delegate at African liquor confce. at Brussels, 1899; one of the Brit. delegates on sundry comms. at Berlin for the settm. of Anglo-German frontier in E. Africa, 1906; one of the Brit. plenipotentiaries at African liquor confce. at Brussels, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comte. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908; chmn. deptl. comte. to enquire into W. African ed. staff, 1909; visited E. Africa, 1911-12.

REAL, HON. PATRICK.—B. 1847; called to the bar, Queensland, 1874; frequently acted as dist. judge, and for some years was Crown proser in int. dist.; mem. of Royal coman. on establishment of Queensland Univ., 1891; judge of sup. ct., Queensland, 1890.

REDDIN, RICHARD.—B. 1837; educ. privately at Central Acad., Charlottetown, P. E. land; barrister of the sup. ct. for some years prior to 1892 when he was apptd. to the office of surrogate and judge of probate for Prince Edward land.
REDINGTON, DUDLEY CECIL.—B. 1883; ed. Longowes Coll., Ireland; trooper, Natal police, 03-11; instr., Zanzibar police, 25th March, 11; supt., 15th Mar., 1912; asst. comdt., 1st sb., 1914.

REECE, EARLDY BRANWELL.—B. 1876; ent. b. service, St. Lucia, Apr., 1892, as supernumerary, g. gov't. off.; 3rd clk., Apr., 1893; 2nd clk. ad acctnt., treas., Apr., 1894; ag. ch. clk. on occasions, 1895-9; honours graduate, 1900 diploma, Ont. business coll., Canada (affil. inst.), Canada; apptd. chief clk. and bookkeeper, treasury, Gold Coast, 26th April, 1901; introduced double entry system of accounts in treasury in conformity with fin. inst., June, 01; acctnt., Jan., 1902; sen. acctnt. treas., July, 05; ag. chief acctnt., G. Coast govt. rlyw., b. 1907; chief acctnt., 1st June, 1907; treas., Coast, 1909; ag. col. sec., May and June, 12.
REECE, FREDERICK ST. AUBYN.—Ck., P.O., Barbados, 1873; 2nd clk., 1875; resig. 1878; dining waiter, St. Lucia, 1883; col. postmtr., St. cia., 1885.

REECE, REV. JAS. EBENEZER.—B. 1847; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. in 1868; M.A. 1874; ag. curate of St. Michael's Cathedral, Barbados, and priv. sec. to Bishop Parry, 1872; t. master of Harrison Coll., and curate of St. Michael's Cathedral, 1877; curate of St. Bartholomew's, 1878; curate of St. Luke's, 1882; hop's chaplain, 1884; instr. of schla., 1885; son of St. Michael's Cathedral, 1905.

REECE, I. RICHARD.—Ed. at St. John's Coll., mb.; B.A. in 1870; called to bar, Inn. Temp., 71; J.P., Barbados, 1873; coroner for parishes Christchurch and St. George, 1873; ag. provostmarshal and serjeant-at-arms, 1874; ag. pol. mag. of St. Michael's parish, 1875; ag. judge of asst. ct. of appeal, 1875-79; comsnr. of probates, 1877; ag. solr.-gen., 1880; regist. of friendly and building societies, 1880; ag. judge of the asst. ct. of appeal, 1880-82; ag. atty.-gen. and ex-officio mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ch. just. (on two occasions for special purposes) and chancellor of the diocese; sole comsnr. to report on a complaint of the vestry of St. Michael (Barbados) against Bridgetown Water Works Co.; comsnr. to report on working of courts of inferior juris. (Barbados); comsnr. to investigate charges against rioters (Barbados); Grenada, 1883, judge of asst. ct. of appeal, 1883; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, 1884-5; local comsnr. of incumbered estates (St. Vincent), Oct., 1884; comsnr. to examine and report on the govt. schl., St. Vincent; comsnr. to perform special duty in Grenada at the request of S. of S.; res. mag., Jamaica, 1888.

REED, HAYTHORNE.—B. 1873; M.A., Cantab.; barrister-at-law; ed. Bath Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; lieut., S. African Constaby, 1902-1908; 2nd mag., Zanzibar, Dec., 1908; ag. asst. judge, June-Dec., 1910; 1st mag., 8th Dec., 1910.
REED, JOSEPH MARTIN.—I.S.O. (1903); B. 1857; ent. public service of Victoria, 1875; survr.-gen., 1899-1914; sec. to dept. of lands.
REEP, ALFRED MILLS.—B. 1896; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil serv., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1914.

REES, LEWIS CHAS. MORRIS PARKER.—B. 1876; ed. Merchant Venturers Tech. Coll., Bristol; art. pupil to Messrs. Foster and La Trobe, architects, survr., Bristol, 1892; asst. to borough engrn., Islington, 1897; ch. asst. borough engrn., Margate, 1900; prin. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1902; also sec., squatters' bd., 1905.

REEVE, HENRY, C.M.G. (1900), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1854; passed C.S. exam., Victoria, 1868; inst., Melbourne Univ., 1870; cadet, Victorian survey dept., 1873; cadet, N.S.W. survey dept., 1874; survr., 1878; survr., Fiji survey dept., May, 1880; staff survr., Jan., 1881; ag. comsnr. for wka., July, 1884, to Feb., 1885; col. engrn., St. Lucia, 1887; dir. of survey, Windward Is., 1891; mem. legis. coun., St. Lucia, 1891; special mem. exec. coun., St. Lucia and St. Vincent, 1891; col. engrn., Gambia, 1894; mem. exec. coun., 1899; ch.
