English comsnr., Anglo-French bindry, comsnr., 1895-6, 1898-9; director of pub. wks., Lagos, Aug., 1901; ag. col. sec., Jan. to May; ag. gov't., May to July, 1902; ag. col. sec., 1902 and 1903; ag. gov't., 1902; deputy gov't., 1903; ret., 1904.

REEVES, HON. WILLIAM PEMBER.—Born Canterbury, N.Z., 1857; ed. in Colony; prov. scholarship holder 1868, and 1872; N.Z. scholarships in classics and English, 1874; Somes scholar, Christ's Coll., Canterbury; first elected mem. of H. of R., 1887; joined Ballance Min., Jan., 1891, holding the portfolios of educn., just. and labour; contd. in off. in Seddon Min. to Jan., 1896; agt.-gen. for N.Z. in London, 1896; represented N.Z. at Internat. Commer. Congress at Philadelphia, 1899; mem. of Pacific Cable Comtee., 1899; high comsnr. for N.Z. in London, June, 1905; resigned, 1908, on apptmt. as director of London schl. of economics; mem. of senate, London Univ.; author of the "Long White Cloud," "State Experiments in Australia and New Zealand," and other publications relating to N.Z.
REFORD, JOHN HOPE, B.A., M.D., B. Ch., B.A.O. (R.U.I.), L.M. (Dub.), D.T.M. (Liverpool).—Civ. surg., South African Field Force, 1901-2; med. offr., Uganda Prot., 1st Aug., 1907.
REID, ALEXANDER, I.S.O. (1904).—Ent. the govt. ser., Tasmania, 1858; under treasurer, 1895.

REID, ARTHUR WILLIAM.—Ed. at Leicester and Stafford; asst. store dept., Mersey rly., Liverpool, Jan., 1886; chief in 1889; asst. loco. supt. (stores), Uganda rly., 1897; sen. asst., 1899; chief storekeeper, 1903.
REID, D. A.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Mar., 1912.

REID, THE RT. HON. SIR G. H., P.C. (1897); G.C.M.G. (1911); K.C.M.G. (1909).—Min. of pub. instructn., N.S.W., Jan., 1883, to Mar., 1884; M.L.A. for E. Sydney; leader of Opposition, 1891; premier, 1894-9; leader of Opposition in the Commonwealth parlmt. from the inauguration of the Commonwealth, 1901-4; premier and min. for external affairs, Aug., 1904, to July, 1905; represented N.S.W. in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee; high comsnr. for the Commonwealth of Australia in London, 1910.

REID, IRVINE KEMP.—M.B., C.M. (1884), M.D. (1887), D.P.H. (1st class honours in practical hygiene) (1891), Abdn. Univ.; J.P. (1904); mem. of Roy. Med. Soc., Edin.; mem. of Soc. of Med. Offrs. of Health; house surg. and sec., W. Norfolk and Lynn hosp., King's Lynn, 1886; surg., res. med. staff, Col. hospital, Georgetown, Br. Guiana, 1887-89; govt. med. offr., Is. of Wakenaam, Rio Essequibo, 1889; med. offr., penal stltmt., Massaruni; and res. surg. pub. hosp., Bartica, 1892; med. offr., Mara dist., 1893; ditto, Peter's Hall dist., and mem. med. bd., 1894; med. offr., Cotton Tree dist., 1895.

REID, HON. JOHN DOWSLEY.—B. 1859; ed. pub. schl., Prescott, Queen's Univ., and Royal Coll. Physicians and Surgeons, Kingston, Ontario; physician; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1891, 1896, 1900, 1904, 1908 and 1911; mem. of privy coun. for Canada, and min. of customs in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911.

REID, THOMAS.—B. 1881; ed. Clongowes Wood, and Queen's Coll., Royal Univ., Dublin (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; off. asst. to govt. agt., E. Prov., Apr., 1907; mun. mag., Colombo, Dec., 1909; landing surv., customa, Colombo, Aug., 1910; dist. judge, Badulla, June, 1913; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, June, 1914.
REILLY, ROBERT O'MALLEY.—Sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. police, 1904; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1909; inspr., Apl., 1914.

REITZ, HON. F. W.—B. 1845; ed. in Cape Colony and at Edin. Univ.; called to the bar, England, 1868; chief just. of O.F.S.; state sec. of S.A.R.; pres. of O.F.S.; pres. of Senate, Union of S. Africa, 1910.

RENDLE, ANSTRUTHER CARDEW.—Ed. at Marlborough Coll., Christ's Coll., and St. Mary's hosp.; B.A. (Camb.) 1888; M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.C.P. (Lon.); M.B., B.C., M.D., (Camb.); D.P.H.; conjoint bd., 1895; med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 12th Jan., 1906.

RENNER, WILLIAM, M.D. (Brux.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.K. and Q.C.P., L.M. (Ireland).—Ed. at the Liverpool Coll., and Univ. Coll., Lond., and Liverpool, Rotunda, Dublin, and St. Jean hosp., Brussels; mem. of the Pathological Soc. of London; late ophthalmic asst. to Prof. Streathfield, Univ. Coll. hosp., Lond.; ag. surg. S. Leone, 1882-83; asst. col. surg., Freetown, 1886; in charge of lun. asyl., and incurable and smallpox hosp., Kissy; J.P., 1891; deputy coroner for dist. of Freetown, 1891; surg.-capt., late S. Leoa. vol. corps, and recd. W. African medal and clasp, 1898-99; has acted on several occasions as col. surg., pres. of quar. bd., inspr. of health, dep. hbr.-mast., inspr. of health and shipping and prin. med. offr.

RENTON, ALEXANDER WOOD, M.A., LL.B. (honours), Edin.—Forensic prizeman; Lect. Rector's essay prizeman; Bacon scholar, Grnt. Inn, 1882; b. 1861; called to the bar, 1885; prac. judge, Mauritius, Aug., 1901; chmn., local comm. of soc. of comp. legis., 1902; chmn. of mounted reserves comsn., 1903-4; ag. procurer ad. advocate-gen., Aug., 1904; puisne judge, Ceylon, Aug., 1905; received thanks of S. of sl. drafting rules of sup. ct. of Mauritius, 1904; mem. of comtes. apptd. in 1912 to prepare code of civil procedure, Ceylon; ag. chief just. Ceylon, May, 1913; chief just., Ceylon, Aug., 1914; author of "Wood Renton on Luns"; joint author of first edition of "Edmund's Patents"; editor of Law Journal; editor of "Encyclopedia of the Laws of England"; editor of "The English Reports"; joint editor of 2nd edition of "Burge on Colonial and Foreign Law."

REW, CHARLES EDWARD DALIEL OLDHAM.—Ed. at Marlborough Coll., and the Roy. Mil. Coll. Sandhurst; 2nd lieut. Nov., 1890; major, W. India Regt., Aug., 1906; retired pay, Dec., 1906; A.D.C. to maj.-gen. Sir H. Bengough, K.C.B., ag. gov. of Jamaica, Mar. to Dec., 1883; brigade signalling offr., Jamaica, 1892-5; served expdt., agt., Fodey Kabba, River Gambia, 1891-2 (medal with clasp); Northern Territory, Gold Coast, 1898 (clasp); special serv. off., Ashanti Field Force, 1900; specially employ. in raising Yorubas for the G. Coast Regt. in Nigeria, and Lagos Hinterland, Jan. to Aug., 1901; inspr., G. Coast Constab., Aug., 1901; appd. company commdr., G. Coast Regt., W.A.F.F., Aug., 1901; major, 2nd in command, Dec., 1904; prov. comsnr., Ashanti, Feb., 1907.
REYNOLDS, A. E.—B. 1881; appd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the er. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1900.
RHOADES, EDMUND LUSHINGTON.—Ed. Rat. Schl.; lieut. and comdr., naval dept., B. C. Africa Prot., 1894; 1st offr., marine transport dept., 1904.

RHODES, HON. ROBERT HEATON.—B. 1857; B.A. Oxford; entd. New Zealand parlmt. 1899; served in S. African war (Queen's med. and clasps); lt.-col., 1st Regt., Canterbury Rifle Yeomanry Cavalry; postm.-gen., min. of telegraphs, min. of puh. health, 1912.

RICE, CECIL N.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; supernum. clk., admtr.'s office, St. Vincent, 1890; ag. 2nd clk., Apr. to July, 1891; Dec., 1892, to Feb., 1893; 2nd clk., and sec. gen. bd. of health, June, 1893; ag. ch. clk., ag. clk. of exec. and leg. couns., med. regt. and nat. of govt. printing dept. on several occasions 1895-1908; assisted in relief work after hurricane Sept.-Dec., 1898; port. offr. and cust. offr., 1898-99; ag. sec., bd. of educ., 1907-8; 2nd clk. to govr., Windward Is. (Grenada), Aug., 1901; ch. clk., Nov., 1901; is also confldt. clk. relieving offr. and sec. to Capt. (now Sir Arthur Young, on spec. miss. to St. Vincent after volcanic eruptions, 1902; spec. ment. by govr. S. of S. in despa.) for services rendered. connection with relief work after volcanic eruptions, 1902; ag. priv. sec. to Sir R. B. Lewelyn, K.C.M.G., govr. Windward Is., 1905-96; priv. sec. to Mr. P. C. Cork, C.M.G., ag. govr. Windward Is., 1906-1907; priv. sec. to Mr. L. J. Cameron, C.M.G., ag. govr., Windward Is., 909; performed duties of clk. to exec. coun., Trinidad, 1911 (in conjunction with substantive duties); ag. priv. sec. to Sir J. Hayes Sadler, L.C.M.G., govr. Windward Is., 1912.

RICHARDS, HON. ALBERT ELWOOD, M.A.—3. 1848; ed. at Upper Canada Coll. and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1874; called to the bar, Manitoba, 1882; bencher of law soc., Manitoba, 1883 to 1899; judge, King's Bench t., Manitoba, 1899 to 1906; judge of ct. of appeal, Manitoba, 1906.

RICHARDSON, E.—Employed on engineering work in Mexico, S. California, and Jamaica; ass't ngrnr., Gold Coast rwy., 1899-1901; ag. dist. ngrnr., 1901-1903; asst. survr., Uganda Prot., 904; ag. asst. chief survr., July-Dec., 1906; ist. survr., 1907.

RICHARDSON, EDMUND L.—Jun. clk., col. ec.'s office, Tobago, May, 1874; ch. clk., Oct., 879; ag. regis. of deeds, sup. ct., July, 1880, to une, 1881; ch. clk., audit office, July to Dec., 881; 2nd rev. offr. and landing waiter, St. Lucia, dec., 1881.

RICHARDSON, THE HON. EDWARD, C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1831; entered the N. Zealand parlmt. 1871 as representative for the city of Christchurch; min. for pub. wks., 1872; held that office through various changes of ministries till Jan., 877, when he was compelled to retire on account of ill-health; again took office as min. of pub. wks., Sept., 1884, to Oct., 1887.
RICHARDSON, JOSEPHUS HARGREAVES.—Intd. N. Zealand govt. insurance dept., 1874; omsnr. of ditto since 1890; mem. of public serv. appeal board, 1913.
RICHTER, JOHANNES EDUARD SIEGFRIED.—B. 888; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1891; 11th; asst. dist. commnr., Uganda Prot., 912; asst. sec., 1913.

RIDICK, CHARLES.—Capt., reserve of offrs; leut., 4th batt. Essex regt., 1902; capt., 1906; lythe schl. of musk., 1898; mil. schl. of instr., Chelsea, 1903; R.I.C. course 1908; served in B. Guiana pol., 1894 to 1900; Sierra Leone batt., V.A.F.F. 1900; asst. dist. commnr., S. Nigeria, 904; dep. inspr.-gen. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 906; commnr. of pol., Uganda, 1908; confirmed as appt., 1910.

RIDGEWAY, THE RT. HON. SIR J. WEST, I.C.B. (1906), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.S.I., I.C. (Ireland)—B. 1844; ret. from the army with the rank of col., 1889; under-sec., foreign dept., India, 1881-5; omsnr. for delimitation of Afghan frontier, 1885-7; under-sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1887; lieut.-gov. of the Isle of Man, 893; gov. of Ceylon, Dec., 1895-1903; chmn. of comm. sent to S. Africa on Transvaal constitution, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comtee. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908; chmn., ct. of trs., Br. N. Borneo Co., 1910.

RIDLEY, A. A.—Clerical asst. in prisons dept., Br. Guiana, 10th Oct., 1878, to 31st Aug., 1887; 3rd class offr. of customs, 1st Sept., 1887; 5th class, 1st Aug., 1888; 4th class, 12th Jan., 1896; 3rd class, 1st Aug., 1898.

RIDLEY, HENRY NICHOLAS, C.M.G. (1911).—M.A. (Oxon); F.R.S.; asst. in botanic dept., Br. Museum, 1880 to 1888; dir. of gardens and forests, S. Stlttns., 26th Sept., 1888; sent on visit of inspection to Cocos-Keeling and Christmas Is., 1890, retired, 1911.

RIES, BERNARD SAMUEL.—Asst. clk., immigr. dept., B. Guiana, Dec., 1877; on spec. duty at offr. of med. offr. to immigr. dept., Feb. to Mar., and Apr. to May, 1881; 5th cls. clk., Apr., 1891; 4th cls. clk., Mar., 1893; 3rd cls. clk., June, 1907.

RIGBY, WALTER.—Served with B.S.A. Pol., Matabeleland, 1896; Cape med. staff, Bechuanaland, 1897; 1 clasp; Impl. Light Horse and Kitchener's Fighting Scouts, Boer war; Queen's medal, 4 bars; King's, 2 bars, 1899-1902; inspr. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 16th May, 1904; asst. dist. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 15th Oct., 1904; supt. of pol., 1910.

RISLEY, JOHN SHUCKBURGH, C.B. (1912).—B. 1867; ed. at Marlborough and Magd. Coll., Oxford (classical exhibitioner); 2nd class classical mods., 1888; 2nd class jurisprudence, 1890; open studentship, Inns of Court, 1892; called to the bar, 1893; M.A. and B.C.L. 1894; author of "The Founding of St. Stephen's Golf Club," 1893; "The Law of War," 1897; "Notes and Echoes," 1902; editor of Dale's "Clergyman's Legal Handbook," 7th edition; joint editor of Waterlow's "Companies Acts Manual," 11th and 12th editions; legal assistant, C.O., March, 1901; legal adviser, 19th May, 1911.
RISSIK, HON. J. F. B.—Formerly min. for lands and native affairs, Transvaal; provincial admstr., Transvaal Province, Union of S. Africa, 1910.

RITCHIE, GEORGE LE HARRIVEL KERR BAINBRIDGE.—Natal Civ. Serv., 1891; lieut., 1st batt. N.R.R., 1892; served with Mashonaland Field Force, 1896, attached to Rhodesian Horse, present at the relief of Ft. Charter, Ft. Salisbury, Maze, and Hartley Hills, ment. in desp. (medal and clasp); asst. collr., Nyassaland Prot., Dec., 1897; 2nd grade resident, Apr., 1907.
RITTER, GUSTAVE A., C.M.G. (1902).—Clk. to the judge of the sup. ct. of Mauritius, 1864; regis. V.-A. ct. 1873; marshal of V.-A. ct., 1882; unoffl. nominated mem. of legis. coun., May, 1891.
RIVERS, GEORGE F.—B. 1884; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to C.O., 16th July, 1902.

RIVERS-SMITH, STANLEY, M.A. (Cantab).—B. 1877; ed. at St. Lawrence Coll., Ramsgate, and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge, (exhbitnr.); entd. Egyptian civ. serv., 27th Sept., 1905; dir. of educn., Zanzibar, 2nd Oct., 1907.
ROACH, B. S.—B. 1864; entd. educn. dept., S. Australia, 1879; head teacher, 1891; editor of "Children's Hour" and lecturer at Univ. Training Coll. on English Literature since 1906.

ROBERT, FREDERIC.—Admitted a solicitor of sup. ct. of Mauritius, 1885; ag. mag., Rodrigues, from Nov., 1895, to Dec., 1896, June, 1897, to Sept., 1898, and Apr., 1900, to June, 1901; dist. clk. and sen. civil status offr., Plaines Wilhems, Curepipe division, 15th July, 1901; ag. regis.-gen., May to Oct., 1903; ag. mag., Rodrigues, 1st Apr., 1904; ag. dist. and stip. mag. of Mauritius, 20th July, 1904; ag. mag., Lesser Dependencies, 19th Sept., 1904, confirmed as such, 11th Sept., 1905; confirmed as dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1st Oct., 1907.

ROBERTS, A. B.—B. 1834; entd. civ. ser., O.F.S., 1878; landdrost of Fauresmith, 1883; res. mag., Bloemfontein, 1896; rlyw. auditor, O.F.S.; served as war secretary, O.F.S.; entd. Transvaal civ. ser., 1903; res. mag. and native commnr., Potchefstroom, 1908; prov. sec., Transvaal Province, Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1910.

ROBERTS, CHARLES JAMES, C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1846; ed. at Sydney Gram. schl.; alderman of Sydney, 1877; J.P., 1878; mayor and ch. mag., 1879; commr. at the Sydney Internat. Exhibn., 1879-80; commr. for N.S. Wales at Melbourne Exhibn., 1880-81; at Amsterdam Exhibn., 1883; at Calcutta Exhibn., 1883-84; and at Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886-7; M.L.A., 1885 and 1888; postm.-gen., 1887; in the Parkes ministry; mem. Centennial Celebration Coman., N.S.W., 1888.

ROBERTS, DAVID Gwyn.—Clk., accounting dept., Cape Govt. rlyws., 1902; passed Cape civ. serv. exam., 1st class, Nov., 1902; clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1903; transf. to treasy., Sept., 1908; voluntarily retired from Cape civ. serv., Feb., 1909; clk., customs dept., Nyassaland Prot., 7th Oct., 1910; astt. traffic auditor, Gold Coast rlyws., 24th Sept., 1913.

ROBERTS, GEORGE FURSE.—B. 1881; ed. Univ. Coll., London; telegraphist, G.P.O., Sept., 1897; 2nd div. clk., exchequer and audit dept., Apr., 1900; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1905; off. astt. to gov't. agt., Uva Prov., Apr., 1907; pol. mag., Panadure, Jan., 1909; Jaffna, Dec., 1909; astt. gov't. agt., Hambantota, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, and commr. of requests and pol. mag., Nuwara Eliya, Hatton, May, 1912.
ROBERTS, J. C., F.I.P.S.—Offic. shorthand writ., Barbados, June, 1896.

ROBERTS, JACOB THOMAS.—B. 1874; ed. at C.M.S. Collegiate schl., Lagos; clerical astt., qrtmr.'s off., Hausa force, 16th Sept., 1898; 6th cls. clk., 3rd Aug., 1899; astt. storekeeper, med. dept., 1st Jan., 1900; ag. med. storekeeper and warden, Lagos hosp., 4th Nov., 1903, to 3rd Jan., 1904; 4th cls. clk., med. dept., 1st Apr., 1904; 3rd cls. clk., audit off., 1st Feb., 1906; 2nd cls. clk., ditto, 1st Jan., 1907; 1st cls. clk., ditto, 1st Jan., 1908; in charge of audit off., Ebute Metta, 21st Jan. to 26th Feb., 1908; astt. chief clk., audit off., 1st Jan., 1911.

ROBERTS, PERCY HAMER.—Prison offr., Br. Guiana, 19th Nov., 1901; served penal settlmt., Massaruni, and at Essequibo and Georgetown county prisons; clerical astt., Georgetown and Essequibo prisons; gaoler, G. Coast, 5th Mar., 1911; keeper, James Fort prison, 1st June, 1911.
ROBERTS, ROBERT.—B. 1872; apptd. to cust. serv., B. C. Africa Prot., July, 1904.

ROBERTS, THOMAS WEBB.—B. 1880; ed. Hertford Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1902; pol. mag., Matara, Aug., 1905; Panadure, Sept., 1906; dist. judge, Chilaw, Feb., 1909; ag. dist. judge, Batticaloa, May, 1912; commr. of requests, Colombo, Feb., 1914.
ROBERTSON, A.—Med. offr., E.A.P., June, 1907.

ROBERTSON, DONALD, I.S.O. (1912)—B. 1860; cadet in post office, N. Zealand, 1873, and filled various offices until his appt. as sec. of the postal and telegraph dept. in 1907; apptd. pub. serv. comsnr., 1st Jan., 1913.
ROBERTSON, GEORGE HAWTHORN MINOT.—B.A. Oxon.; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1908; sent to China to study Hokkien, June, 1909.

ROBERTSON, THOMAS ROBERT.—B. 1875; ed. Glenalmond, M.B., Ch.B., Edin.; formerly house surgeon and physician, Swansea hosp. and Victoria Park hosp. for chest diseases; civil surg., S. African Field Force, 1901-2 (medal and three clasps); astt. commr. and gen. med. offr., Salt Cay, Turks Is., 1905; dist. commr. and gen. med. offr., Caicos Is., 1905; gen. med. offr., Grand Turk, 1909.
ROBERTSON, WALTER BANNERMAN.—B. 1864; ed. at dist. schls., Marabfield, P. E. Island, high sheriff of Queen's Co., 1896 to 1902; coll. of dist. for the port of Charlottetown, Apr., 1902.

ROBERTSON, W. C. F.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; exhibitioner, 1886 and 1887; classical scholar, 1888; classical moderator, 1889; inst. of schls., G. Coast, 1896; director of educ., 1896; agr. comptroller of dist., 1899; compiled census rept., 1901; ag. sec. for native affairs, 14th June to 19th Nov., 1905; prov. commr., 20th Nov., 1905; sec. for native affairs, 18th May, 1907; ag. col. sec., 4th July to 25th Sept., 1908; chief astt. col. sec., 22nd Jan., 1909; ag. col. sec., 1909; deputy gov'r., Jan., 1910; ag. col. sec., Sept., 1910; 11th May to 15th June, 1911, 14th July, 1911; 5th Jan., 1912, 30th Oct. to 26th Dec., 1912, and 7th Mar. to 21st Aug., 1913; dep. gov'r., Dec., 1912, and April-May, 1913; ag. gov'r., 1st May to 27th Aug., 1914; dep. gov'r., 28th Aug. to 30 Sept., 1914.

ROBERTSON-EUSTACE, ROBERT WILLIAM BARRINGTON.—Ed. at Shrewsbury; capt., regt. of officers and late 4th Batt. S. Stafford Rgt.; served in the S. African War with Canada Mounted Rifles and A.S.C. (medal and six clasps); astt. dist. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 10th May, 1904; sup't. of pol., 1910.

ROBINSON, FRANKLIN.—B. 1873; M.A. Durham Univ.; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1902; head of Malay Coll., Malacca, Apr., 1903; demo. cation offr., Malacca, 4th Dec., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1904; mag. Jus., 1904; passed cadet, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., Jassin, Dec., 1905; ag. dep. reg. of sup. ct., sheriff, mag., commr. of ct. of requests, reg. of marriages, and comm. of bd. of licensing justices, Malacca, Sept., 1907; ag. 3rd mag., Penang Dist., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1908; 3rd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1910.
ROBINSON, FREDERICK ALBERT.—B. 1875; Out Is. comanr. (4th div.), Bahamas, 1911.

ROBINSON, J. ALEX.—J.P. and notary pub. for the col. (Newfild.) ed. Vict. Coll., Jersey, and New Kingwood, Bath; prin. of Caribou gram. schl., 1883-92; elected for Bonavista, 1877; col. sec. of Newfild. and mem. of exec. com., 1897; accepted leadership of legis. com., Dec., 1897; May, 1898, returned to H.A. for dist. Trinity; mem. treasy. bd., 1889; M.L.C., 1910.

ROBINSON, JAMES REGINALD WINDHAM.—B. 1880; ed. at Marlborough and New Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mods., 1901; 3rd cls. lit. hist., 1903; apptd. after open exam., clk. in higher div., chief sec.'s office, Dublin, 18th Oct., 1901; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 14th Aug., 1905; priv. sec. to Sir J. Anderson, 5th Nov., 1914; sec. to veterinary comittee, 1907; sec. to deptlmt. comittee on W. African med. staff, 1909; jt. editor of C.O. List, 1913.

ROBINSON, MAJOR SIR THOMAS BULL, K.C.M.G. (1913), KT. BACH. (1910).—B. 1853; formerly major in command of 1st Queenslanders, and senior offr. in Cent. Queensland; man. and dir. of various public companies; J.T. Queensland, 1897; pres. of Rockhampton chamber of commerce; Queensland commr. for Franco-British exhibn.; agt.-gen. for Queensland, 1910.

ROBINSON, VICTOR GEORGE MILLOR (J.P., Natal).—Ast. teacher, ed. dep., Natal, 1st Feb., 1890; clk., Eshowe magistracy, 22nd Aug., 1892; gov'r.'s off., 1st Nov., 1892; treasy., 27th Oct., 1893; res. commr.'s off., 1st Oct., 1893; 1st cls., 27th Nov., 1897; 2nd cls. clk., chief mag.
ROBSON, WILLIAM.—Trained at Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew; curator, botanic station, Fontseret, Apr., 1905; hon. sec., permanent exhibit. comte.

ROBSON, WM. JOHN.—Ed. at Birkenhead schl. and Trinity coll., Camb.; served in S. Africa (Queen's medal and four clasps); chief audit clk. (in charge), Transvaal, 1st Nov., 1900; mt. aud.-gen., Apl., 1901; aud.-gen., 1st Sept., 1902; aud.-gen., B. Guiana, 13th May, 1908.

ROCHE, HON. WILLIAM JAMES, M.D., LL.D.—B. 1859; ed. pub. schl., Lucan, Ontario, and high schl., London, Ontario, Trinity med. coll., Toronto; grad. M.D., with 1st cl. hon., Western Univ., London, Ontario, 1883; 1st grad. in medicine of that Univ.; LL.D., 1911; removed to Minnedosa, Man., where he is a practising physician; territorial rep. for Manitoba med. jun., 1885-1901; has held highest offices in O.O.F., Manitoba; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1896; re-elec. g.e., 1900, 1904, 1908 and 1911; conservative whip for the West, 1901-1911; mem. of the privy coun. for Canada, and sec. of state Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; min. of the interior, 1912.

ROCK, JOHN CHRISTOPHER WILBERFORCE.—B. 1882; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Jesus Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; off. asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., June, 1905; Sabaragamuwa, Jan., 1908; pol. mag., Anuradhapura, Dec., 1909; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Kavakachcheri, Nov., 1911; ag. dist. judge, Angalla, Oct., 1912; ditto, Matara, Oct., 1913.
ROCKETT, E. D.—B. 1863; apptd., after exam., compt. exam., olk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Nov., 1880; sup't. of printing dept., 1894.
ROCKS, M. J. W.—Formerly clk. to various instr. commsrs., S. Leone; afterwards gaoler, Ambia, 1891-8; 1st clk. traff. branch, S. Leone ovt. rly., 1901.
RODD, A. J.—B. 1886; apptd., after exam., 1st library attendant, C.O., 11th Dec., 1905; library attendant, 18th Feb., 1911; supplementary clk., col. audit dept., Feb., 1914.

RODEN, ROBERT BLAIR.—B. 1860; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; clk., public library, Antigua, 1880; 2nd clk. to the gov., Leeward Is., 1881; acted on several occasions as ch. clk., and as clk. to the legis. and exec. coun., and as dist. mag., Antigua; also acted as priv. sec. to different govs., Leeward Is., in 1883, 1890, 1895 and 1896; mag., coroner and J.P., Nevis, 1890; mem. St. Kitts and Nevis legis. couns., 1891; mem. fed. leg. coun., Leeward Is., 1898-9; mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, 1903; ag. judge, petty debt k., Bridgetown, on several occasions; judge asst. k. of appeal, 1903-4, and from 1911; visiting justice, Glendairy priz., 1904; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, mem. of ex. and leg. couns., mem. of bd. of educn., and legal adviser to govt., June to Nov., 1909; chief just., B. Honduras, 1914.

RODESSE, MAURICE.—B. 1871; Priv. sec. to several puisne judges and ch. justices, S. Stlitims., from 20th May, 1889; acted as dep. regir. or as sheriff and dep. regir., Singapore, on several occasions since 1893; confirmed as sheriff and dep. regir., 1st Jan., 1902; ag. offl. assignee in addition to own duties, Sept. to Oct., 1907.
RODRIGO, EDMUND.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attached to Kurunegala Kachcheri, Mar., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Nov., 1913.
RODWELL, CECIL HUNTER, C.M.G. (1909).—Ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb., B.A., 1897; served on staff of high commr. for S. Africa since Dec., 1900; Imperial sec., Mar., 1904.
ROE, AUGUSTUS S.—Called to the bar, Perth, W. Australia, 1872; special commr., sup. ct., 1872; pol. mag., Perth, Oct., 1897.

ROGER, CAPT. ARCHIBALD, I.S.O. (1913).—Ensign 2nd Roy. Lanark militia, Aug., 1859; gazetted to the 2nd W. I. regt., Nov., 1860; lieut., Aug., 1861; capt., Oct., 1865; exchanged to the 1st batt., 17th regt., Feb., 1867; sold out of the service, Jan., 1870; was priv. sec. to Mr. Bayley, gov. of the Bahamas, Dec., 1860, to July, 1863, and fort adjt., Jamaica, Dec., 1863, to Sept., 1864; pol. mag. of the rural dist., St. Christopher, June, 1873; dist. mag., May, 1874; commr. of oaths, M.L.C., 1896 to 1900; ag. admstr., St. K.N., May, 1912, June, 1912 to Jan., 1913, and in April and June, 1913; mem. execo. coun., St. K.N., Feb., 1914.

ROGERS, HON. BENJAMIN.—B. 1837; M.L.C., P.E.I., 1878-1893; pres. of coun., 1890-93; mem. of amalgamated coun. and assem., 1893-97, 1900-04; mem. of several admstrs., and prov. sec., treas., and min. of agric., 1900-04; lieutenant-gov., P.E.I., 1910.

ROGERS, CLAUDE SOMERSET.—B. 1867; obtnd. higher standard certif. at Imp. Forest schl., Dehra Dun, India, with medals for forestry and forest engnrng., 1893; apptd. to Indian forest dept. as forest ranger, 26th May, 1893; promoted to extra asst. conserv. of forests, 4th grade, 26th Mar., 1894; passed exam. in Burmese language, higher standard, 1897; promoted to 3rd grade, 3rd Sept., 1897; 2nd grade, 27th Nov., 1899; forest officer, Trinidad, 24th Mar., 1901; capt. and adjutant, Trinidad Light Horse, 1908.
ROGERS, RICHARD EDWARD ELMORE.—Govt. printer and comptroller of stationery, S. Australia, 1st June, 1910.

ROGERS, HON. ROBERT.—B. 1864; ed. Lachute, Berthier and Montreal, Quebec; in business in Southern Manitoba for several years, and later at Kenora, Ontario; unsuc. cand. for H. of C. in Lisgar, Manitoba, at g.e. 1896; mem. of Manitoba legis., and min. of pub. works for 11 years; mem. of privy coun. and min. of interior in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; mem. of H. of C. for Winnipeg by acclamation, 1911; min. of pub. wks., 1912.

ROGERSON, WALTER JOHN LANCASHIRE, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1889; Cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attached to Kandy Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; ag. additional office asst., Anuradhapura Kach., Jan., 1914; ag. asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, May, 1914; addtl. office asst., Anuradhapura, June, 1914; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Kandy, June, 1914; ag. asst. govt. agt., Matale, Sept., 1914; office asst. to govt. agt., Badulla, Sept., 1914.

ROHRWEGER, FRANK, C.M.G. (1899)—B. 1859; ed. at Radley and Brasenose; called to the bar, Inn Tem., Hilary, 1885; joint author of "Parochial Assessment Rules"; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1894; ag. Queen's advoc., 1894; D.C.E., dist., 1895; pol. mag., 1896; resigned.

ROLANDO, FRANÇOIS ADOLPHE.—B. 1845; regiar. and offr. of civil status, Seychelles, 6th Jan., 1890; ag. judge and stip. mag., 17th July, 1891; ag. stip. mag., 17th Nov., 1891; Crown atty., Mauritius, 10th Sept., 1897; ag. recvr. of registn. dues and conservator of mortgages, 1st Oct., 1905.

ROOS, J. DE V., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1869; advoc. of sup. ct., Cape Colony; ditto, S.A.R.; attorney, etc., of sup. ct. of S.A.R.; sworn translator, sup. ct., Transvaal; sec. of law dept., Transvaal, Dec., 1908; sec., dept. of justice, Union of S. Africa, 1910.

ROSE, JAMES FREDERIC.—B.1853; Istcla.certd. schl. mast., B. Guiana; ast. mast., normal schl. for 2 years; clk. to inspr. of schls., July, 1873, to May, 1877; jun. educutnl. dist. offr., May, 1877, to 1882; sen. educutnl. offr., 1882 to 1891; 2nd ast. inspr. of schls., 1891 to 1903; acted as 1st ast. ditto on various occasions, 1902-1903; 1st ast. ditto, July, 1903; ag. chf. inspr., 11th Mar. to 13th Sept., 1905; 2nd to 25th Jan., 1907; and from 16th Nov., 1907, to 22nd July, 1908.

ROSEBY, THOMAS ERNEST.—B. 1868; ed. at Sydney Univ.; M.A.; honourman in French and German; senr. clk., Royal Mint, Sydney, 1883-1896; called to the bar, New S. Wales, 1896; also to high ct., of Australia; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1912; crown proser., N.S. Wales assizes, 1902-3; Brit. judge of joint ct. and judicial comnr., for the New Hebrides, Nov., 1908; thanked by S. of S. for memoranda on land suits, 1910; co-author of the joint ct. civ., criminal and land registration procedure codes.

ROSS, ALFRED ERNEST CLARENCE.—B. 1856; 2nd clk., surg.-gen.'s offr., Trinidad, 1875; clk. to dir. pub. wks., Feb., 1876; savings bk. clk., recvr.-gen.'s dept., Nov., 1880; acctnt., savings bk., Oct., 1882; sec. to trade and taxes comnr., 1886; acctnt., recvr.-gen.'s dept., May, 1889; ch. man., savings bk., Jan., 1901; P.M.G., Trinidad and Tobago, Sept., 1903; ag. collr. of cust., Mar. to Dec., 1908.

ROSS, CHARLES RICHARD.—Clk., P.O. savings bank, Cape, 1890; agric. dept., 1894; clk. and acctnt., office of conservator of forests, Midland Conservancy, Cape, 1895; dist. forest offr., George, Cape, 1897; Kokstad, Transkei, 1901; astt. conservator of forests, Transekan conservancy, 1907; transf. to King William's Town, 1910; conservator of forests, 2nd grade, Eastern conservancy, Cape, 1st Jan., 1912.
ROSS, GEORGE, I.S.O.—B. 1853; ent. civ. ser., Canada, 1875; apptd. ast. postmr., Toronto, 1900; ch. P.O. sub., Canada, 1902.
ROSS, HENRY TAYLOR, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1861; educ. Dalhousie and Acadia Univ.; called to the bar, Nova Scotia, 1889; astt. dep. min. of finance, 1906.

ROSS, PHILIP HEDGELAND.—Ed. at Christ's Coll., New Zealand, Otago Univ. and St. Bartholomew's hosp.; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., (Lond.), 1900; D.P.H.(Camb.),1902; bacteriologist, E. Africa and Uganda Prots., Aug., 1903.

ROSS, REGINALD JAMES BLAIR, B.A.—B. 1871; ed. at Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., Apr., 1894; dist. comnr., Lagos, Mar., 1898; ag. pol. mag. on various occasions in 1898, 1899 and 1900; ag. ast. col. sec., 1900; ag. res., Ibadan, Oct., 1901, to Sept., 1902; pol. mag., Jan., 1903; ag. atty.-gen., 1903, 1904 and 1905; ag. judge of sup. ct., Feb. to May, 1906; ag. atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, June to Oct., 1906; psuine judge, Cent. Prov., Oct. to Dec., 1906; again in 1907; comnr. of Niger lands, Cent. Prov., Apr., 1907; psuine judge, Nigeria, 1914.

ROSS, S. B. C.—Ed. Uppingham coll. (1889-93); Owens Coll., 1893-96; B.A., 1896; (Eng. lang., lit. honours); univ. schlr., 1896; prize for Roman Law, Constituti. Law, 1896; cadet, F.M.S., 1899; sent to Canton to stud. Chinese, 1900; transf. to Hong Kong, 1901; regiar., land ct., 1902 and 1903; ag. astt. postm., 1903; sec. to comnr. of inquiry into sup. ct. regist., 1903; ag. astt. P.M.G., 1903; P.M.G., 1903-4; ag. astt. col. sec., and clk. couns., 1904; ag. pol. mag., New Territories, 1905; ag. P.M.G., Apr., 1907; ag. astt. tax offr., New Territories, Tai Po, Aug., 1907; nat. land offr., N.T., 2nd July, 1908; ag. dist. offr., 6th Dec., 1908, to 1st Feb., 1909, and from 23rd Mar., 1909, to 3rd July, 1911; temporary serve as 2nd cls. clk. in C.O., from May to Dec., 1912; apptd. dist. offr., Tai Po, 30th Nov., 1912; returned to Hong Kong, Feb., 1913; ag. P.M.G., Feb. to Aug., 1913; assumed duty as dist. offr., Tai Po, 26th Aug., 1913.

ROSS, WILLIAM McGUIRE.—Ed. at Southampton Gram. schl. and Liverpool Univ.; M.S. (Hons. in engineering, Vict.), B.E., (Hons.) B.A. (R.U.I.), M.Inst.C.E.; astt. engineer, Uganda rly., 18th Apr., 1900; D.P.W., E. Africa Priv. 1st Apr., 1905.

ROTH, WALTER E.—B.A. (Oxon), late Denz of Magd. Coll.; M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.C.P. (Lond.); hon. mem. anthorp. societies of Beris and Florence, and of anthorp. inst., Lond.; late chief protector of aborigines, Queensland; astt. Royal comnr. to inquire into condition of natives of W. Aust.; stip. mag., dep. prot. of India; and govt. med. offr., Pomeron River, B.Guma 8th Dec., 1906.

ROTIMI, J. F. AMOSU.—Ed. Baptist elementary schl. and academy, Lagos; marine clk., Calabar, Oct., 1895; astt. regir. of ct., Calabar, June, 1896; dep. regir. of ct., Jan., 1899; trav. super. visor of native cts., S. Nigeria Prots., Sept., 1901, to Apr., 1907; ditto, E. Prov., S. Nigeria, Apr., 1907; has read, thanks of govt. for services in connection with reorganization of native courts, S. Nigeria, 1902-3.

ROUILLARD, JEAN EDMOND.—Ed. Roy. Coll. Maur., and King Henry VIII.'s schl., Coventry; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1891; licent. laws, Feb., 1891; ag. dist. mag., Plaines Wilhelms, Mar., Oct., 1899; ag. Crown pros., Feb., 1900; Crown pros., leg. advr., pol. mag., etc., Seychelles, 4th Sept., 1900; mag., Rodrigues, July, 1902; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1913.

ROUND, FRANCIS R., M.A., C.M.G. (1871—B. 1845; ed. at Marl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford, where he obtained a double 2nd class in mods. and a 2nd in classics in final exam., 1868; apptd. after a compet. exam., clk. in the office of the S. of the Cols., Feb., 1869; 2nd class elk., Sept., 1872; accompanied Sir P. Julyan to Mauritius in 1873 as sec. on special mission of inquiry into the crit. estabmt. and gen. expenditure of that col.; astt. priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon Sir Michael Hicks-Beach, Mar., 1878; priv. sec., 1st Apr., to 27th Apr., 1880; priv. sec. to Mr. Herbert, Jan., 1881; 1st class clk., and financial clk., Dec., 1881; on specs. service to Br. Bechuanaland and Mauritius, 1886.

ROUSSEAU, JAMES TOD, M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1860; ent. civ. ser., 1882; asst. sub-intendant crown lands, Trinidad, 1897; magistrate and warden of Tobago, 1899; sub-intendant of crown lands, 1903; mag. and warden, Tobago, 1904.

ROUTH, REGINALD LAMY.—B. 1856; ed. St. Mary's Hall Southsea, and Cadet school; frigate, H.M.S. "Conway"; extra master's certif., 1893; master of govt. vessels, Lagos, Apr., 1897; asst. marine supt., Lagos, Dec., 1902; ag. marine supt., 1903, to June, 1904; comdr. H.M.C.S.Y., "Ivy," S. Nigeria, June, 1906; sen. marine ftr., E. Prov., S. Nigeria, May, 1907.

ROWBOTHAM, HERBERT W., Hon. LL.D., t. Joseph's Coll., Rome, 1912.—B. 1868; served with Cape mounted pol., 1890; mem. (student) of inst. of actuaries, 1893; Rhodesian civ. ser., 1896; (medal for rebellion of 1896-1897); served as an officer in field and on staff, S. African war, 1899-1902 (two medals and five clasps); Transvaal civ. ser., 1903; clk. to atty.-gen., B. Honduras, 1910; acnt. and traffic ast., Stann Creek rly., B. Honduras, Sept., 1910.

ROWE, CHARLES FREDERICK.—Lieut. 1st V.B. royal War. regt., 1896-98; Natal pol., 1898-99; served throughout S. Africa war, in Imp. Light Horse, 1899-1900, and in S. African constab., 1900-1903; asst. Oct., 1901; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 4th May, 1904; 3rd cls. res., Apl., 1905; 2nd cls. res., 1911.

ROWELL, THOMAS IRVINE, C.M.G. (1890), M.D., C.M. (Aberdeen).—B. 1841; prin. civ. med. dir., S. Stiltsm., July, 1877; and health offr., port Singapore, J.P.; acted as col. surg. on several occasions from May, 1868, to Mar., 1876; accompanied Sir W. Robinson on mission to invest King of Siam with G.C.M.G., Nov., 1878; regis. of births and deaths, Dec., 1882, and of Mohammedan marriages, Dec., 1883; pres. fisheries inquiry comm., 1887; pres. Singapore mun. coun., 1888-9; et, 1890.
ROWLAND, J. W., C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1852; ent. col. surg., G. Coast Col., May, 1880; dist. commr., Lagos, 1887; col. surg., 1887; ret., 1897.

ROWLEY, THOS. WM.—B. 1859; ed. Cheltenham coll.; 2nd lieut., 2nd L.I. Regt., Roy. Guernsey Militia; attached to 105th Regt., L.I., at Guernsey, for two trainings, and passed school; lieut., Mar., 1879; passed prelim. exam., R.M.C., Sandhurst, Apr., 1880, and qualifying exam. for line, July, 1881; joined armed constab., N.Z., 1885; transf. to mtd. pol., Jan., 1886; and to N.Z. Artill., June, 1886; resign., Aug., 1888; inspr. of roads, Perak, Nov., 1889; asst. coll. land rev., mag. and J.P., Perak, Dec., 1890; ag. mag., Matang, June, 1892; asst. dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1893; ag. dist. mag., Matang, Jan., 1894; ag. upt. of prisons, Apr., 1895; asst. mag., Tanjong Balini, 1898; asst. commr. of pol., Selangor, 1899; ditto, Perak, Mar., 1900; dist. treas. for different districts, Jan., 1903, to Feb., 1904; test. dist. offr., Matang, Apr., 1905; mag., Taiping, Apr., 1906; dist. offr., coast, Negri Sembilan, Jan., 1907.

ROXBURGH, T. LAURENCE, C.M.G. (1910).—Ed. Edin. Univ.; clk. petty sess., St. Eliz., Jamaica, st. Jan., 1882; clk. of cts., 1st Apr., 1888; 1st class clk., col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, Jan., 1890; sen. clk., 1899; asst. col. sec., 2nd Jan., 1902; acted as col. sec., Jamaica, 29th Mar. to 10th Apr., 1902; 2nd July to 21st Nov., 1902; 2nd June to 10th Sept., 1903; Jan. to Mar., 1904; 25th May to 9th June, 1904; and 9th June to 10th Oct., 1906; was a major in Jamaica militia, and was Hon. A.D.C. to Gov. Sir A. Hemming, 1898 to 1904; also acted as priv. sec. to Sir A. Hemming, Mar. to Aug., 1901; admstr., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1906.
RUDALL, SAMUEL BRUCE.—Mem. House of Assembly, S. Australia, 1906; chmn. of comtees. 1912.

RUGGLES, NEFEAN CLARKE, K.C.—Barrister of sup. ct., Nova Scotia; Bachelor of Laws at Dalhousie Coll., Halifax, Canada, with honours, 1891; called to the bar, Nova Scotia, with highest honours, 1891; K.C., Nova Scotia, Jan., 1911; served with the Canadian forces during S. African War; now on the reserve of officers, Canadian militia; public prosecutor at Barberton, Transvaal, 1901; asst. res. mag., 1903 to 1907; mag. Dist. F., Dominica, Oct., 1909.

RUNDLE, GEN. SIR HENRY MACLEOD LESLIE, R.A.—B. 1856; G.C.B. (1911); G.C.M.G. (1914); G.C.V.O. (1911); K.C.B., 1898; K.C.M.G., 1901; D.S.O., 1897; ed. Roy. Mil. Acad., Woolwich; entd. R.A., 1876; maj.-gen., 1896; served in Zulu war, 1879; Boer war, 1881; Egyptian war, 1882; Nile expdn., 1884-5; Soudan Frontier field force, 1885-7; Soudan Frontier, 1889; Dongola expdttn. force, 1896; adjt.-gen., Egyptian army; Khartoum expdn., 1898; comdg. S.E. Dist., 1898-9; dep. adjt.-gen. to the forces, 1899-1900; in comd. of div., Aldershot, 1900; lieut.-gen. on staff in comd. of 8th Div., S. African field force, 1900-1902; lieut.-gen. of Nthn. Comd., 1905-7; col.-comdt., R.A., 1907; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Malta, 1909-1915.

RUSHMER, JAMES.—B. 1876; entered C.O., Oct., 1893; apptd., after exam., library attendant, 1st July, 1896; promoted, after exam., under order-in-council of 1910, clk. of the 2nd div., 18th Feb., 1911.
RUSHTON, CHARLES BERKELEY.—Ck. comsnr. of rlys. dept., W. Aust., Apr., 1896; ch. clk., July, 1896; ag. sec. to comsnr. of rlys., 23rd Aug., 1901; sec. to ditto, 18th Dec., 1901.
RUSSELL, ALEXANDER DAVID, LL.B.—Puise judge, Trinidad, 6th Sept., 1906; 1st puisne judge, 1913; ag. chief just. in 1910 and 1912.

RUSSELL, HON. BENJAMIN, K.C., M.A., D.C.L.—Ed. at Halifax gram. schl. and Mount Allison Univ.; called to the bar, 1872; for some years law adviser to legia. coun., Nova Scotia; elec. to H. of C. Canada, 1896; judge of supreme ct., Nova Scotia, 1904.
RUSSELL, E. N.—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.B., Bac. surg.; med. offr., E.A.P., Oct., 1913.

RUSSELL, JAMES GEORGE, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1848; master of the sup. ct., S. Australia, Dec., 1878; regis. of V.-A.ct., of companies, of trades unions, 1878; comsnr. of inland rev. and ag. regis. of probates, 1884; comsnr. of taxes, 1884; of stamps, 1886; coman. of insolveney, 1889; special mag. local ct., Adelaide, 1889; pres. of state bd. of conciliation, 1895; ag. judge of sup. ct., Mar. to Oct., 1897.

RUSSELL, JOHN WILLIAM PEMBERTON.—Ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey; subaltern, 3rd Batt. East Surrey Regt., 1887; asst. transport offr., Uganda Prot., 21st Aug., 1899; chief of customs, 1st Apr., 1904; director, Uganda transport, 1st July, 1905; Nandi medal, 1905; recd. badge of honour from H.H. the Duke of Anhalt, 1909.
RUSSELL, THOMAS BROWNLEE.—B. 1873; ed. Pembroke Coll., Oxford, M.A.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1897; office asst. to govt. agt., E.Prov., Mar., 1899; pol. mag., Balapitiya, Nov.,
RYAN, H. S. K.—Town mag., E. Afr. Prot., Oct., 1913.
SABINE, GUSTAVUS.—Ch. govt. surr., Papua, 30th Aug., 1908.
SACHSE, HON. ARTHUR OTTO, C.E.—M.L.C. for N.E. Prov., Victoria; min. of pub. inst., Sept., 1903.

SADLER, Sir James Hayes, K.C.M.G.(1857), C.B. (1902).—Lieut.-Col. Indian Staff Corps late foreign dept., govt. of India; awarded degree of honour, with gold medal and diploma of govt. of India, in Persian, Nov., 1879; Urdu, Oct., 1884, and Arabic, Dec., 1890; 1st comm., 6th July, 1870; served with 61st Foot in Canada and Ireland, and 40th Foot and 33rd Regt. Native Infantry in India; apptd. to polit. dept. in India, July, 1877; asst. agt. to govt. in Baroda, May, 1881; asst. sec. legis. dept., July to Oct., 1886; Aug. to Oct., 1888, and Apr., 1890; polit. agt. with the ex-Amir of Afghanistan, Apr., 1891; agt., Kotal & Jhalawar, July, 1891; on spec. duty with Pin Damong of Siam, Feb., 1892; polit. agt. and consul at Muscat, Nov., 1892; ag. res. and cons. gen., Persian Gulf, June, 1893; asst. sec. gen. of India, foreign dept., Apr., 1895; polit. agt. and consul, Somaliland Prot., Aug., 1898; ret. from Indian ser., Mar., 1899; continued to be employed as consul-gen., Somaliland, till 1901; comsnr., Uganda Prot., Dec., 1901; comm. and comdr.-in-chief, E. Africa Prot., 11th Dec., 1905; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, R. Africa Prot., 9th Nov., 1906; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Wazeward Is., 6th May, 1909; retired, 1914.

SAINTSBURY, John Seymour.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1906; 1st cls. in Evidence Procedure and Criminal Law, bar exam., 1894; certif. of honour from council of legal exam. at Bar Final, 1905, and a prize of 50L, from them; passed civ. serv. test exam., Barbados, 1892; supernumerary, ool. sec.'s off., 1883; in P.O.; 7th clk., P.O., June, 1886; 4th clk., aud. of Sept., 1890; offr. of cust., Dec., 1890; inh. rev. offr., Oct., 1905; ag. pol. mag., dist. "A," July to Nov., 1906; ag. pol. mag., dist. "A," June to Nov., 1911, and from Apr., 1912 to Feb., 1913; ag. judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., Nov. June to 31st Oct., 1913.
SALKEID, ROBERT EDWARD.—Late Oxfordshire Light Infantry, compy. comdr., 3rd K.A.R.; ollr., E. Africa Prot., 12th Jan., 1906.

SALMON, ROBT. HUGH.—Apptd. 3rd asst., Vasaland Prot., Dec., 1898; transf. to treasy.; nd asst. treas., Jan., 1901; 1st asst. treas., Dec., 908; dep. treas., Apr., 1910; ag. treas., Apr., 1910 nd Dec., 1912.
SALMON, T. G. A.—Asst. dist. comanr., S.A.P., Dec., 1913.
SALMOND, JOHN WILLIAM, M.A., L.L.B., C.C.—B. 1862; ed. at Univ. of New Zealand and Univ. of London; parly. draftsman, New Zealand, 1907-1909; solr.-gen., 1910.

SAMUEL, SILVERIO L.—Ed. at St. Gregory's tom. Cath. gram. schl., Lagos; messr. and copyt., dua. off., Oct., 1897; clk. to inspr. of schls., lar., 1899; sec. to comte. for exhibtn. of schl. rks., 1902 and 1903; clk. and interpreter to trav. omanr., Ilesha interior dept., Feb., 1904; 3rd ls. clk. govr's off., Aug., 1904.
SANDESON, G. M.—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., med. offr., Nvasaland, 1910.

SANDESON, JAMES.—Clk. to ch. just., Barbados, 1867; clk. to gen. bd. of health, Barbados, Apr., 1871, also clk. to quarantine bd. in 1880; solr., itty., and proctor, Barbados, in 1873; was deleg. or Barbados to quarantine confce. at Demerara in 888; govt. dir. on bd. of Barbatos rly. co. in 1891; omanr. of probates, 1904; J.F., 1904.
SANDESON, L.—Sec. to comanr., Somaliland, August, 1911.
SANDESON, STANLEY.—3rd treasy. asst., S. Africa Prot., 1st Oct., 1901; supt. of inland ev. and conservancy, 6th Feb., 1903.

SARWAR, HAFIZ GHULAM.—Cadet, S. S., Sept., 1897; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Jan. to June, 1900; and from Sept., 1900, to Nov., 1901; passed final exam., Chinese, July, 1900; 3rd mag., Sing., 29th Apr., 1902; ag. sheriff, deputy-regis., and asst. official assignee, Penang, May, 1902; lep. regist., sup. ct., Penang, Dec., 1905; asst. regist., sup. ct., Malacca, May, 1907; dep. regist., sup. ct., Penang, Apr., 1908; ag. regist., July, 1909.

SAUNDERS, CHARLES JAMES, B.A.—B. 1868; ed. Merchant Taylors and St. John's Coll., Oxon; cadet, S. S., 1891; passed final exam. in Chin., Feb., 1895; ag. 3rd asst. prot. of Chin., Feb., 1895; ag. 3rd mag., Sing., May, 1895; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Dec., 1896; ag. dist. offr., Penang, Mar., 1897; dist. offr., Malacca, May, 1897; ag. asst. prot. of Chin., Sing., Aug., 1897; ag. asst. prot. of Chin., and supt. of Ind. imigrn., Penang, and munic. comanr. for Penang, 1898; ag. asst. prot. of Chin., Sing., Aug., 1898, to Feb., 1899; also ag. 3rd mag., Sing., Sept., 1898; 2nd asst. prot. of Chin., Penang, Aug., 1898; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., May, 1900; ag. offl. assignee, S. S., Sept. to Dec., 1900, Jan., 1901, to Feb., 1905, and Feb., 1906; sheriff, dep. regis., supreme court, and asst. official assignee, Penang, May, 1902; munic. comanr. for Sing., Oct., 1900, and Sept., 1903; also ag. prot. of Chin., Sing., Apr., 1904; also asst. supt. of Indian imigrts.; asst. prot. of Chin., Penang, May, 1904; ag. sec. for Chin. affairs, S. S. and F. M.S., Apr., 1907; ag. comanr., et. of requests, Singapore, Jan., 1908; ag. dist. judge, Apr., 1908; sec. for Chinese affairs, S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1910.

SAUNDERS, SIR CHARLES JAMES RENAULT, K.C.M.G., 1906. C.M.G., 1895.—B. 1857; held various apptmts. in Natal civ. ser., 1876-87, when he ratified treaty with Queen of Amatongaland (desp.); res. mag., Eshowe, and J.P. for Zululand, 1888; defined bdy. between Amatonga land and Zululand, 1889 (desps.); variously employed under Zululand Govt., 1888-95 (desps.), when he was apptd. spec. comanr. for Trans Pongoland territory, carried out final act of annexation of that territory and declared British prot. for Amatongaland; acted as res. comanr. and chief mag. of Zululand, 1896; apptd. chief mag. and civ. comanr. for prov. of Zululand on its incorporation with Natal, 1897; medal, S. African War, 1899-1902; col. representative on Zululand Lands Delimitation Comsn., 1902, and comanr. for native affairs, 1905; ret., 1909.

SAUNDERS, JAMES BERNARD.—B. 1852; harbrmr., Trinidad, Sept., 1894; also J.P. and mag. under harb. and drogher ordcs., regist. of births and deaths for the waters of the Col., coroner under harbrs. ordcs., supt. of lighthouses, mem. of quar. authority, comanr. of piloting, and supt. of mercantile marine.

SAUNDERS, REGINALD G.—Ed. at Tonbridge and Cheltenham Coll.; ag. 3rd asst. P.M.C., Ceylon, 13th Jan., 1898; ag. asst. collr. of cust., Trincomalee, Sept., 1898; office assist. to govt. agt., N. Prov., and asst. collr. of cust., 1st June, 1901; pol. mag., Matara, 22nd Apr., 1903; extra asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., 1st Mar., 1904; ag. asst. govt. agt., Maanar, 2nd May, 1904; comanr. of requests and pol. mag., Chilaw, 12th Feb., 1906; ag. asst. govt. agt. and dist. judge, 11th Apr., 1906; ag. dist. judge, Chilaw and Puttalam, Jan., 1909; ag. pol. mag., Hatton-Nuwara Eliya, Feb., 1909, and dist. judge, May, 1909; office asst., Colombo Kachcheri, Nov., 1911; lieut., Ceylon Light Infantry.
SAUVAGE, DANIEL.—Govt. pilot and asst. port offr., Seychelles, Feb., 1894, and port offr., May, 1897.
SAVI, VICTOR GEORGE.—Pol. probationer, S. Stlitims, Nov., 1904; asst. supt. of pol., Jan., 1908.

SAXTON, GEORGE SHADWELL—B. 1858; ed. at Clifton Coll.; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., 1878; pol. mag. Haputale, 1887; asst. govt. agent, Mannar, 1887; Chilaw, 1888; dist. judge, Tangalla, 1890; asst. govt. agent, Matale, 1890; Matara, 1899; Kalutara, 1902; govt. agent, Sabaragamawwa, 1902; dist. judge, Matara, Nov.. 1906: gov't. agent, Anuradhapura, Dec., 1906; ditto, Kurunegala, Oct., 1907; ditto, W. Prov., and mem. exec. and legis. couns., 3rd Oct., 1911; ditto, Cent. Prov., ceasing to be M.E.C., 3rd Jan., 1912.

SAYER, GEOFFREY ROBLEY, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. at Highgate schl. (foundationer) and Queen's Coll., Oxford; open exhibitioner in classics and Fitzgerald exhibitioner; 2nd cls. class. mods.; 3rd cls. Lit. Hum. (B.A., 1910); cadet, Hong Kong, 24th Oct., 1910; passed in Cantonese, Nov., 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr. (South), Nov., 1912 to Feb., 1913; and from Mar. to Nov., 1913; ag. asst. supt. of police, 22nd Nov., 1913.

SAYER, W. F.—Ed. Univ. Coll. Schol., Lond.; solr., 1879; bar and solr. in W. Australia, 1891; sec. law dept., Mar., 1897; ag. commr. of titles, Mar., 1899; atty.-gen., 1901; parly. draftsman, 1902; crown solr., Dec., 1902; atty.-gen., 1904.
SCADDAN, HON. JOHN, M.L.A.—B. 1876; leader of Parly. Lab. party in W. Aust. since Aug., 1910; elected for Ivanhoe, June, 1904; premier of W. Australia, 1911.

SCHOCHE, HERMAN EUGENE.—Survr.'s asst., Cape, 1878-1883; survr., Cape and Transvaal, 1889 to 1902; examr. of diagrams, survr.-gen.'s dept., Transvaal, 1st Apr., 1902; registry survr., 1st Dec., 1902; asst. survr.-gen., 1st May, 1905; asst. survr.-gen., 1st Apr., 1912; survr.-gen., Transvaal, 1st Apr., 1913.

SCHOFIELD, WALTER.—B. 1888; ed. Univ. of Liverpool, M.A.; non-coll. student, Oxford, 1908-1911, B.A.; apptd. cadet, Hong Kong civ. ser., 17th Nov., 1911; attached to secretariat for Chinese affairs, 19th Mar., 1914.

SCHOLEFIELD, WILFRED JOCELYN STUART.—Bechuanaland Border pol., Jan., 1891; line mag., Bechuanaland rly. construction, 1st Feb., 1896; res. mag., Ngamiland, 22nd Jan., 1897; native commr., N.W. Transvaal, 22nd June, 1902; served in Matabele campaign, 1893; Matabele rebellion, 1896; S. Africa, siege of Mafeking, etc.; A.D.C. to Lieut.-Gen. Lord Methuen, etc.; comdt., North. Prot., and lines of communication, 1st Mar., 1902; mentioned in despatches; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 23rd June, 1906; dist. commr., 1910.

SCHOMBURGK, OTTO HEINRICH.—Clk., engrn.-in-chief's office, S. Aust., Jan., 1874; clk., ch. sec.'s office, Aug., 1877; clk., regis.-gen. of deeds office, July, 1880; clk., sheriff's office, Nov., 1882; dep. sheriff, 1891; also returning offr. W. Adelaide, Jan., 1893, to June, 1902; sheriff, July, 1903; also Commonwealth electoral offr., for the state of S. Aust., Aug., 1903; major, comdg. field batt. of artillery (retired).

SCHRADER, LOUIS WILLIAM CONRAD.—B. 1873; ed. Highgate School and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1896; off. asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, April, 1897; pol. mag., Galagedara, July, 1899; off. asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Nov., 1899; N. C. Prov., Mar., 1901; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1901; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Jan., 1903; asst. coll. of cust., Trincomalee, Dec., 1904; dist. judge, Tangalla, Feb., 1905; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, April, 1907; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1909; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Aug., 1912; dist. judge, Matara, May, 1913; commr. of requests, Colombo, Oct., 1913; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Feb., 1914; dist. judge, Kalutara, June, 1914.

SCHREIBER, COLLINGWOOD, C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1831; emigrated from England, 1852; entered service of Hamilton and Toronto Rly., and became ast. engrnr., 1852; suptdng. engrnr. of W.N. Rly. of Canada, 1860-64; dir. engrnr., No. Scotia govt. service, on Truro and Picton Rly., 1864-5; survey engrnr., Dominion govt. service, inter-col. rly., 1886; suptdng. engrnr., N. Brunswick E. Rly., 1869-71; ditto, inter-col. railway, 1871-3; ch. engrnr. of govt. railways in operation, 1873-8; also gen. man., 1878-80; also engrnr.-chief, Canadian Pacific Rly., 1880; engrnr.-in-chief, rly. and dep. min. of rlys. and canals, 1892; gen. consulting engrnr., 1905.

SCHREINER, THE HON. W. P., C.M.G. (1899).—K.C.—B. 1857; ed. S. A. Coll., Capetown, Car. Univ., and London; advoc. of sup. ct., Cape; legal adviser to high commr., 1887-93; atty.-gen. in Mr. Rhodes's ministry, 1893; mem. of James Raid Comm.tee; premier and col. sec., 1888-1901; M.L.A., Queenstown, 1908; high commr. London of the Union of S. Africa, 1914.

SCIORTINO, J. C.—B. 1875; ed. at Lyons Univ., Malta; in service of telegraph company, 1891-1903; rev. offr., N. Nigeria, 26th Sept., 1913; title altered to asst. repr., 1904; 3rd cls. rep., 25th Apr., 1907; 2nd cls. rep., 15th June, 1910.
SCOFFHAM, W. H.—B. 1883; apptd. site compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service, assigned to census office, 20th Feb., 1910; transf'd to C.O., 17th Dec., 1902.

SCONGE, HERBERT W.—Ed. at Hurst Coll., Sussex; Gram. Schol., Bristol; Trin. Coll., Cam. B.A. math. tripos, 1886; M.A. 1897; called to bar, Inner Temple, July, 1908; asst. master, Faversham Gram. Schol.; Godolphin Schol., Hammersmith, and Grocers' Co.'s Schol., Hackney, to 1893; asst. mnr., Queen's Coll., Br. Garth, Apr., 1893; asst. inspr. of schls., July, 1893; ag. inspr., May to Nov., 1898; June to Nov., July, 1902, to Apr., 1903; inspr. of schls., offl. visitor of Onderneemind Indust. Schol., 1903; dir. of primary educn., Sept., 1914; ass't bd. of agric. for educntl. matters; exam. prelim. exam. for govt. clerical serv.

SCOTT, D'ARCY—B. 1872; ed. Ottawa schl. and univ., and Osgoode Hall, Toronto; barr. at law; practised profession in Ottawa for seven years; mayor of Ottawa, 1907-08; ass't chief, rly. commr., Canada, 1908.
SCOTT, HON. DAVID LYNCH, K.C.—B. 1846; ed., Gram. Schol., Brampton, Ont.; called to bar, Ontario, 1870; K.C., 1885; judge of supreme ct., N.W.T., 1894, and of supreme ct. of Alberta, 1907.

SCOTT, DUNCAN CAMPBELL, B.E. 1862; apptd. to Indian commr.'s off., Canada, 1879; jun. 2nd cl. clk., dept. of India affairs, 1880; promoted to sen. 2nd cl., 1883; int. cl., 1889; ch. clkship., 1893; ch. acctnt. and engt. of Indian educn., 1908; deputy supt.-gen. of Indian affairs, 1913.
SCOTT, HERBERT SEPTIMUS.—B. 1875; ed. at Eton and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1896; inspr. of schools, Transvaal, 1902; examr., sec. to educnt. dept., Transvaal, 1911.

SCOTT, JOHN.—B. 1878; ed. Bath Coll. and King's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Carib. civ. ser., Nov., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the off. admnig. the govt., Nov., 1903; off. ast. govt. agt., N. Prov., Jan., 1904; ast. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1905; Munnar, Apr., 1907; ast. govt. agt., Munnar, Feb., 1910; dist. judge, Negombo, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Chilaw and Puttalam, Mar., 1913; ast. govt. agt., Chilaw and Puttalam, Sept., 1913; addtl. ast. col. sec., Aug. 1914.

SCOTT, J. H.—Mag., Tembuland, Aug., 1876; capt. in Tembu levies, Sept., 1877; ag. ch. mag., Tembuland, Apr. to July, 1878; ch. elk., native affairs dept., Oct., 1878; sec. to atty.-gen., on special duty to N. Border during war there, Feb. to Apr., 1879; ag. special comsnr., N. Border, May to July, 1879; special comsnr. and special mag. of N. Border, July, 1879; res. comsnr., Pondo-land, July, 1888.

SCOTT, RALPH.—B. 1874; B.A. Lond. Univ.; adet, S.S., Nov., 1895; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Mar., 1897; passed final exam. in Malay, Sept., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Sept., 1897; confirmed, Aug., 1898; apptd. to Cls. V., Jan., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Apr., 1903; attached to land off., Sing., on spec. duty, July, 1905; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Jan., 1906; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1906; ag. comsnr. of ct. of requests, Singapore, July, 1906, to Feb., 1907; dist. offr., Dindings, Aug., 1906; collr. of land rev., Penang, Feb., 1907; ag. comsnr. of ct. of requests, Singapore, Dec., 1907; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Jan., 1908; ag. ollr. of land rev., Malacca, Oct., 1908; in charge of sttlmt., Feb. to May, 1909, and from Feb. to July, 1910; assumed duties as collr. of land rev., Penang, July, 1910; ag. senr. dist. offr., Prov. Vellesley, Feb., 1911; promoted to cls. ii., Dec., 1913.

SCOTT, THOMAS.—Ck., audit office, Transvaal, 18th Feb., 1901; inspr., 1st Jan., 1904; chief ck., revenue div., treasy., 1st Oct., 1904; revenue inspr., 1st Mar., 1905; prin. ck., audit office, 1st Dec., 1908; chief inspr. of expendi-ture audit, control and audit office, Union of Africa, 1st Apr., 1912.

SCOTT, HON. WALTER.—B. 1867; ed. pub. hls., Middlesex Co., Ontario; journalist; elec. H. of C., Canada, 1900 and 1904; resig. 1905, and sworn first pres. of coun. for Sask. and win. pub. wks.; elec. to Sask. legis. assem. for Ramsden dist., 13th Dec., 1905; and for Swift current dist., 14th Aug., 1908; re-elec., 1912.

SCOTT, WALTER.—B. 1862; apptd., after compet. exam., ck. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., 2nd Jan., 1883; promoted to higher grade, 2nd div., 3rd Nov., 1896; staff ck., first grade, 311.
SCOTT, WALTER DARE.—B. 1870; ch. ck., In Selangor, F.M.S., 1891; ast. dist. offr., Kuala Lumpur, 1893; ast. dist. offr., Krian, 1903; dist. offr., Raub, 1905; British agt., rengganai, 1910.

SCOTT, WM. ALEXANDER.—B. 1871; ck. to ty.-gen., Fiji, 15th Oct., 1888; ag. stip. mag., avua and Nadroga, tax inspr., Oct., 1897; stip. ag. and ast. comsnr., Colo West, 1898; chief k., native dept., and stip. mag., Kadavu, 1899; ov. inspr., Kadavu and Lomaiviti, 1901; ag. ip. mag., Rewa, and comsnr., Naitasiri, 1902; stip. mag., Navua, and comsnr., Namosi, 1903; ast. native comsnr., 1904; despatched to christchurch exhibition in charge of body of ijian natives, 1906-7; ag. chief pol. mag. and fl. recvr. in bankruptcy, 1907; dep. native comsnr., 1908; 1st grade stip. mag., Lautoka, id gov'ts comsnr., Ba, Oct., 1912.
SCOTT, WILLIAM DUNCAN.—B. 1861; comsnr. r Canada to the Paris Exhibn., 1900; supt. of immigr., 1903.
SCROGGS, Lieut. HAROLD CHAS., R.N.—Harbour mast. and supt. of merc. marine, Mauritius, 23rd June, 1903; also ag. collr. of customs, 1910.
SEALY, FRANK DEAN.—Supernum. elk., immigr. dept., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1884; 4th class elk., Apr., 1891; 3rd class, Jan., 1894; 2nd class, Apr., 1895; immigr. agt., Oct., 1898.

SEALY, GORDON COURLAND MILLER.—B. 1863; elk. to the atty.-gen., Tobago, Jan., 1882; elk. in col. sec.'s dept., 1883; acted as confid. elk. to the admstr. and elk. of couns., 1883 to 1886; 3rd elk., surg.-gen.'s dept., Br. Guiana, 1887; 2nd elk., 1887; has acted as ch. elk.; 4th class elk., treasury dept., 1st June, 1901; 3rd class elk., ditto, 14th May, 1906.

SEALY, JAMES ERNEST CAMERON.—B. 1871; 2nd elk., audit and registry dept., Tobago, Feb., 1888; elk. to comsners. and financial bd., Nov., 1890, and Oct., 1893; sec. to bd. of educn., Sept., 1892; ch. elk. treasy., June, 1893; postmr. of Tobago, Dec., 1895; elk. to sub-recvr., Tobago, Jan., 1899; elk. to warden, Tobago, Sept., 1899; ch. elk. and cashier, treasy., Tobago, Feb., 1904.

SEARCY, ARTHUR.—Boarding offr., cust., S. Aust., 1873; elk., cust., 1874; elk., audit offr., 1875; elk., cust., 1876; tide inspir. and measurer of ships, cust., 1889; correspdg. elk., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1890; ag. dep. comsnr. of taxes and stamps, 1891; correspdg. elk., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1891; sec., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1894; elk. asst. and sergeant-at-arms, H. of A., 1894; dep. comsnr. of taxes and stamps, Jan., 1896, to Mar., 1897; comsnr., ditto, Mar. to Oct., 1897; dep. comsnr., ditto, Oct., 1897, to Feb., 1911; also pres., marine bd., 1902; also inspir. of kerosene, 1902; comsnr. of trade marks and registr. of copyrights, 1904-7; also controller, outer harbour, Port Adelaide, Nov., 1907; also controller, ocean steamers wharf, Apl., 1909; also chief inspir. under inflammable Oils Act, June, 1909; also supt. of life-saving service, Aug., 1909; also controller of harbours, July, 1911; also chmn. of harbs. bd., 1914.
SEARLE, HON. MALCOLM WILLIAM, K.C.—Puisne judge of sup. ct. of S. Africa (Cape Prov. Div.), June, 1910.
SEATON, G. L.—B. 1860; apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 17th May, 1899; 2nd cl., 18th Feb., 1900; King's mess., 16th June, 1904.

SEELY, Rt. Hon. Col. JOHN EDWARD BERNARD, P.C. (1909). D.S.O. (1900).—Ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; lieut.-col. commdg. Hampshire Carabiniers; served with I.Y. in S. Africa, 1900-1901 (deeps., Queen's medal, five clasps, D.S.O.); M.P. for Isle of Wight, 1900-06; M.P. for Abercromby div., Liverpool, 1906-1910; M.P., Ilkeston div., Derbyshire, 1910; ree. gold medal (French govt.) for saving life at sea, 1891; parly. under-sec. of state for the colonies, 1908; chmn. of comte. to inquire into organisation of crown agents office, 1908; under-sec. of state for war, 1911; sec. of state for war, June, 1912.

SEIGNORET, CLARENCE AUGUSTUS.—B. 1867; supernumerary treas., Dominica, 1890; sec. to special yawm coms., Mar., 1891, to Sept., 1892; ch. elk., reg.'s office and elk. pub. market, 1892; ast. sec. to Sir R. Hamilton during his inquiry in Dominica, 1893; govt. offr., treasy., 1895; sec. bd. of health and quarantine, 1895; ag. elk., admstr.'s office and exec. coun., 1895; ag. treas., Dominica, July, 1901; harbrmr., 1906; survr. of shipping, 1907; ag. treas., Sept., 1910, to Sept., 1911.
SIGNORET, G. B.—Cik. to postm., Dominica, 1889; govt. offr., treasy., 1893; ch. clk., regist. offr., 1895; ag. postm., 1896-7; clk. govt. offr., Roseau, 1897; govt. offr., treasy., 1901.

SELBORNE, 2nd Earl of (creat. 1882), Viscount Wolmer, Baron Selborne (U.K.), K.G. (1909), P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1905), The Rt. Hon. Wm. Waldegrave Palmer—B. 1830; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. hist.), 1882; asst. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-85; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1888-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-95; succeeded his father, 1895; Under S. of S. for Cola., June, 1895; chmn., Pacific cable comtce., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comtce., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov. 12th, 1900; high commr. for S. Africa, and gov. and comdr.-in-chief of Transvaal and O.R.C., 1905 to 1910; ceased to be gov. of O.R.C. on grant of resp. govt., June, 1907.
SELDON, A. A.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913.

SELLS, H. C., B.A. (Oxon.)—Cadet, S. Stltmte., Oct., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1898 to Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, Jan., 1900; passed fin. exam in Malay, Apr., 1900; ag. dist. offr., prov. Well., May, 1901; supt., money order branch and govt. savings bank, Sing., May, 1902; head of Malay coll., Malacca, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Apr., 1905; ag. asst. postm.-gen., Penang, Nov., 1906; confirmed, May, 1907; ag. postm.-gen., Apr. to Dec., 1909.

SEMPER, D. H.—Cik. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1893; dep. coroner dist. B., Oct., 1898; ag. Federal treas., Oct., 1902; fed. treas., Mar., 1903; acted as clk., leg. coun., Antigua, Dec., 1902; ditto, ex. coun., Montserrat, Mar., 1903; ag. ch. regist., Leeward Is., and regist. and prov.-mar.-hal., admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank, regist. of titles, regist. of friendly societies, and regist.-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Antigua, Feb. to July, 1905; apptd. regist. and prov.-marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., and regist. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1905; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1905; ag. mag., dist. C., Feb., 1911; M.L.C., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1911.
SENEVIRATNE, Alexander de Alwis.—Mem. of legis. coun., Ceylon; advoc. of the sup. ct.; mem. mun. coun., Colombo, and coun. of legal educn.; chmn. Ceylon national assoc.

SENIOR, Bernard, L.S.O. (1906).—B. 1865; clk. to the bindry, comsn. (Redistribution of Seats Act), 1884; attached to Scotch educn. dept., 1885, and again 1888; priv. sec. to Sir F. R. Sandford, under-secr. for Scotland, 1887; 1st class clk., col. sec.'s office, G. Const., 1888; local auditor, Lagos, Aug., 1889; ditto, G. Const., Dec., 1889; ditto, Br. Bechuanaland, Feb., 1894; ditto, Cyprus, 1895; aud.-gen., O.R.C., Sept., 1902; receiv. thanks of S. of S. for war for services rendered in connection with audit of Boer refugee camp acts, after the S. African war; mem. of comsn. to inquire into law relating to mining for diamonds and precious stones in O.R.C., 1903, for which receiv. thanks of govt.; ag. col. treas., 12th Aug., 1903, to 15th Feb., 1904; M.L.C., May, 1904; deleg. for O.R.C. at S. African freights confere., 1904-5; mem. of O.R.C. civ. ser. coman., 1905; chmn. of govt. tender bd.; dir. of National Bank of O.R.C.; col. auditor, Ceylon, 1st Mar., 1907; ag. treas. from 16th Nov., 1908, to 5th Feb., 1909; treas. and comsnr. of stamps, Ceylon, 6th Feb., 1909; is also ch. comsnr. of loan bd., a dir. of the W. and O. pension fund, and mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; author of "A Guide to the examination of Government Accounts in Cyprus."
SERGEANT, John.—Ed. at Athelhampton schl., Birkdale; chief acctnt., pub. wks. dep. E. Africa Prot., 30th May, 1905.

SETH, G. G.—B.A., Calcutta; cadet, S. S. Nov., 1901; ag. sheriff, Singapore, Jan., 1904; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1904; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, 5th Jan., 1904; ag. dep. regur. sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., N. Tebal, June, 1906; ag. dep. regur., sup. c. Penang, Sept., 1906; asst. to atty.-gen., Apr., 1907.

SEVERN, Claud.—B. 1869; M.A. Cantab.; temporary clk. in librarian's dept., foreign offr., Mar., 1891, to June, 1892; priv. sec. to gov., S. Stltmte., Feb., 1894; junr. offr., Selangor, Dec., 1895; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Selangor, June, 1897; asst. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1899; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1900; asst. sec. to res.-gen., F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. fac. to F.M.S., Apr. to Nov., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., 1906, to Apr., 1907; ag. sec. to hu comsnr., F.M.S., and priv. sec. to gov., S. Stltmte., May, 1907; sec. to high comsnr., Malay Stat., Feb., 1911; col. sec., Hong Kong, Feb., 1912; admstrd. govt., 16th Mar. to 3rd July, 1912; and from 21st Aug. to 24th Dec., 1913; pass in Malay and law, 1898.

SEYMOUR, Alfred Wallace.—B. 1881; ed. Warwick Schl. and St. Andrew's Univ.; ed. Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; off. ass't. to gov. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1906; N.C. Prov., Apr., 1907; W. Prov., Nov., 1908; asst. govt. agt. Mullaitivu, Aug., 1910; ditto, Trincomalee, Feb., 1911; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Dec., 1911; ditto, Nuwara Eliva, Jan., 1913; 2nd batt. Ceylon Planters' Rifle Corps.

SHARE, Hamnet Holditch.—B. 1864; ed. R. Navy, 1880; served in H.M.S. "Tornado" and numerous other ships (Egypt, medal 1882 and Khedive's Bronze Star); sec. to commodr. chief, Pacific station, 1899-1900; ditto, Australia station, 1901-1903; priv. sec. to gov. of N.S. Wales, May, 1903; priv. sec. to gov. gen. of Australia, 22nd Nov., 1904.

SHARPE, Sir Alfred, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.B. (1897).—B. 1853; H.M.'s vice-consul, Nyasaland 14th Feb., 1891; ditto, for territories under British influence North of the Zambesi, 15th Feb., 1893; consul for same territories, 1st Feb., 1894; ag. comsnr. and consul-gen. at Zomba, 7th May, 1894, to 3rd May, 1895; given rank of deputy comsnr., 1st Apr., 1896; and again ass't as comsnr. and consul-gen., 16th Apr., 1896, to 14th July, 1897; comsnr. and consul-gen. for territories under Brit. infl. N. of Zambesi, 13th July, 1897; receiv. Jubilee medal, 1897; comsnr. commdr.-in-chief, and consul-gen., Br. Cen. Africa Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; receiv. Coronation medal, 1902; gov. of Nyasaland (late B.C. Prot.), 6th Sept., 1907; ret., 1910; repres. E. African Protos. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

SHARPE, W. S., C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1878; capt. 4th batt. R.I. Rifles, 1893; major, Oct. 1903; inspr., Sierra Leone frontier force, May 1894; 3rd comsnr. to Anglo-French border comsnr. and delimited frontier from Atlantic ocean to Little Mola River, 1896; dist. comsnr., Karo dist., Jan., 1897; served in the S. Leone rebel, 1898-9 (despatches and medal); F.R.G.S.; J.P. for the col.; transf. to N. Nigeria as a res., Dec., 1900; apptd. 2nd class res. to Konso. Prov., Oct., 1901; in Dakkakari expdn., N. Nig., 1904 (medal); in Dareng expdn., N. Nig., 1906 (dangerously wounded); in Dakkakari expdn., N. Nig., 1908.

SHAW, BERNARD VIDAL.—Capt., late 5th Batt. Rifle Brig.; instr. of musk. (1st cls. certif., Hythe); served unofficially for a number of years under Sir Eyre Shaw, K.C.B., Metropolitan Fire Brigade; priv. sec. to director of crim. investig., Scotland Yard; comsnnr. of pol., Cape Town, 1882 to 1888; received thanks of govt. for services rendered during Malay riots of 1886; visiting mag., Breakwater convict station; transfd. to Brit. Guiana, 1889; supt., H.M. penal stltmtn., Massaruni, Brit. Guiana, 1893, also from 1914, H.M. inspr. of prisons and magistrate; author of "Epitome of the Cape Criminal Law."

SHAW, WALTER SIDNEY.—B. 1863; ed. Brighton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1888; pol. mag. and ag. chief just., St. Vincent, 1906; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; confirmed as chief just., 1907; ag. chief just., Grenada, Feb. 1908 to June, 1909; revised and consolidated laws of St. Vincent, 1912; admstd. govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1911 to Jan., 1912, and for various shorter periods; chief just., B. Honduras, 1912.

SHAWE, HENRY BENJAMIN.—Ck. at Clanwilliam, Cape Colony, 12th May, 1883; col. secy.'s off., Cape Town, Dec., 1883; sen. examnr. of acctns., 14th Apr., 1890; chief ditto, 1st July, 1894; acctnt., 10th July, 1895; acctng. offr., 31st Mar., 1899; ch. ck. and acctng. offr., 1st Mar., 1901; asst. under col. sec., 1st Apr., 1902; ag. under-sec. for agric., Sept., 1907, to Mar., 1908; mem. of Rhodesian pub. serv. bd. of enquiry, Apr. to Sept., 1909; under-sec. for the Interior, Union of S. Africa, May, 1910.
SHEA, THE HON. GEORGE.—M.H.A., Ferryland (Newfld.), 1889-93; again in 1897; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1897; mem. of treasy. bd., 1898; mayor of St. John's, 1902; mem. exec. coun., 1904.

SHEARMAN-TURNER, PERCY.—B. 1874; ed. at Forest Schl., Walthamstow and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; M.A., 1898; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; Oxford Circuit, C.C.C.; recorder of titles, E.A.P., 1907 to 1910; ag. dep. prin. registr. of documents and admstr.-gen., 1908 to 1909; legal mem. of coun. and atty.-gen., Zanzibar, 14th Oct., 1910.
SHEIL, HON. JOHN DEVONSHIRE.—B. 1855; puisne judge of sup. ct. of C. of G.H. (Eastern Dists. Local Div.), April, 1902.
SHELTON, R. PAUL.—B. 1880; ed. Harrow and in Germany; ag. consul, Bordeaux, 1907; asst. collr., Zanzibar, 27th Dec., 1907; collr. (now dist. comsnnr.), Pemba, 16th Oct., 1912.

SHEPHERD, ARTHUR LINDSEY MOORE.—B. 1891; Matric. Lond. Univ., July, 1911; ck. to gov., Leeward Is., Nov., 1909; asst. priv. sec., June, 1912; priv. sec. to gov. of Fiji and high comsnnr. for W. Pacific, June, 1912.

SHEPHERD, FREDERICK REECE.—B. 1859; supt. of Skerrett's training schl., Antigua, Leeward Is., 1891-1905; ag. supt., sugar cane experiments, Antigua, 1899-1904; deleg. to various agric. confes. held by Impl. comsnnr.; ag. supt. of agric., Leeward Is., July to Nov., 1910.
SHEPHERD, N.—B. 1891; apptd. after compet. exam., ck. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to B. or T., Apr., 1911; gen. registr.'s office, census office, 1st Nov., 1911; C.O., May, 1913.

SHEPPARD, WALTER SYDNEY, B.A., M.B., B. Ch. (Cantab.).—B. 1871; civil surg. in charge of H.M.'s troops N.E. dist., Eng., May, 1898, to May, 1899; spec. plague med. offr., India, May, 1899, to Nov., 1900; house surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, 19th Nov., 1900; super. col. surg., S. Stltmtns., 1st Mar., 1901; spec. mission to Brunei, Aug., 1904; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1905; senior med. offr., Singapore, July, 1911.

SHEPSTONE, H. C., C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1840; priv. sec. to the lieut.-gov., Natal, 1867; clk. to exec. coun., 1863; Indian immigr. agt. and J.P., 1864; R.M. and admstr. of native law, Alfred co., 1870; ditto, Durban co., 1878; acted as sec. to Mr. Justice Phillips, C.M.G., on special service to Barbados, 1876; served on the staff of Sir Theophilus Shepstone, K.C.M.G., H.M.'s special comsnnr. in S. Africa, 1877; sec. for native affairs, Transvaal, July, 1877; attended Cetywayo on his visit to England, 1882; sec. for native affairs, Natal, 1884; ret., 1893.

SHEPSTONE, JOHN WESLEY, C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1827; July, 1846, govt. interp., Natal; July, 1847, accompanied Capt. Kyle on special mission to Zulu king; Dec., 1850, accompanied Capt. Gordon on special mission to Pondos; Oct., 1851, interp. to recorder and mag.'s offices; Dec., 1851, asst. R.M., Pietermaritzburg co.; Aug., 1852, R.M., Lower Umvoti, and govt. agt., Noniti country, and J.P.; 1857, commanded native forces against Chief Usidoi and tribe, and against Chief Matyana and tribe; Nov., 1858, supt. of the cultivation of cotton; Apr., 1861, ag. sec. for native affairs and judicial assessor; July, 1861, commanded a native force in the Fort Buckingham expdn.; May, 1862, ag. R.M., Inanda div.; Aug., 1863, asst. R.M., Pietermaritzburg co.; ag. R.M., Umvoti co., Sept., 1867; May, 1874, comsnnr., Klip River and Weenen counties; July, 1874, ag. sec. for native affairs and judicial assessor; Oct., 1874, J.P. for the col.; Sept., 1876, sec. for native affairs and mem. exec. and legis. couns.; Mar., 1878, mem. of the bndry. coms.; Dec., 1878, delivered ultimatum to Zulu delegs. at Lower Drift, Tugela; Aug., 1879, accompanied Sir Garnet Wolseley to arrange the settlement of Zululand; mentioned in despatches; Dec., 1882, Br. comsnnr., Zululand; Jan., 1884, judge, native high ct.; ret., 1896.

SHERIDAN, JOSEPH.—Ed. at Castleknock Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; called to the bar, Ireland, 1907, went Connaught circuit; judcl. clk., Nyasaland Prot., July, 1908; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. judge of high ct., Dec., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. atty.-gen., July, 1911, to Mar., 1912; apptd. asst. to atty.-gen., Apr., 1912; ag. atty.-gen., Aug., 1912; town mag., E.A.P., 1913.
SHERIDAN, JOSEPH CLERC.—Impl. civ. ser. from 1880; attached to Transvaal (leave from Impl. ser.) as inspr. of rev., 1902; ch. inspr. of rev., 1903; comsnnr. of inland rev., 1st June, 1910.

SHERIFF, PERCY MUSGRAVE CRESSWELL.—B. 1869; called to the bar, Mid. Temple, June, 1892; admitted to the bar of B. Hond., 1893; of Jamaica, 1895; ag. dist. comsnnr., Belize, for six months in 1898; apptd. mag. and coroner, So. dist., Grenada, 1900; visiting just. of prisons, 1900; chairman of St. George's Road Board, 1901; ag. mem. legis. coun., June, 1902; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, Dec., 1902, to Dec., 1903, when confirmed; admtd. govt., and ag. col. sec. and treas., Aug., 1903; mem. bd. of educn.; prov. mem. of exec. coun., and legal adviser to admstr., and draughtsman of govt. bills, Dec., 1902, to Apr., 1906; M.L.C., 1904; ch. just., St. Lucia, 1906; ch. just., Bermuda, Apr., 1912; compiled "Comparative Index of Laws of Grenada and St. Vincent," Dec., 1905; and "Index of Laws of St. Lucia," Dec., 1907; "Admiralty Rules, St. Lucia," 1911.
SHERWOOD, ARTHUR PERCY, C.M.G., M.V.O., A.D.C.—B. 1854; supt. of Dominion pol., Canada, 1882; comsnr., Dominion pol., 1885; ch. comsnr. of pol. 1911.
SHERWOOD, M. E.—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Jan., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Labuan, Jan. to Nov., 1909.
SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT.—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd div. clk., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1898; minor staff clk., 1911.
SHIRCORE, JOHN OWEN, M.B., Ch.B.—Med. offr., Nyasaland, 1908; transf. to Uganda, 1909; re-transf. as med. offr., Nyasaland, 1910; med. offr., E.A.P., 1912.

SHOLL, LIONEL HENRY, C.M.G. (1911), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1844; clk., P.W. office, S. Australia, July, 1858; clk., waterwks. dept., Jan., 1859; clk., audit office, Sept., 1863; ch. clk., Jan., 1872; ch. clk. and acctnt., treasy., July, 1874; acctnt. and receiver of rev., treasy., July, 1876; cashier and acctnt., treasy., July, 1879; under-treas., July, 1880; under-sec. and govt. statist., May, 1890; also clk. of exec. coun., July, 1894; also sec. to min. of industry, July, 1908, to June, 1911.

SHORT, EDWARD MORRISON DE COUCY.—B. 1857; Ceylon writer, 1878; pol. mag., 1883; ag. dist. judge, Chilaw, 1884; ag. asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, 1886; ditto, Mannar, Jan., 1889; ditto, Hambantota, Sept., 1889; ditto, Kegalla, 1890; ditto, Varuniya and Mullaitivu, 1890; ditto, Kalutara, 1893; ditto, Matara, 1894; ditto, Chilaw, 1895; ditto, Varuniya, 1896; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Oct., 1896; ditto, Matara, 1897; ditto, Matale, 1900; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, 1900; ag. govt. agt., Badulla, 1903; ditto, N.W. Prov., 1904; chrmn. of mun. coun. and mayor of Colombo, 1905; lieut., Ceylon volunteer reserve.

SHORTT, ADAM, C.M.G. (1911); M.A., F.R.S.C.—B. 1859; ed. at Walkerton High Schl. and Queen's Univ., Canada (B.A., 1883, M.A., 1885) and Glasgow and Edin. Univ.; asst. prof. of philosophy, Queen's Univ., 1885; lect. and prof. of polit. science, 1889-1908; apptd. civ. ser. comsnr., Canada, 1908; writer on history, banking and economics.

SIDNEY, T. S., K.C.—Ed. Harrow and Trin. Hall, Camb.; M.A., 1888; called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1885; advoc., high ct. of Madras, 1894; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1901-1903; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 1909.
SIERRA LEONE, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. JOHN WALMSLEY, D.D.—B. 1867; formerly vice-principal of Wycliff Hall, Oxford, vicar of St. Ann's, Nottingham, and hon. canon of Southwell; consecrated, 1910.

SIFTON, HON. ARTHUR LEWIS, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1859; ed. Wesley Coll., Winnipeg and Victoria Univ., Cobourg; called to the bar, Manitoba, 1883; elec. to North-West Admin., 1901; comsnr. pub. wks., 1901; K.C., 1903; ch. just. of N.W.T., 1903; ch. just. of Alberta, 1905; resig. May, 1910, to become premier of Alberta; re-elec., 1913.
SILBERRAD, HUBERT.—Ed. at Wren's and Finishing Tech. Coll. (engineering); asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 8th May, 1903; transf. to Nyassaland Prot. as 2nd grade res., July, 1909.
SILVESTER, J. M.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913.
SIM, HON. WM. ALEXANDER.—B. 1858; judge of supreme ct. of New Zealand, 16th Jan., 1911.

SIMMONS, HON. WM. CHAS.—B. 1865; ed. as Collingwood and Owen Sound High Schls.; B.A., Toronto Univ., 1895; called to the bar, N.W.T., 1900; elec. to legis. assem. of Alberta for Lethbridge dist., 1906; resig., 1908; puisne judge of sup. ct., Alberta, 12th Oct., 1910.
SIMPSON, ARCHIBALD HENRY, M.A.—B. 1843; chf. judge in equity, N.S. Wales, 1896.
SIMPSON, EVERARD DONWES.—B. 1876; M.A., Oxon.; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, 26th June, 1901; dist. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1905; ag. pol. mag., E. Prov., Feb. to Mar., and July to Sept., 1907.
SIMPSON, GEORGE.—Inspr. of schls., Bermuda, 1880; also inspr. of dockyard schls.
SIMPSON, HON. SIR GEORGE BOWEN, Kt., B.A. (1909).—B. 1838; judge of sup. ct., N.S. Wales, since 1894; judge of divorce and matrimonial jurisdiction, 1896; ret., 1910.

SIMPSON, SAMUEL.—B. 1876; ed. at Owen's Coll., and Edin. Univ.; B.Sc.; Stevens scholar; sec. to the Union; sen. pres. of students' representative coun.; Highland and agric. soc'y. prizeman; life mem. and silver medallist of roy. agric. soc'y.; holder of nat. diploma in agric.; sen. lecturer in agric. to govt. agric. coll., Ghizah, Egypt; cotton expert to B.C.A. Prot., Mar., 1905; dir. of agric., Uganda, 1912; author of "Report on Cotton Growing Industry in B.C.A. Prot."; reported on the agric. resources of Angola, Trinidad, Tobago, B. Guiana and Surinam.
SIMSON, COLIN COAPE.—Chief med. of Papua, 19th Oct., 1908.
SIMSON, J. A.—Ed. in England, Germany and Switzerland; agt.-gen. in India for the British Protectorates in Africa.

SINCKLER, EDWARD GOULBURN.—B. 1856; entered col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Nov., 1874; acted as clk. to licent.-gov., 1878; 2nd clk. corpride branch, col. sec.'s office, 1879; 4th asst. clk. to Gov. W. Robinson, 1880; 1st clk. record branch, col. sec.'s office, and clk., courts of ordinary and error, Jan., 1883; ag. ch. clk., 1886; comsnr. of census, 1891; ch. clk. to judges, ass't ct. of appeal, 1892; J.P., Oct., 1892; ag. reg'n. friendly socs., June, 1893; ag. sen. pol. mag., Bridgetown, and dist. A, Aug. to Oct., 1895; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. B, Sept. to Oct., 1894; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. F, July, 1896; to Jan., 1897; ag. pol. mag. judge, and coroner dist. B, Sept. to Oct., 1897; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. D, Mar. to Apr., 1898; ag. coroner St. Michael, 1898 (at time of hurricane), and has repeatedly acted as such; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. B, 1899; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, Feb., 1900; ag. comsnr. of probate, May to Nov., 1900; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, July, 1900, to Sept., 1901; pol. mag. and judge, dist.
