African war, 1901-02 (medal and four clasps); re-apptd. to 2nd div., and assigned to B. of T., 1902; clk., P.W.D., B.C. Africa Prot., July, 1906; treasy. asst., Uganda, Jan., 1909.
TAYLOR, E. R. S.—Asst. supt. of police E.A.P., May, 1912.
TAYLOR, FRANCIS BRYANT OLUDELE.—Ed. at C.M.S. Collegiate schl., Lagos; 3rd cls. certif., Lond. Coll. of Preceptors, 1903; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., Lagos, Feb., 1907.
TAYLOR, GEORGE WILSON.—B. 1866; ed. at Toronto; apptd. to excise branch, inland revenue dept., 1888; engaged in spec. organization work.
QUEBEC, 1898-1909; asst. sec., inland rev. dept., 1909; sec., ditto, 1913; asst. deputy min. and sec., 1914.

TAYLOR, HAROLD BLAKE, F.C.H., M.I.C.E.—B. 1862; trained at R.I.E. Coll.; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Bengal, India, 1882; transf., to state rlyws., May, 1886; under-secr. to govt. of Bengal, Feb., 1890; exec. engrnr., Feb., 1894; lent to Gwalior Durbar from Jan., 1893 to Mar., 1905; suptdg. engrnr., Dec., 1907; gen. man., Uganda railway, 1912.

TAYLOR, JOHN.—B. 1863; entd. Royal Navy, 1883; served in Soudan war, 1884-85 (medal and Khedive's bronze star); impl. prison serv., Lond., Dec., 1887, to June, 1897; ch. warder, Belize pris., B. Hond., July, 1897; keeper of prisons, 1898; offl. recr. in bank., and offl. adminstr., 23rd Oct., 1908 to 22nd May, 1909; ag. dist. comsnr., on numerous occasions, 1905 to 1914; dist. comsnr., Toledo dist., 18th May, 1914; J.P. for the colony, 1908.

TAYLOR, ROBERT WALTER.—B. 1883; ed. Emmanuel Schl. and King's Coll., London; clk. in crown agt's. office, 1903-1906; head acctnt., Somaliland, 1906; ag. mil. paymstr., Aug., 1907, to Jan., 1908; Somaliland expdn., 1908-1910 (G.A.S. medal and clasp); asst. treas., Uganda, 1910; asst. treas., Somaliland, 6th Mch., 1914.

TAYLOR, STUART CAMPBELL—Joined K.O. Yorks. Light Infantry in India, 1892; brig. sig. offr., 4th brig., Tirah expedy. force, 1897-1898 (medal with two clasps); A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Sir Chas. Bruce, G.C.M.G., gov. of Mauritius, 1898; served throughout S. African war 1890-1902 (Queen's medal with four clasps, King's medal with two clasps, twice ment. in desps.); adj. 2nd K.O. Yorks. L.I., 1901-1905; staff offr., British troops, Crete, during internatl. occuptn., 1903-1904; adj. 3rd (militia) batt. K.O. Yorks. L.I., 1906-1907; seed. from army and apptd. asst. res., N. Nigeria, 20th July, 1907; employed under C.O. in connection with recption of coronation troops and visitors from overseas, 1911; coronation medal; retired 'from army, 1911.
TAYLOR, WILLIAM AINSLIE CHARLES.—B. 1877; asst. storekeeper and acctnt., colonial stores, Ceylon, Oct., 1905; storekeeper, 1908.

TAYLOR, SIR W. T., K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1848; collr. of customs and excise, Larnaca, 1879; ch. collr. of customs, Cyprus, 1882; ag. recvr.-gen., Nov., 1883, to Feb., 1884; ag. comsnr., Larnaca, in addition to his own duties, 1883 and 1885; ag. recvr.-gen. in addition to duties as ch. collr. of cust., Jan. to Nov., 1887; recvr.-gen. and ch. collr. of cust. and excise, 1891; audr.-gen., Ceylon, May, 1895; ag. col. sec., Ceylon, Oct., 1895, to Feb., 1896, Mar. to Dec., 1896, Mar. to Nov., 1899, and Apr., 1900, to Nov., 1901; col. sec., S. Settmts., June, 1901; admntd. govt. of S. S., Oct., 1903, to Apr., 1904, and Feb. to June, 1906; ag. res.-gen., F.M.S., Sept., 1904; confirmed, Jan., 1905; ret., 1910; in charge of F.M.S. development agency, London, Oct., 1910; mem., W. African lands comtee., 1912.

TEMPANY, HAROLD AUGUSTINE, B.Sc.(Lond.), F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1881; asst. gov. chemist, Leeward Is., 1903; ag. govt. chemist and supt. of agric., July to Dec., 1906, and Jan. to Nov., 1909; govt. chemist and supt. of agric., Leeward Is., Nov., 1909; J.P., Antigua, 1910; ag. offl. mem. legis. coun., Antigua, Mar. to July, 1912; author of publications on agric. chemistry and tropical agric.

TEMPLE, CHARLES LINDSAY, C.M.G. (1909).—B. 1871; ed. Sedbergh and Cambridge; Royal Geog. Soc. diploma surveying, 1896; ag. consul., Para, Apr. to Nov., 1898; nominated vice-consul, Maanos, 21st July, 1899; passed exam. and apptd. to that post, 29th June, 1900; ag. consul., Para, July to Nov., 1900, and from Dec., 1900 to July, 1901; transf. to col. ser. as 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, 6th Nov., 1901; 2nd cls. res., June, 1902; ment. in desps. Burmi operations, 1903; 1st cls. res., Apr., 1906; chief sec. to govt., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; admntd. govt., Jan. to Aug., 1911, and from June to Oct., 1912.

TEMPLETON, HON. ROBERT STANSEER, A.I.C.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1855; asst. survr., Ceylon, 15th Oct., 1887; dist. survr., 1st Aug., 1890; supt. of topographical surveys, 1st Jan., 1899; supt. of surveys, 8th Sept. 1902; asst. survr.-gen., 23rd June, 1904; survr.-gen., 15th Oct., 1910; M.I.C.; mem. of mun. coun.; dir. of widows' and orphans' pension fund.

TENNANT, HERCULES, C.M.G. (1906).—Barrister-at-law, Inn. Tem.; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Col., 1873; sec. of law comsn., 1879; represented Caledon in house of assem., 1879-81; lieut., D.E.O.V.R., 1878-81; extra A.D.C. to gov. and comdr.-in-chief, July, 1879; served in Basuto war, 1880-81 (medal), with rank of capt., as asst. staff offr. to Gen. Clarke, C.B., and afterwards to Col. Carrington, C.M.G.; sec. to ch. just., 1882; librarian of the sup. ct., 1882; asst. registr., sup. ct., and official reviser of authorised edition of Cape statutes, 1884; asst. registr., ct. of appeal, Mar., 1886; J.P. for Cape-town and dist., and Capt. dist., July, 1886; ag. taxing offr., sup. ct., Sept., 1884, and Aug. to Dec., 1885; Apr. and Oct., 1886; Mar. and June, 1887; and Oct., 1887, to Jan., 1889; taxing offr., Feb., 1889; high sheriff, Oct., 1889; registr. of sup. ct., 1894; sec. to law dept., Transvaal, 1st June, 1901; J.P., 1901; advocate of sup. ct., 1902; examiner of candidates for admission as conveyancers, 1902; M.L.C., 1906; retired 1909; editor and compiler of "The Notary's Manual"; "The Justice of the Peace's Manual"; "Rules of Court"; "Chronological Table and Index of the Statute Law of the Colony, 1714-1883"; "Masters and Servants Laws of the Colony"; joint editor (by authority) of a revised edition of "The Cape Statutes, 1652-1886, and of the Griqualand W. Statutes"; also compiled (under authority), "The Index of Government Proclamations and Notices, 1803-1881."

TENNYSON, Rt. Hon. LORD, 2ND BARON (U.K., creat. 1884), G.C.M.G. (1903), K.C.M.G. (1899). HALLAM TENNYSON, succeeded 1892 (on the death of his father), Alfred Lord Tennyson, poet laureate, 1850-92.—B. 1852; ed. Marl. Coll., Trin. Coll., Camb., and Inner Temple; J.P. for Hants; mem. of Marl. Coll. exec. coun.; prepared his father's memoir, published in 1897; gov. S. Australia, 1899; gov.-gen., Commonwealth of Australia, 1902-1904.

TENNYSON, CHARLES BRUCE LOCKER, C.M.G. (1915), B.A. (1902).—B. 1879; ed. at Eton, and King's Coll., Camb. (scholar); 1st div. 1st cls. class, tripods, 1902; Whewell scholar (Internat. Law), Camb. Univ., 1903; Arden scholar of Gray's Inn, 1904; called to the bar, 1905; legal asst., C.O., Nov., 1911.
TEW, GEORGE MOLROY.—Indian pol., Berar, 1893-7; Uganda rly. pol., 1899-1903; asst. dist. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1903.

THAINE, ROBERT NIEMANN.—B. 1875; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1898; off. astt. to govt. agt. S. Prov., Apr., 1899; Cent. Prov., Aug., 1900; comsnr. of requests and pol. mag., Balapitiya, June, 1901; off. astt. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, Dec., 1901; astt. govt. agt., Kalutara, Mar., 1902; dist. judge, Badulla, May, 1902; pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1904; landing survr., cust., Colombo, Sept., 1904; astt. govt. agt., Trincomalee, June, 1906; Puttalam, Aug., 1906; dist. judge, Jaffna, July, 1908; comsnr. of requests, Colombo, Jan., 1911; astt. govt. agent, Mannar, Sept., 1911; govt. agt., Province of Sabaragamawela, Feb., 1913.
THATCHER, J.—Ed. at Bristol Gram. schl.; examr. of loan accts., audit dept., O.R.C., 1st Jan., 1904; examr. of accts., ditto, 1st July, 1904.
THERON, HON. H. S., B.A.—Inspr. of mines, Orange Free State, 1896; resident J.M.P., Koffyfontein, 1898; min. of lands, Union of South Africa, 1913.
THOMAS, E.J.—Entered Victoria service, 1852; receiver of revenue, etc., 1863; acted as sec. to numerous boards and comsrs.; priv. sec. to Sir B. O'Loghlen, Bart., 1881; sec., premier's dept., 1883.

THOMAS, HON. JONATHAN.—B. 1863; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894-1901; elected to first H. of R., C. of A., 1901; re-elected in 1903 and 1906; P.M.G., C. of A., Apr., 1910; min. for external affairs, 1911-1913.
THOMAS, T. S. W.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Aug., 1909; 3rd ast. sec., Apr., 1911; sen. ast. sec., Apr., 1912.

THOMPSON, AUGUSTUS WM.—Ast. clk. of ct., G. Coast, 1873; clk. to Queen's adv., 1874-5; dep. regisr., cent. prov., 1877; oh. regisr., 1880; postmtr., Cape Coast, Mar. to Aug., 1884; regisr. and interp., W. Prov., 1884; regisr. of deeds and taxing master, W. Prov.; dist. comsnr., G. C. col., May, 1889.
THOMPSON, C. B.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911.

THOMPSON, JAS., M.I.C.E.—B. 1863; entd. Queen's Coll., Cork, 1879; B.E., Roy. Univ. of Ireland, 1882; pupil at pub. wks. and docks bd., Dublin, 1882-3; astt. engnr., Southport and Cheshire lines extension rly., 1883-84; astt. engnr., survey, design, etc., Cavan, Leitrim and Roscommon light rly., 1884-85; res. engnr. construction of rlys. and harb. wks., Ireland, 1885-89; draftsmen and astt. survr., Victorian rlys., 1889-91; astt. engnr., P.W.D., W. Australia, 1891-93; res. engnr., Yilgarn rly., May, 1893; dist. engnr., Nov., 1895; engnr.-in-charge of rly. constn., June, 1896; engnr.-in-charge of harb. and rivers (in addition to rly. construction), Aug., 1898; inspect. engnr., July, 1902; ag. engnr.-in-chief, Mar., 1904; engnr.-in-chief and consulting engnr. to the Fremantle Harb. Trust, Sept., 1904.

THOMPSON, J. V., B.A.—B. 1872; ed. Dulwich Coll., Blair Lodge, and Selwyn Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1895; English and math. master, Idadi schl., Nicosia, Cyprus, 1899; headmaster, Queen Victoria schl., Fiji, 1906; inspr. of schls., 1907; J.P. for the Colony, 1909; represented Fiji at Imm. conf. on educn., 1911.

THOMPSON, JOHN.—Ent. Royal Irish constab., Belfast, 1864 to 1870; obtained apptnt. in the convict ser., Woking, England; astt. in reforming prison ser. in Jamaica, Oct., 1883; prin. warder and storekr., gen. penitentiary, to July, 1888, then dep. supt. of same; ag. supt. to Dec., 1894; astt. of St. Catherine dist. prison, Spanish Town, Jamaica, Nov., 1897.
THOMPSON, J. H.—M.B. Bac. Surg.; med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1913.

THOMPSON, JOHN HARRISON.—B. 1892; appt. after compet. exam., astt. clk., commercial intelligence branch, B. of T., 23rd June, 1912; library attendant, C.O., 6th Apr., 1914; after further compet. exam., apptd. as clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to C.O., 24th Aug., 1914.
THOMPSON, PERCY G.—Called to the bar, Mid. Tenn., 1891; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1904; has acted on several occasions as Crown prosecutor and as pol. mag.

THOMPSON, THOMAS AUGUSTUS.—Called to bar, Oct., 1872; in Easter term, 1874, received comsnr. to act as public prosecutor in the crim. ct., Bahamas; acted as judge of court of common pleas from June to Dec., 1875; pol. mag., Bahamas (being still allowed priv. practice), Jan., 1876; chmn. of man. comttee. of prisons, and presides over investigations into cases of wreck and other marine casualty; mem. of the Bahamas legislature and trustee of the Nassau Museum and Library, 1881; stip. and circuit mag., 1886; ag. atty. gen., 1887; ag. ch. just., July, 1890, June to Oct., 1891, and June to Nov., 1892; ag. col. sec. and chmn. of bd. of educn., Oct., 1890; judge and col. sec., Falklands, 1893; admstd. govt., July to Nov., 1894; registr. and marshal, sup. ct., Trinid., 1897.

THOMPSON, WALTER.—Supt.'s ast., Skerritt's training schl., Leeward Is., June, 1892 to Apr., 1894; 4th outdoor offr., treasv. dept., Apr., 1894, to Jan., 1895; 3rd ditto, Jan., 1895, to Dec., 1902; 2nd ditto, Dec., 1902, to Feb., 1903; 1st ditto, Feb., 1903; admeasurer of ships, Nov., 1903; ag. 1st indoor offr., treasv. dept., Dec., 1903; ag. harbrmr., Feb., 1904; 1st outdoor offr. and ag. harbrmr., May, 1904; appt. 1st outdoor offr. and harbrmr. on the amalgamation of offices, June, 1904.

THOMPSON, WILLIAM AUSTIN.—2nd dist. supervsr., G. Coast, Apr., 1894; ag. ast. treas. Cape Coast Castle, Mar., 1895; cashr., Accra, Mar., 1896; ag. ast. treas., Apr., 1896; 1st cl. supervsr., Sept., 1897; sent on serv. in hinterland, Feb., 1898; invalided and retd. from West Afr., Feb., 1900; treas., etc., Falklands, July, 1901; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., in addition to duties as treasr., May to Nov., 1902, Apr., 1903 to Jan., 1904, July to Sept., 1905, June to Nov., 1909, and from Jan. to June, 1911.

THOMPSTONE, SYDNEY WILSON, C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1863; F.R.C.P. (Edin.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Camb.); form. house surg. Reg. Infir., Glasgow; apptd. dist. med. offr., Oil Etr. Protec. (now S. Niger), Mar. 22nd, 1883; 1st prin. med. offr., 1897; ag. prin. med. offr., Aug.-Nov., 1898; Nov., 1899, to June, 1900, and June, 1901, to Oct., 1902; prin. med. offr., N. Nigeria, May, 1903; ret., 1911.

THOMSON, ALEX. DONALD.—Ed. Abb. Univ., M.A.; 1st class hon. math., 1883; ast. prof. of math., Aberdeen, 1886-7; Bacon scholar, Gray's Inn, 1888; cadet, Hong Kong, 1887; attached to C.O., 1888; passed cadet, Dec., 1890; ag. sup. Victoria gaol, Apr., 1891, to Mar., 1892; ag. chmn. of couns. Jan., 1891, to Oct., 1892; ag. ast. col. sec., Mar. to Oct., 1892; ag. regisr.-gen. and provisional mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1893; ag. ast. col. sec., 1894; ag. col. treas., Mar., 1895; ag. mem. exec. and legis. couns.; mem. govern. body of Queen's Coll., 1894; postm-r.-gen., 1897; col. treas., and mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1898; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1902, Nov., 1903, to Jul., 1904, and in 1907, 1909, 1910, 1912, 1st Jan., 24th July, and from 21st Aug. to 24th Dec., 1913; has served on various comttees and comsrs.

THOMSON, HON. DUGALD.—B. 1848; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894, 1895, and 1898; mem. of Commonw. parl., Mar., 1901, Dec., 1903, and Dec., 1906; min. for home affairs, ditto, Oct., 1904; has retired from politics.

THOMSON, JOHN HASTINGS.—B. 1873; ed. Wallace Hall Academy, Dumfriesshire, Scotland; apptd. asst. dist. offr., Somaliland, May, 1901; ag. consul Berbera, Sept., 1901, to Aug., 1903; ag. ist. offr., Bulhar, Jan., 1904-5; polit. offr. with Gen. Swayne in Abyssinia, Mar. to Apr., 1905; polit. offr. with troops on Abyssinian border, May to July, 1905; ag. sub-comsnt., Zeyla, 2nd June, 1905; Somaliland medal with clasp, 1902-4; dist. tr., Makhir Coast, Aug., 1906; ag. sub-comsnt., Eylala, 5th June, 1907; dist. comsnt., E.A.P., pl., 1914.

THORBURN, J. JAMESON, C.M.G.(1907).— Writer, Ceylon, Oct., 1886; ag. gov't. agt., N. prov., Mar., to Aug., 1889; ag. pol. mag., Alle, Dec., 1893; ag. off. astt. to gov't. agts. various dists. from Oct., 1889, to 1905; ag. dist. col. sec., Jan., 1896; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan. and Dec., 1898, and June, 1899; ag. asst. atmr.-gen., Jan., 1899; 2nd asst. col. sec., June, 99; ag. sec. central irrigation board, Jan., 99; principal asst. col. sec. and joint sec., loan ward, May, 1900; senior prov. comsnt. (in anticipation of proposed amalgamation of the adminis. Lagos and S. Nigeria), performed duties of sec. adminis. of S. Nigeria, dep. high comsnt., S. geria, Mar. to June, 1905; ag. gov't., Lagos, and high comsnt., S. Nigeria, July, 1905; ag. col., Lagos, 12th Feb. to 30th Apr., 1906; lieut.-v. and col. sec., S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906; dep. v., 24th Dec., 1906, to 4th Jan., 1907, 6th-14th n., 1907, and 27th Jan. to 17th Mar., 1907; ag. v., 14th May to 27th Oct., 1907; gov't., Gold ast. 24th Oct., 1910; ret., 1912.

THORNE, WM. HOBART HOUGHTON.—B. 1875; at St. Paul's Schol, and King's Coll., Camb.; led to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1900; encé de Droit de la Faculté de Paris, v., 1903; British representative on Internat. sims Coman, at Casa Blanca, 7th May, 1908; employed at F.O. in investigation of Anglo-american pecuniary claims, 15th Sept., 1910, to July, 1911; pres. of dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, July, 1911; passed exam. in mod. Greek, v., 1912; has acted as King's Advoc.

HORNE, W. H. G.—B. 1875; ed. Malvern 1; 2nd lieut., 6th Lanc. Fus., 1901; lieut., 2 (hon. lieut. in army, 1902); served with 1st Connaught Rangers M.I. during S. African, 1901-2 (Queen's medal and four clasps); sub-x. of constab., Trinidad, May, 1906; staff, local forces, and adjutant to constab., with k. of capt., Apr. to Sept., 1909; three months' ing with R.I.C., Dublin, 1910 (Colonial pol. ser't cert.); ag. inspr., N.E. division, and t. Arima fire brigade, Oct., 1909, to Mar., 1912; sub-inspr. Barbados pol. and asst.-supt. of brigade, 26th June, 1912; J.P., 1912; A.D.C. ion. Major Burdon, C.M.G., Nov.-Dec., 1912; inspr.-gen., 25th June to 29th Sept., 1913; r. of pol. and capt., 1st July, 1913.

HORNHILL, PONSONBY.—Superannuary of cust., B. Guiana, Aug.-Sept., 1887; asst. rd clk., P.W.D., Oct., 1887; asst. to clk. of (1st cls. offr.) P.W.D., New Amsterdam, 1888; 5th cls. offr., P.W.D., Apr., 1892; clk. of wks. (1st cls. offr.) Berbice and dists., to Aug., 1893; 5th cls. clk., G.P.O., June, 1894; clk., P.O. savings bank, Jan., 1899; 4th clk., G.P.O., Jan., 1900; 3rd cls. clk., treasy., Georgetown, Jan., 1901; ag. cashier, June, 1904, to July, 1905, and May to Nov., 1908.

THORNTON, GEORGE.—M.D. (Edin.); M.R.C.P.(Lond.); D.P.H.(Oxford); house physm., Edin. Royal Infirm., 1890; house surg., Metropolitan hosp., Lond.; house surg., children's hosp., Hull; astt. med. offr., Tooting Fever hosp., 1893-99; civil surg. to H.M. forces in S. Africa; med. supt., Pretoria hosp., 1900-1908; med. supt., gen. hosp., Colombo, Ceylon, Nov., 1908.

THRELFALL, WM. HERBERT.—B. 1875; apptd., after open compet., exam., to controller's off., London postal service, Apr., 1898; supt. of registn., G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1905; ag. supt. of mails, G.P.O., Singapore, Nov., 1905; supt. of regisn., ditto, Sept., 1906; supt. of mails, Penang, Sept., 1907.

TIBBITS, ARTHUR CHARLES KENT.—B. 1877; employed col. sec.'s off.; treas. and aud. off., Antigua, Apr. to Dec., 1894; clk. sav. bank, Jan., 1895; 1st clk., post off., Jan., 1898; ag. postm. in 1899, 1900, 1901, 1903, 1904, 1905; and from June, 1906, to Mar., 1907; postm. of Antigua, and federal postm. of Leeward Is., 1st Apr., 1907; apptd. a library trustee and a J.P., Antigua, 1910; income and trade tax assessor, 1908 and 1913; chmn., bd. of guardians, May to Nov., 1911, and May, 1913; dir., P.O.G. fund, May, 1912.

TIEBITS, ELWOOD D'ARCY.—B. 1880; ag. 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, Jan., 1899, to Nov., 1900 (almost continuously); jun. audit clk., 1st Dec., 1900; 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., 8th July, 1901; ag. clk. to adminstr. and clk. to exec. coun., Dominica, 2nd Aug., 1905, to 5th Apr., 1906; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, 23rd Sept., 1906; 2nd clk., clk. to exec. and legis. couns. and supt. of gov't. printing off., Antigua, 11th Oct., 1911; ag. priv. sec. to gov., 17th Oct., 1903, to 5th May, 1904; 28th May, 1911 to 31st Jan., 1912, and 15th Apr. to 18th July, 1912; ag. astt. col. sec., 13th Nov. to 11th Dec., 1907, 11th to 22nd Jan., 1908, 21st Aug., 1911 to 31st Jan., 1912, 5th Feb. to 4th Mar., 1912; 15th Apr. to 19th June, 1912; ag. ch. clk. on abolition of off. of astt. col. sec., 20th June to 1st Oct., 1912; ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Leeward Is., 2nd Oct., 1912.

TIBBITS, RICHARD WHITE LONGMUIR.—B. 1846; ed. at high schl., Quebec and Lennoxville coll., formerly coroner, J.P., and sec.-treas., Victoria, New Brunswick; elec. to legislature, N.B., 1882; dep. prov. sec., N.B., 1885; King's printer, 1895.
TICE, W. G.—B. 1867; apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 19th Feb., 1900; 2nd cl., 1st Feb., 1902.

TINNEY, LOUIS HERBERT JOHN, R.N.R.—B. 1873; apptd. dep. harbor., Georgetown, B. Guiana, Apr., 1904; ag. harbm'r., June, 1904, to June, 1905, and May to Nov., 1907; has recd. R.H.S. silver medal for saving life at sea.
TOBIT, PURREW MAHON.—L.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.F.P.S. (Glas.); med. offr., G. Coa't., 16th Dec., 1899; served as P.M.O. in Transi expdn., 1902 (ment. in desps.).

TODD, JOHN SPENCER BRIDGES, C.M.G.(1878).—B. 1840; jun. clk., col. sec.'s office, Cape, Aug., 1860; 2nd clk. to C.C. and R.M., Swellendam, Dec., 1862; 2nd cl. clk. to C.C., Robertson, Nov., 1864; 1st clk. to C.C., Swellendam, May, 1867, to Aug., 1874, but was ag. C.C. and R.M., Swellendam, June, 1870, to Jan., 1872, and again during Sept. and Oct., 1872; 1st corrpdng. clk. to the rly. engrnr. of the col., Aug., 1874, to Mar., 1875; clk. in charge of money orders and stamps, G.P.O., Capetown, Apr., 1875; employed on special service as sec. to a govt. coman. investigating the accts. and balances of the col. treasy-chest, Capetown, May to Dec., 1875; was exec. comsnr. for the col. at the Paris Exhibition of 1878; acmnt., col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1876; in June, 1880, served with Sir H. White and Mr. Lawson (W.O.), and Sir W. B. Gordon (Treasy.), on a mixed coman. to investigate the expenditure incurred by the Impl. and col. govts. in the Transkei war of 1878; proceeded in June, 1881, on special service to Kimberley, with a view to adjusting the accts. of the prov. of Griqualand W.; acted as C.C. and regisr. of deeds at Kimberley from Sept. to Dec., 1881; and as asst. comsnr. of Crown lands and pub. wks. in Capetown from Jan. to Apr., 1882; sec. and acct. to the agt.-gen. for the col. in London, Oct., 1882; ag. agt.-gen., 1895-6; ret., 1905.

TOLMIE, RODERICK FINLAYSON.—B. 1858; ed. coll. schl., Victoria, British Columbia; clk. in bank of B. Columbia (Cariboo agency) 1874, entd. B.C. govt. serv., 1877; gen. sec. B.C. mine owners' assoc., 1899; re-entd. govt. serv. as dep. min. of mines, 1902.

TOMALIN, HERBERT FREDERIC.—B. 1862; M.I.C.E., F.R.I.B. Architects, and F.G.S.; ed. Northampton Gram. schl.; asst. to C.E. and on L. and N.W. rly. wks., 1880-6; dist. engrnr. P.W.D., Ceylon, 1886-90; reported on projected lighthouse sites, southern coast of Ceylon, 1887; dist. engrnr. Colombo harb., foreshore, and drainage wks., 1887-8 and 1890-2; 2nd finan. and office asst. P.W.D., Ceylon, 1890-6; designed and constructed post and telegr. bldgs., Colombo, 1892-5; dist. engrnr., Colombo, 1893-6; dist. engrnr. scheme for water supply to the town of Jaffna, Nov., 1896, to Apr., 1897; engrnr., N.W. Prov. and E. Prov., Aug., 1899, and scheme for a water supply to the town of Trincomalee and to the naval and military cantonment; prov. engrnr., Cent. Prov., Mar., 1903; ditto, W. Prov., Mar., 1904; asst. D.P.W., Jan., 1905; prov. engrnr. Cent. Prov., Sept., 1905; ditto, S. Prov., 31st Mar., 1908; ditto, Uva, Apr., 1913; ditto, C.Prov., May, 1913; ag. conservator of forests, Oct., 1914.

TOMKINS, STANLEY C., C.M.G. (1900).—Appd. by F.O. as asst. dist. offr., Uganda, Feb., 1896; and ch. offr., Uganda rifles, 26th Apr., 1897; served during mutiny in Uganda, 1897-8 (ment. in desp. medal with two clasps); dist. offr. in charge of Kavirondo, Aug., 1897; ag. sub-comsnr. in charge of Uganda Kingdom, May, 1900; jud. offr., Uganda Prot., 21st Mar., 1900; ag. sub-comsnr., W. Prov., May, 1902; sess. judge, May, 1903; sub-comsnr., Sept., 1904; in charge of Uganda Kingdom, Dec., 1904; ag. dep. comsnr., 19th May, 1906, to 20th Feb., 1907; in charge of Uganda Kingdom, 4th Dec., 1907, to 28th Sept., 1908; ag. dep. comsnr., 29th Sept., 1908; ag. gov., 29th Sept. to 19th Oct., 1908; ag. chief sec. to govt., 1st Nov., 1908; ag. gov., Uganda, 1909; retired, 1911.

TOMLINSON, GEORGE JOHN FREDERICK, B.A. (Oxon), Barrister-at-law (Inner Temple).—Served in educn. dept., Transvaal, Feb., 1903, to Oct., 1904; asst. res., N. Nigeria, July, 1907; seconded to G. Coast as dir. of educn., Dec., 1909, to Dec., 1910; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, 1st Apr., 1911.
TOMLINSON, THOMAS SYMONDS.—B.A. Trin. Coll., Camb.; B. 1877; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1901; town mag., Zanzibar, 5th May, 1907; asst. judge of H.B.M. ct. for Zanzibar, 2nd Jan., 1911.
TONNET, LOUIS ARTHUR.—Joined Mauritius police, 16th Feb., 1885; transf. to Seychelles 1893; sub-inspr., 1st Mar., 1902; ag. inspr. since 8th May, 1907; a visiting magistrate for outlying islands.

TOPPIN, CHARLES SAMUEL.—B. 1866; ed. r Diocessan schl., Waterford, Ireland; served as book keeper and acctnt. with W. Australian land co. (Great Southern rly.), 1887 to 1897; on takeover of line entd. govt. ser. as sub-acctnt. rh. dept., 1st Mar., 1897; asst. acctnt., 1st Jul., 1900; ag. ch. acctnt., 1st July, 1903; ag. gen. for State of W. Aust., Feb., 1904; also dep. agen. for Commonwealth, Feb., 1904, to Jan., 1909.

TORONTO, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. J. T SWENNEY, D.D.—B. 1857; ed. at McGill Coll., Montreal, and Trin. Coll., Toronto; ordained 1880; rector of St. Luke's, Montreal, 1889-92; hon. Canon, Toronto, 1889-1905; archdeacon Simcoe, 1905; rector of St. Philip's, Toronto, 1882-1909; archdeacon of York, Toronto, 1909; bishop of Toronto, 1909.

TOTHILL, FRANCIS JAMES.—Extra ast. postmtr.-gen., Ceylon, 28th Oct., 1898; ass. inspr. of post offices, Jan., 1899; asst. sec., crit. irrtn. bd., 15th Jan., 1900; sec., ditta, crit. office asst. to dir. of irrtn., 15th May, 1901; ast. acctnt., gen. treasy., Jan., 1907; ast. financial ast. and acctnt., general treasy., Apr., 1911 to Mar., 1912.

TOUGH, JOHN.—B. 1879; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin; served 11 years in 42nd Highland; 1st cl. cert. of educn., 1896; cert. of mil. educn., 1898; Hythe cert. of musk. (distinguished) 1904; mounted infantry cert., 1905; served in South African War, 1899-1902; Queen's medal with 3 clasps; King's medal and 2 clasps; severely wounded at Magersfontein; sub.-inspr. of pol. drill instr., St. Kitts, 1907; J.P., 1907; ag. inspr. of pol. and adjt. of local forces, 1908; chain tel. bd., 1908; estab. helio. commun. between St. Kitts and Nevis, 1909; sub.-inspr. of pol. and inspr. of roads and works, inspr. of weights and meas., Montserrat, 1909; J.P., 1909; mem. b. health, 1910; sub.-inspr. of pol. and mil. instr., Antigua, 1910; ex-officio J.P. for Leeward Is.; returned to Montserrat, 1913.

TOWNER, H. V.—Mem. Sam. Inst. Asst. mem. C.E.A.; asst. supt. of wks., S. Selma, Mar., 1901; supt. of wks. and surveys, Malaya, Oct., 1904; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, Apr. to Sept., 1902, and from Nov., 1904, to Mar., 1908.
TOWNSEND, ALFRED ERNEST.—Ed. at Brisbane gram. schl.; asst. survr., G. Coast survey, 1902-1905; survr., E. Africa Prot., 24th Aug., 1905.
TOWNSEND, W. H. M.—Ch. offr., Uganda river lake steamers, June, 1903; comdr., July, 1905.

TOWNSEND, WM. RICHARD, B.A., Dublin Univ., 1894; called to the bar, Ireland, 1894; atty.-gen., Gambia, 1st May, 1902; inspr. of schls., regisr. and mem. of exec. and legis. councils, during tenure of atty.-generalship; ch. mag. and M.L.T., Gambia, 6th Nov., 1906; atty.-gen., G. Coast, 1911.

TOWNSEND, SIR CHARLES JAMES, K.B.A. (1911).—B. 1844; ed. Collegiate schl., King's Coll., Windsor, Nova Scotia; B.A., B.C.L., D.C.L.; called to bar, 1866; Q.C., 1881; elec. legis. assem., 1878 and 1882; H. of C., 1884; j. of the sup. ct. of N.S., 1887; ch. just. of N.S., 1897.
TOWNSON, HARRY WALLS, M.R.C.V.S.—Ch. inspr. of stock, Falkland Is., May, 1910.
TOZER, HON. SIR H., K.C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1844; formerly col. sec., Queensland; agt.-gen. for Queensland in London, 1888-1910.
TRAFFORD, H. H.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1913.
TRAFFORD, JOSEPH LEWIS.—B. 1888; ed. Beaumont Coll.; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, Aug., 1914.
TRAILL, FRANCIS STUART FORBES.—Asst. llr., E. Africa Prot., 10th Oct., 1898; joined, 1st pr., 1903.

TRATMAN, DAVID WM.—Ed. at Clifton Coll., Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1900; B.A., 1904; det., Hong Kong, Dec., 1904; passed cadet, 1907; asst. land offr. in New Territories, and registrar land ot., Mar., 1907; mem. of land ct., June, 1907; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, July, 1907; asst. dist. offr., May, 1909; 2nd asst. registr.-n., Sept., 1909; ag. supt. of imports and exports, 1910; 1st asst. registr.-gen., July, 1911; ag. dist. offr., N.T., July, 1911; ag. head of sanitary pt., Nov., 1912; asst. dist. offr. N.T. (South), Nov., 1912; ch. asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, n., 1914.

TREACHER, SIR W. H., K.C.M.G. (1904), M.G. (1890), M.A. (Oxford).—B. 1849; ag. pol. gt., Labuan, Nov., 1871, to June, 1872; ag. col. aud., Oct., 1873; col. sec., aud., and pol. gt., Apr., 1877; admstd. the govt., Dec., 1876, Feb., 1877; and Oct., 1877, to Apr., 1880; mem. of the legis. coun., Dec., 1874. During the period he admstd. the govt. he acted as consul- gen. in Borneo, and visited Sulu and N. Borneo in connection with Spanish claims; apptd. (1881) first gov. of Br. N. Borneo, to Dec., 1887; and acted as admstr. of Labuan and consul-gen. in Borneo, from Feb., 1884, to Nov., 1885, and was instrumental in saving Brunei from an attack by the Limbang rebels; sec. to Perak, June, 1888; ag. res., Sept., 1888; res., Selangor, July, 1889; res., Perak, July, 1896; res. gen., Fed. Malay States, Sept., 1901; ret., 1905.
TREFLE, HON. J. L.—M.L.A., N.S. Wales, 1877; hon. min. and asst. min. of agricul., Oct., 1877; min. of agricul., 7th Nov., 1911; sec. for ds., 1914.

TRENHOLME, HON. NORMAN W.—B. 1837; McGill univ., B.A. and Chapman medal, 1837; B.C.L. and gold medal, 1835; D.C.L., 1837; prof., faculty of law, McGill univ., 1868-73; dean, 1888-1896; K.C. and botomer of the bar, Montreal, 1888-1889; counsel for Quebec in纠纷 between Ontario and Quebec and the minister, 1897-1901; judge, super. ct., Montreal, 1904, and of K.B. and appeal ct., since 1904.
RESIDDER, CAPT. TOMIE JOHN, R.E., C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1850; was engr. for Malta drainage.

RIAY, J. B.—Passed compet. exam. for complementary clerkships, Gibraltar, 1886; sec. Brit. Consulate in Andalusia (Cadiz), 1887; ag. e-consul and ag. consul, 1888; supplementary engr., Gibraltar civ. serv., 1889; after further opt. exam., promoted 3rd cls. clk., 1891; 2nd cls., 1893; 1st cls. clk., 1913; sec. and treas., lors' home comte., since 1895.
RICKETT, W. J., M.L.C.—Postmr.-gen., N. Wales, May, 1883; min. of instruction, May, 4, to Nov., 1885; chmn. of comtees, and dep. of leg. coun., 1900-1912.
RINIDAD, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. JOHN INCIS WELSH.—Ed. at Christ Church, Oxford, 1881; M.A. (1886); D.D., Oxford Univ., 1894; consec. Bishop of Trinidad, 28th Oct., 1904.

ROTTER, CECIL DOUGLAS.—B. 1886; ed. King's Coll.; joined B.S.A. Pol., Jan., 1906; transfd. to native affairs dept., 1907; placed on fixed estabm., 1908; asst. nat. commr., Apr., 1911; J.P. for S. Rhodesia, May, 1911; asst. mag., Belingwe, Dec., 1911 to Mar., 1912; ch. clk. to native affairs dept., native commr., and asst. mag., Sebungwe, May, 1912 to Nov., 1913; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914.

TROWBRIDGE, EDMUND.—B. 1881; ed. pub. model and high schls., Toronto, Ontario; asst. clk., exec. comm., 1906; sec., Alberta coal comm., 1907; sec., Alberta pork comm., 1908; dep. prov. sec. and reg. of companies, prov. of Alberta, 1910.

TROWELL, WM. JOHN.—B. 1864; M.I.N.A.; engnr. and shipwright survr. and examnr. of engns., Liverpool, Feb., 1898; inspr. under bd. of agric., Dec., 1898; inspr. of marine surveys, S. Stltlnta, 16th Jan., 1903; on special ser. in England in connection with expropriation of Tanjong Pagar Dock Co., Ltd., July to Nov., 1905.

TUBOKU-METZGER, ALBERT EMERIC, B.A.—Ed. in C.M.S. Gram. schl. and Fourah Bay Coll., Sierra Leone; gained Fourah Bay Coll. foundation scholarship, Jan., 1877; grad. in Arts, June, 1880; first cls. honors in theology, Durham Univ., Dec., 1881; jun. cls. tutor, Fourah Bay Coll., 1881; entd. S. Leone civ. serv. as extra clk., secretariat and treasy., 1st Mar., 1885; govr.'s office, 1886; col.-treasy., 1887; served as finan. clk. in the Yonni expedn. under Sir Samuel Rowe, 1887; ch. clk., registr.-gen.'s dept., 1890; registr. of births and deaths, Freetown dist., 1890; police clk. and clk. of ct. of requesta, Freetown, Apr., 1895; ag. registr.-gen., 1904 to 1905, and 1907; asst. dist. commr., 1st May, 1908; apptd. Crown prosecutor in the Sept. sessions of the sup. ct. at Sherbro, Oct., 1908; is a J.P. for the col.

TUCKER, LESLIE.—Ed. at Liverpool Collegiate Institn. and Univ. Coll., Liverpool; prelim. and inter. B.A.; 1st cls. scholarship; 1st cls. both years certif. exam.; parchment certif., 1897; head master, govt. boys' schl., St. Helena, 1904; head master, senior schl., St. Helena, 1905; hon. sec., govt. Lace schl., 1908; supervisor of govt. schls., 1910; hon. sec., bd. of educn., 1911.

TUCKETT, GEORGE HENRY.—Imperial postal service, 1891; postmaster, B.C.A. Prot., 1897; asst. agt. and P.M.R., Chinde, 1898; ag. British vice-consul, Chinde, 1899; ag. P.M.G., B.C.A. Prot., 1900-11; ditto, Nyasaland, 1910; ag. paymaster and acctnt. to armed forces, Nyasaland, 1910; deputy P.M.G., Nyasaland, 1911; P.M.G. Nyasaland, Aug., 1912.
TUDHOPE, W., M.B., Bac. Surg.—Medical offr., E.A.P., Sept., 1913.

TUDOR, DANIEL THOMAS, K.C.—B. 1866; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Nov., 1890; eq. dr.; also practised at West Circ.; atty.-gen. of Grenada and St. Vincent, July, 1903; K.C. of both colonies, May, 1908; acted as chief just., also as col. sec. of Grenada on several occasions; admstd. govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1907, to Jan., 1908; and govt. of Grenada, May and June, 1908, and Mar. to May, 1910; commr. to consolidate and revise the Laws of Grenada, 1911; ag. legal ass't., C.O., May to Oct., 1911; chief justice, Bahamas, Nov., 1911.

TUDOR, HON. FRANK GWINNE.—B. 1866; M.P. for Yarra, Victoria; pres. of Melbourne trades hall coun., 1900-1901; elec. to first H. of R., C. of A., 1901; min. of trade and custs., C. of A., 1910-1913, and since Sept., 1914.
TUDOR-OWEN, H.F.G.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1912.

TUPPER, THE RIGHT HON. SIR C., BART. (creat. 1888).—P.C. (1907), G.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.M.G. (1879), C.B. (1867), M.D., L.R.C.S., Edin. (1843).—Born 2nd July, 1821; is an M.A. and D.C.L. of Acadia Coll., Nova Scotia; is gov. of Dalhousie Coll., Halifax (apptd. by Act of Parlmt., 1862); pres. of the Canadian med. assoc. from its formation, 1867, until 1870; mem. of exec. coun., and prov. sec. N.S., 1857-1860; and from 1863 to June, 1867; prime min. of that prov. from 1864 until he ret. from office with his govt. on the Union Act coming into force, 1st July, 1867; deleg. to England on public business from N.S. govt., 1858 and 1865; from the Dominion govt., Mar., 1868; leader of the delegation from N.S. to the union confce. at Charlottetown, 1864; to that in Quebec in same year, and to final col. confce. in London, to complete terms of union, 1866-7; holds patent of rank and precedence from Her Majesty Queen Victoria as an exec. coun. N.S.; sworn of the P.C., June, 1870, and pres. of that body until 1st July, 1872, when apptd. min. of int. rev.; min. of customs, Feb., 1873; resig. office with Sir John Macdonald, 5th Nov., 1873; min. of pub. wks., Oct., 1878; min. of rly. and canals, 1879; represented Cumberland in parlmt. for 29 years in N.S. Assem., from 1855 until confederation in 1867, and in the Commons from that year until he resig. his seat in the cabinet and was apptd. high comsnr. for Canada, in London, May 24th, 1884; acted as exec. comsnr. for Dominion at the Antwerp Exhibn., 1885, and the Col. and Ind. Exhibn., London, 1886; resumed office in Canadian ministry as finance min., 1887; one of H.M.'s plenipotentiaries at fisheries confce. at Washington, 1887; re-apptd. high comsnr., May, 1888; joint plenipo., with the Marquis of Dufferin and Sir J. A. Crowe, for negotiating a commercial arrangement between Canada and France, 1892; S. of S. for Canada, Jan., 1896; premier, Mar. to June, 1896; leader of opposition in H. of C., 1896-1900.

TUPPER, HON. SIR CHAS. HIBBERT, K.C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1865; son of Sir C. Tupper, mem. of N.S. bar; grad. Harvard law schl.; mem., house of commons, Canada, 1882-1900; min. of marine and fisheries, 1888-1895; deleg. to Washington in connection with Behring Sea difficulty, 1890; Br. agt. for the arbitration with the U.S. on the Behring Sea question, 1892-3; min. of just., 1895; resig., 1896; mem. of priv. coun. for Canada.

TURGEON, WILLIAM FERDINAND ALPHONSE, K.C. B.A.—B. 1877; ed. at New York city and Laval univ., Montreal; barrister; atty.-gen. of Sask., 23rd Sept., 1907; elec. to Sask. legis., 1907; re-elec., 1908 and 1912.
TURLEY, HON. HENRY.—B. 1859; M.L.A., Queensland, 1893-1902; elected to the Senate, C. of A., 1903; pres. of Senate, July, 1910-1913.

TURNBULL, AUBREY MARRIOTT DALWAY.—Ed. Bath Coll.; clk., treasry., B.C. Africa, Jan., 1903; editor, B. C. A. Gazette, Nov., 1903, to Sept., 1906; 3rd cls. res., May, 1906; ag. registrar of high ct., offl. recvr. and liquidator, May to Dec., 1908; ag. res. Mlanje dist., July, 1909, to Mar., 1910, and May to Nov., 1912; dist. mag., July, 1906; ag. supt. of native affairs and comm'l. intell. offr., May, 1910, to Mar., 1911; attached to deputy govt's. office for special duty, Sept., 1911, to Apr., 1912; ag. sec. to the administration, Nyasaland Prot., Dec., 1912; asst. ch. sec., Mar., 1914.

TURNER, THE RT. HON. SIR GEORGE, P.C.-K.C.M.G. (1897), LL.D.—B. 1851; premier and treas. of Victoria, 1894-9 and 1900-1; represented the col. in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee 1897; pres. of conf. of premiers at Melbourne, 1899; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as treasurer, Jan., 1901; treas. in 2nd (Deakin) ministry; treas. in 4th (Reid-Turner) coalition ministry, 1904; retired from politics, 1906.

TURNER, SIR GEORGE, K.B. (1913).—M.R.C.S. L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.B., D.P.H. (Cantab.); lect. on Hygiene, Guy's hosp., 1882-1895; examin. for D.P.H., roy. coll. of phys. and surgs., Lond., 1890-1895; employed by L.G.B. to report upon diphtheria, 1884; and by L.C.C. to inquire into epidemics of enteric fever and the sanitation of River Lea, 1894-95; med. offr. of health, Lond., 1895-1900; mem. of Cape med. coun., 1898-1900; dir. of govt. Rinderpest experimental sta., Kimberley, 1897-98; med. offr. of health, Transvaal, 1st Aug., 1900; census comm., Transvaal, 1904; ret., 1908; del. for Transvaal and O.R.C., Internat. Leprosy conf., Bergen, 1910.

TURNER, JAMES POGSON.—B. 1871; clk., parl. library, St. Kitts, 1890; clk., regiar's off., St. Kitts, 1897; comsnr. to administer oatha, 1897; clk. to mag., dist. "C," St. Kitts, May to Oct., 1905; 1st clk., regiar's off., Antigua, 3rd May, 1906; comsnr. to admstr. oatha, Antigua, May, 1906; clk. to trustees, sup. ct. library, 1906; dir. to regiar. of jt. stock companies, Nov., 1906; regiar. and prov.-marshal, Dominica, Oct., 1910, to Feb., 1912; ditto, Antigua, and ch. regiar., Leeward Is., 17th Feb. to 31st March, 1912.

TURNER, EDINBURGH LEWIS JAMES BARNETSON.—B. 1886; ed. Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon ser., Oct., 1908; offr. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Dec., 1909; pol. mag., Gampola, Nov., 1910; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., May, 1913; comsnr. of excise, Nov., 1913; asst. comsnr., Div., Jan., 1914.

TURNER, WILLIAM.—Ed. at Abdn. and Edin. Univas.; M.A. (Abdn.), 1876; M.B. Edin., 1879; M.D., 1881; res. phys., roy. hosp. for sick children, Edin., 1879; non-res. phys., roy. infirmary, Edin., 1880; res. accoucheur, Glasgow maternity hosp., 1881; mem. roy. med. soc., Edin., fell obstetric soc., Edin.; asst. surg. civil hosp., Gibraltar, 1882; also surg. div. prison, med. offr. lunatic asyl., and dist. med. offr., 1882; pub. vaccinations, pd. surg., 1888; surg. to col. hosp., Oct., 1889.

TUTCHER, WM. JAMES, F.L.S.—B. 1868; ed. at Merchant Venturers' schl., Bristol; entered Royal Gardens, Kew, 1888; asst. sup. botan. and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1890; aspt., 1910; J.P., 1911; sometime hon. lecturer on botany, coll. of med. for Chinese; author of "Gardening for Hong Kong"; joint auth. with S. T. Dunn, of "Flora of Kwangtung and Hong Kong."

TWYCROSS, HENRY WILLIAM STEPHENS.—Entd. teleg. dept., Cape Col., 1st Dec., 1875; cls. clk., secretarial branch, G.P.O., 28th Aug., 1893; prin. clk. 1st July, 1897; attached to agt. gen. off., in London, on special duty, Apr. to Sept., 1902; ag. survr. and dist. engnr., Western post dist., Cape, 7th Dec., 1902, to 30th Sept., 1903; ch. clk., secs', office, 1st Jan., 1905; asst. clk., 1st Feb., 1908; under sec., for posts and telegr., U. of S. Africa, 19th Oct., 1910; is a J.P.
TYLER, JAMES ERNEST.—Govt. printer, Stltims., 1st July, 1905.

TYRRELL, FRANCIS GRAMME.—B. 1876; Pembroke coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1900; ag. priv. sec. to H.E. the governor, Aug., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the admstr. the govt., Apr., 1902; off. asst. to govt. agt., Cent. prov., Jan., 1903-Dec., 1904; ag. priv. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1904; second

TYSER, Sir C. R., Kt. B.A. (1909).—Ed. at Ugbey and Trim Coll., Camb., M.A. (1872); called to bar, In Tem., 1873; asst. ag. judic. msnr. and leg. advsr., Cyprus, 1880-1; atty.-gen., sewards, 1886-9; pres. dist. ct., Kyrenia, Cyprus, 95; puisne judge, Cyprus, Jan., 1902; ch. just., 96; author of "Marine Insurance Losses," 94; wrote translation of Omar Hilmi Effendi's vaf Law, 1899; and of the Mejjelé, 1901.

TYSEN, Francis Dashwood.—Ed. at Melbourne Church of England Cram. schl., and in Coll., Melbourne Univ.; lieut. 2nd Scottish orse, S. African War; inspr. of pol., E. Africa, Oct., May, 1908; asst. dist. supt. of pol., 1st liv., 1906; supt. of pol., 1910.

UNIACKE, Capt. Andrew Gore, D.S.O.—Served with N.W. mtd. pol., Canada (including ukon exped.), 1893-1898; served in S. African war, with Canadian forces and M.I., 1899-1902 (medals, 8 clasps, D.S.O.); dist. supt., Nigeria police, 1903; served with exped. against Okpotos, 1903-4 (W. African medal and spn); comsrr. and ag. dep. inspr.-gen., Nigeria police, 1911.

UNIACKE, G. L.—Served as trooper in mtd. l., W. Australia, 1895 to 1899; private in 2nd contingent W. Austn. M.I., S. African war, 1899-1901 (Queen's medal and 5 clasps); recd. men. in 4th Batt. King's Own (R. Lancaster gt.), 1902; seconded for serv. with N. Nigeria gt., W.A.F.F., 6th Sept., 1902; served in the ane-Sokoto campaign (medal with clasp), 1903; company comdr., N. Nigeria regt., 1908; noted brevet-majority, 1909; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 10, with appt. dated 6th Sept., 1902; still holds men. as lieut. in 3rd Batt. King's Own (R. Lancs. Regt.), special reserve.

URICH, Frederick William, C.M.Z.S., E.S.—Ed. in Trinidad, Real Gymnasium, Obertzen, and Geneva Univ.; ledger keeper, wt. printing dept., Trinidad, Jan., 1896; acctnt., extensions, Aug., 1895; 2nd clk., educ. pt., Jan., 1899; store-keeper, P.W.D., Mar., 91; acctnt., educ. dept., Sept., 1905; also clk., coll. coun., Oct., 1906; ch. clk., educ. dept., dist. asst. inspr. of schls., Dec., 1906; mem. of L. of management and hon. sec. to Victoria ut., 1894 to 1895, and 1899 to 1901; ag. sec. to vic. soc'y., May to June, 1894; ag. sec. to hd. educ., Feb. to Apr., 1900; ag. statistical clk., librarian, col. secretariat, Apr. to Oct., 1907; forest offr., Apr. to Oct., 1908. holds a coman. lieut. in local forces, Trinidad, since 1897; capt. and adjt., T.L.I.V., 1908; seconded to dept. of vic. as entomologist, 1909.
URQUHART, Charles Frederic Robert Liddiard.—B. 1867; entd. Crown Agent's off., Jan., 1885; supt. of stock transfer off., 1895; registrar, inscribed stocks and head of stock dept., 1905.

UTIDJIAN, H. A. S., I.S.O. (1914).—English translator to Turkish Adm'y., and translator and iv. sec. to Admiral Augustus Hobart Pasha 1874; joint treas. and acctnt., T. Squadron, Cretan isters, 1876; dragonman and traner. under Mr. Walter Baring, 2nd sec. to H.B.M.'s Embassy, Constantinople, during sittings of spec. comm. apptd. try the authors of the Bulgarian atrocities in 76-77; 2nd in charge and res. Turkish professor student dragomans establishment under H.B.M.'s embassy, Constantinople, Jan. to Aug., 1878; translator of state documents, Cyprus, since 21st Aug., 1878; acted as attache to comsrr., Nicosia, and as asst. collr. of tithes in 1878-1880; Turkish examiner, 1879-1907; Turkish translator to leg. coun., Cyprus, 1883; traner., land registn. comm., 1901; sec. to Moslem secondary educ. bd., 1902-1904; apptd. to examine and report on general state of Turkish instruction in Idadi schl., 1905; 4th cls. Mejidieh and Osmanieh Orders.

VALANTIN, W. Adolphe.—Asst. clk. and messenger, Queen's advoc.'s chambers, S. Leone, Jan., 1878; acted as asst. sec. to Br. comsrr. on the N.W. Liberian Bndry. Comsrr. in 1879; clk. to crown solr., June, 1880; acted as clk. to ch. just., 1882; clk. to Queen's advoc., 1883; dep. sheriff, Mar., 1890; clk. to ch. just., Aug., 1891; dep. coroner, July, 1895; ch. clk. atty.-gen.'s chambers, Dec., 1897; curator, intestate estates, Jan., 1902.

VANDER BEEK, J. N., I.S.O. (1903).—Extra clk., col. sec'y.'s office, Singapore, Sept., 1871; 8th clk., Jan., 1873, and attached to govt. house; 6th clk., Dec., 1875; guardian of govt. house, May, 1877; 5th clk., col. sec'y.'s office, Apr., 1879; 4th clk., June, 1879; 3rd clk., June, 1880; clk., govt. house, Dec., 1883; financial asst., pol., 1905.

VAN DER MERWE, Johannes Lodewicus.—Served under govt. of late S.A.R. as mining comsrr. from 1885 till British occupation, 1st Sept., 1900; mining comsrr., Barberton, 1885; ag. spec. land-drost, Barberton; chrmn., diggers' comtee., Barberton; chrmn., Barberton hosp. bd.; mining comsrr., Johannesburg, 1893; chrmn., Johannesburg hosp. bd.; chrmn., Jameson Raid comsrr., 1895-6; mem. of comsrr. to inquire into causes of dynamite explosion at Johannesburg, 1896; mem. of dynamite relief comtee.; mem. of Johannesburg liquor comsrr.; pres., Witwatersrand agric. soc'y.; hon. mem. Witwatersrand chamber of mines; served during Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1902; prin. clk., townships (mines dept.), Transvaal, 1903; ag. deputy asst. regisr. of mining rights, Johannesburg, 1906; mem. of trading bd., Witwatersrand, 1907.

VAN DER MEULEN, Frederick A.—B. 1875; ed. at Blundell's Schl., Tiverton and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hons. in mod. hist.), 1896; M.A., 1900; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1900; Western circuit; asst. dist. comsrr., Sierra Leone, 1907; solr.-gen., 1908; ag. atty.-gen. and also ag. puis-ne and circuit judge on various occasions, 1908-1911; ch. mag., Gambia, 1913.

VAN DE VELDE, Marcel Arthur Maurice.—Ed. at Mt. St. Mary's Coll., Derbyshire; clk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., 29th May, 1900; tempy. asst. audr., E. Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., 9th Mar., 1903; asst. audr., Uganda Prot., June, 1903; asst. audr., E. Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., 1904; local audr., Uganda Prot., 16th Nov., 1909; audr., Uganda Prot., 1st May, 1910.

VANE, Henry G. B.—B. 1861; asst. audr., Perak, Dec., 1884; ag. asst. audr.-gen., S.S., Apr., 1887; audr., Sungei Ujong and Negri Sembilan, May, 1888; treas., pol. imk., etc., Sungei Ujong, Oct., 1890; audr., Selangor, Dec., 1892; state audr., Perak, Apr., 1895; rev. audr., Perak, Jan., 1903; ag. fin. comsrr., Oct., 1903, to Oct., 1904; ch. audr., cent. audit off., Jan., 1904; ag. finance comsrr., Oct., 1906, to 31st Jan., 1907; treas., F.M.S., 26th Nov., 1906.

VAN EEDEN, WALTER CLARENCE.—Entd. cust. dept., B. Guiana, 1891; lieut., B.G. militia, 1897-1900; Hythe schl. of musk. certif. for musk. and maxin guns, 1899; attached to 2nd batt. Dorsetshire regt., and gained certif. of proficiency, 1899; asst. inspr. of constab., G. Coast, 30th June, 1900; Ashanti war, medal and gratuity, 1900; offr. commdg. dist. of Bole, 1901; lieut., 3rd batt. Dorsetshire regt., Aug., 1901; lieut., G. Coast regt., W.A.F.F., 1901; certif. E.511, schl. of instr., Chelsea barracks, Dec., 1901; on expmdn. against the Fra Fras., 1902; offr. commdg., Black Volta dist., 1902; transf. to cust. dept., G. Coast, 3rd Aug., 1903; supervisor in charge, Accra cust. dist., 1904; supervisor in charge, S. dist., W. Frontier preventative service, 1906-7; supervisor in charge, Axim cust. dist., 1907-10, 1912-13, and harbrmr., Axim, 1909-10 and 1912-13; supervisor in charge, Addah-Quittah cust. dist., 1910-11; supervisor in charge, Saltpond dist., 1911; supervisor in charge, Winnebah dist., 1913; attached to Impl. customs, London, for a general course, 1909, and at Southampton in Aug.-Sept., 1913; 1st cls. supervisor of cust., 24th Feb., 1909.
VAN HEERDEN, HON. H. C., M.L.A.—Formerly chmn. of comtes.; min. of agric., Union of S. Africa, 1913.

VANIER, ABRAHAM.—B. 1887; ed. Queen's Coll. B. Guiana; Primary and Mitchell scholar; admitted solr., B. Guiana, 30th Sept., 1911; clk. of ct., Georgetown judl. dist., Apr., 1912; ditto, S. Essequebo, May, 1912; ditto, Berbice, June, 1912.
VAN REENEN, REENEN JACOB.—Supt. of roads and local wks., O.F.S. Prov., Union of S. Africa, 10th May, 1912.

VAN SOMEREN, ROBERT ABRAHAM LOGAN.—Ed. at Edin. Univ. and Royal Coll. of Surg., Edin; M.B., Ch.B.; spec. univ. certif., Edin.; trop. diseases, and certif. of London Schl. of Trop. Med.; med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prote., 10th Oct., 1905.
VAN SOMEREN, V. G. L.—Med. offr., E. Africa Prote., April, 1912.

VAN VELDEN, DIRK ELIZA.—B. 1869; ed. at Gymnasium and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; clk., auditor-gen.'s off., 1896; ass't sec., second volkraad, S. African Repub., 1896; sec. to ditto, 1899; sec. to Transvaal govt., Sept., 1900, to 1902; clk. ass't to legis. coun., Transvaal, 1907; is now clk. of the prov. coun., Transvaal, and clk. of the exec. comte.; author of "Peace Negotiations between Boer and Briton in South Africa."

VASSALEO, S.—Ed. Malta Lyceum and Malta Univ.; M.D., 1913; govt. exhibtn. for science, 1906-1907; ditto for medicine, 1909-1913; post-graduate work at Univ. Coll. Hosp. and London Hosp.; clinician asst., St. John's Hosp., W., and Royal Ear Hosp.; supernumerary med. offr., Leeward Is., 1914.

VAUDIN, WILLIAM MARSHALL, I.S.O. (1914).—B. 1866; ed. Royal Coll., Maur.; writer, P. W. D., Maur., Jan., 1888; sworn id. surv., May, 1890; ag. asst. govt. surv., May to Oct., 1890, May, 1891, to July, 1892, Dec., 1897, to Sept., 1900; asst. surv., Seychelles, Dec., 1900; supertit. of pub. wks. and surveys, Aug., 1901; and chrmn., local bd. of health, central dist., Apr., 1902; ag. auditor Seychelles, 9th May to 10th Nov., 1907, 8th Feb. to 20th Sept., 1908, 2nd Feb. to 10th Apr., 1911, and from 9th May, 1912; ag. pol. mag., 26th Mar., 1912; mem. exec. and legis. couns. Seychelles, 26th Mar., 1912.

VAUDREY, CLAUDE HENRY SLADE.—B. 1882; ed. Bilton Grange and Rugby; served in S. African war, 1901-1902 (Queen's medal and threepenny); lieut., 6th Manchester Militia; capt., reserve of officers, 1913; passed final exam. to M. Soc., 1908; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 1909; J.P., 1909; ag. pol. mag. in 1909, 1911; apptd. circuit judge for one session, 1909; ag. pub. prosecutor in 1911; attached to Anglo-French Boundary Com., 1912; assisted crown prosecutor on special comm. court in connection with Human Leopard Society trials, 1913; sec., 1914; attached to Army Service Corps ag. transport offr., Freetown, Aug., 1914.

VAUGHAN, CHARLES STEWART.—B. 1887; ed. Harrow and Trin. Coll., Cambridge (M. cadet, Ceylon cir. ser., Oct., 1889; off. asst. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, Aug., 1891; fiscal Off. Prov., and off. asst. to govt. agt., Kandy, 1896; asst. collr. of cust., Trincomalie, May, 1898; asst. govt. agt., Vavuniya and Mullaitivu, Sept., 1898; dist. judge, Batticaloa, June, 1902; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalie, Feb., 1902; Aug., 1906; Kegalla, May, 1902; asst. govt. agt., N.C. Prov., June, 1906; Oct., 1907; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Mar., 1909; govt. agt., Prov., Sept., 1909; ditto, Cent. Prov., 1911; asst. govt. agt., E. Prov., Jan., 1912; dist. judge, May, 1913; ag. govt. agt., N. Prov., Aug., 1913; ditto, C. Prov., Feb., 1914.
VAUGHAN, CRAWFORD.—M.H.A., Australia, 1905; treas., and commr. of lands and imgrm., 1910-1912; now leader of opposition.

VAUGHAN, JOHN ALFRED.—M. Inst. M.I. Mech. E., M. Amer. Soc. E.K.; B.M. engineer offr., R.N., 1887-1902; chief inspr. of machinery, mines dept., Transvaal, 12th Aug., 1901; ch. inspr. of machinery, mines and industries dept., Union of S. Africa, 1910.

VAUTIER, JOHN FRANCIS.—B. 1863; repatriation dept., O.R.C., 30th Dec., 1897; acctnt., govt. relief dept., 1st Nov., 1898; examr. of accts., treasy., 1st July, 1904; bookkeeper, treasy., 4th Apr., 1906; principal clk., prov. admtn.
VECCHIO, THOMAS JOSEPH.—Marshal, ex-bkrptcy, ct. and V.-A. ct., Gibraltar 1866; interp., 1866-76.

VELLA, TOMMASO.—Entd. Malta service, 1865; clk., May, 1866; dep. collr. of cust., 1866; dep. postmr., 1896; collr. of cust. and postmaster, Sept., 1902; postmr.-gen., May, 1903; asst. of ex. comm. and comm. of govt., 1903; postmr.-gen., 1911.

VELLACOTT, PHILIP NORTHCOTT, Lond. (hona. in for. and obst. med.), 1888; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Eng.), 1896; F.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1901.—House surg., res. obstetrician and asst., Guy's hosp.; asst. curator of museum, George's hosp.; civ. surg., S. African field hosp., 1901 to 1902; capt., S.A. Constab., 6th Jan., 1902; transf. to O.R.C. govt. serv. as med. offr., National hosp., Bloemfontein, 1st Dec., 1902; fellow of R. Inst. of pub. health; deleg. from O.R.C. to 2nd Internat. Congress on School Hygiene.

VENNING, G.—B. 1879; apptd., after further exam., asst. clk., and assigned to local govt., May, 1899; after further exam., asst. clk., and assigned to W.O., 8th Jan., 1900; transf. to C.O., 10th Apr., 1903.

VEREKER, STANDISH HENRY PRENDIMBE, R. 1878; served in I.Y. in S. African War, 1901 (med. in desps., awarded medal "For distinguished conduct in the field"); served under F.O. as British vice-consul, and frequently ting consul, Cherbourg, Jan., 1902 to Sept., 1905; assisted partly, comte, on vagrancy, Nov., 1905, to Mar., 1906; asst., res., N. Nigeria, 1st pt., 1906.

VERGETTE, EDWARD DUDLEY.—B., 1878; ed.; Schil., Peterborough; admitted solr. of p. ct., England, 1903; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1907; ag. pol. mag., ne to Sept., 1908; crown prosecutor, Jan., 1913.
VERRAN, HON. JOHN.—M.H.A., S. Australia, 1911; premier and comsnr. of pub. wks., 1910-12; also min. of mines, 1910-1912.

VICKERS, EDWARD.—B., 1842; Emmanuel ll., Camb.; B.A., 1864; called to the bar, In m., Jan., 1868; advoc. in the high ct., mbay, Dec., 1868; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1878; acted on several occasions as puisne judge, 1904 to 1904; jun. puisne judge, 1st Apr., 1904.

VICTORIA, RIGHT REV. GERARD HEATH NDER, Lord Bishop of Victoria, Hong Kong—1861; ed. at Trin. Coll. and Ridley Hall, mb., B.A., 1884; M.A., 1888; D.D., 1907; sec. at Lambeth Parish Church, 29th June, 1876; curate of St. Bride's, Liverpool, 1884 to 1888; vicar of St. Benedict's, Liverpool, 1888 to 1896; of Litherland, 1896 to 1905; of St. Cyprian's, Liverpool, 1905 to 1907; chmn., Litherland schl., 1898 to 1903; chaplain to Liverpool dist. lay elders, 1892 to 1905.
VIDAL, M. R. R.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1912.

VILJOEN, WILLEM JACOBUS, M.A., L.N.C., ll. D.—B., 1869; ed. at Normal Coll., Cape Town, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and S. African ll., Capetown; apptd. to staff, Normal Train. Coll., Capetown, 1889; after graduation studied at Unive. of Amsterdam, Leyden, Strasbourg, and in 1894 took degree of Ph.D. (in modern languages and comparative philology); ptd. prof. of modern languages, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Sept., 1894; elected mem. of coun. Univ. of C. of G.H., 1897; mem. of Deutscher Verband der Niederländische Letteren in den, 1900; dir. of educn., O.F.S. Prov., Apr., 1910; chmn., Grey Univ. Coll. coun., 1911; presented Union of S. Africa at Impl. Educn. office, in London, June, 1911; joint editor of Vederlands Woordenboek voor Zuid-Afrika, and "English-Dutch and Dutch-English Dictionary" (in the simplified spelling).

VILLIERS, FRANCIS JOHN, C.M.G. (1880).—1851; entered the C.O., Sept., 1869; priv. sec. the admtr. of Grigualand W., Oct., 1875, and of the coun.; ag. col. sec., May, 1877, to Oct., 1879; mem. of legis. coun., 1879; engaged on special vice in connection with the annexation of the n. to the Cape Col. from Nov., 1880, to May, 1881; audr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882; ag. gov't. sec., various times between 1883 and 1896; ret., 1898.

VINCENT, JOSEPH ULRIC.—B., 1872; B.A., Ph., Univ. of Ottawa; called to the bar, Ontario, 1897; K.C., Ontario, 1910; comsnr. of cost of living comsnr., 1913; dep. min. of educn. and revenue, Canada, Oct., 1914.

VIRET, ALFRED PERCIVAL.—B., 1865; rev. offr., minica, May, 1882; sec. bds. of health and quarantine, Aug., 1884; sec. to local comte., Ind. Col. Exhibn., 1886; conf. clk. to pres. and exec. coun., May, 1886; gov't. offr., treas. and quarantine offr., June, 1891; 1st sl. supervisor of st., G. Coast, Oct., 1897; asst. col. treas., S. Leone, July, 1899; acted as col. treas., S. Leone, July, 1900, and from Oct. to Mar., 1902; apptd. collector of customs, 3rd Nov., 1902; ag. col. treas., Nov., 1902, to Apr., 1903, and from May to Aug., 1904; selected in May, 1905, to carry out transfer of the Isles-de-Los to France, under Anglo-French convention of 1904.

VISCHER, HANS.—B., 1876; ed. in Switzerland and Germany, St. Lawrence Coll., Ramsgate, Emmanuel Coll. and Ridley Hall, Cainb.; M.A. Cantab.; R.G.S. diploma for surveying; Royal Geographical Back. Bequest; African General Service Medal, 1904; served under C.M.S. at Loko, N. Nigeria, 1900 to 1901; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Sept., 1903; 3rd cls. res., 15th Feb., 1906; 2nd cls. res., 1st Apr., 1910; seconded as director of educn. since 1908.
VITALIS, NICHOLAS.—Ch. interp. and clk. to comsnr., Larnaka, Cyprus, July, 1878; ditto, Nicosia, Mar., 1879; clk. and interp. to Queen's advoc., Feb., 1882; registr., sup. ct., Cyprus, 1905.

VOLNEY, JOSEPH HENRY LOUIS.—B., 1875; entd. pub. ser., St. Lucia, Feb., 1892, as superm. clk., post off.; ag. 4th clk., treasy., Apl. and May, 1893; ag. rev. offr., June to Nov., 1893; clk., immig. off., Feb., 1894; 4th clk., treasy., Apl., 1894; ag. 3rd clk., in 1895, 1896, and 1897; 3rd clk., treasy., Jan., 1898; ag. 2nd clk., treasy. acctnt., May to Oct., 1898; ag. 2nd clk. and cashier, treasy., Aug., 1900, to Apl., 1901; 2nd clk., registr.'s offr., Apl., 1901; ag. clk., Aug. to Oct., 1901; ag. audit clk., Aug. to Nov., 1902; transfd. on temporary duty to pub. wks. dept.; ch. clk., registr.'s dept., Apl., 1903; ag. registr. on three occasions in 1908-10-11.

WADDELL, GEORGE, A.M.I.C.E., Diploma R.I.E.C.—B., 1862; ed. Lurgan Coll. and Royal Indian Engineering Coll., Cooper's Hill; asst. engr., Clougher Valley rly., 1884; asst. surv., Ceylon survey dept., 1886; dist. surv., 1887; dist. engr., P.W.D., Anuradhapura, 1888; Matara, 1889; Dikoya, 1893; Negombo, 1897; seconded for serv. under the govt. of Seychelles, 1898; dist. engr., Dikoya, 1900; ag. prov. engr., N. Prov., Feb., 1904; confirmed in apptnt., Oct., 1904; prov. engr., prov. of Sabaragamuwa, 1907; senr. prov. engr., 1913; dep. D.P.W., 1914.
WADDELL, HON. THOMAS.—B., 1854; M.L.A. for various districts in N.S.W. since 1887 (with only short interval); col. treas., 1901-4; premier, 1904; col. treas., 1907.
WADDINGTON, E. J.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913.
WADE, A. DE V.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912.

WADE, HON. CHAS. GREGORY, K.C.—Called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1886; Crown proct., New S. Wales, 1891; proct. for West. circuit and cent. crim. ct., 1894, and resigned, 1902; ag. dist. ct. judge and chmn. of sess. on various occasions; M.L.A. for Willoughby, New S. Wales, 1903; M.L.A. for Gordon, 1904; atty.-gen. and min. for just., Aug., 1904; premier of N.S.W., 1907-1910.

WAGNER, WILLIAM QUINN.—B., 1849; ed. at S. African Coll., Capo Town; sec. to the Krijgsraad under pres. Burgess, in the first Secoocent war, 1876; filled various ag. appts. in Landdrost's off., Rustenburg, subsequently becoming postmaster and town clk. of that place; served in various capacities under O.F.S. govt., 1881 to 1900; mine inspr., Jagersfontein, 1891 to 1900; supt., refugee camp, Heilbron, O.R.C., May, 1901, to Mar., 1902; joined mines dept., Transvaal, May, 1902.

WAIAPO, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. WILLIAM WALMSLEY SEDGWICK, M.A., B., 1888; ed. Maidstone Gram. Schl. and Christ Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1894; served in marine batt. with ships at Suakin, 1884-5 (Egyptian medal with Suakin clasp and Khedive's bronze star); vicar of Waikari, New Zealand, 1901-3; of Akarva, 1903-4; of St. Luke's, Christchurch, 1904-13; Bishop of Waiapu, 1914.

WAIT, WALTER ERNEST.—B. 1878; ed. Fettes and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1902; addl. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., and adml. comnr. of requests and pol. mag., Minnara and Puttalam during pearl fishery, Feb., 1905; asst. land settm. offr., May, 1905; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Dec., 1910; asst. land settm. offr., Feb. 1911; lieut., Ceylon Planters' Rifle Corps.

WAKEMAN, GEORGE HERBERT.—Ed. Bradford Coll., Berks, and Camb.; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, July, 1891; asst. land offr., Hong Kong, June, 1900; J.P., Dec., 1900; ag. dep. land offr. and offr. recvr. in bankruptcy, Apr. to Dec., 1901; ag. land offr. and offr. recvr. in bankruptcy, Jan. to Nov., 1902, and Jan. to Aug., 1905; comnr. for oaths, sup. ct., Hong Kong, and comnr. for taking acknowledgments of married women, Aug., 1905; land offr. and offr. recvr. in bankruptcy, Aug., 1905; ag. regis. of trade marks and patents, Aug., 1910; ag. regis. of sup. ct., offr. adminstr., offr. trustee and regis. of companies, Sept., 1910 to Aug., 1911, and June, 1914.

WALCOTT, HENRY BARCLAY.—C.M.G. (1913); B. 1866; excise and customs offr., St. Lucia, 1884; treas. acctnt., 1890; ag. ch. clk., treas., 1891; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., St. Vincent, 1892; ag. treas. acctnt., Grenada, 1892; ch. clk., audit office, Dec., 1893; ag. postmnr., 1895-6; ag. ch. of pol. and excise, 1897; 2nd audit clk., col. sec's off., Br. Honduras, 1897; auditor, Feb., 1898; ch. asst. treas., Gold Coast, Jan., 1900; ag. treas., and mem. ex. and legis. couns., dir., pub. offr.'s guarantee fund, mem. bd. of educn., July, 1900, to Jan., 1901, and May to Oct., 1902; ag. comnr. of police and supt. of prisons, 1901-1902; pres. Accra town coun., 1901-1902; treas. and collr. of cust., regis. of shipping, pres., quarantine bd., chmn. poor relief comm., mem. of bd. of educn., mem. ex. and legis. couns., Br. Honda, Mar., 1903; J.P., Apr., 1903; ag. col. sec., 22nd July to 6th Aug., 1905, 10th Nov., 1905, to 12th Aug., 1906, and from 8th May, 1908; collr. of cust. and detaining offr., M.S. Act, Trinidad, 1909.

WALKER, HENRY.—2nd asst., land dept., Br. N. Borneo, Jan., 1883; asst. supt., lands and pub. wks., Kudat, July, 1883; comnr., land, and recorder of lands, May, 1884; mem. coun., Oct., 1883; ag. supt., P.W. dept., Jan., 1887.
WALKER, Hopson Puckney, B.A.—Jesus Coll., Camb., 1833; LL.B. (Lond.), M.A. (Melb.); barrister-at-law (Mid. Temp.), 1888; ch. clk., sup. ct., Melbourne, Dec., 1883.

WALKER, JAMES HUTCHINSON.—Ed. Abdn. Univ., M.A. (nat. sci. hon.), 1876; M.B., C.M. (highest hon., Murray scholar, and gold medal), 1878; M.D. 1884; asst. prof., botany, Abdn., 1876; pin. med. offr., N. Borneo, Aug., 1881.

WALKER, JOHN C.—4th batt. Yorkshire regt.; Coorga Nagpore rifles; served with Yorks. I.Y. in S. Africa (two medals with 5 clasps); asst. polit. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1905; mentioned in despatches, 1906; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1910.

WALKER, Lt.-Col. R.S.Frowd, C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1850; entered Sandhurst, 1869; ensign 28th foot, 1871; capt., June, 1881; hon. lieut.-col., 1889; A.D.C. to govt., S.S., 1878-9; attached to Perak armed pol., Mar., 1879; dep. comnr. of the force, Feb., 1880, and in command from Nov. 1882; acted on various occasions as pol. mag., asst. res., and as sec. to govt.; has received the thanks of govt. several times for his action in dealing with Chinese and coolie disturbances; in command of Perak expeditionary force, 1892 and 1894; ag. sec. to govt., Perak, Oct., 1894, to Aug., 1895; organised Mal. States Guides, 1896; and since commands regt.; ag. Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1899; Jan., 1900; ditto, Perak, Apr., 1900, to Jan., 1901; ret. 1910.
WALKER, HON. THOMAS.—M.L.A. for Australia, since 1905; atty.-gen. and minister of educn., Oct., 1911.

WALKER, WILLIAM HENRY, I.S.O. (1912).—B.A.—B.1864; apptd. 3rd class clk., govt. office, Canada; 1887; promoted to 2nd cls. 1st cls., 1896; ch. clk., 1908; chf. clk., dep. external affairs, 1909; asst. under-secretary, external affairs, 1912.
WALKEY, F. A.—B. 1895; apptd. to compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the service, and assigned to India office, 19th May, 1913; transfd. to C.O., 27th June, 1913.

WALL, JAMES HOBNE DARRELL.—B. 1877; clk. and storekeeper, Holberton Instit., Antigua, Nov., 1891; shorthand reporter to W. Ind. Comsn., 1897; aud. def. force accts.; ag. chmn. to aud.-gen., Leeward and fedrl. accts., Jan., 1899; also clk. to trade and income tax comm., Sept., 1899; ch. clk. to aud.-gen. and fedrl. accts., May, 1900; ag. treas. of Antigua and fedral; May-Oct., 1909, and Aug., 1910; ag. insp. schls., Leeward Is., Apr., 1911, to Aug., 1912; also ag. treas. of Antigua, and federal treas., Mar.-Apr. and Aug.-Sept., 1912.
WALLACE, LAWRENCE AUBRET, C.M.G. (1910).—Acting admstr., N.E. Rhodesia, July-Nov., 1907; ag. admstr., N.W. Rhodesia, Jan., 1908.

WALLACE, SIR WM., C.M.G. (1897).—Senior ex. offr., Roy. Niger Co., 1886; later as agt.-gen., until 1900, when territories were handed over to H.M. govt.; exchanged numerous treaties with native potentates, with the Sultan of Sokoto, 1894; commanded with over 30 expdtions; deputy high commissioner and senior res., N. Nigeria, 1900; admstr. for May to Dec., 1901, May to Nov., 1902; Apr. to Dec., 1903, and July, 1906, to Apr. 1907; African medals, 5 clasps, coronation med., received Murchison medal from R.G.S. for important services rendered to geography during many years' residence in Nigeria; ret. 1910.
WALLACE, WILLIAM HENRY.—Ed. F. Ser., 1882; sec. of mines, Tasmania, 1886.

WALLACE, WILLIAM THOMAS EWART.—Capt., 4th batt., the King's regt.; served in African war, 1900-1902; supt. of telegraphs, E. Africa construction party, S. Nigeria, 1903-5; asst. of telegraphs, E. Africa and Uganda Prot., Dec., 1905; asst. telegraph engr., and asst. P.M.G., Uganda Prot., 1st Jan., 1912; P.M.G., Gold Coast, 12th Mar., 1913; P.M.G., Mar. to Sept., 1913.

WALLER, DESMOND DALRYMPLE.—3rd treas. asst., E. Africa Prot., 12th May, 1899; asst., 23rd Sept., 1899; offr., in-charge of lazaretto, Nov., 1902; prot. of imigts., 1st Apr., 1904; dir. of transport and principal imigts. offr., E.A.P., Dec., 1911.

WALLIS, CLAUDE DUDLEY.—Proc. Consul, Havre, 1906 and 1907; vice-consul, 11th Apr., 1907; ag. consul-gen. in each year, 1907 to 1910; asst. collr. (now asst. dist. comnr.), Zanzibar, Sept., 1910; ag. dist. offr. (now dist. comm.), Pemba, Feb., 1914; seconded for secret duties, Sept., 1914.

WALLIS, Capt. C. Braithwaite, F.G.S., F.R.G.S.—Ed. privately and at Mill Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., 4th Manchester regt., 1894; lieut. 1895; capt., 1897; apptd. to S. Leone frontier force, Jan., 1898; ag. dist. commr., 1899; Mendiland and Sherbro' expedns., 1898; Protectorate expdn., 1899; in command of a column; recd. thanks of govt., West Africa medal and clasp; despatches, London Gaz., Dec., 1899; Jub. Medal 1897; transfd. to the Cameronians (Scottish Rifles), 1899; served in India, 1899-1901; selec. for serv. in Somaliland, 1901; asst. dist. commr., S. Leone, 1901; ag. commr., 1902; ag. commr., inspr. of police; sub-col. of customs and commr. of ct. of requests of Sherbro', 1903-4; entrd. Middle Temple, London, 1905; promoted dist. commr., 1905; sent by F.O. to act as consul at Monrovia, 1905-6; operations on Liberian frontr. (African General Service medal and clasp), 1905; apptd. H.M. consul for Liberia, 1906; promoted H.M. consul-gen., 1908; transfd. and promoted to Dakar as H.M. consul-gen. for French West Africa and the Sudan; also consul-gen. for German Togoland and for Portuguese Guinea, 1st Nov., 1909; recd. coronation medal, 1911; awarded silver medal and certif., R. Humane Soc., Dec., 1901; J.P. for S. Leone; author of "The Advance of our West African Empire"; "West African Warfare"; various consular reports; papers in R. Geographical and African societies journals, etc.
WALLIS, Frederick Samuel.—M.L.C., S. Australia, 1907; ch. sec. and min. of industry, 27th Mar. to 5th June, 1909; ch. sec., 1910-1912.

WALLIS, Henry Richard, C.M.G. (1911).—B. 1866; asst. agt., Chinde, 15th Sept., 1883; Brit. vice-consul, Fort Johnston, 1st Oct., 1897; 1st cls. asst., B. Cent. Africa Prot., 1st July, 1898; ag. ch. judicinal offr., Jan. to Nov., 1899; recd. commn. as H.M. vice-consul, 9th June, 1900; ag. consul, Mozambique, Feb. to Dec., 1901; apptd. vice-consul, Tete, 1st Apr., 1901; ag. dep. commr., B. C. Africa Prot., July, 1902, to Feb., 1903; asst. dep. commr., 1st Apr., 1904; ag. commr. and commr.-in-chief, B.C. Africa Prot., May to Nov., 1905; ag. dep. commr., Mar., 1907; asst. dep. gov., Oct., 1907; ag. dep. gov., and govt. sec., Nov., 1907; ag. dep. gov., 24th Mar., 1910; ag. gov. and commr.-in-ch., July, 1910, to Mar., 1911; chief sec. to gov. of Uganda, Oct., 1911; ag. gov. and commr.-in-chief, Uganda Prot., July to Dec., 1912, during June, 1913, and from 23rd May, 1914.

WARD, RIGHT HON. SIR JOSEPH GEORGE, Bart. (1911), P.C. (1907), K.C.M.G. (1901).—Prime min., col. treas., postmtr.-gen., commr. of electric telegraphs, and min. of defence, New Zealand; rep. New Zealand at Impl. confce., 1907 and 1911; mem. of Imp. confce. on naval and mil. defence, 1909; rep. New Zealand at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; resigned premiership, 1912; now leader of the opposition.

WARD, LEONARD KEITH, B.A., B.E.—Ed. Sydney Gram. Schl. and Brisbane Gram. Schl.; entd. univ. of Sydney, 1897, as Queenslant gov't. exhibiti-ner, and graduated in arts, 1900, and in engineering, 1903; in service of Broken Hill Proprietary Mining Co., till Oct., 1903; lecturer in geology, mineralogy, petrology and mining geology, Kalgoorlie schl. of mines, W. Australia, 1903-7; asst. gov't. geologist and inspr. of mines, Tasmania, 1907-11; gov't. geologist, S. Australia, Jan., 1912.

WARD, W. F.—Assoc. Roy. Schl. of Mines, Lond. (1876).—Analyst, etc., to the gov't. of Tasmania, 1882; asst. to Dr. T. Percy and Prof. W. C. Roberts-Austen, etc., in chemical and metallurgical laboratories of Roy. Schl. of Mines, Royal Mint, and Normal Schl. of Science, S. Kensington (1876-80); special apptmt. to Potosi Gold Mining Co., Venezuela (1881); in charge of chemical testing of smoke-consuming appliances at exhibition, S. Kensington (1881).

WARING, EDWARD LENNON.—Ed. at St. Paul's schl. and Crystal Pal. Engnr. Compy. Schl.; asst. engnr., Uganda rly., 1900-03; asst. ch. survr., E. Africa Prot., 27th July, 1903; dep. dir. of surveys, cadastral branch, 1st Apr., 1906.
WARNE, OSWALD HORNBY.—B. 1891; ed. Rossal Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; lieut., South Staffordshire Regt.; asst. dist. commr., Ashanti, G. Coast, 29th Apr., 1914.

WARNER, BASIL HALE, B.A.—B. 1868; ed. St. Edward's Schl., and Keble Coll., Oxford, served with C.M.R., 1891-95; schoolmaster, 1895-1900; served in S. African war, with Nesbitt's Horse, 1900-2; clk. to Swaziland admtn., July, 1902; ch. clk., June, 1903; dep. asst. commr., Swaziland, Dec., 1903; asst. commr., Ubombo dist., April, 1911.

WARNER, GEORGE BRUNTON.—Extra clerical asst., G.P.O., P.M. Burg., from Sept. 1st, 1881, to Feb. 16th, 1882; 3rd class clk. civ. ser., and filled the post of 7th clk., G.P.O., Feb. 16th, 1882; as 6th clk., Jan. 18th, 1883; clk., col. engineer's dept., Jan. 13th, 1886; border customs offr., De Jager's Drift, Jan. 8th, 1879; issuer of native passes, Dundee div., Jan. 16th, 1888; sheep inspr., De Jager's Drift, May 7th, 1888; J.P., Dundee div., Dec. 13th, 1892; chief examining offr., Customs Point, Durban, Nov. 21st, 1896; ag. clk. of the court, Newcastle, June 16th, 1900; clk. of the court, Newcastle, Aug. 9th, 1900; J.P., Newcastle div., Aug. 16th, 1900; deputy registrar special court, Newcastle div., Aug. 23rd, 1900, in terms of sec. 25 of Act No. XIV., 1900; has acted as asst. and ag. mag. of Newcastle on many occasions; also for the following periods:—Ag. asst. mag. from Nov. 19th, 1900, to March 24th, 1901; ag. mag. from June 6th, 1901, to Sept. 8th, 1901; ag. asst. mag., Sept. 9th, 1901; mag., Impendhle div., 1912.

WARREN, Lieut.-Gen. Sir CHARLES, R.E., K.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1878).—B. 1840; ed. Chelt. Coll.; was specially employed in various military and administrative capacities in Griqualand W. and the Cape Col. in 1876-8; lieut.-col. for distinguished service; adminstd. gov't. of Griqualand W. 1879 to 1880; served in Egyptian campaign, 1882, and brought to justice the murderers of Prof. Palmer and his party; H.M.'s special commr. in Bechuanaland, 1884-5, with local rank of maj.-gen., comdg. expeditn.; apptd. to command at Suakim, with rank of maj.-gen., and as gov.-gen., 1886; commr. of metropolitan pol., 1886-8; C.R.E., and in command of troops with rank of maj.-gen., Singapore, 1889 to 1894; commanding a div. in war against Transvaal and Orange F.S., 1899-1900.

WARREN, EDGAR HERBERT.—Served with 28th I.Y., S. African war, 1902 (medal with 2 clasps); S. African constab. (civil), 1903-1906; lieut., Transvaal H.A., 1907; clk. in customs, Nyasaalnd Prot., 1908; offr. of customs, 1909; also imigrn. offr., 1913; principal immigrn. offr., May, 1914.

WARREN, ERNEST, D.Sc. (Lond.)—Demonstrator of zoology at Univ. Coll., Lond., 1894; life mem. of convocation of the Univ. of Lond., 1898; asst. lecturer and museum curator at Univ. Coll., Lond., 1899; elected fellow of Univ. Coll., 1898; asst. prof. of zoology at Univ. Coll., 1900; mem. of the faculty of science, Univ. of Lond., 1902; dir. of Natal gov't. museum, 1st Feb., 1903.

WARREN, PHILIP DAVID, C.M.G. (1906); Assoc.Inst.C.E., F.R.G.S., F.Met.Soc.—B. 1851; asst. survr., Ceylon, 8th Feb., 1873; dist. survr., 1st Oct., 1879; office asst. to survr.-gen., 16th June, 1885; asst. survr.-gen., 26th Mar., 1897; survr.-gen., 23rd June, 1904; M.I.C., Ceylon, 1907-8; ret. 1910.

WATKINS, FREDERICK HENRY, I.S.O. (1904).—B. 1859; acted as rev. offr., St. Lucia, 1882; inspr. of schl., St. Vincent, 1883; headmt. of gram. schl., St. Vincent, 1887; inspr. of schl., Leeward Is., 1889; ag. pol. mag. Grenadines; reported on, St. Lucia schl., 1888; adjt. St. Kitts-Nevis volr. forces, 1897-9; commr. of Montserrat, Jan., 1900; commr., Turks and Caicos Is., Mar., 1906; ag. judge of sup. ct., June-Oct., 1906; June-Oct., 1908, and from 1909 to 1913.

WATKINS, OSCAR FERRIS.—Ed. at Marlborough and All Souls, Oxford, B.A., M.A., 1910; served in South Africa with Oxfordshire Light Infantry (43rd), March, 1900, to April, 1901 (Queen's Medal and 4 clasps); S. African constab., Mar., 1902, to Apil, 1904; served in col. sec.'s office, Transvaal, Mar., 1904, to Oct., 1907; asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., 1908 June.

WATSON, A. H.—Served with Canadian troops in S. African war, 1900 (Queen's medal and 3 clasps); asst. collr., Uganda Prot., 8th May, 1902; asst. collr., Entebbe, June, 1902, to Jan., 1904; in charge of Mbale dist., Jan. to Dec., 1904; June to Sept., 1911, and from Dec., 1912 to Oct., 1913; ag. collr., Ankole dist., Sept., 1905, to Oct., 1907; collr., Oct., 1907, to Feb., 1908, and Oct., 1908, to Dec., 1909; ag. town mag., Kampala Dist., Dec., 1909, Apr. and May, 1910, and Oct.-Nov., 1912; ag. prov. commr., E. Prov. Uganda, Sept., 1911, to Feb., 1912.

WATSON, EDWIN HENRY.—Ed. Meth. Coll., Belfast, and Queen's Univ.; Irish law soc'y prelim., Oct., 1899; clk., med. dept., Nasrald Prot., Jan., 1913; transf'd to P.W.D., Apr., 1913; transf'd to customs dept., Sept., 1913.
WATSON, E. C.—M.H.A., Trinity, Newfild., 1885-9; aspt. fisheries, 1899; dep. min. for marine and fisheries, 1898.

WATSON, ERNEST CHARLES.—B. 1874; ed. at Shrewsbury; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1897; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, July, 1902; agt. ty.-gen., Sept. to Nov., 1904; apptd. atty.-gen., Nov., 1905; agt. ch. just., Mar. to Oct., 1906; lay to Aug., 1907; Feb. to Apr., 1908; Mar. to Sept., 1909; puisne judge, Gold Coast, Jan., 1912.

WATSON, HERBERT GORDON.—Clt. in governor of Zululand's office, 1895; acted as sec. for Zululand at different periods in 1896; taken over by the Natal govt. on the annexation of Zululand to Natal and apptd. clk. in the governor's office on 30th Dec., 1896; transferred to the Cape Civil Service and apptd. clk. governor's office 6th March, 1901; chief clk., 1st July, 1904; performed duties of clk. of the exec. coun. from 6th Apr., 1904, to 22nd Apr., 1907; agt. priv. sec. to the offr. admstg. the govt., Major-General Sir S. Brook, C.B., from 13th July, 1904, to 13th Feb., 1905; clk. of exec. coun., 22nd Apr., 1907; priv. sec. to Maj.-gen. Sir Henry Scobell, C.V.O., 15th Dec., 1909, to 30th May, 1910; st. clk. of the exec. coun. of the Union of South Africa, 31st May, 1910.

WATSON, JOHN LINCOLN.—B. 1860; entd. surveyor-gen.'s office, Natal, 1899; examr. of diagrams, 30th June, 1894; sworn land surveyor 1901; agt. sec. to min. of agri. and ofl. member land board, 3rd Dec., 1907; agt. surveyor-gen., 1st Jan., 1908; surveyor-gen., Natal, at Jan., 1909.
WATSON, P. C. M.—Treas. asst., E.A.P., May, 1914.

WATSON, REGINALD GEORGE, C.M.G. (1911).—Ed. at Haileybury; cadet, S.S., 1883; priv. sec. to Sir C. C. Smith, 1884-5; attached to land office, Malacca, 1887; agt. 2nd st. prot. Chinese, Singapore, 1888; agt. ass't. itto, 1888-9; dist. offr., South, prov. Wellesley, 1889; agt. dist. offr., Balik Pulau, 1889; 2nd st. prot. of Chinese, Penang, 1888; agt. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1890; prot. of Chinese, Perak, Oct., 1891; agt. sec. to govt., Perak, Sept., 1895, and lar., 1898; sen. mag., Selangor, July, 1896; sec. o govt., Selangor, Apr., 1899; agt. sen. mag., Perak, June, 1899; senior mag., Sept., 1901; agt. sec. to res., Perak, May, 1902; comsnr. of lands and mines, F.M.S., 27th Feb., 1904; comsnr. and surveys, 1906; federal sec., 1st Jan., 1909; agt. res.-gen., 26th Feb., 1910; resident, Selangor, 1911; resident, Perak, 1912.

WATSON, HON. ROBERT.—B. 1868; M.E.C. nd M.H.A.; Newfoundland; ed. at St. John's nd Rugby; elected for Trinity, 1897, 1902, 1908 nd 1909; mem. exec. coun.; col. sec.; and early head of depts. of post office, telegraphs, duem., rly., pub. health and pub. charities, 1909; mem. exec. comttee, Diocesan Synod, 1910; agt. premier, 1910; attended Imp. Conf. nd the Coronation, 1911; hon. corres. sec., loyal Col. Inst.
WATT, ALEXANDER STUART.—B. 1884; ed. din. Acad.; writer to the signet, 1909; lieut.-batt. Royal Scots; asst. dist. comsnr., G. east, 23rd Apr., 1914.

WATT, ISAIAH.—B. 1861; joined Coldstream guards, 1880; served in Egyptian campaign; medall and clasp for Tel-el-Kebir; Khedive's star nd long service medal; sergt.-major of 1st Victoria regt., Melbourne, Nov., 1888, to June, 1893; ret. rom Coldstream Guards in 1901; clk. to col. sec., Falkland Is., and sergt.-major of vols., 22nd Jan., 1901; lieut., 15th July, 1905; capt. and adjutant, 9th May, 1906.
WATT, JAMES.—Ed. at Oxford; classical schlr., Balliol Coll., 1889; B.A. 1895; apptd. asst. dist. comsnt., Southern Nigeria, 1899; dist. comsnt., Feb., 1902.

WATT, HON. WM. ALEXANDER.—M.H.A.; treas. of Victoria Jan., 1909; premier and treas., 1912, and again in Dec., 1913; resig. premiership to enter H. of R., C. of A., to which he was elected in Sep., 1914.

WATT, SIR THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1912); C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1857; ed. at priv. schls. and Glasgow Univ.; admtd. a solr. to Scottish cts.; practised in Natal; served as subaltern in Imperial L.I., 1900; apptd. capt. and offr. commdg.
