Newcastle Town Guard, and ment. in desps.; M.L.A., Natal, 1901-1910; formerly min. of just. and educn., Natal; min. of posts and telegraphs and pub. wks., Union of S. Africa, 1912.

WATTLEY, ERNEST CHARLES.—Ed. gram. schl., St. Kitts, Crawford Coll., Maidenhead, and London Internat. Coll.; called to bar (Leeward Is.) Aug., 1877; J.P., St. Kitts, 1885; loc. comsnt. of encumbered estates, 1886; visiting just. of the gaol, 1887; escheator-gen., St. K.-N., 1889; agt. asst. to attorney-gen., 1900; mem. ex. and leg. couns., and asst. atty.-gen., Aug., 1901; crown atty., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1912.

WATTS, FRANCIS, C.M.G. (1904), D.Sc., F.C.S., F.I.C.—B. 1859; analytical chemist, Antigua, Jan., 1889; mem. legis. couns., 1897; analytical and agricultural chemist, Jamaica, May, 1898; Leeward Is., May, 1899 (in connection with the Imperial dept. of agriculture for the W. Indies); office subsequently made that of govt. chemist and supt. of agric. for the Leeward Is.; mem. exec. couns., Leeward Is., Oct., 1899; mem. exec. couns., Antigua, Dec., 1903; Imperial comsnt. of agric. in W. Indies, 1905; author of numerous reports and papers on W. Indian agriculture, etc.

WAY, Rt. Hon. SIR SAMUEL JAMES, Bart. (1899).—B. 1836; ch. just., S. Australia, Mar., 1876; lieut.-gov., Jan., 1891; Hon. D.C.L. (Oxon.), 1890; LL.D. (Adelaide), 1892; hon. LL.D. (Queen's Univ., Kingston, Canada), 1895; (Cantab.), 1897; (Melbourne), 1901; called to the bar, S. Australia, 1861; Q.C., 1871; mem. of house of assem., and atty.-gen., 1875; vice-chancellor of the Univ. of Adelaide, 1877 to 1883, and chanc. from 1883; admstd. the govt. S. Australia ten years (twice), 1878, 1883, 1889, 1893-4, 1995, 1898-9, 1902-3; mem. of the judicial comttee of H.M.'s P.C., 1897.

WEATHERHEAD, ARTHUR EVELYN.—B. 1880; served in S. African constab., 1900-1907; S. African war, Queen's medal with 5 clasps; dist. supt., Uganda police, 1908; asst. dist. comsnt., Uganda Prot., 4th Apr., 1910.
WEBB, FRANK.—Apptd. 3rd asst., Nyasaland Prot., May, 1901; 2nd grade res., Mar., 1908.

WEBB, FREDERICK WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1837; sess. clk., legis. coun., N.S. Wales, 1851; clk., G.P.O., 1853; clk., legis. assem. dept., 1860; agt. clk. of assem., 1877-86; clk., 1888; apptd. J.P. of col., 1888; sec. to National Australasian Convention, 1891.

WEBER, ARTHUR FREDERICK CLARENCE.—B. 1873; ed. Merton Coll., Oxon., B.A., 1896; called to bar, Inn. Tem., Feb., 1896; agt. stip. mag., 1898, and March to Oct., 1900, Br. Guiana; stip. mag., Essequibo, Oct., 1900; ditto, Whim Corentyne, July, 1902; ditto, Berbice judicial dist., 1904; agt. solr.-gen., Nov., 1906, to Jan., 1907, and in July, 1909; puisne judge, S. Nigeria, Sept., 1909.

WEBER, OSCAR WM.—B. 1871; elk. ast., prisons office, B. Guiana, 1887; 5th cls. elk., P.O., 1889; transf. to secretariat, 1896; 4th cls. elk., 1896; 3rd ditto, 1899; 2nd ditto, 1901; 1st cl. elk., 1910; sec. to bd. of agric., 1910-1910; mem. of bd. of agric., 1910; priv. sec. to gov., Feb.-Aug., 1911.
WEBSTER, G. W.—Ed. at Rossall; served with Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 16th Nov., 1901.

WEDDERBURN, MAXWELL MACLAGAN.—B. 1883; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1906; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Chavakachcheri, Apr., 1908; Matara, Aug., 1909; Kurunegala, June, 1909; ast. land stltmt. offr., Jan., 1911; ag. addtl. ast. col. sec. in addition to own duties, July, 1912; ag. ast. col. sec., July, 1912; ast. land stltmt. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordece," Aug., 1912; ast. comsnr., Aug., 1914.
WEEKS, REGINALD.—Ed. in Germany and Switzerland; ast. collr., E. Africa Prot., 10th Apr., 1902; collr., 17th Dec., 1906; S. African medal, 1900.

WEERAKOON, WILLIAM AUGUSTUS.—B. 1878; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1899; off. ast. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, Sept., 1900; Batticaloa, Nov., 1901; Kurunegala, May, 1902; Jaffna, Mar., 1904; Ratnapura, May, 1904; pol. mag., Matara, Jan., 1908; Point Pedro, Aug., 1908; addtnl. pol. mag., Kurunegala, N.W. Prov., Jan., 1911; pol. mag., Panadure, Nov., 1913.
WEIL, VICTOR MAURICE, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1887; ast. chemist, scientific and tech. dept., Impl. Institute, May, 1908; ast. agric. chemist, Leeward Is., Feb., 1910.
WEIR, E., M.D. (Tor.).—M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), med. offr., S. Sttlmts., May, 1907.
WELBY, H. E.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911.
WELCH, H. H. V.—M.R.C.S., L.C.R.P., med. offr., E.A.P., Oct., 1913.

WELLINGTON, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. THOMAS HENRY SPOTT, M.A., D.D.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; Hebrew prize, 1877; B.A., 1878; (Respondent) 1887; Hebrew Testamur, 1879; Divinity Testimonium (1st cls.) and Church Formularies Prize, 1879; M.A., 1882; B.D. and D.D. (jure dignitatis), 1911; deacon, 1879; priest, 1880; curate of Holy Trinity, Kingston-on-Hill, 1879-1882; St. John the Evangelist, Waterloo Road, 1882-1886; vicar of St. Barnabas, Auckland, 1886-1891; examining chaplain to Bp. of Auckland, 1888-1891; vicar of St. Paul's, Wellington, 1892-1911; examining chaplain to Bp. of Wellington, 1892-1911; consecrated Bp. of Wellington, 6th June, 1911.

WELMAN, CHARLES WELLESLEY.—B. 1878; ed. Christ's Hosp.; open scholarship, Pemb. Coll., Oxon., 1897; 1st cl. hons. class, mods., 1899; 2nd cl. hons. lit. hum., 1901; M.A. Oxon., 1907; bar final exam., 1909; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1911; enlisted in 130th co. I.Y. and served in S. Africa (Queen's medal, 2 clasps), 1902; educn. dept., Transvaal, Nov., 1902, to 1905; sec., Transvaal Resp. Govt. Assoc. and Transvaal National Assoc., 1905-1907; in office of agt.-gen. for the Transvaal in London, 1907-1909; ast. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1909; ast. col. sec., Gold Coast, 1913.

WESSELS, HON. SIR JOHANNES WILHELMUS, KT. BACH. (1909).—B. 1862; ed. at S. African Coll., Cape Univ. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; obtained scholarship at Middle Temp.; joined Cape bar, 1886, and Transvaal bar in 1887; legal adviser to Lord Roberts, 1900-1901; puisne judge of sup. ct., Transvaal, Mar., 1902; puisne judge of sup. ct. of S. Africa (Transvaal Prov. Dist.), May, 1910.
WESSELS, W. G. W.—B. 1878; ed. Kroonstad and Stellenbosch; organising inspr. of sch., O.F.S., 1912.

WESTBROOK, WALTER FRANCIS.—B. 1861; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Nov., 1880; supt. of the registry, 1892; dist. regis., 1896.

WHEAR, JOHN FREDERICK.—B. 1867; ed. public schools and at Prince of Wales Coll., P.E.I. Island; admitted as a barrister and attorney-at-law, 1890; served for four years as a city councillor in Charlottetown; elec. to the leg. assembly in 1900; as a mem. of the exec. com. was associated with the late premier A. Peck and the present heut. governor Rogers in delegates from the P.E.I. govt. for the settlement of provincial claims against the federal govt.; apptd. postmaster of Charlottetown, 1904; post office inspr., 1st Apr., 1911.
WHEELER, W., C.M.G. (1904).—Treas. R. Cent. Africa Prot., 28th Feb., 1892; ag. govt. sec., Nyasaland Dec., 1912, to Apr., 1913.
WHITE, ALEXANDER HAROLD.—B. 1880; ast. collr. (now ast. dist. comsnr.), Zanzibar, 7th Dec., 1911.

WHITE, ANDREW.—Educ. Edin. Acad. and Univ. of Edin.; writer to the Signet, 1889; ast. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 22nd Feb., 1902; dist. comsnr., 1st Jan., 1904; ag. prov. comm., Dec., 1905 to Apr., 1906; chief regis. and ass't, 20th Jan., 1907.

WHITE, AUBREY, C.M.G. (1914)—B. 1846; ed. Royal schl., Dungannon, and grm. sch., Carrickmacross, Ireland; forest ranger, Ont. Canada, 1877; crown lands act., 1879; inspr. of ser. of dep., 1882; dep. min. of lands and forests, Ontario, 1887.
WHITE, ERNEST COSTLEY.—Clerk to armed forces, B.C. Africa, Apr., 1900; ast. collr., Nyasaland Prot., March, 1904; 2nd grad. req., Jan., 1911.

WHITE, LT. COL. FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1901)—B. 1847; clk., dept. of just., Canada, 1869; transf. to dept. of S. of S. as clk. in-charge of mounted police branch, Apr., 1876; ch. clk., Sept., 1878; comptroller of N.W. M.P. force of Canada, Nov., 1878; dep. head, July, 1883; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir John Macdonald, 1880 to 1882; comm. of N.W. Territories, 24th Aug., 1905; resigned comptrollership of pol., 1912.

WHITE, JAMES, F.R.G.S., Grad. R.M.C., Kingston, Canada.—B. 1863; entd. cir. ser., Canada, 1884; geographer and ch. draughtsman, geog. surv., 1894; ch. geographer, dep. of interior, 1899; sec. coman. of conservation, 1909.

WHITE, WALLIS HARRY BRINSLEY.—Ed. at Bruton and Line. Coll., Oxford; ast. master, St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown and Grad. Reinet Coll.; 1st class teacher's certif., Cape Colony; served during S. African War in Brabant Horse (Queen's medal and four clasps); inspr. of schl., O.R.C. 5th Jan., 1901; ch. ditto, 1st Feb., 1904; ag. dir. of educn., Aug., 1905, to Feb., 1906, and Apr. to July, 1907.
WHITE, W. ARTHUR.—Apptd. after compet. exam. for home civil serv. to P.O., Jan., 1890; 2nd div. clk., acctant-gen.'s dept., July,
WHITE, WILLIAM CLARENCE.—B. 1847; ed. Cent. Acad., Charlottetown, P.E.I.; reg. of deeds, P.E.I., 1891; reg. of deeds and comsrs. of pub. lands, 1901.

WHITE, HON. WILLIAM THOMAS, B.A.—B. 1866; ed. High Schools, Oakville and Brampton, Ont.; Univ. of Toronto (B.A.), graduate of the Ontario law schl.; apptd. manager National Trust Co., Ltd., Toronto, 1899; subsequently general manager and vice-pres. of the same institution; mem. of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of finance in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911.
WHITHEAD, CHARLES BASIL.—Asst. supt. of pol., S. Stltms., Sept., 1900; ag. second supt. of pol., Singapore, June to Sept., 1908, and from Sept., 1909, to Feb., 1910.

WHITEHORN, ROGER HERBERT, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 17th Nov., 1911; attached to col. sec.'s office, 15th Dec., 1911; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Aug., 1912; ag. pol. mag., Gampola, Jan., 1913; office asst., Ratnapura Kach., Feb., 1914; pol. mag., Galle, May, 1914; 4th asst. col. sec., Sept., 1914.
WHITFIELD, H. S.—Ck. and examr., audit dept., W. Australia, 1886; ch. ck., Aug., 1891.
WHITING, JOHN BEERY.—B. 1859; entd. public serv., S. Australia, 1873; sec., state children's dept., 1886; sec. and reg. of stock in ag. gen.'s dept., London, 1903; ag. agent-gen., 1914.
WHITRIDGE, MATHEW WM.—Served in Natal civ. serv., 1898 to 1899; sub-native comsrs., Ubono, Swaziland, Feb., 1903, to Mar., 1907; asst. comsrs., ditto, 22nd Mar., 1907.

WHITTALL, PERCIVAL FREDERICK.—B. 1877; ed. Felsted; joined army, 1896; lieut., Lincolnshire Regt., 1902; served in S. African war, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal and three clasps); lieut., G. Coast Regt., 1907; recd. thanks of S. of S. for services during outbreak of yellow fever at Secondoone, 1910; asst. dist. comsrs. N. Territories, G. Coast, 1912.

WHITTY, JAMES LIONEL.—Ed. Portarlington, and gram. schl., Tipperary; passed exam. in Chinyanja; ck. in treas., Brit. Cent. Africa Prot., 21st May, 1901; asst. treas., Somaliland Prot., 31st Mar., 1904; second asst. acctnt., gen. treas., Ceylon, Oct., 1911; ag. 1st asst. acctnt., Apr., 1912 to May, 1913.

WHYAN, WILLIAM HENRY, I.S.O. (1906).—B. 1848; entd. R.N., 1864; ret., July, 1873, and joined the Leeward Island colonial yacht "Union"; dist. mag., Dominica, Nov., 1874; nominated mem. of legis. assem., Mar., 1877; list. mag. and inspr. of prisons, Antigua, Feb., 1878; ag. pres. of Nevis, 1882; hon. comsrs. or Antigua at Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; mem. ags. coun., Antigua, 1887; ag.-pres., Dominica, Apr. to Nov., 1889; escheator-gen., 1890; ag. comsrs., Montserrat, May to Sept., 1894; May to June, 1898; pres. legis. coun., and mem. exec. coun., Dec., 1896; ag. admstr., Dominica, July, 1896, to Feb., 1897, and Apr. to Sept., 1899; visiting just. gaol, 1898; offi. mem. legis. coun., July, 1898; non-elective mem. legis. coun., L. Is., Dec., 1898; ag. admstr., Dominica, July to Nov., 1900; ag. aud.-gen., L. Is., Nov., 1899, to May, 1900; June-July, 1900; Nov., 1900, to Mar., 1901; apptd. mem. exec. coun., L. Is., 1st Mar., 1902; ag. admstr., Dominica, 29th Aug., 1902, to 4th Feb., 1903; dist. mag., dists. A and B, Antigua, 1st June, 1903; del. to W. Indian quarantine confco., at Barbados, Apr., 1904.

WHYTE, JAMES WILKINSON.—B. 1852; solr. to lands titles comsrs., Tasmania, 1883; recorder of titles, collr. of stamp duties, reg. of public trusts, dep. regis. of deeds, 1884; mem. of bd. of advice on stamp duties, 1891; mem. of civ. ser. bd., 1901; ditto to comsrs. of taxes, 1903; comsrs. of pub. debts sinking fund, 1903.

WICKREMESINGHE, CYRIL LEONARD.—B. 1890; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Apl., 1912; attached to Badulla Kachcheri, Apl., 1912; Matara Kachcheri, Oct., 1912; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, July, 1913; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Nov., 1913.

WIDDUP, JOHN CHAS. PONSONBY.—L.R.C.S., 1869; L.M. and L.R.C.P., 1870; supernum. med. offr., Br. Guiana med. ser., 1881; govt. med. offr., Canye dist., 1886; health offr., pol. prison, and port surg., New Amsterdam, 1889; mem. Br. med. assoc.
WIGGINS, CLARE AVELING, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.); med. offr., E. Africa Prot., 4th Apr., 1901; med. offr., UgandaProt., 4th Apr., 1909.

WIGHTON, ALFRED RITCHIE.—Served with chartered acctnts. in Scotland, 1888 to 1902; joined impl. military rly., S. Africa, June, 1902; audit inspr., ch. acctnt.'s dept., 23rd June, 1903; acctnt., engrn.'s dept., 1st Feb., 1904; ch. ck., ditto, 1st July, 1908; inspr., dept. of audr., S. African rly. and harba., 1st Apl., 1910; ch. inspr., asst. controller and audr.-gen.'s dept., Union of S. Africa, 1st Apl., 1912.
WIGHTWICK, CLAUDE.—B. 1881; ed. at Tonbridge schl. and Pembroke Coll., Camb., 3rd cls. class. tripos; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 18th Aug., 1906.

WIGLEY, WILFRED MURRAY.—Ed. Derby schl.; held various clerical appts. under govt., St. Kitts-Nevis, Feb., 1895, to Apl., 1898; called to the bar, Mid Temp., Jan., 1901; nom. mem., Basseterre town bd., 1903; elected dep. chmn., 1905; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis V.D.F., 1905; res. mag. and dep. judge, sum. juris. ct., Anguilla, Sept., 1906; dist. mag., dep. judge, sum. juris. ct., legal adviser to comsrs., Montserrat, Apl., 1909; ag. ch. regis., Leeward Is., regis., sup. ct., Antigua, Sept., 1911, to Feb., 1912; dist. mag., Roseau, Dominica, escheator-gen. and registr.-gen., Feb., 1912; ag. asst. to atty.-gen. and crown attorney on various occasions; mem. exec. and legis. couns.

WILBRAHAM, D. F., B.A. (Trin. Coll., Oxon.)—Master sup. ot. and regist.-gen., S. Leone, 1896; pol. mag., Oct., 1903; atty.-gen., June, 1908; ag. ch. just., June to Nov., 1902; June to Sept., 1904, Oct., 1909, to Mar., 1910, Mar. to Aug., 1911, Feb. to Mar., 1912, and May to Oct., 1913; edited revised edition of laws of S. Leone, 1909.

WILDERS, WILLIAM EDWARD.—Joined R.I.C., Aug., 1887; B. of T. certif. of inspr. of weights and measures, Apr., 1900; inspr. of weights and measures, inspr. of food and drugs, and inspr. of explosives, Ireland, 1901; apptl. to Leeward Is. police force in Montserrat, Apr., 1902; sub.-inspr. of pol., 1905; inspr. of weights and measures, Oct., 1906; ag. inspr. of pol., Dominica, Oct., 1906, to Aug., 1907; ag. inspr. of wks. and roads, Montserrat, Sept., 1907; inspr. of wks. and roads, Montserrat, Apr., 1908; mem. of quarantine, health and schl. bds.; J.P. and mil. instr. to Montserrat defence reserve force; inspr. of pol. and adjutant of St. Kitts-Nevis defence force, Feb., 1909; visiting justice of the gaol, June, 1909; capt. and comdg. offr., "A" company, St. Kitts-Nevis defence force, Oct., 1910; ag. chief inspr. of police, May, 1911, to Jan., 1912; comdg. offr. def. force, Jan., 1913; supt. fire brigade, and inspr. of weights and measures, Sept., 1913.

WILHELM, THOMAS ANDREW.—B. 1853; entd. col. serv., S. Leone, 10th May, 1878; ag. librarian at govt. house for different periods, 1878-1888; clk., treas., 19th Aug., 1881; accompanied Govr. Pinkett to Talliala war, 1882-1883; ag. 2nd clk., col. secretariat, 1887; ag. ch. clk., ditto, 1887; priv. sec. and accompanied Govr. Rowe on numerous expedns. to the Hinterland, between 1878 and 1888; 2nd clk., regist.-gen.'s dept., 1st Oct., 1888; ag. ch. clk., ditto, and dep. regis. of births and deaths, 1888-1889, and Jan. to July, 1890; ch. clk., off. of mast. of sup. ct. and regist.-gen., 1890; dep. regist.-gen., 3rd Aug., 1890; dep. regist. of births and deaths, Freetown dist., 30th Aug., 1890; ast. inspr. of wks. and roads, survey dept., 1st Feb., 1891; acted at various times as offr. in charge of survey dept., 1891-1894; offr. in charge, survey dept., May to Oct., 1894; ditto, merchants' powder magazine, May to Oct., 1894; ch. draughtsman, survey dept., 15th Mar., 1895; offr. in charge of pub. wks., Moyamba, Mar. to June, 1899; ch. draughtsman, rly. wharf extension scheme, Mar. to Dec., 1901; ag. survr. of Crown lands, July to Sept., 1904.

WILKINSON, ERNEST EDWARD.—B. 1874; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 2nd div., civ. ser., and assigned to the bd. of agriculture, May, 1894; transf. to C.O., June, 1896; minor staff offr., ch. clk.'s dept., 1902.

WILKINSON, H.S.—Clk., high commr.'s office, S. Africa, 1899; clk., polit. sec.'s office, Transvaal, 1900; acctnt. to sec., Transvaal adminstrn., 1901; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1902; controller of stores, educn. dept., 1902; acctnt., health dept., 1904; distributor of stamps, treas., 1908; distributor of stamps, dept. of finance, Union of S. Africa, 1910.
WILKINSON, N.—Ast. supt. of wks., S. Sttlmts., Aug., 1904.

WILKINSON, REGINALD WARREN HALE.—B. 1882; ed. France, Germany, Leamington Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hons.), 1904; M.A., 1911; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1907; ast. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 1909, admtd. to Sierra Leone bar, 1909; frequently acted as crown prosecutor, 1909-1911; ag. pol. mag., coroner and regist.-gen. in 1911 and 1913; J.P., Sierra Leone, 1911; ast. col. sec., 1912; acted as master of sup. ct. and as sol.-gen., S. Leone, 1913; senior crown counsel, Gold Coast, 1914; ag. solr.-gen., G. Coast, 1914.

WILKINSON, RICHARD JAMES, C.M.G. (1912).—B. 1867; ed. Trin. Coll., Camb. (exhibitioner); cadet, S. S., Oct., 1889; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1891; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Dec., 1890, to Aug., 1891; ditto, Nibong Tebal, June to Aug., 1892; ag. ast. postmr.-gen., Dec., 1892, to Mar., 1893; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., Sept., 1893; ag. sheriff, Singapore, Sept., 1894, to May, 1895; ag. ast. audr.-gen., July, 1896; ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, Mar., 1896; 3rd mag., Singapore, Mar. to Apr., 1896; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, Apr., 1896; also ag. supt. of educn., Penang, July, 1896; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, Apr., 1897; ag. ast. col. sec. and clk. of coun., July, 1897; ag. dist. offr., P.W., Aug., 1897; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Apr., 1898; ag. inspr. of schls., S. Sttlmts., Aug., 1898; apptd. ast. postmr.-gen., Penang, but still ag. inspr. of schls., Jan., 1899; ag. supt. educ., Penang, Aug., 1900; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., Sep., 1900, to Jan., 1901; dist. offr., Dindinga, Jan., 1901; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Apr., 1903; inspr. of schls., F.M.S., Oct., 1903; dist. offr., Batang Padak, Perak, Nov., 1906; ag. sec. to res., Perak, Nov., 1906; confirmed, Jan., 1909; ag. res., Neg. Sembilan, 25th Feb., 1910; col. sec., S. Sttlmts., 1911; admtd. govt., Nov.-Dec., 1911, and Jul.-Aug., 1914.
WILLAN, REGINALD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Ed. Oakland Schl. and Guy's Hosp. med.; med. to Baro-Kano rly., Nigeria, 1909-1911; joyn. West African med. staff, 10th Jan., 1912.

WILCOCKS, LEEKUT.—GEN. SR JAMES K.C.B. (1914), K.C.M.G. (1900, after relief of Coomans, K.C.S.I.; C.B., 1907; C.M.G. (1899); D.S.O.-B. 1857; Leinster regt., 1st comm., Jul., 1878; capt., 1884; major, 1893; tempor. lieut.-col., 1897; temporary col., 1898; station staff offr., 1st cl., Bengal, Jul., 1890, to June, 1893; D.A.A.G.; Bombay, Aug., 1894, to Nov., 1897; West African F.F., Nov., 1897, active service: Afghan war, 1878-80; Mahsood Wuzeree expedition, 1881; Soudan expdn., 1885, Suakin, med. with clasps bronze star; Burmese expdn., 1885-89, as transp. offr., and in charge of field comsrt., also acted as rd. comdt.; desp. Lon. Gaz., Sept. 2, 1888, med. with 2 clasps, D.S.O.; China-Lushai expdn., 1889-90, clasps; expdn. to Manipoor as transp. offr.; desp. Lon. Gaz., Aug. 14, 1891, N.W. frontier of India, 1897; A.A.G., Tochi field force, desp. Lon. Gaz., Feb. 11, 1898; 2nd in comm. W. African F.F., Nov., 1898; officiating comm., Sept., 1898; in commd. of Ashanti field force, relief of Kumasi, 1900; joined field force in E. Africa, 1902 (medal and clasp); commd. No. 1 Brigade, India, 1902-7; Zakka Khel expdn., 1908; commd. a division in India, 1908.

WILLETT, THOMAS GRAHAM.—B. 1883; ed. Merchant Taylors and St. John's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1906; ast. ast. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Aug., 1908; pol. mag., Chilaw, Dec., 1909; addl. pol. mag., Galle, Matare, Tangalla, Hambantota and Balangoda, July, 1910; pol. mag., Jaffna, Jan., 1911; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Jan., 1913; ag. dist. judg., Kegalla, Aug., 1913; ast. land settlm. offr., and special offr. under waste land ordce., Jul., 1914.
WILLIAMS, A. D. J. B.—Med. offr., R.A.F., Aug., 1912.

WILLIAMS, CHAS. RIBY, C.M.G. (1902)—B. 1857; supervisor of customs, G. C. Col., 1884; on special mission to Aquamoo and Crepepe, 1885; dist. commr., 1887; in charge Salagha recruiting expdn.; commr. with Tavievo expeditionary force, and on special mission to King of Buena 1888; on special mission to E. Wassaw, 1889; controller of custom, Dec., 1890; on special mission to Krobo, 1901; dist. commr., Anglo-German bndry., commr., 1892; treas., Nov., 1895; offr. in charge of headqu. during governor's absence, Mar., 1900; ag. dist. sec. in 1897, 1898, 1899, 1900, 1903, 1904, 1905, 1906 and 1907; retired, Dec., 1909.

WILLIAMS, EDWARD LLOYD.—B. 1875; ed. at Brecon and Victoria Univ. (Univ. Coll., Liverpool); indentured for three years to the water engr. of Liverpool; student of the instit. civil engrs.; contractor's engr. on rly. construction, G.W.R.; res. engr. on waterworks construction, N. Wales county asylum; contractor's sub-agt. and engr. on rly. construction, G.W.R. served with 29th I.Y. (Denbighshire Hussars), South Africa, 1900-1; asst. engrnr. on rly. survey, Gold Coast and Ashanti, 1901-2; asst. surveyor of mines, G. Coast, 1902-3; ch. surveyor, 1904-5; contractor's agt., waterworks construction for Liverpool corporation, 1905-6; exec. engrnr. P.W.D., Northern Nigeria, 9th June, 1906; ag. lep. D.P.W., in 1909, 1910, 1911 and 1912.

WILLIAMS, SIR HARTLEY, K.T. BACH. (1894).—B. 1844; second son of Sir E. Eyre Williams, former judge of Victoria; ed. at Repton and Trin. Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1865; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1867, and immediately went to Australia and commenced practice; judge of the sup. ct. of Victoria, 1881, and became sen. puisne judge; was a vice-pres. of the Victorian free educn. league; and pres. of the Victorian league for promoting the opening of museums, etc., on Sundays; ret., 1903.

WILLIAMS, JAMES ALEXANDER, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1866; messenger and copyist, secretariat, Lagos, Dec., 1871; clk. and keeper of debtors prison, June, 1875; 2nd clk., gov.'s office, G. Coast, Dec., 1875; ch. clk., 1877; clk., legis. coun., 1886; ist. comsnr., Dec., 1890.

WILLIAMS, JAMES ROWLAND.—B. 1860; d. at Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and Trin. Coll., Oxford; scholar, 1879; 1st cls. class. tds., 1881; 2nd cls. final class. schools, 1883; A.A., 1883; M.A., 1888; inspr. of schls., Jamaica, 1884; ag. ast. col. sec., Nov., 1906, to Feb., 1907; ov't. deleg. to Canadian reciprocity confce. at Barbados, 1908; suptdg. inspr. of schls., Jamaica, 1909; nom. mem. legis. coun., 1910.
WILLIAMS, JOHN, F.C.S.—B. 1869; astt. analyst, Br. Guiana, 1893; sen. ditto, 1893.
WILLIAMS, J. L., B.A.—Barrister-at-law; formerly ch. clk. of dept. of atty-gen. and of justice, New South Wales; under-sec. of the dept., July, 1905.

WILLIAMS, RIGHT HON. SIR JOSHUA TRANGE, P.C. (1913), KT. BACH. (1911), M.A., L.M.—B. 1837; ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st class in law tripus, 1858; in optime and chancellor's legal studies medallist, 1859; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, ov., 1859; dist. land-registr., Canterbury, N.Z., 1871; registr.-gen. of land under the Land Transfer act, 1872; puisne judge, sup. ct., of N. Zealand, far., 1876; is chancellor of Univ. of Otago.

WILLIAMS, MYLES.—Clerk-treas. comsnr., Bech. rot., 1902; ch. clk. and regstr., 3rd Feb., 1906; gov't. sec., Nov., 1908, to May, 1909, and com. Apl. to Oct., 1911; acctnt., Bech. Prot., pl., 1912; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1909; passed 2nd grade Sechuan exam., 1912; astt. res. mag., Apr., 1913.

WILLIAMS, O. G. R.—B. 1836; ed. Lancashire coll. and Oxford; exhibitioner, Hertford Coll., 1866; 1st cls. mods., 1867; 1st cls. Lit. Hum., 1869; Aubrey Moore studentship, 1910; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. in sec's off., In. Rev., ct., 1910; 2nd cls. clk., O.C.O., Mar., 1911.

WILLIAMS, SIR RALPH CHAMPNEYS, K.C.M.G. (1907), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1848; ed. at oasall; explored parts of Patagonia and Terra del Fuego, 1873-4; and Cent. Africa, 1883-4; head civil intell. dept., Bechuanaland expdn., 1884; author of "The British Lion in Bechuanaland"; Br. consulnr. offr., S. African republic, 1887; Br. agt. there (with letter of credence), 1888; col. treas., and mem. of sanitary comnr., Gibraltar, 1890; also contract offr., 1891; and so capt. of the port, 1895; rec'd. certif. and ed. from the Italian govt. for services in connection with the wreck of the "Utopia," Mar., 1891; col. sec., Barbados, 1897; res. comsnr., Bechuanaland Protec., 1901; gov'r., Windward Is., 8th Sept., 1906; gov'r., Newfoundland, 6th May, 1909; assumed govt., 6th Sept., 1909; ret., 1912.

WILLIAMS, R. GREGSON.—Ed. at Rossall Schl., Caious Coll., Camb.; B.A. 3rd cls. hona. Science Tripos; Royal Schl. of Mines, A.S.R.M., 1st cls. diploma; post-graduate course, Mysore goldfields, India; astt. res., N. Nigeria, 22nd Mch., 1911; ag. inspr. of mines, June, 1911; ag. chief survr., Sept., 1911.

WILLIAMS, WILLIAM.—B. 1856; mun. water wks., Cyprus, 1878; surveying on military roads and draughtsman and supt. of wks. under R.E. offrs. when civil wks. were under their charge; draughtsman, chief storekeeper, and astt. supt. of wks. under civil wks. dept., 2nd divisional engrnr., Dec., 1898, and 1st divisional engrnr., 1903; ag. offr. in charge of pub. wks. dept. on three occasions, and ag. D.P.W. on two occasions of three to six months' duration.

WILLIAMSON, VICTOR ALEXANDER, C.M.G. (1882).—Ed. at Westminster and Chr. Ch., Oxford, of which he is M.A., barrister-at-law of the In. Tem., and goes the N.E. circuit; one of the Royal comsnrs. who in 1872 and 1873 inquired into the treatment of Indian imigrts. in Mauritius; served for more than two years in Fiji without remuneration as chimn. of comsnr. for settling titles to land; was mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1879-81; sec. to roy. comsnr. on London bd. of wks., 1888.

WILLIS, NORMAN EDWARD OMNANEY.—B. 1872; ed. at Eton; entd. crown agent's office, 1st Jan., 1891; astt. cashier, 4th Jan., 1899; astt. acctnt., 1st July, 1902; acctnt. and dep. chief clk., 9th Jan., 1913.
WILSON, C. J.—Medical offr., E.A.P., May, 1911.

WILSON, SIR DAVID, K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1891), V.D. (1895).—B. 1838; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond; home civ. serv., 1856-61; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov. of N. Brunswick, 1861-6; special comsnr. to report upon the salmon fisheries of N.B.; capt. and adjt. 1st York militia, N.B., 1863; also at camp of instruction, 1865-6; major, St. John volr. batt. when on service on the American frontier, 1866; priv. sec. to gov. of Trinidad, 1866-9; supt. of prisons, 1869-70; stip. mag. E. dist. of St. George, 1870-8; ag. S.J.P., Port of Spain and W. dist. of St. George, Sept., 1873-4; comsnr. of the N. prov. and sub-intendant of Crown lands, 1873 to 1897; mem. of exec. coun., 1894; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, various dates from Apr., 1875, to Jan., 1897; raised and organised Trinidad volr. force, 1879 to 1890; col. comdg. L.I. volr., 1890-7, and is now hon. col.; gov. of Br. Honduras, Jan., 1897; ret. May, 1903.
WILSON, ERNEST GEORGE.—Ed. at Whitgift Coll.; G.N. rly., Apr., 1894, to June, 1901; astt. traffic man., Uganda rly., June, 1901.

WILSON, HON. FRANK, C.M.G. (1911).—B. 1859; mem. of city coun., Perth, W. Australia, 1895-98; M.L.A. for Canning, 1897, and for Perth, 1901; min. for rlys. and mines, 1901, defeated on seeking re-election; M.L.A. for Sussex, 1904; min. of wks., Aug., 1905; col. treas., min. for educn. and agric., May, 1906; premier and col. treas., 1910-11.
WILSON, GEORGE, C.B. (1899).—Sub-comsnr., Uganda Prot., 30th Aug., 1894; dep. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1902.

WILSON, G. GORDON.—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Apr., 1902; passed final exam. in Chinese, Aug., 1902; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, May, 1903; ag. astt. postmr.-gen., Penang, 2nd Oct., 1903; supt., money order branch and sav. bank, Sing., but cont. to act in Penang.
WILSON, GEORGE HAROLD SUMNER.—B. 1887; ed. Chigwell Sch., Essex; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914.

WILSON, SIR HENRY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1908), C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; foundation scholar, 1878; sen. Bell's Univ. scholar, 1879; Chancellor's medal for English verse, 1880; 6th in 1st class classical tripos, and distinguished in exam. for Chancellor's class, med., 1882; fellow of Trin. Coll., 1884; M.A., 1885; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1888; sec. to Trinidad judicial inquiry coms., 1892; sent to Malta to collect evidence for the Protestant communities in connection with the Marriages Case, 1893; priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. J. Chamberlain, M.P., S. of S. for cols., June, 1895; legal astt. in the C.O., 1897; went to S. Africa as legal astt. on high comsnr.'s staff, Feb., 1900; sec. to administration of Orange River Colony, 1901; col. sec., June, 1902; acted as lieut.-gov., Apr. to Aug., 1913; Dec., 1903, to Aug., 1904, and from Aug. to Dec., 1906; mem. of I.C.C., 1903-1907 (represented O.R.C. on rly. comtece.).
WILSON, JAMES PHILLIPS.—M.L.C., S. Australia, 1906; min. of industry and agric., 1910-1912.

WILSON, JEREMIAH.—Entd. Impl. telegraph ser., 22nd May, 1876; transf'd. to Cape telegraph ser., 1880; prin. clk., foreign mails branch, 1883; chief clk., G.P.O., 1888; asst. sec., 1905; sec., 1st Feb., 1908; under sec., under Union admin., 31st May, 1910; postmr.-gen., Union of S. Africa, 19th Oct., 1910.

WILSON, HON. JOHN CARNEGIE DOVE, K.C., M.A., LL.B.—B. 1865; ed. privately and at Aberdeen and Edinburgh Univs., advocate of the Scottish bar; second puisne judge of sup. ct., Natal, Oct., 1904; judge pres. of the sup. ct. of S. Africa (Natal Prov. Div.), Jan., 1911.

WILSON, JOHN PLOWRIGHT.—Entd. serv. of Uganda Prot. from Imperial Br. E. Africa Co. as astt. collr., 1st Apr., 1893; collr., 1st Aug., 1895; sub-comsnr., 29th July, 1899; I. B. E. A. Co.'s medal, Uganda, 1891-2; East and Central Africa medal, Uganda, 1897-8.

WILSON, LESLIE ORME.—R.M.L.I.; joined Royal Marines as 2nd lieut., 1st Feb., 1895; lieut., 1st Jan., 1896; capt., 1st Apr., 1901; served in H.M.S. "Hibernia" and H.M.S. "Australia"; astt. instructor of musketry, Gravesend, 1901; served with naval brigade in South Africa, 1899-1900 (ment. in desp., D.S.O., medal with five clasps); A.D.C. to gov. of N. S. Wales, 12th July, 1903.

WILSON, T.—B. 1864; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the G.P.O., Apr., 1882; transf'd. to the C.O., Dec., 1883; astt. registr., July, 1896; higher grade, Jan., 1898; staff clk., first grade, 1911.

WILSON, T. R.—Ck. to comsnr. of Crown lands, co. of Bourke, Victoria, Dec., 1852; ch. clk., med. dept., Mar., 1853; sec. to cent. board of health, Aug., 1865; ch. clk., ch. sec.'s office, Jan., 1881; under-sec. Victoria, 1881; J.P., 1883; hon. mem., Br. Med. Assoc., 1890.
WINGATE, G. R.—Ed. at Dulwich; astt. collr. of customs, N. Nigeria, 27th Feb., 1904; has acted as collr. on several occasions.

WINGFIELD, MACRIK E., C.M.G. (1912).—Ed. at Winchester and Trin. Coll. Camb.; M.A., 1896; sec. to royal coms., hut tax, S. Lee, 1898; astt. treas., collr. of cust., postmr.-astt. mem. ex. and leg. couns., Falkland Is., 1899-1902; priv. sec. to gov., of Queensland, 1902; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Australia, 1903-4; astt. sec. S. African constitutions comtece., 1906; astt. sec., Gambia, Mar. to July, 1911; astt. gov. Gambia, 14th July to 18th Aug., 1911.
WINSLOW, H. P.—Dep. traffic mnr., Shanghai-Nanking rly., 1908; man., Kowloon Canton rly. (British section), 1911.

WINTER, SIR FRANCIS PRATT, KT. (1906), C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1848; ag. atty.-gen., Fiji, 1888; ch. judicial offr. and mem. exec. and leg. couns., Br. N. Guinea, 1888; ag. admstr., Sep., 1894, to June, 1895, and Oct., 1898, to Mar., 1899; ret., 1903.
WISE, BERNHARD RINGROSE, K.C.—Att.-gen., N.S. Wales, May, 1887, to Feb., 1888, 1890, 1901, and 1901-4; mem. of Austn. Fed. Cor., 1897-8.

WISEMAN, R. A.—B. 1886; Ed. Monmouth; Gram. Schl. and Oxford; math. demy, Magdal. Coll., 1905-1906; 2nd cl. math. mods., 1906; 2nd cl. hist., 1908; 2nd cl. jurisprudence, 1908; apptd. after compet. exam., clk., sec.'s dep., Admiralty, Moh., 1910; 2nd cl. clk., C.O., May, 1911.
WITHYCOMBE, ROBERT.—B. 1880; dir. rly. and elec. dept., Zanzibar, 12th Dec., 1911.

WITTENOOM, HON. SIR E. H., K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1854; elected M.L.C., W. Australia, 1883; resig. in Dec.; re-elected 1884; resig. 1886; elected, M.L.C., July, 1894; min. for mines, Dec., 1894; agent-gen., July, 1898; resigned, 1901; M.L.C.

WOODEHOUSE, CLARENCE B.—Entd. Selhurst Coll., 1875; gazetted to 77th Regt. R.N.; resigned coms., 1880; local commdt. of pace, Cyprus, 1st Jan., 1894; ag. comsnr., Limassol, 12th July, 1894; ditto, Papho, 1st Apr., 1896; comsnr., Papho, 7th June, 1895; comsnr., Larnaca, 1st Nov., 1907.

WOODEHOUSE, H. E., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1845; ed. Repton Schl.; Hong Kong cadet, 1877; student interp., 1869; supt. of Chinese pol., 1870; astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1875; pol. mag., 1880; hon. sec. to the local coms. in connection with the Indian and Col. Exhib., and special comt. in London, 1886; mem. exec. coun., May, 1886; ret.

WOODEHOUSE, Lieut.-Gen. Sir JOSEPH HENRAIGH, G.C.B. (1913), K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1890), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; comsnr. in R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan war, 1880; Sudan, 1883-1894; gov. of Eng. frontier, 1878-1894; comdld. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1894-1898; with Malakand field force (severely wounded, 1888) in action with Egyptian forces at Arjan; infantry div. at Toski; Presidency dist., 1888-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, 1902; Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in comd. of W. Comd., 1905; Zulu war (medal and clasps); Soudan war (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cl. Medjidiye; 2nd cl. Osmanie; Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, French Govt.; gov. of Bermuda, 1907-1908; late G.O.C. Northern Army, India.

WOODEHOUSE, P. P. J.—Ed. Eliz. Coll., Guernsey; first clk., reg.-gen.'s dept., Hong Kong, 1897-1900; passed in Chinese, 1899; census offr., 1900; astt. supt. of police, 1901; attached Punjabi police, 1901-2; passed in Urdu, 1902; J.P., 1902; census offr., 1906; passed in Punjabi, 1908; mem.

WODEMAN, Guy Stanley.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1899; office asst. to gov't. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1910; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1912; asst. commr. of excise attached to the office of the excise commr., Apr., 1913; asst. commr. of excise, Cent. Div., Jan., 1914.

WOLFE, E. D. C.—Tonbridge schl. (1890-94); exhib. in mod. langs., Caesius Coll., Camb. (1894); exhib. from Tonbridge schl. (1894); 3rd class mod. langs., tripos. (1896); 1st class Pt. I, and 2nd class Pt. II, Law Special (1897); B.A. (1897); cadet, F.M.S., 1898; sent to Canton to study Cantonese (1899); transf. to Hong Kong, 1901; passed cadet, Sept., 1901; regiar., land ct., Nov., 1901; ag. 2nd pol. mag., Oct., 1902; ag. asst. regiar.-gen., Nov., 1902; sec. to pub. wks. inquiry commn., 1902; collr. of Crown rent, New Territory, Feb., 1903; regiar., land ct., Mar., 1903; ag. pol. mag., N.T., and asst. supt. of pol., 1903-4; collr. of Crown rent, N.T., 1904; seconded to Transvaal gov't. serv., 1905; ag. 2nd pol. mag., Apr. and May, 1907; ag. inspr. of schls., 1907; ag. regiar.-gen., Aug., 1907; asst. regiar.-gen., Oct., 1907; ag. inspr. of schls., Oct., 1907, to Mar., 1909; asst. regiar.-gen., Mar. to June, 1909; ag. head of sanitary dept., June, 1909; head of sanitary dept., Oct., 1909; ag. dir. of educn., in addition to other duties, July to Nov., 1911; postmr.-gen., April, 1913; ag. col. treas., Apr., 1914.

WOLFERTSTAN, LITTLETON EDWARD PIPE.—Ed. Eton and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1888; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1889; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1891; ag. priv. sec. to Sir C. Smith, Jan. to Apr., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Feb. to Mar., and June to Nov., 1892; ag. dist. offr., S.W. dist., Penang, Mar., 1893; 3rd mag., Penang, Apr., 1895; ag. dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, June, 1897; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore Aug., 1897; sheriff and dep. regiar., sup. ct., Singapore, 1898, but still ag. 2nd mag., Singapore; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Feb., 1900; ag. collr. ld. rev., Penang, May, 1900; ag. sec. to H. Cr. for Fed. Malay States, May, 1901; ag. sen. dist. offr. Prov. Wellesley, May, 1902; collector of land rev. and regiar. of deeds, Penang, Aug., 1902; offl. assignee, S.S., Sept., 1903; sen. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Feb., 1907; ag. 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1907; ag. inspr. of prisons, S.S., Apr., 1909; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1909; ag. dist. judge (civil), Singapore, Feb., 1910; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Singapore, Apr., 1910; res., Malacca, July, 1910; ag. British adviser, Kedah, June, 1914.

WOLFF, ERNEST CHARTERIS HOLFORD.—F.M.S. civ. ser.; b. 1875; ed. at Merchiston and Trin. Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1897; cadet, Pahang, F.M.S., Nov., 1897; passed final exam. in Malay, May, 1899; ag. treas., supt. of posts and tele., and collr. of stamp duties, Pahang, Jan., 1898; ag. asst. dist. offr., Pekan, May, 1899; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, May, 1901; sec. to ditto, Oct., 1901; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Jan., 1903; dist. treas., Telok Anson, Jan., 1905, but still ag. sec. to res., N. Semb.; sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1905; ag. asst. sec. to res.-gen., F.M.S., May, 1906; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1909, but still ag. asst. sec. to res.-gen.

WOLLASTON, SIR HARLEY NEWTON PHILLIPS, K.C.M.G. (1912); C.M.G. (1907); I.S.O. (1903); LL.D., J.P.—B. 1846; apptd. (after exam.), to dept. of trade and custs., 1863; grad. with hons., in law, Melb. Univ., 1884; called to the bar, 1884, and apptd. counsel to marine bd., Victoria, which apptmt. he held till 1901; promoted to be ch. clk. of dept., 1899; apptd. permanent head of Victorian dept. of trade and cust., 1891; compt.-gen. of Commonwealth dept., of trade and cust., and permanent head, 1901-1911; represented Australia at Impl. shipping conference, London, 1907; retired, 1911.

WOLSELEY-BOURNE, J. F.—B. 1878; served in S. African war, 1899-1902 (Queen's medal and five clasps; King's medal and two clasps); formerly 2nd lieut., Princess Charlotte of Wales' (Royal Berks Regt.); lieut., S. African constab.; afterwards in Swaziland civ. serv.; asst. supt. of police, E. Africa Prot., Jan., 1911.

WOOD, A. E.—Ed. at Liverpool Coll.; scholar of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1903; 1st mods.; 2nd lit. hum.; cadet, Hong Kong, 1907; offr. for exam. of male assisted emigrants, July to Nov., 1908; ag. asst. regiar.-gen., Nov., 1908, to Apr., 1909, and in May-June, 1909; passed cadet, 1st Apr., 1910; ag. asst. regiar.-gen., Apr.-May, 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., N.T., 18th May, 1910, to Dec., 1911; 2nd asst. regiar.-gen., 4th July, 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., South dist., New Territories, Dec., 1911, to Dec., 1912; passed in Hakka, 14th June, 1912; title of asst. regiar.-gen. altered to 2nd asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 25th July, 1913; ag. 1st asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 7th Nov., 1913; ag. chief asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1st Jan., 1914.

WOOD, DAVID.—Ed. Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1891; supt. of accts., corres., and stores, P.W.D., 1894 was sec. to comm. on housing the Chinese during the plague, 1894; hon. sec. and ag. hon. treas., Queen Victoria Diamond Jubilee comtee.; ag. govt. assessor of taxes, in addition to P.W.D. duties, July, 1900, to Nov., 1901, May to Oct., 1902, Oct., 1905, to Oct., 1906, and from 10th Sept., 1912, to 25th Sept., 1913; dir. of W. and O. pensions fund, July, 1906; J.P., 1902.

WOOD, FIELD-MARSHAL SIR HENRY EVELYN, V.C., G.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1838; entered the navy 1852, and the army 1855; served in the Crimea; received the V.C. for his gallantry in hunting down the rebels in the Seronge jungles; admstd. the govt. of Natal from Feb. to Dec., 1881; commanded the forces in Natal and the Transvaal, and was a mem. of the roy. coman. for the settlement of the affairs of the Transvaal, 1881, but dissented from the report; commanded the 2nd brigade, 2nd div., in Egypt, 1882; apptd. by the Khedive to the command of the Egyptian troops, 1883; served in the Nile expdn., 1884; comdr. of the E. dist., 1885; qrtmr.-gen. of the forces, Oct., 1893; apptd. to command of army corps, 1901.
WOOD, HENRY FREDERICK.—B. 1878; apptd. clk. to Uganda rly. comtee., 7th Aug., 1897; transfd. to C.O. as supplementary clk., 1st Apr., 1905.

WOOD, JOHN R.—Ed. City of Lond. schl. and Camb.; foundation scholar of Jesus Coll., 1896; 1st cl. class. tripods, 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; regiar. of land ct., Nov., 1900; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, May to July, 1902; J.P., 1902; passed cadet, 1902; mem. land ct., 1903-4; asst. land offr. in N. T., Jan., 1905; ag. asst. reg.-gen., Apr. to Aug., 1905; mem. of squatters' bd., May, 1906; ag. 2nd pol. mag., Mar., 1908; dep. registr. and appraiser, sup. ct., Nov., 1908; 2nd pol. mag., 21st Mar., 1909; ag. dir. of educn., Nov., 1911, to Sept., 1912.

WOOD, JOSEPH.—B. 1843; ed. at Mt. Allison Coll., Sackville, New Brunswick; graduated, 1863; M.A., 1866; called to the bar, New Brunswick, 1866; afterwards entered mercantile business; head of firm (Wood & Son); mayor of Sackville, 1893 to 1908; unsuccessful candidate for provincial assembly, 1878; elec. to H. of C., 1882; re-elec., 1887 and 1891; called to the Senate, 1895; lieut.-gov. of New Brunswick, 1912.
WOOD, LESLIE WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylor's schl. and Queen's coll., Camb.; 1st cls. math. tripod pt. I.; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 18th Aug., 1914.

WOODCOCK, GEORGE ALBERT.—Ed. at St. John's Coll., Battersea; apptd. asst. master Victoria Coll., Hong Kong, 15th Mar., 1890; final bar, Gray's Inn, 1897; ag. sec., sanitary bd., Mar., 1900, to July, 1901; sec. of san. bd., July, 1901; J.P., 1902; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 5th July, 1905; ag. dep. registr. and acctnt., sup. ct. and commr. for oaths, Dec., 1908; 1st clk., magistracy, Sept., 1909; sec. to licensing bd., 17th Dec., 1909; ag. asst. land offr., Nov., 1911; ag. asst. crown solr., 18th Mar., 1913; mag., 17th Oct., 1913.

WOODE, BENJAMIN TREGASKIS, M.R.P.S.—B. 1869; ed. at Wesleyan High school, Freetown, S. Leone; jun. clk., Queen's advocate off., S. Leone, Apr., 1890, to July, 1891; cust. offr., Forcados, Benin and Warri dists. in Oil Rivers Prot., Aug., 1891, to July, 1892; cust. offr., Bonny and New Calabar dists., July, 1892, to Aug., 1892; postmtr., Bonny, N.C.P., from Aug., 1892, to Nov., 1894; beachmaster, storekeeper, and supervisor of workmen at Bonny and Cawthorne channel, Jan., 1893, to Nov., 1894; transf'd. to Old Calabar, to take up duty there, Mar., 1895; postmtr., Brass, May, 1895, to Apr., 1899; Opobo, Apr., 1899, to Apr., 1901; Bonny, from May, 1901; Opobo, Dec., 1901, to Aug., 1902; Bonny, 1902 to 1905; Calabar, Aug., 1905, to Sept., 1906; Forcados, from Sept., 1906; Warri, 1907.

WOODFORD, CHARLES MORRIS, C.M.G. (1912).—B. 1853; ed. at Tonbridge schl.; clk. in rec. gen.'s off., Fiji, 1883; ag. consul., Samoa, 1895; dep. commr. for W. Pacific, 1895; res. commr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1897.

WOODHOUSE, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1867; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; math. trip.; M.A. of the Middle Temple; Campbell-Foster prizeman, 1910; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1892; asst. collr. of cust., Jaffna, Dec., 1892; Trincomalee, Dec., 1895; dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1897, and in Mar., 1899; addtl. dist. judge, etc., Matara, Oct., 1898; addtl. pol. mag., Matare, Mar., 1899; pol. mag., Galle, Dec., 1901; dist. judge, Negombo, Feb., 1903; Batticaloa, Dec., 1905; dist. judge, Matara, June, 1912; dist. judge, Kurunegala, May, 1913.
WOODS, HENRY J. B.—Mem. exec. coun. and survr.-gen., Newfoundland, 1889-1893, and from 1895 to 1897; mem. exec. coun., 1900; postmtr.-gen., 1902.

WOODS, PERCY SINCLAIR COMYN.—B. 1879; ed. St. Michael's Schl., Lyme Regis, and Durham Univ.; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., Somerset House, 1st Jan., 1903; asst. auditor, Lagos and S. Nigeria, 29th Apr., 1905; dep. ch. acctnt., G. Coast rly., 30th Jan., 1909; ag. ch. acctnt., 21st Mar., to 26th Sept., 1909; promoted rly. acctnt. on abolition of office of ch. acctnt., 31st Jan., 1910.

WOODS, WILFRID WENTWORTH.—B. 1876; B.A. Oxon.; 2nd cls. final hon. schl., mod. hist., 1901; clk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., 2nd Sept., 1901; asst. auditor, G. Coast, 22nd Feb., 1902; local auditor, N. Nigeria, 30th Oct., 1904; asst. col. auditor, Ceylon, 8th Feb., 1908; ag. col. auditor on several occasions.

WOODWARD, LIONEL MARROTT.—B. 1864; ed. at Harrow, and Trin. Coll., Camb. (scholar); 1st class honours, classical tripos; B.A., 1886; M.A., 1892; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final exam. in Tamil, July, 1890; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore Nov., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Apr., 1891; 3rd mag., Penang, Apr., 1891; 3rd mag. and asst. Indian imigrn. agt., Singapore, July, 1892; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1893; sheriff and dep. registr. sup. et., Singapore, Mar., 1896; 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1898; ag. offl. assignee, S. Sttlmts., and regiar. of deeds, Apr., 1898, to June, 1899; ag. sen. dist. offr., Prov. Well., Oct., 1900; asst. registrar, supreme court, Penang, May, 1902; ag. solr.-gen., May, 1903, dep. pub. proscr., Mar., 1904; seconded for spec. serv. in connection with expropriation of Tanjong Pagar Dock Co., Ltd., May, 1905; judi. commr., F.M.S. Jan., 1906.

WOOLLEY, HOWARD MARK.—B. 1879; apptd. after open compet. exam., to G.P.O., London, and served in different depts.; asst. postmtr.-gen., N. Nigeria, 22nd Apr., 1905; deputy P.M.G., 1st Apr., 1908; P.M.G., 31st Oct., 1910.
WOOSNAM, R. B.—Game warden, K.A.P., 1910.

WORSLEY, RALPH MARCUS MEABURN, B.A. Oxon.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1st Nov., 1910; attached to Colombo Kachchell, Dec., 1910; attached to col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1911; extra office asst. to govt. agt., W. Prov. Oct., 1911; asst. land settm't. offr., Jan., 1912; office asst. to govt. agt., Central Prov., July, 1914.

WRAY, CHARLES JAMES HILL, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Surgeon, imigrn. service, Queensland, 1876; health offr., Brisbane, 1879; med. offr. penal estabmt., St. Helena, and boys' reformatory, Lytton, and visiting surg. benevolent asylum, Dunwich, 1879; inspr. of orphanages, 1882; sec. to central bd. of health, 1884.

WRIGHT, ARTHUR EDGAR.—B. 1880; Fellow of the Surveyors' Institution; ed. at Tiffin's Endowed Schl., Kingston-on-Thames; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 5th May, 1905; exec. engrnr., 13th Apr., 1911.

WRIGHT, EDWARD JAMES.—B. 1855; ed. at Cranbrook, England; civil engrnr.; served in N.W. mounted pol., Canada, 1866-1904; sec., dept. of educn., and sec., coun. of educn., 1895-1901; dep. territorial sec., 1901-1905; dep. prov. sec. and regiar. of joint stock companies, Sask., from 1905.

WRIGHT, ERIC BLACKWOOD.—B. 1860; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin (B.A., 1882); 1st jun. med. in history, polit. econ. and law and silver medallist); LL.B. (1st cls. in honours); LL.D. (stip. cond.) prizeman in German and French; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1885; holder of two 1st scholarships of 100 guineas each and 50 guineas respectively, and a 2nd scholarship of 50 guineas at the Middle Temple; prizeman of coun. of legal educn.; ch. just., Seychelles, 13th May, 1905; admtd. govt., May to Nov., 1907; stip. mag., Trinidad, 1909; 2nd puisne judge, 1913; author of "W. Wright's Law of Principal and Agent," 1st edit., 1893, 2nd edit., 1901; and the 2nd edit. of "Saunders on Negligence"; author of a translation with notes of the French Civil Code, 1908.

WRIGHT, H. PELLEW.—Served in S. Africa war in I.Y. and S.A.C., 1899-1902 (Queen's medal and 3 clasps, King's medal and 2 clasps); staff offr. to O.C., Riet River div., O.R.C.; dist. commr., Edenburg and other districts, O.R.C., 1903-1908; asst. dist. commr., Uganda Prot., 15th Oct., 1908; reeved Royal Humane Society's medal for saving life.
WRIGHT, H. O.—B. 1862; out Island commr. (3rd div.), Bahamas, 1909.

WRIGHT, REGINALD BARTON.—Assoc. of City Guilds cent. instr. engrnr.; late asst. survr., trigonometrical survey, Perak, Malay Peninsula; late asst. engrnr., Uganda rly.; chief survr., E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1903; land offr., May, 1903.
WRIGHT, WM.—B. 1855; clk. to pub. trustee, S. Aust., 1885; ch. clk. to pub. trustee, and ch. elk., succession duties dept., 1891; pub. trustee and curator of convicts' estates, 1903.

WRIGHT, W. S.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin (B.A.), 1905; 1st place, LL.B. degree; cert. of hon. incorp. law soc.; asst. collr., Uganda, 1907; 1st cls. mag., Uganda, 1908-9; reglar. of high ct., E.A.P., Aug., 1909; is also reglar. of joint stock companies, sec. of inventions and designs, reglar. of trade marks, reglar. of bills of sale, reglar. of newspapers and books, and reglar. of the ct. of appeal for Eastern Africa.

WRIGHTSON, WALSH, C.M.G. (1900), M.I.C.E.—B. 1852; served articles with Chas. Fowler, Leeds; asst. to boro' engineer, Leeds, 1872; dist. engineer, P.W.D., Ceylon, 1875; seconded for serv. on Nannuoya Rly. extensions, 1883; spec. serv. to restore Kalawewa irrigation tank, 1884; on successful completion, promoted prov. engr., N.W. Prov., 1888; asst. to dir. pub. wks., 1892; prov. engr., E. Prov., 1894; dir. pub. wks., Trinidad, and offl. mem. legis. coun., Jan., 1895; apptd. mem. ex. coun., June, 1895; rea. engr., harbour, water and sewerage wks., 1896; ret., 1907.

WYATT, ROBT.—B. 1870; Lond. Metro. pol., 1891 to 1895; B. Bond. pol., June, 1895; asst. supt., Feb., 1902; ag. supt., May, 1902, to May, 1903; ag. dist. commr., Orange Walk, July to Aug., 1903; ag. dist. commr., Corosol, 26th Apr. to 11th July, 1905; ditto, Cays, Feb., 1906; ag. supt. pol., 14th July, 1905; J.P. for Colony; Hythe extra certif., 1899; supt. of pol., 1st Apr., 1906; certif. of proficiency for field offs. with spec. distinction, schl. of instr., Chelsea barracks, 1907; certif. for management of English convict and local prisons, 1907; attached to London fire brigade for training, 1907.

WYLIE, THOS. ALEXANDER.—B. 1866; asst. traffic supt., Ceylon rlys., 17th May, 1888; ag. traffic supt., on several occasions; ag. acctnt., 27th Apr., 1893; acctnt., 11th May, 1894; traffic supt., 1st Feb., 1906; ag. gen. man., 16th Apr., 1907.
WYNNE, HON. AGAR.—M.L.C., Victoria, 1889; mem., H. of R., C. of A., 1906-1914; postmaster-gen., C. of A., June, 1913.

YOUNG, ALFRED KARNEY.—B.A., Magd. Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., May, 1889; 2nd clk., ool. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., Aug., 1891; priv. sec. to Sir A. Maloney, and clk. of councils, 1892; priv. sec. to admnstr. of the govt., 1895; ag. regiar. gen., 1896-7; dist. commr., Orange Walk, 1896; regiar. gen., June, 1898; ag. supt. of police, May to Nov., 1899; J.P. for col.; crown prosecutor, legal adviser, and police mag., Seychelles, 23rd Sept., 1903; atty.-gen., B.C. Africa Prot., 17th Mar., 1906; stip. mag., Trinidad, 1908; ch. just., Seychelles, Aug., 1909; admnstd. govt., Apl. to Nov., 1911, and from 16th to 27th Dec., 1912; atty.-gen., Fiji, May, 1914.

YOUNG, CAPT. SIR ARTHUR HENDERSON, K.C.M.G. (1908); C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1854; late 27th Inniskillings; ed. at Edin. Acad., Rugby, and Sandhurst; local comdt., mil. pol., Kyrenia, Aug., 1878; asst. commr., Paphos, Nov., 1878; commr., Paphos, Sept., 1879; ditto, Famagusta, Jan., 1882; dir. of survey and prin. forest offr., 1892; mem. legis. coun., 1892; ch. sec., 1894; admnstd. the govt., July to Dec., 1895, Jan. to Apr., 1898; sent on special mission to St. Vincent in connection with relief work, Nov., 1902; col. sec., S. Sttlmts., June, 1906; admtd. govt., Feb. to May, 1909; ch. sec., F.M.S., 1910; gov., S. Sttlmts., 8th Aug., 1911; assumed govt., 2nd Sept., 1911; is also high commr. for Malay States and for Brunei and British Agent for N. Borneo and Sarawak.
YOUNG, FREDERICK WILLIAM, LL.B.—Mem. of H. of A., S. Australia, 1909; commr. of crown lands and immigrtn., 1912; agt.-gen. for S. Australia, 1915.
YOUNG, HENRY ALFRED.—Ed. at Derby Schl. and Edin. Univ.; called to the bar (Scotland), July, 1899; asst. crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 22nd June, 1906.

YOUNG, HON. HENRY EGSON, B.A., M.D., C.M., LL.D.—B. 1867; ed. Queen's Univ., Kingston, and McGill Univ., Montreal; elec. to B. Columbia legis. for Atlin, 1903; re-elec. 1907; sworn of the exec. coun. as prov. sec., min. of educn., Feb., 1907; re-elec. 1907 and 1909; hon. LL.D., Toronto Univ., 1907.

YOUNG, MARK AITCHISON.—B. 1886; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; off. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Aug., 1910; ditto, Kandy, Nov., 1911; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1913; 4th asst. col. sec., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd ditto, Feb., 1914; 2nd ditto and clk. of leg. coun., Sep., 1914.

YOUNG, ROBT. HEYDEN.—B. 1858; sub-survr.; survey dept., S. Sttlmts., 28th Jan., 1873; computer and draftsman, Malacca, 1st May, 1881; dist. survr., Oct., 1882; ch. survr., 12th July, 1888; senior survr., Jan., 1891; senior survr. and supervisor of survey schl., 1902; suptg. re-survey of Singapore, 4th May, 1903; senior survey offr., Dec., 1903, but cont. to act as supt. of re-survey and supervisor of survey schl.

YOUNG, WILLIAM DOUGLAS, C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. at Charterhouse; clk. in govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1877; ch. clk., 1889; ag. priv. sec. on several occasions, 1877-1893, to govrs. of B. Guiana, Trinidad, and G. Coast; ag. asst. govt. sec., B. Guiana, 1892-3-4-5; ag. govt. sec., Dec., 1892; ag. govt. agt. N.W. dist., 1895; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1893; ag. col. sec., 1896-7-8-9, 1900-01; admtd. govt., Mauritius, Aug., 1897; commr., Turks and Caicos Islands, 1901; admstr., Dominica, 19th Apr., 1906; ag. govt., Leeward Is., 1909; admnstr., St. Lucia, 1913; gov., Falkland Is., 1914.
